metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to exercise quality control unit responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a risk assessment regarding the change in the active ingredient for (b)(4) drug products

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: You lacked process validation data to demonstrate that you have adequately validated the manufacturing processes for drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a risk assessment and any follow-up actions to be taken for the distributed drug products produced without performing any process validation studies

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate stability program with data from long-term or accelerated storage conditions, demonstrating that the drug products remain acceptable throughout the labeled expiry period.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective risk assessment showing how you will ensure that marketed drug products meet stability specifications throughout their shelf life

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your claim concerning compounded semaglutide products is false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: The claim identified in this letter put you on notice of our concerns but does not represent an exhaustive list of misbranding violations.
deficiencies.required_action: Please be advised, the receipt in interstate commerce of misbranded drugs, and the delivery or proffered delivery thereof, is also a violation of section 301(c) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. § 331(c)].

deficiencies.title: Failure to prevent recurrence
deficiencies.description: You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.
deficiencies.evidence: It is your responsibility to ensure that your operations comply with all requirements of federal law, including FDA regulations.
deficiencies.required_action: Please take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product).

conclusion: Within fifteen (15) working days of receipt of this letter, please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints

deficiencies.required_action: Conduct the necessary investigation and submit MDRs as needed by February 28, 2023

metadata.company_name: Pushclean Technology Plastik Sanayi Ve Ticaret Ltd. Sirketi
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures OTC drug products, such as (b)(4) wipes. Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested your finished drug products prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: Testing is an essential part of CGMP to ensure that the drug products you manufacture conform to all pre-determined quality attributes appropriate for their intended use. Drug products must be tested for identity and strength of the active ingredient, prior to release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence to assure that your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested the identity of incoming components used in the manufacture of your drug products. Specifically, you failed to conduct identity and component testing for (b)(4), and you failed to conduct conductivity and total organic carbon testing on your (b)(4) used to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that your components conform to appropriate specifications prior to use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

deficiencies.title: 3. Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products
deficiencies.description: The records and information you provided demonstrate that your quality unit (QU) did not effectively exercise its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations. For example, your QU failed to establish an adequately validated manufacturing process and to adequately ensure that the stability data supported the expiry of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU is responsible for fully exercising its authority and responsibilities. FDA is concerned that your QU may not be conducting appropriate oversight regarding CGMP operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate processes whose results cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test
deficiencies.description: The water leak test method used for in-process and final inspection, complaint investigations, and for tests performed during validations and revalidations has not been validated according to your firm's own procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: Document No. M0666.0001, IQ/OQ/PQ Protocol for Validation of Test method for Leak in water Test, version 1.0
deficiencies.required_action: Revised protocol should be provided for review; completed validation reports should also be provided.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately review and investigate complaints involving the possible failure of a device to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm's complaint handling procedure allowed the closure of over 41,000 'Type 1' complaints without investigation.
deficiencies.evidence: Complaint 2204208
deficiencies.required_action: Plan to address devices which do not conform to specifications which have already been distributed; retrospective review of using revised procedures on complaint data involving leakage, loss of physical integrity, adhesive failure, and infection.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: CAPA 1627223 was initiated on February 23, 2023 to address a trend in complaints received for leaking Inset I and Inset II infusion set products.
deficiencies.evidence: Nonconformance reports related to leak failures on Inset I and II products
deficiencies.required_action: Plan to conduct retrospective review of complaints involving serious injury and/or death; plan to implement procedures to regularly audit complaint and 'non-complaint' records.

conclusion: We have reviewed your responses and conclude that they are not adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test that the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure, as required by 21 CFR 820.75(a).
deficiencies.description: During puck assembly of the Freestyle Libre 3 sensor, uncontrolled adjustments can result in damaged and nonconforming product.
deficiencies.evidence: Review of the assembly process validation on the lines used in the manufacture of the sensor pucks found no defined setting or acceptable range for the equipment used to set or change the puck alignment.
deficiencies.required_action: Update the Qualification protocols/reports to formally qualify the use and effectiveness of the setup tool (thickness) for Puck lines

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action, as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a).
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not adequately identify the actions needed to correct and prevent recurrence of nonconforming product and other quality problems.
deficiencies.evidence: After your firm opened a CAPA on 4/1/2025, it did not identify the actions needed to correct and prevent recurrence of broken components or connections due to lack of defined specification for the alignment.
deficiencies.required_action: Clarify the technical analysis and strengthen guidance for action plan implementation and verification

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Notice of Unlawful Sale of Unapproved Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.bigpharmausa.com introduces into interstate commerce unapproved and misbranded opioid drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims that provide evidence of the intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of these products as drugs for human use include the following: ...
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: A drug is misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act if its labeling fails to bear adequate directions for use.
deficiencies.evidence: Prescription drugs, as defined in section 503(b)(1) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 353(b)(1)], include those that, because of their toxicity or other potentiality for harmful effect, and/or the method of their use, and/or the collateral measures necessary for their use, are not safe for use except under supervision of a practitioner licensed by law to administer them.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Offering Prescription Drugs Without a Prescription
deficiencies.description: By offering the aforementioned drugs without requiring a prescription, www.bigpharmausa.com jeopardizes patient safety and misbrands the drugs under section 503(b)(1) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Dispensing a prescription drug without a prescription is an act which results in the drug being misbranded while held for sale, in violation of section 301(k) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 331(k)].
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering prescription drugs without a prescription.

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.bigpharmausa.com because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase misbranded and unapproved new drugs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and implement procedures for corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.description: Your firm was found to have 544 open tickets for software defects, including 111 categorized as Catastrophic or Severe patient harm.
deficiencies.evidence: You provided an Issue and Defect Tracking procedure and stated that closure of “Tickets” will be tracked in a risk-prioritized manner by June 30, 2025 and conduct training. You state that you established a quality metric that monitors the timely closure of “Defects” and presented to the FY2024 Q3 Quarterly Management Review meeting to determine the definition of metric.
deficiencies.required_action: You have not provided evidence of implementation of these corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: For 8 complaints during the December 2023 to January 2024 time period, none of the complaints were evaluated for MDR reporting.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated that complaint investigations were not comprehensive with event details, patient or device information, and components linked to complaints were not always requested to be returned. You state that your database has limitations in making updates or corrections to Case Notes details.
deficiencies.required_action: You have not provided evidence of implementation of corrective actions for these deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure of design validation to include adequate risk analysis
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s System Hazard Analysis for the MedStation ES System failed to take into consideration higher severity risks such as a complaint reporting a patient death possibly due to delay in accessing medications.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated that all Pyxis systems are IEC 60601 compliant except MedBank because MedBank is not intended for patients as it is used in a pharmacy. You produced a certifying organization (UL) email apparently stating MedBank is a medical device but does not fall within the scope of IEC 60601-1.
deficiencies.required_action: You have not established a process for assessing the exploitability of cybersecurity vulnerabilities and the severity of patient harm if the vulnerability were to be exploited.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to the FDA
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed or refused to furnish material or information respecting the device that is required by or under section 519 of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360i, and 21 CFR Part 803 – Medical Device Reporting.
deficiencies.evidence: The MDR#2016493-2020-00001 received by FDA describes an adverse event where the patient sustained a serious injury resulting from your firm’s same device malfunction. This report establishes precedence that the referenced malfunction would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury, if it were to recur.
deficiencies.required_action: You have not provided information included for the complaint that justifies that the referenced malfunction would not be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury.

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm’s response cannot be determined at this time.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including semaglutide, with claims that are false or misleading.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: • “Weekly injectable GLP-1 with the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy” • “Clinically proven ingredients”

deficiencies.title: Introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of misbranded products
deficiencies.description: The introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of these misbranded products is a prohibited act.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: • “Weekly injectable GLP-1 with the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy” • “Clinically proven ingredients”

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately document the results of the device software validation
deficiencies.description: The documentation for the validation of the FANhp/PyloPlus device software revision 1.49 was not adequately documented.
deficiencies.evidence: No documentation of the software revision plan, no documentation and approval of the input software specification requirements, no approved verification and validation test plan with acceptance criteria and methods to be used to test the software, and no adequate documentation of the testing results.
deficiencies.required_action: Revise SOP 'Software Development' and SOP 'Software Change and Maintenance Process' to comply with applicable regulations, revise previous software documentation for FANhp software version 1.49, train personnel on new SOPs, evaluate all previous software revisions to ensure they have been properly validated and documented, re-validate the software validation for the FANhp software version 1.49.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: The Corrective and Preventive Measures SOP does not include adequate requirements which identify the sources to be analyzed and the statistical methods used to detect recurring non-conformities.
deficiencies.evidence: No documentation of the root cause investigation for CAPA 2022-13, no available documentation of the investigation into the failure using the 'CAPA procedure', and the Form record for CAPA 2022-13 was signed prior to the completion of all corrective actions.
deficiencies.required_action: Revise SOP to insert requirements for sources to be analyzed and statistical methods to detect recurring non-conformities, develop a procedure/form sheet that all activities and corrective actions are identified and documented, train employees on the new SOP.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: The SOP does not include adequate requirements which ensure timely and uniform processing of complaints, nor does the procedure ensure oral complaints are documented upon receipt and that complaints are adequately evaluated as to whether the complaint may be subject to Medical Device Reporting (MDR) requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: No documentation of the investigation into the failure using the 'complaint process', no record of the investigation including the dates and results of the investigations, the corrective actions and any replies to the complainant are recorded in the complaint record.
deficiencies.required_action: Revise the complaint process with inclusion of a reporting decision tree, redefine responsibilities for complaint processing, implementation of all requirements of EU regulations and 21 CFR 820.198, re-evaluation of FANhp (PyloPlus) complaint cases since the PMA in 2020, training of all employees that any feedback that could indicate a quality problem within the company or on the products must be documented and forwarded to the complaint handler.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate software used as part of the quality system for its intended use
deficiencies.description: The Excel spreadsheets used to record complaint records and Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) activities have not been validated to ensure the records are adequately and accurately recorded.
deficiencies.evidence: Changes made to the complaint and CAPA records are not retained with identification of the change, the date of the change or the identification of the individual making the change, and the spreadsheets allow records to be deleted without any records of the deletion, the rationale for the deletion, or the identification of the individual deleting such records.
deficiencies.required_action: List and evaluate all Excel spreadsheets used in their Quality Management System for correct documentation methods, search for a suitable method to keep records in conformity with regulations, update the SOP to include new procedures and requirements for document control, file complaints and CAPA in paper form until further notice, train employees who work with Excel spreadsheets.

conclusion: Your response dated November 25, 2022 is not adequate.

metadata.company_name: Prime Vitality, Inc. dba Prime Peptides

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various products as “peptides” including, “Semaglutide” and “Retatrutide.” These products are unapproved new drugs introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act).
deficiencies.evidence: Despite statements on your product labeling marketing your products for “research purposes only” and “not for human consumption or clinical use,” evidence obtained from your website establishes that your products are intended to be drugs for human use.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations. Include an explanation of each step being taken to prevent the recurrence of violations, as well as copies of related documentation.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to conduct microbiological testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform microbiological testing for your AllShield E2 Sanitizing Hand Soap. Batch records for lot 25009 indicated you did not take the required sample for microbiological testing, and quality assurance was unable to explain why the sample was not collected or the testing performed.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response acknowledges that you lacked established product specifications, including microbiological limits, for your AllShield E2 Sanitizing Hand Soap. You identify the root cause as “lack of product development and not following federal regulations.”
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial test methods and specifications used to analyze each lot of your drug products before making a lot disposition decision, and the associated written procedures.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish and follow a written testing program
deficiencies.description: You informed investigators during the inspection that you lacked a stability testing program for your AllShield E2 Sanitizing Hand Soap. Without appropriate stability studies, you do not have scientific evidence to support whether your drug products meet established specifications and retain their quality attributes throughout the use period.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response acknowledges that you did not perform stability testing for your AllShield E2 Sanitizing Hand Soap and commits to creating a product development plan that includes stability testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. Your remediated program should include, but not be limited to: ...

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate identity testing on your incoming components, including active ingredients, before using them in manufacturing your AllShield E2 Sanitizing Hand Soap.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response acknowledges that your raw materials procedure “is not robust enough to support raw material testing for drug products.” You plan to revise your raw materials procedure, create testing methods, and train your employees.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. ...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean, maintain, and sanitize equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately clean and maintain your filling rooms and tube filling equipment used to manufacture your (b)(4) and (b)(4) over-the-counter (OTC) drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The presence of mold and dirt on the walls in your filling and sealing rooms. Soiled and stained air handling units. Unidentified product buildup inside your (b)(4) manufacturing vessel and tube filling equipment. More than one drug product was found on the packaging line.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent retrospective assessment of your cleaning effectiveness to evaluate the scope of cross-contamination hazards. Include the identity of residues, other manufacturing equipment that may have been improperly cleaned, and an assessment whether cross-contaminated products may have been released for distribution.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain adequate space for orderly placement of equipment and materials
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s packaging and labeling operations are performed without adequate controls to prevent mix-ups and cross-contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: Employees performing manual packaging and labeling operations on the floor of the manufacturing room. Multiple drug products were packaged simultaneously without adequate separation and spacing. The drug products were also packaged without the use of batch records.
deficiencies.required_action: Your CAPA plan to improve packaging and labeling operations, including equipment and facility upgrades, effective preventive and corrective maintenance, enhanced performance monitoring, improved operations management oversight, and any other needed actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) did not provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Review and approval of written procedures for production and process controls that are designed to assure the quality of your drug products, i.e., process validation. Establishment of an adequate, ongoing stability program. Adequate batch production and control records that include complete documentation of the accomplishment of each significant step in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of the batch.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting. Your investigation should include: a detailed investigation protocol and methodology; a summary of all laboratories, manufacturing operations, and systems to be covered by the assessment; and a justification for any part of your operation that you propose to exclude.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to follow its CAPA procedure, SOP 128, Rev 15, including opening CAPAs as required by your procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: 179 patient false positive complaints (PATFP) associated with the use of certain third-party collection tubes used with your LeadCare II Blood Lead Test Kit
deficiencies.required_action: Open a CAPA to address at least 31 complaints of false positives after this date and did not take any further action, such as the opening of a new CAPA, as required by your procedure.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that the design requirements relating to a device are appropriate
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish design inputs for the Level 1 and Level 2 Controls used in LeadCare II Blood Lead Testing Systems as required by your Design Control Procedure, SOP 131.
deficiencies.evidence: Instructions for Use for LeadCare II Blood Lead Testing System states that Level 1 and Level 2 controls are intended to verify system performance and accuracy before testing patient results
deficiencies.required_action: Open a CAPA to evaluate the level 1 and 2 controls and will also include a retrospective review of complaints to further evaluate the issue.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not validate the cleaning and decontamination instructions written in the Instructions for Use (IFU) for Blood Lead Testing Systems - LeadCare II, LeadCare Ultra, and LeadCare Plus Analyzers.
deficiencies.evidence: Increase in false positive complaints associated with the use of certain third-party collection tubes used with your LeadCare II Blood Lead Test Kit
deficiencies.required_action: Open a CAPA to review and revise risk management files and perform retrospective review of ECO's to ensure risk management files will be updated appropriately.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate a process with a high degree of assurance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have validation data to demonstrate how calibration verification with only 50% of data can ensure reliable device performance across the reportable range for a diagnostic device.
deficiencies.evidence: Calibration verification procedure 65055 allows exclusion of up to 20% of test results when they exceed the reportable range
deficiencies.required_action: Open a CAPA to address the above items and ensure re-validation is performed, as necessary, when manufacturing operations are revised.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate incoming inspection procedures for capillary collection tubes used with your blood lead testing kits.
deficiencies.evidence: Sample of 100 units per lot size for packaging verification only
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product, including inspecting, testing, or verifying as conforming to specified requirements.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm's responses and concluded they are not adequate.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls

deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow an adequate written testing program
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have adequate stability data to demonstrate that the quality attributes of your drug products remain acceptable throughout the labeled expiry period.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. Your remediated program should include, but not be limited to: ...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your products are drugs as defined by section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act because they are intended to prevent, treat, or cure disease conditions and/or affect the structure or function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims observed on your website that establish the intended use of your products as drugs intended for human use include, but may not be limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: No FDA-approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your “Elily Veronvy” and “Elily Veronvy 40+” products are misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act because the product labels and/or websites include statements that misleadingly suggest that the drug products are approved or endorsed by FDA in some way.
deficiencies.evidence: For example: the webpage for “Elily Veronvy” and “Elily Veronvy 40+” includes the image of a blue circle with the claim, “FDA APPROVED” along with the statement, “Veronvy Metabolic Boost Drops . . . was approved by the Food and Drug Administration last year.”
deficiencies.required_action: Therefore, these products are misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act because their labeling is false or misleading.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you an opportunity to address them. Failure to adequately address this matter may result in legal action including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain design history file (DHF) for each of the products that GH manufactures, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(j).
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established a Design History File (DHF) for its line of Class II Monopolar Laparoscopic Instruments with reusable instrument tips, exported and intended for the US market.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection on July 20, 2022, of your firm's CNC Department, the investigator identified multiple raw materials that according to staff had not gone through your firm's incoming acceptance process, but which had been labeled with your (b)(4) system generated inventory sticker and staged next to approved raw materials also labeled with your (b)(4) inventory sticker.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response does not mention a plan to analyze existing records to determine whether there is a risk that the inappropriately received raw material impacted in-process or finished device specifications and to determine the risk that nonconforming product was released for distribution (including actions to be taken to mitigate such risks).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product as required by 21 CFR 820.80(b).
deficiencies.description: During the inspection on July 20, 2022, of your firm's CNC Department, the investigator identified multiple raw materials that according to staff had not gone through your firm's incoming acceptance process, but which had been labeled with your (b)(4) system generated inventory sticker and staged next to approved raw materials also labeled with your (b)(4) inventory sticker.
deficiencies.evidence: Per your firm's staff, the (b)(4) inventory sticker identifies approved raw materials, and these raw materials are used in the manufacturing of medical devices by your firm, however they were not properly identified to ensure that only product which has passed the required acceptance activities are distributed or used.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response does not mention a plan to analyze existing records to determine whether there is a risk that the inappropriately received raw material impacted in-process or finished device specifications and to determine the risk that nonconforming product was released for distribution (including actions to be taken to mitigate such risks).

deficiencies.title: Monopolar Laparoscopic Instruments are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g) for the device.
deficiencies.description: The Monopolar Laparoscopic Instruments are also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution this device with significant changes or modifications that require a premarket notification to FDA under section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k), and 21 CFR 807.81(a)(3).
deficiencies.evidence: The 510(k) Summary for K171523 includes, in part, the following Device Description (emphasis added): The Monopolar Single Use Laparoscopic Instruments of Getsch + Hiller is available in two different designs. It is available as system consisting of two parts and as a system consisting of four parts.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm needs to submit information to obtain approval or clearance for the device, which can be found on the Internet at https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/device-advice-comprehensive-regulatory-assistance/how-study-and-market-your-device

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to verify a process that cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedures.
deficiencies.description: The validation activities and results, including the date and signature of the individual(s) approving the validation and where appropriate the major equipment validated, shall be documented.
deficiencies.evidence: Catheter wash limitation process justifications dated October 31,2018 and June 10, 2021
deficiencies.required_action: CAPA to address Observation 1 and identified four root causes and training corrective actions, and conducted a retrospective review of processes requiring validation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for implementing and maintaining corrective and preventive actions.
deficiencies.description: The firm's Supplier Corrective Action Request (SCAR) Process requires that the firm open a SCAR when it is determined that a supplier corrective action is needed for an NCR.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of nonconformance reported quality problems where no SCAR was opened by your firm as required by its procedure
deficiencies.required_action: CAPA to address Observation 5 and identified five root causes. Your firm updated its procedure to provide clarification to its employees as to when a SCAR needs to be initiated.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.description: The evaluation of nonconformance shall include a determination of the need for an investigation and notification of the persons or organizations responsible for the nonconformance.
deficiencies.evidence: Nonconforming product is identified as a 'reject' during production and quality inspection
deficiencies.required_action: CAPA to address Observation 3 and implemented an IT solution to label those samples separately as 'destroyed' instead of 'reject'.

conclusion: Please notify this office in writing, within 15 working days from the date of receipt of this letter, that you have taken or will take corrective action to address these violations. Failure to correct these violations may result in further regulatory action.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures to prevent contamination or product by substances that may have an adverse effect on product quality
deficiencies.description: Specifically, CAPA 2404, dated 08/09/2024, was opened in response to a BSI finding identifying that your firm does not perform LAL endotoxin testing for your SurgiWrap devices.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm stated in pre-market correspondence to the FDA on 11/21/2020 that you would test SurgiWrap FROST for endotoxins to ensure that it is produced with endotoxin levels at or below FDA accepted limits of ≤ 20 EU/device. Additionally, your firm also stated to the FDA that you included a product lot-release endotoxin specification of ≤ (b)(4) for the SurgiWrap FROST.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should evaluate your manufacturing process to identify why there is a large uptick in endotoxin between the (b)(4) test presented in (b)(4) and (b)(4) in your current process. Your firm should take appropriate measures to enhance endotoxin monitoring to ensure your mitigation measures result in acceptable endotoxin levels.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints by a formally designated unit
deficiencies.description: Specifically, Your firm’s procedure, Complaint Process, Rev. T (effective date: 09/2022), Rev. U (effective date: 05/2023), Rev. V (effective date: 10/05/2023), Rev. W (effective date: 04/30/2025), is inadequate, in that, your firm has not adequately defined and documented complaint handling requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, Complaint C23004, dated 05/15/2023 alleges 8 out of 10 patients developed abdominal swelling after undergoing procedures involving SurgiWrap. Your firm asked your customer if they wanted to classify this complaint as feedback or a complaint.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should not reduce monitoring (e.g., reducing sample size to (b)(4) or raising the acceptance criteria to (b)(4) or (b)(4)).

deficiencies.title: Failure to report to us the information required by § 803.52 in accordance with the requirements of § 803.12(a)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, The information included for the complaint C23002 reasonably suggests that a patient experienced injuries (e.g., postoperative ileus) following the use of your firm’s SurgiWrap device.
deficiencies.evidence: As such, medical and surgical interventions such as nasogastric (NG) tube insertion, antibiotic therapy, and total parenteral nutrition (TPN) were administered and deemed necessary to prevent permanent impairment of a body function or permanent damage to a body structure. Your firm became aware of the event on February 1, 2023.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter. Failure to adequately address this matter may result in regulatory action being initiated by the FDA without further notice.

conclusion: A follow up inspection will be required to assure that corrections and/or corrective actions are adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Records collected during the inspection show that your firm received at least one lot of glycerin from a region with confirmed or suspected contamination with high levels of DEG and EG.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to conduct adequate finished product release testing for each batch of your oral drug products and hand sanitizer drug products...
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial test methods and specifications used to analyze each batch of your drug products before making a batch disposition decision, and the associated written procedures.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP...
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your QU failed to ensure: ... adequate training for employees engaged in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

conclusion: We have not received a response from your firm, after numerous requests, stating the actions you are taking to address the deficiencies identified during the inspection...

deficiencies.title: Unapproved and Misbranded Opioids

deficiencies.evidence: Tapentadol, marketed as “Tapentadol 100 MG (Aspadol)” and “Aspadol® Tab.”
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded opioids for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved and Misbranded Benzodiazepines

metadata.company_name: Guangzhou Four E’s Scientific Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity (21 CFR 211.22).
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) failed to have adequate oversight and procedures for your contract manufacturing organization (CMO) to ensure that they operate in compliance with CGMPs.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Your firm utilized a CMO, (b)(4), to perform manufacturing, processing, and packaging activities on your behalf. You received drug products from this CMO and declared your firm as the manufacturer on import records.
deficiencies.description: The records and information you provided demonstrate that your firm did not effectively exercise its CGMP responsibilities.
deficiencies.required_action: A complete evaluation of drug product specifications for the (b)(4) to determine whether the specifications are appropriate.

deficiencies.title: Your CMO, (b)(4), failed to perform manufacturing, processing, and packaging activities in compliance with CGMPs.
deficiencies.description: A 704(a)(4) records review of your CMO, (b)(4), found significant violations of CGMP including, but not limited to, inadequate testing of raw materials, incomplete process validation and inadequate testing of finished products.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/PMA/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: The entire device design was not validated to ensure that devices conform to defined user needs under actual or simulated use conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Document (b)(4), “Design Rules” version 13 defines Design Validation as, (b)(4). However, User-Assessment Procedures for URF-V3/V3R and UFR-P7/P7R Validation Report Document # (b)(4), dated October 4, 2017, states that only the, “(b)(4)” of the URF-V3/V3R and UFR-P7/P7R needed to be evaluated.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm decided they would re-validate the URF-P7 to cover all user needs. Your firm will determine the due date for the validation activities after your firm completes the design validation plan (due October 14, 2022).

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test that the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance
deficiencies.description: The bonding/gluing processes using the (b)(4) and (b)(4) adhesives during final assembly have not been validated.
deficiencies.evidence: Docking Work Instruction, Document # (b)(4), among others, follows the application of adhesive with “(b)(4)” which cannot fully verify the application of the adhesive.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm will conduct a health hazard evaluation (HHE) by August 31, 2022 to determine the health risk from the screws loosening. Your firm will update their process validation procedures with criteria for when a process is verifiable by October 31, 2022.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that Design History Record (DHR) for each batch, lot, or unit are maintained
deficiencies.description: The curing time (b)(4) hours or more) for the adhesive bonding/gluing process during final assembly was not documented on all 11 URF-P7 DHRs reviewed during the inspection.
deficiencies.evidence: AMS A001139W1 Device History Record (DHR) Management, rev 14; AMS A00112W1 Preparation of Assembly Work Standards, rev 28; AMS A05209W1 Controls of Process Parameters, rev 36; N-OIS M22003 Cleaning Work Element Standard, Rev 11.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm will update the DHR for the URF-P7 to include process parameters for the bonding and cleaning processes by August 3, 2022. Your firm will then review all DHRs for currently manufactured devices and remediate gaps as necessary.

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed or refused to furnish material or information respecting the device that is required by or under section 519 of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360i, and 21 CFR Part 803 - Medical Device Reporting.
deficiencies.evidence: During this inspection, your firm acknowledged that it had not established written MDR procedures.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm developed two documents titled “CoE Global MDR: Handling US MDR Reporting for Potential Adverse Event Complaints”, GSOP-8.15-7, Rev. AD, dated 7/13/2022 and “Medical Device Reporting Work Instruction”, OMBS W 8.2.3_01_001, Rev. 13, dated 7/26/2022.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Medical Device Reporting/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to FDA no later than 30 calendar days after the day that your firm received or otherwise became aware of information, from any source, that reasonably suggests that a device that your firm markets has malfunctioned and this device or a similar device that it markets would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury, if the malfunction were to recur
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to submit an MDR for each of the referenced complaints within the required 30-calendar day timeframe.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing, within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter, of the specific steps your firm has taken to correct the noted violation, as well as an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent the violation, or similar violation, from occurring again.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure process validation
deficiencies.description: You failed to define and document procedures for process validation. For example, you have no objective evidence that the following manufacturing and cleaning processes consistently produce a result or product that meets predetermined specifications with a high degree of assurance: Scanning and CAD Design, 3-Dimensional (3D) Printing, Post-printing Quality Control (QC) Validation, and Cleaning.
deficiencies.evidence: You utilize FDA listed 3D scanners and computer assisted design and manufacturing (CAD/CAM) software to manufacture each patient specific device (which has been validated by the manufacturer as part of their regulatory process) and that internal validation and verification of the patient specific device is performed as part of the QC Validation Process to ensure that the partial denture fits the patient model since each product has different patient specifications.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for rework
deficiencies.description: You failed to define and document procedures for rework which include requirements to retest and reevaluate reworked devices and document the rework and reevaluation, including adverse impact assessments on reworked devices, in the device history record (DHR).
deficiencies.evidence: Approximately 90% of manufactured ResetSmile Partial Denture Devices undergo final adjustment (rework) during the Final Quality Control (QC) step of the 'Quality Control Validation Process' which states that upon completion of the scan of the finished device, where nonconforming areas indicate the need for readjustment,
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on the progress of your identified corrective actions, including any supporting evidence of completion for review, timelines for completion, and plan for how you will address the inadequacies pointed out in your response.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for controlling nonconforming product
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements, including the identification, documentation, evaluation, segregation, and disposition of nonconforming product.
deficiencies.evidence: The 'Control of Nonconformity Process' procedure (8300-01; Rev. 0; Effective: 10/25/24) and 'Non-Confirming Material Report' form (8300-01-01; Rev. 0; Effective: 10/25/24), which were implemented after the preannouncement of the FDA inspection on 10/24/24, are inadequate in that there are no requirements to document nonconformances in sufficient detail to describe what failed to meet specification(s); evaluations of nonconformances to determine the need for investigations; and dispositions documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm's response and concluded that it is not adequate. You have not identified any corrective actions to ensure that (1) your procedures and forms require that nonconformances are recorded in sufficient detail to include what failed to meet specification(s) to allow determination of potential causes and evaluations of nonconformances to determine the need for investigations and (2) retrospective review of nonconformances, for a reasonable period of time, to document what failed to meet specifications, conduct evaluations to determine the need for investigations, and record dispositions.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)

deficiencies.title: Adulteration

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Compounded Drug Products Under the FDCA
deficiencies.description: Section 503A of the FDCA describes the conditions under which human drug products compounded by a licensed pharmacist in a State licensed pharmacy or a Federal facility, or a licensed physician, qualify for exemptions from three sections of the FDCA: compliance with current good manufacturing practice (CGMP) (section 501(a)(2)(B)); labeling with adequate directions for use (section 502(f)(1)); and FDA approval prior to marketing (section 505)
deficiencies.evidence: Your compounded retatrutide drug products fail to meet the conditions of sections 503A and 503B. Drug products compounded using retatrutide are not eligible for the exemptions provided by section 503A, because retatrutide is not the subject of an applicable USP or NF monograph, is not a component of an FDA-approved human drug, and does not appear on the 503A bulks list.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Sections 503A and 503B
deficiencies.description: Your compounded retatrutide drug products fail to meet the conditions of sections 503A and 503B. Drug products compounded using retatrutide are not eligible for the exemptions provided by section 503A, because retatrutide is not the subject of an applicable USP or NF monograph, is not a component of an FDA-approved human drug, and does not appear on the 503A bulks list.
deficiencies.evidence: Your compounded retatrutide drug products fail to meet the conditions of sections 503A and 503B. Drug products compounded using retatrutide are not eligible for the exemptions provided by section 503A, because retatrutide is not the subject of an applicable USP or NF monograph, is not a component of an FDA-approved human drug, and does not appear on the 503A bulks list.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs & Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: There are no FDA-approved applications on file for your compounded retatrutide drug products. Under sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FDCA, a new drug may not be introduced into or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce unless an application approved by FDA under section 505 of the FDCA is in effect for the drug.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no FDA-approved applications on file for your compounded retatrutide drug products. Under sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FDCA, a new drug may not be introduced into or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce unless an application approved by FDA under section 505 of the FDCA is in effect for the drug.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Claims
deficiencies.description: The following claims concerning your compounded retatrutide, semaglutide, and tirzepatide products appear on your website: • “Compounded Retatrutide [--] Active Ingredient in Mounjaro and Zepbound” • “Compounded Retatrutide [--] Active Ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy” • “What does the treatment plan generally look like? The standard, FDA-approved dosing schedule for Retatrutide is: Month 1: 0.25 mg per week…” • “Doctors frequently recommend Compounded Tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) or Compounded Semaglutide (Wegovy, Ozempic), which are both GLP-1 agonists.”
deficiencies.evidence: The following claims concerning your compounded retatrutide, semaglutide, and tirzepatide products appear on your website: • “Compounded Retatrutide [--] Active Ingredient in Mounjaro and Zepbound” • “Compounded Retatrutide [--] Active Ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy” • “What does the treatment plan generally look like? The standard, FDA-approved dosing schedule for Retatrutide is: Month 1: 0.25 mg per week…” • “Doctors frequently recommend Compounded Tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) or Compounded Semaglutide (Wegovy, Ozempic), which are both GLP-1 agonists.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations

conclusion: As previously stated, the violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Sponsor

deficiencies.title: Failure to permit an authorized officer or employee of the Food and Drug Administration to have access to and copy and verify records and reports relating to the conduct of a clinical investigation
deficiencies.description: Applied Therapeutics failed to adhere to these requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, for Protocol (b)(4), Applied Therapeutics used Pearson’s Q-global®, a Web-based administration system for capturing data for certain electronic clinical outcome assessments (eCOAs) performed for measuring primary and secondary efficacy endpoints. These eCOAs included the (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: FDA regulations require sponsors, upon request from an authorized officer or employee of the FDA, at reasonable times, to permit such an officer or employee to have access to and copy and verify any records and reports relating to a clinical investigation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide FDA with a description and analysis of any other data or information relevant to an evaluation of the safety and effectiveness of the drug product
deficiencies.description: Applied Therapeutics, as the applicant of (b)(4), failed to adhere to this requirement.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, according to Applied Therapeutics’ October 19, 2023, Clinical Study Report for Protocol (b)(4) which was submitted to FDA on December 28, 2023, the (b)(4) was provided by (b)(4) as a (b)(4) mg/mL (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: FDA regulations require applicants for new drug applications to provide FDA with a description and analysis of any data or information relevant to an evaluation of the safety and effectiveness of the drug product obtained or otherwise received by the applicant from any source, including information derived from clinical investigations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Notice of Unlawful Sale of Unapproved Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.24hreup.biz introduces into interstate commerce unapproved and misbranded semaglutide drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no approved drug applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act in effect for the 'Ozempic 0.5mg,' 'Ozempic 1mg,' and 'Rybelsus Semaglutide 14mg' offered by www.24hreup.biz.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.24hreup.biz offers 'Ozempic 0.5mg,' 'Ozempic 1mg,' and 'Rybelsus Semaglutide 14mg' for sale without a prescription.
deficiencies.evidence: The labeling for these drugs fails to bear adequate directions for use, causing them to be misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Dispensing Prescription Drugs Without a Prescription
deficiencies.description: www.24hreup.biz offers 'Ozempic 0.5mg,' 'Ozempic 1mg,' and 'Rybelsus Semaglutide 14mg' without requiring a prescription.
deficiencies.evidence: Dispensing a prescription drug without a prescription is an act which results in the drug being misbranded while held for sale, in violation of section 301(k) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.24hreup.biz because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase unapproved and misbranded drugs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failed to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: Site assessors inconsistently and incorrectly applied the (b)(4) and routinely tested subjects on assessment items beyond established (b)(4) testing requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: At least 11 of the 29 subjects whose records were reviewed during the inspection had additional items administered beyond what was needed to establish the (b)(4) for the (b)(4) assessments at Baseline. However, it is impossible to retrospectively account for all instances of noncompliance for the 42 enrolled subjects at your site, because of the inadequate and inconsistent documentation of the assessments.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failed to correctly administer and score the protocol-required (b)(4) assessments
deficiencies.description: The inconsistent and incorrect process of establishing the (b)(4) for the (b)(4) assessments may have yielded differential opportunities for subjects to demonstrate their level of (b)(4) skills, thus limiting the interpretability of the scores between different subjects in the study and between different time points for the same subject.
deficiencies.evidence: According to Protocol (b)(4), you were required to administer and score the (b)(4) assessments. However, at your clinical site, the procedures for establishing the (b)(4) for the (b)(4) assessments were not followed and were established inconsistently.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

metadata.company_name: Turkuaz Sağlik Hizmetleri Medikal Temizlik Kimyasa
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design including documenting the results of the design validation in the design history file (DHF)
deficiencies.description: Your firm conducted both microbiological aging stability and challenge validations for your firm's sterile and non-sterile ultrasound gel products. However, your firm has no data to support the following validation testing: ...
deficiencies.evidence: Sterile Ultrasound Gel Stability Accelerated Report, dated November 15, 2015; Sterile Ultrasound Gel Challenge Realtime Test Report, dated February 4, 2015; Non-Sterile Ultrasound Gel Stability Accelerated Report, dated February 22, 2015; Non-Sterile Ultrasound Gel Challenge Realtime Test Report, dated June 8, 2014
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of raw data supporting the aforementioned validation tests. Documentation of corrective and preventive action (CAPA) effectiveness. Documentation of a retrospective review of other testing to ensure results of all (other) design validations are documented in DHF. Documentation demonstrating personnel were trained on the updated DHF procedure.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s customer complaint procedure has no requirement to ensure that complaints received from distributors, marketing and distributing the device, are provided to your firm.
deficiencies.evidence: Complaint was received from your firm's distributor on April 8, 2021, related to the Sterile Ultrasound Gel (lot number SJ30071120), containing “black/dark blue fragments inside the gel packs.”
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of CAPA effectiveness. Documentation demonstrating personnel were trained on the updated procedure.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain requirements for suppliers, contractors, and consultants
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not documented an evaluation for your firm's irradiation contract sterilizer, ... listed on your firm’s approved supplier list.
deficiencies.evidence: Supplier Selection and Evaluation Procedure (dated March 29, 2021); Supplier Evaluation Form (dated October 7, 2022)
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of CAPA effectiveness. Documentation demonstrating personnel were trained on the agreement establishment with suppliers.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for the identification, documentation, validation or where appropriate verification, review, and approval of design changes
deficiencies.description: Your firm made design changes to its Konix sterile ultrasound gel, switching the neutralizing agent from triethanolamine to sodium hydroxide, and switching the preservative from methylchloroisothiazolinone, methylisothiazolinione to phenoxyethanol, ethylhexyglycerin.
deficiencies.evidence: Risk analysis, Konix Sterile failure modes and effects (FMEA) (dated July 27, 2022); Konix Non-Sterile FMEA (dated March 04, 2021)
deficiencies.required_action: An updated FMEA table incorporating the risk associated with changing Neutralization Agent for the Konix Sterile Ultrasound Gel. Documentation of CAPA effectiveness.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain requirements for the health, cleanliness, personal practices, and clothing of personnel
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s cleanroom entry and application instructions states “put on your cap bonnet by gathering your hair so that it does not stick out.” However, a filling line operator with her hair exposed outside of the disposable hair bonnet was observed.
deficiencies.evidence: Hygiene control procedure (dated July 20, 2022)
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of CAPA effectiveness.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have personnel with the necessary education, background, training, and experience
deficiencies.description: Throughout the inspection, your firm was not able to demonstrate that all activities required by this part are correctly performed.
deficiencies.evidence: None
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of personnel training and experience.

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm's response cannot be determined at this time.
