metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Unapproved Products Related to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain design control procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not establish design control procedures prior to the distribution of the CovClear COVID-19 Rapid Antigen Tests.
deficiencies.evidence: Design validation activities and/or their documentation for the release of the CovClear Rapid Antigen Test were inadequate.
deficiencies.required_action: You must establish and maintain procedures to ensure that specified design requirements are met, this would include validating the device design to include reading results for the specified timeframe.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and implement corrective and preventive action procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm indicated that an internal audit / gap analysis was conducted by a third-party auditor from May 20-24, 2021 that revealed major nonconformances to your quality system.
deficiencies.evidence: No systematic investigation to identify root causes of these reported issues or to identify similar quality issues was conducted; no corrective action plan was defined to systematically address the root causes; and no effectiveness verifications were conducted to address the deficiencies identified by the third-party auditor.
deficiencies.required_action: You have developed procedures that are compliant with the regulation and have conducted training. A follow-up inspection will be necessary to evaluate the effectiveness of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your procedure QP 822, CUSTOMER FEEDBACK AND COMPLAINT HANDLING, Revision A, Effective June 22, 2020 until June 29, 2021 (Revision B) was inadequate in that the procedure did not include the requirement for complaints to be evaluated to determine whether the complaint is reportable.
deficiencies.evidence: There were two notification transcriptions dated October 7, 2021 and October 14, 2021 that appeared to require further investigation and were not forwarded to QA for investigation or evaluation of reportability.
deficiencies.required_action: You have revised your procedure and have conducted training. A follow-up inspection will need to be conducted to evaluate the implementation and the effectiveness of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for finished device acceptance
deficiencies.description: Manufacturing Batch Record, MBR 900-005, Final QC Testing for Antigen Test which covers production lot number 02-365-03, lot size 1,270 kits/ 50 test strips with expiration dates of February 14, 2021 and June 2022 did not demonstrate the lot met the acceptance criteria for release.
deficiencies.evidence: Lot 02-365-03 was distributed to customers in the United States.
deficiencies.required_action: You have updated the procedures, forms, and acceptance criteria, and have conducted training. Lot 02-365-03 was subject of the recall and product destruction will be documented within a CAPA.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: You did not ensure that your suppliers perform contracted processes that impact CovClear’s performance and effectiveness in a manner that guarantees your quality specifications are consistently met.
deficiencies.evidence: The packaging process for the CovClear Rapid Antigen Test’s individually foil-wrapped configuration performed by your contract packager was not adequately validated to ensure that finished product will meet performance specifications during transportation, storage and use.
deficiencies.required_action: You must ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements.

conclusion: We request that you take immediate action to prevent distribution of such unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence:
During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
1. Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: In response to this letter, provide a full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). Take prompt and appropriate actions to determine the safety of all lots of the component(s) and any related drug product that could contain DEG or EG, including customer notifications and product recalls for any contaminated lots.

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence:
For example,
Adequate procedures describing roles and responsibilities of the QU, including the handling of vendor qualification, batch release, and complaints (21 CFR 211.22(a) and 211.22(d)).
deficiencies.required_action:
In response to this letter, provide:
A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Laboratory Records Inadequacy
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.
deficiencies.evidence:
For example,
Our investigators observed original data (e.g., pH test records) discarded in the trash located in your in-process laboratory.
deficiencies.required_action:
In response to this letter, provide:
A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting.

conclusion: Data Integrity Remediation

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conduct Identity Testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm manufactures over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, such as ... , sunscreens, skin protectants, ... , and hand sanitizer. You failed to conduct adequate identity testing on incoming components, such as inactive ingredients, used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Written Procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process controls designed to assure that the drug products have the identity, strength, purity, and quality that they are purported or represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: You lacked documentation showing that you investigated out-of-specification microbiological test results obtained during validation of your ... that indicated your ... may not be in a state of control.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your ... design, control, and maintenance. A thorough remediation plan to install and operate a suitable ... .

deficiencies.title: Failure to Qualify and Maintain Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment.
deficiencies.evidence: You have not demonstrated that your cleaning practices are adequate to remove contaminants from the shared equipment used to manufacture your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A timeline for performing appropriate process performance qualification (PPQ) studies for each of your marketed drug products. Also include an explanation how you will ensure proper satisfactory PPQ studies are performed prior to future distribution of any drug products.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: ... A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. ... A full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). ... A description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator (Sponsor)

deficiencies.title: Failed to submit an Investigational New Drug application (IND) for the conduct of a clinical investigation with an investigational new drug that is subject to 21 CFR 312.2(a)
deficiencies.description: ABRI failed to comply with these requirements. Specifically, ABRI initiated and conducted Protocol ABRI-002, a clinical investigation of the investigational drug Relaxium Sleep (Relaxium) subject to section 505 of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. 355], without submitting and having in effect an IND.
deficiencies.evidence: The study design of Protocol ABRI-002 shows that Relaxium was studied for use in the treatment of insomnia, which is a disease.
deficiencies.required_action: ABRI should submit an IND before conducting any future clinical investigations with investigational drugs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately test your over-the-counter (OTC) drug products for strength (assay) of each active ingredient prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to conduct testing for identity of each component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform adequate testing for your raw materials that were used to manufacture the following: OTC (b)(4) and (b)(4) drug products
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system, including but not limited to: Evaluating all suppliers of materials (components, containers, and closures) to determine if they are reliable and appropriately qualified.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate your equipment cleaning process to demonstrate its effectiveness to prevent potential cross-contamination between your OTC drug products and non-pharmaceutical products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to disco

metadata.company_name: Zhejiang Uniquality Nursing Products Technology Co., Ltd. dba ZheJiang YouQuan Care Products Technology Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Component testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly

deficiencies.title: 2. Laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications, standards, sampling plans, and test procedures designed to assure that components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, labeling, and drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly

deficiencies.title: 3. Quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Adulteration
deficiencies.description: The Relumins Premium Derma Pen, Relumins Premium Derma Pen Plus, MESOpower Pen and Needle Modules, Relumins Branded 35 needle Dermastamper, Relumins Branded 80 needle Dermastamper, Relumins Branded 1200 needle Dermaroller 1.0 mm, Relumins Branded 1200 needle Dermaroller 2.0 mm, and Relumins Intense Glow Beauty Care Tool are adulterated because an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) is not in effect.
deficiencies.evidence: Product packaging and labels, user manuals, brochures, and product labeling on your firm's website
deficiencies.required_action: Submit an application for PMA or investigational device exemption

deficiencies.title: Misbranding
deficiencies.description: The listed devices are misbranded because a notice or other information respecting the devices was not provided as required by section 510(k) of the Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Product packaging and labels, user manuals, brochures, and product labeling on your firm's website
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a notification for each device

deficiencies.title: Failure to Register
deficiencies.description: The listed devices were manufactured, prepared, propagated, compounded, or processed in an establishment not duly registered under section 510 of the Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Annual establishment registration and device listing information
deficiencies.required_action: Register your firm annually with the FDA

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Sponsor/Unapproved Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit an Investigational New Drug (IND) application
deficiencies.description: Prodrome Sciences failed to submit an IND before conducting Protocol Pro-040-Plasm-01, a clinical investigation of the investigational drug ProdromeNeuro™ Oil.
deficiencies.evidence: The investigator representing FDA reviewed the role of Prodrome Sciences as the sponsor of a clinical investigation (Protocol Pro‐040‐Plasm‐01, “ProdromeNeuro: An Open-Label Study of Omega 3 Oil Nutritional Supplementation for Aging-Related Cognitive Decline”) of the investigational drug ProdromeNeuro™ Oil (Omega-3 Plasmalogens).
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective and preventive action plan to prevent similar violations in the future.

deficiencies.title: Failure to comply with IND regulations
deficiencies.description: Prodrome Sciences failed to submit, and to have in effect, an IND before initiating Protocol Pro-040-Plasm-01 with an investigational drug.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA’s records indicate that Prodrome Sciences failed to submit an IND before conducting Protocol Pro-040-Plasm-01, in which 29 human subjects were enrolled and received investigational drug between July 2020 and March 2021.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective and preventive action plan to prevent similar violations in the future.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate corrective and preventive action plan
deficiencies.description: Prodrome Sciences’ written response did not include sufficient information to address how Prodrome Sciences, as a sponsor, will prevent similar violations in the future.
deficiencies.evidence: Your written responses, you stated that Prodrome Sciences has hired a consulting partner to assist with remediation and resolution of the Form FDA 483 observation, and to provide ongoing FDA compliance support. However, your corrective action plan did not include sufficient details on how Prodrome Sciences, as a sponsor, will determine whether IND regulations apply to future clinical investigations of products regulated by FDA.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective and preventive action plan that includes sufficient information to address how Prodrome Sciences, as a sponsor, will prevent similar violations in the future.

metadata.company_name: Las Villas Health Care LLC dba Villas Health
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including semaglutide and sildenafil. Your claims concerning these products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: The following claims concerning compounded semaglutide and sildenafil products appear on your website: • “Semaglutide GLP-1 is a once-a-week injection that contains the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy….” • “Sildenafil 2%: the active ingredient in Viagra. Clinically proven to improve outcomes in women with sexual arousal disorder by increasing blood flow to the genitals.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)’s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

conclusion: The receipt in interstate commerce of misbranded drugs, and the delivery or proffered delivery thereof, is also a violation of section 301(c) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. § 331(c)].

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/PMA/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: RoyalVibe Health Ltd.
deficiencies.description: Your firm markets, distributes and manufactures the CellQuicken Analyzer (Smart-Watch, and Software), RoyalVibe Ultrasound Generator, Envirovibe Water Restructuring Pad, Brainvibe Neuroplasticity Visual Program, RoyalVibe Therapy Balls, and RoyalVibe Application. Under section 201(h) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (“Act” or “the Act”), 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), these products are devices because they are intended for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions, or in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or are intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: During an attempted inspection of your firm located in the Woodlands, TX on June 17, 2022 through July 5, 2022, an investigator from the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) determined that your firm markets, distributes and manufactures the CellQuicken Analyzer (Smart-Watch, and Software), RoyalVibe Ultrasound Generator, Envirovibe Water Restructuring Pad, Brainvibe Neuroplasticity Visual Program, RoyalVibe Therapy Balls, and RoyalVibe Application.
deficiencies.required_action: You should provide your plan for addressing the previously distributed adulterated products and provide details on the steps you’ve taken to remove your products from the US market.

deficiencies.title: RoyalVibe Bioresonance Focused Ultrasound Generator
deficiencies.description: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will use sound vibration technology to “resonate with” and heal damaged and stressed body cells, and can treat Alzheimer’s, diabetes, stroke, and cancer show an intent to treat disease or to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (www.royalvibehealth.com) promotes your firm’s products for, among others, the following intended uses: • “Diagnosed Condition’s Root Causes We Can Help Treat Cancer Type 1 Diabetes Type 2 Diabetes Cardiovascular Disease Alzheimer’s Kidney Disease Liver Disease Cerebrovascular problems (stroke) Lung Disease Depression”
deficiencies.required_action: Based on this, the RoyalVibe Bioresonance Focused Ultrasound Generator product is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), because it is intended for use in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or is intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.

deficiencies.title: Envirovibe Water Restructuring Pad
deficiencies.description: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will restructure water molecules to increase absorbability and heal body cells and can treat pain, inflammation, wounds, broken bones, diarrhea, and constipation show an intent to treat disease or to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (www.royalvibehealth.com) promotes your firm’s products for, among others, the following intended uses: • “Envirovibe Water Therapy (Heal Your Body Cells From The Inside Out)”
deficiencies.required_action: Based on this, the Envirovibe Water Restructuring Pad product is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), because it is intended for use in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or is intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.

deficiencies.title: CellQuicken Analyzer
deficiencies.description: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will scan the body and detect the root cause of a condition and help a user understand why a disease formed show an intent for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (www.royalvibehealth.com) promotes your firm’s products for, among others, the following intended uses: • “‘CellQuicken Analyzer’ is the tool that helps detect the root cause of your condition. It helps you understand why the disease formed in the first place.”
deficiencies.required_action: Based on this, the CellQuicken Analyzer product is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), because it is intended for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions.

deficiencies.title: Therapy Balls Spine Alignment
deficiencies.description: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will affect nerves associated with the spine and can treat severe scoliosis and symptoms associated with Parkinson’s Disease, depression, glaucoma, headache, and migraine show an intent to mitigate or treat disease or to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (www.royalvibehealth.com) promotes your firm’s products for, among others, the following intended uses: • “RoyalVibe Therapy Balls’ – a unique set of metal balls specifically designed to treat the nerves that controls the muscles that align the spine.”
deficiencies.required_action: Based on this, the Therapy Balls Spine Alignment product is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), because it is intended for use in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or is intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.

deficiencies.title: BrainVibe Neuroplasticity Program
deficiencies.description: Claims or statements that suggest that this product can reduce or treat addiction show an intent to mitigate or treat disease.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (www.royalvibehealth.com) promotes your firm’s products for, among others, the following intended uses: • “9. On your RoyalVibe application, log into your Health Advice Hub account on the application. Find your treatment under the correct dependent and run the program.”
deficiencies.required_action: Based on this, the BrainVibe Neuroplasticity Program product is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 321(h), because it is intended for use in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease.

conclusion: Based on this information, your firm has failed to comply with the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) and FDA regulations. You are hereby notified that these products are adulterated under section 501 of the FFDCA because they were introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce without being in compliance with the applicable requirements.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: In Vivo Bioavailability-Bioequivalence Studies – Clinical

deficiencies.title: Failed to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to adhere to the requirements of the investigational plan, specifically failing to collect safety assessments, including hematology and pulmonary function tests, every week after the first dose of investigational drug in Period I (during the study washout period) and for six weeks after dosing in Period II.
deficiencies.evidence: Your written response dated February 14, 2025
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our findings and provides you with an opportunity to address the deficiencies noted above.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release, and for each batch of drug product required to be free of objectionable microorganisms, appropriate laboratory testing, as necessary.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to conduct adequate release testing of all your drug products prior to distribution. Specifically, you did not test your drug products for identity, assay, impurities, or microbiological attributes.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before a batch disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to perform adequate identity testing of each component lot used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products, including for your active ingredient menthol and glycerin. Additionally, you relied on your suppliers’ certificates of analyses (COAs) without establishing the reliability of each of your suppliers’ test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. If you intend to accept any results from your supplier’s COA instead of testing each component lot for strength, quality, and purity, specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier’s results through initial validation as well as periodic revalidation.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to prepare batch production and control records with complete information relating to the production and control of each batch of drug product produced.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to document the manufacturing instructions for your OTC drug products and did not have any executed batch records for distributed drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide examples of batch production and control records your firm will use moving forward that include all elements required per 21 CFR 211.188.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm manufactures OTC topical analgesic drug products. Your quality unit (QU) did not effectively exercise its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations. Specifically, you failed to have written procedures in place governing manufacturing and quality oversight.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: ...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Finished Pharmaceuticals/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded/OTC

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” is an unapproved new drug introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug & Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d). In addition, these products are misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples from the “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” product labeling, including the websites listed above, that provide evidence of the intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of the product as a drug include, but may not be limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” is a new drug within the meaning of section 201(p) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(p), and there is no evident basis under section 505G of the FD&C Act under which this product would be legally marketed without an approved application.
deficiencies.evidence: Your “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” is distributed as a combination of three packets containing benzocaine 20%, lidocaine HCl 4%, and tetracaine 2%, respectively. Your website states, “Empty the contents of each sachet gradually and mix them thoroughly to ensure even distribution of the pack contents” and includes a video instructing users to mix the three packets together prior to use as well.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” is misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee), because this product is a nonprescription drug subject to section 505G of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355h, but does not comply with the requirements for marketing under that section and is not the subject of an application approved under section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355.
deficiencies.evidence: Your “BLT 1-2-3 Cream” product does not conform to the conditions specified in M017 for the reasons described below.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that may exist in connection with your product. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Adulteration under Section 501(h) for Failure to Conform with Current Good Manufacturing Practice Requirements
deficiencies.description: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for the identification, documentation, validation, or (where appropriate) verification, review, and approval of design changes before their implementation
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm-initiated changes to the tips used with the plasma pens. These changes include changing the tip from sterile to non-sterile and a change to a new tip manufacturer from a different country.
deficiencies.required_action: Initiation of a CAPA to address this 483 observation, completing a design change request to document the tip design change, conduction of a risk assessment of the tip change, implementation of the tip change upon approval, completion of a document change request, updates to the design history file, and management of inventory per risk assessment results and actions from design change request.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.description: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm initiated a change to improve the power control of your plasma devices. This change was not conducted or documented in accordance with your CAPA procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Initiation of a CAPA to address the distributed devices, conduct a risk assessment for the distributed devices, and to manage inventory disposition per risk assessment and CAPA actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm has received 17 complaints since September of 2019 and did not utilize electronic form EF-190-1 for the documentation of complaints.
deficiencies.required_action: Update procedure QOP-85-02, reassess and update all 17 complaints taken since September 2019 per your procedure once updated, and to train your quality complaint unit on the updated complaint procedure and record-keeping processes.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product
deficiencies.description: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's procedures, QOP-74-02, Purchasing (Rev. B; Effective Date: 11/15/2019) & QOP-74-03, Verification of Purchased Product (Rev. A; Effective Date: 7/25/2019) were not followed in that activities surrounding acceptance of devices were not documented showing that your firm is inspecting, testing, or otherwise verifying that devices received from contract manufacturers conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: Initiation of a CAPA to address this 483 observation and the retrospective assessment and acceptance or all devices and tips in inventory.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain device history records (DHR's)
deficiencies.description: Failure to maintain device history records (DHR's)
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm does not maintain device history records (DHR’s) for the plasma devices received from contract manufacturers which are then further packaged with instructions for use (IFUs) and other products as part of a convenience kits that is then distributed.
deficiencies.required_action: Initiation of a CAPA to address this 483 observation, completing a design change request to document the tip design change, conduction of a risk assessment of the tip change, implementation of the tip change upon approval, completion of a document change request, updates to the design history file, and management of inventory per risk assessment results and actions from design change request.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct adequate strength and identity testing before releasing batches of drug products for distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: Test results from retained samples of Zermat brand antiperspirant deodorants showed a concentration of aluminum chlorohydrate that far exceeded the product labeled amount.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide test results, an action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate microbiological specifications for your OTC drug products, including oral suspension and topical antiperspirant deodorants.
deficiencies.evidence: Microbiological specifications were set at times greater than the United States Pharmacopoeia (USP) guidance for each of the dosage forms.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide microbial specifications, an action plan and timelines for conducting microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish adequate written procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate water system design and maintenance, inadequate process validation, and lack of documentation and control over critical processing steps subject to variation.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your water system design, control, and maintenance; a thorough remediation plan to install and operate a suitable water system; and a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A list of chemical specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your claims concerning compounded sildenafil, tadalafil, semaglutide, and tirzepatide products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: The following claims concerning compounded sildenafil, tadalafil, semaglutide, and tirzepatide products appear on webpages that offer only compounded products: • “SILDENAFIL (GENERIC VIAGRA)” • “TADALAFIL (GENERIC CIALIS)” • “SEMAGLUTIDE (GENERIC OZEMPIC/WEGOVY)” • “TIRZEPATIDE (GENERIC MOUNJARO)”
deficiencies.required_action: Please be advised, the receipt in interstate commerce of misbranded drugs, and the delivery or proffered delivery thereof, is also a violation of section 301(c) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. § 331(c)].

conclusion: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

metadata.company_name: ASP Global, LLC. dba Anatomy Supply Partners, LLC.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received products and services conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately implement supplier management procedures to ensure that products purchased from your critical supplier and manufacturer of Safe-T-Fill Capillary Collection Tubes, (b)(4), conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: A. (b)(4) supplier evaluations using Form Q-F-256 were not performed for (b)(4) in (b)(4) and (b)(4) as required.
deficiencies.required_action: Development of effectively implemented supplier controls based upon supplier history and product risk assessment

deficiencies.title: Failure to verify that (b)(4) maintains adequate design history files (DHF) for the Safe-T-Fill products
deficiencies.description: ASP Global did not verify that (b)(4) maintains adequate design history files (DHF) for the Safe-T-Fill products during the supplier evaluation period (12/2022) following the acquisition of 510(k)'s K981973 and K983517 from (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: The Quality Agreement states in Section 2.2 that '(b)(4) will maintain 'Design History Files' with all design inputs, outputs and changes,' but ASP Global was unable to provide the full DHF during the inspection due to deficiencies noted during (b)(4) FDA inspection.
deficiencies.required_action: Development of procedures to properly evaluate and audit suppliers once acquired

deficiencies.title: Failure to review Safe-T-Fill product information to determine if specifications adhered to original 510(k) clearances or if a new 510(k) was required for changes to the originally cleared products
deficiencies.description: Your firm's failure to have adequate purchasing controls to include design controls for the Safe-T-Fill product demonstrates significant deficiencies in the design and manufacture of these products.
deficiencies.evidence: The 300 μL size Safe-T-Fill tubes have been marketed since 2001 without proper evaluation of whether a new 510(k) clearance is required, as only 125 μL, 150 μL, and 200 μL tubes were cleared in the original 510(k)s K981973 and K983517 in 1998.
deficiencies.required_action: Development of procedures to properly evaluate and audit suppliers once acquired

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed or refused to furnish material or information respecting the device that is required by or under section 519 of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360i, and 21 CFR Part 803 - Medical Device Reporting.
deficiencies.evidence: Section 6.2 references a reporting timeframe for initial (b)(4) reports; however, it incorrectly specifies instructions pertaining to (b)(4) reports.
deficiencies.required_action: Development of written MDR procedures as required by 21 CFR 803.17(a)(3)

conclusion: Your firm's responses dated August 22, 2025, and October 22, 2025, are inadequate. Your firm's commitment to implementing corrective actions is acknowledged; however, the response does not include adequate details and supporting documentation regarding ongoing corrective actions.

metadata.company_name: Zhejiang Easyclean Daily Chemical Co., Ltd
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct adequate finished drug product testing on drug products shipped to the United States (U.S.) market.
deficiencies.evidence: Without adequate testing, there is no scientific evidence that your drug product batches conform to appropriate specifications prior to release.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate stability program and determine appropriate expiration dates for the OTC drug products that you manufacture.
deficiencies.evidence: The records and information you provided for stability studies include only three months of accelerated stability data and did not include ongoing or long-term stability data, as required under 21 CFR 211.166(b).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to prepare batch production and control records with complete information relating to the production and control of each batch of drug product
deficiencies.description: The batch records you provided lacked adequate production details, including but not limited to, product formulation, identity of major equipment, duration of execution of each significant step, and identification of person(s) performing each significant step in the drug manufacturing operation.
deficiencies.evidence: Without adequate and complete batch records, you cannot assure the uniformity of your drug products from batch to batch. In addition, this may impact your ability to adequately investigate any product deviations and complaints.
deficiencies.required_action: A complete assessment of documentation systems used throughout your manufacturing and laboratory operations to determine where documentation practices are insufficient. Include a detailed CAPA plan that comprehensively remediates your firm's documentation practices to ensure you retain attributable, legible, complete, original, accurate, contemporaneous records throughout your operation.

deficiencies.title: 4. Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: In your 704(a)(4) records request response, you indicated that no validation activities had been performed for your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Process validation evaluates the soundness of design and state of control of a process throughout its lifecycle. Each significant stage of a manufacturing process must be designed appropriately and ensure the quality of raw material inputs, in-process materials, and finished drugs.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for acceptance activities
deficiencies.description: Your firm's current incoming inspection and acceptance procedure for Biological Indicator (BI) is not adequately established.
deficiencies.evidence: The inspection revealed that your firm does not perform BI Population verification tests on the received BI batches to ensure that the population has not changed during shipping or storage.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain procedures for acceptance activities, including inspections, tests, or other verification activities.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that where the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm's ultrasonic cleaning process using the (b)(4) Cleaner is not adequately validated.
deficiencies.evidence: The inspection revealed that critical details—such as the description of the NeoZoline Ventilation Tube (including part number, size, and shape), the number of validation batches (runs), validation batch size, and sample size used—were not documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should validate the process with a high degree of assurance and approve it according to established procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm's Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) procedure fails to ensure that the quality data is adequately analyzed to identify potential and existing causes of nonconformances or other quality issues.
deficiencies.evidence: The CAPAs were closed after implementing corrective actions, but your firm did not verify the effectiveness of these actions.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action, including analyzing quality data to identify potential and existing causes of nonconformances or other quality issues.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: The FDA investigator reviewed six NC records related to your firm's NeoZoline Ventilation Tubes, but showed the following deficiencies in documentation.
deficiencies.evidence: The NC records did not adequately document the evaluation of the identified nonconformities, and the rationale for not conducting related investigations was inadequately documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements, including identifying, documenting, evaluating, segregating, and disposing of nonconforming product.
