metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for acceptance activities
deficiencies.description: Your firm distributed at least (b)(4) SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Test kits from 9/30/2021 – 2/24/2022 without establishing procedures for incoming product and finished device acceptance activities to ensure the device(s) conform to specified requirements and meet acceptance criteria, and acceptance records are maintained.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for acceptance activities

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures to ensure that nonconforming product is identified, documented, evaluated, segregated, and dispositioned.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures to control nonconforming product

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for control and distribution of finished devices
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established and maintained procedures for the control and distribution of your SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Qualitative Test system to ensure only devices approved for release are distributed, and that purchase orders are reviewed to ensure that ambiguities and errors are resolved before devices are released for distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for control and distribution of finished devices

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for evaluation of suppliers, including the quality requirements that must be met by suppliers, to ensure that received products and services conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures to ensure conformance of purchased or otherwise received product and services

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for corrective and preventative action
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for implementing and documenting corrective and preventive action, including requirements for: 1) analyzing quality data sources; 2) investigating the cause of nonconformities; 3) identifying the actions needed to prevent occurrence or recurrence of nonconformities; 4) ensuring actions implemented are effective; 5) documenting changes in methods and procedures; 6) disseminating information related to quality problems to appropriate individuals; and 7) submitting relevant information on quality problems for management review.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for corrective and preventative action

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for complaint handling and documentation to ensure that complaints are: 1) processed in a uniform and timely manner, 2) oral complaints are documented upon receipt, 3) complaints are evaluated to determine if the reported event is required to be submitted to the FDA as a Medical Device Report.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures to control environmental conditions
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures to control environmental conditions to ensure that the SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Test products (raw materials and finished devices) are stored within the required temperature range(s) and monitored to ensure that any deviations are noted and addressed.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures to control environmental conditions

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that mix-ups, damage, deterioration, contamination, or other adverse effects to product do not occur during handling
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established and maintained adequate procedures to ensure that mix-ups, damage, deterioration, contamination, or other adverse effects to product do not occur during handling.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain adequate procedures for handling

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for quality audits
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for quality audits to ensure that the quality system complies with the quality system requirements and to determine the effectiveness of the quality system.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for quality audits

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for management review
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for management review of the suitability and effectiveness of the quality system.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for management review

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for training and identifying training needs
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established training procedures to identify training needs and to ensure that all personnel are trained to adequately perform their responsibilities.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for training and identifying training needs

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish a policy, and objectives for, and commitment to, quality
deficiencies.description: Your Management with executive responsibility has not established it policy and objectives for, and commitment to, quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish a policy, and objectives for, and commitment to, quality

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written Medical Device Reporting (MDR) procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of medical device reports.
deficiencies.required_action: Develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures

conclusion: We request that you take immediate action to prevent the distribution of such unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your claims concerning compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: • “Manage weight affordably with compounded GLP-1, the same active ingredients as Wegovy, Ozempic, Zepbound & Mounjaro” • “Same active ingredient in Mounjaro and Zepbound” • “Same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy”
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from using the language cited above that misbrands the product. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

conclusion: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you have not adequately implemented your complaint handling procedure titled “Customer Complaint Handling”, Doc. # EQA 6.2, in that complaints were not adequately documented, investigated, and evaluated for Medical Device Reportable (MDR) events.
deficiencies.evidence: 19 of these complaints were associated with reagents and controls for your Drew3 analyzer, while 6 complaints were associated to the veterinary (b)(4) analyzer. Only one of these complaints, C21-04-01, was documented as per EQA 6.3, evaluated for MDR, and investigated.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response indicates that you plan to (b)(4). You also stated that (b)(4). The adequacy of your response cannot be determined at this time. A follow-up inspection by FDA is necessary to verify compliance.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish CAPA procedures
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the investigations of failures were not extended to evaluate the adequacy of the root cause analysis process and to identify other products potentially affected by the root cause, and CAPAs were closed without identifying root cause or completing actions.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, CAPA 21-002 was initiated on 09/19/2021 to investigate multiple complaints from different customers reporting boxes of products arriving broken/damaged/wet, leaking bottles, and kits that appeared to have been hastily put together during April – July 2021.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response indicates that you plan to (b)(4). It also states your intention to (b)(4) and describes that you gathered data for complaints, services, non-conformances, out-of-specification lab results, manufacturing deviations, troubleshooting, and preventive maintenance for review and trend analysis. The adequacy of your response cannot be determined at this time. A follow-up inspection by FDA is necessary to verify compliance.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish a process whose results cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you have not adequately implemented your process validation procedure, “Validation Procedure”, Doc # EMF 1.0.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your firm has no documented evidence of the process validation for the mixing, filling, and capping of the Drew3-Pac Diluent and Enzymatic Cleaner reagents and the mixing validation for the Lysing reagent currently manufactured at your facility which compose the Drew3-Pac kit for use with your Drew3 Analyzer.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response indicates that you plan to (b)(4). It also states your intention to (b)(4) and describes that you gathered data for complaints, services, non-conformances, out-of-specification lab results, manufacturing deviations, troubleshooting, and preventive maintenance for review and trend analysis. The adequacy of your response cannot be determined at this time. A follow-up inspection by FDA is necessary to verify compliance.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Clean Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to clean, maintain, and, as appropriate for the nature of the drug, sanitize and/or sterilize equipment and utensils at appropriate intervals to prevent malfunctions or contamination that would alter the safety, identity, strength, quality of purity of the drug product beyond the official or other established requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple pieces of manufacturing equipment, including the (b)(4) and the Tablet Press, were observed to have drug product residue and oxidized metal surfaces, which were documented as being clean and were released by your quality unit. Additionally, the dedicated (b)(4) equipment showed visible product residue, oxidized metal, and foreign material, despite being cleaned and released by your quality unit.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent retrospective assessment of your cleaning effectiveness to evaluate the scope of cross-contamination hazards. Include the identity of residues, other manufacturing equipment that may have been improperly cleaned, and an assessment whether cross-contaminated products may have been released for distribution.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Maintain Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately maintain your equipment. For example, the metal detection equipment MFG-1276 was found to be nonfunctioning during the inspection. Your preventive maintenance procedures were also inadequate, focusing only on the metal detector’s physical appearance rather than its operational functionality.
deficiencies.evidence: A metal screw was found in a sealed bottle of (b)(4) mg, lot (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent retrospective assessment of your cleaning effectiveness to evaluate the scope of cross-contamination hazards. Include the identity of residues, other manufacturing equipment that may have been improperly cleaned, and an assessment whether cross-contaminated products may have been released for distribution.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Maintain Buildings
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to maintain buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products in a good state of repair.
deficiencies.evidence: Gaps were observed in the protective wall, covering the original underlying wall surface in the (b)(4) machine room, thus creating areas that are difficult to clean. An unknown brown-yellow material was visible in the wall gaps located near the (b)(4) equipment, indicating potential contamination risks in the manufacturing environment.
deficiencies.required_action: Your CAPA plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

metadata.company_name: Huangshi Hygienic Material Medicine Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform appropriate testing on your drug products prior to release for distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to assure that the drug product bore an expiration date that was supported by appropriate stability testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate stability program and lacked chemical and microbial testing data to demonstrate that your over-the-counter (OTC) drug products will remain acceptable throughout their assigned expiry period.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. Your remediated program should include, but not be limited to: Stability indicating methods. Stability studies for each drug product in its marketed container-closure system before distribution is permitted. An ongoing program in which representative batches of each product are added each year to the program to determine if the shelf-life claim remains valid.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality unit to exercise its responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit failed to ensure the API and intermediates manufactured at your facility are in compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific deviations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your quality unit is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate document control
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit failed to ensure adequate document control over paper and electronic records.
deficiencies.evidence: You manufactured and released lots of APIs without creating or maintaining batch manufacturing records.
deficiencies.required_action: A complete assessment of documentation systems used throughout your manufacturing and laboratory operations to determine where documentation practices are insufficient.

deficiencies.title: Deficient data integrity practices
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit engaged in deficient data integrity practices, including replacing original CGMP documents without a documented investigation.
deficiencies.evidence: You replaced original CGMP documents consisting of numerous pages of executed batch production records and a laboratory-testing record in which original pages were discarded or destroyed without a documented investigation.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan that comprehensively remediates your firm’s documentation practices to ensure you retain attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original, accurate, and complete records throughout your operation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to perform comprehensive retrospective review
deficiencies.description: You do not provide a plan or procedures to holistically improve your document lifecycle controls, issuance traceability, and quality unit release processes.
deficiencies.evidence: You do not provide results from comprehensively investigating past incidents of non-contemporaneous documentation, nor have you reconciled existing batch manufacturing records for completeness and release by the quality unit.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive retrospective review of the replaced CGMP document pages to ensure that original data will be recorded contemporaneously and indelibly.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: False & Misleading Claims/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Risk Presentation
deficiencies.description: The sponsored links are misleading because they include representations about the efficacy of Adzenys XR-ODT but fail to communicate any risk information.
deficiencies.evidence: Prescription drug advertisements and labeling (promotional communications) misbrand a drug if they are false or misleading with respect to risk. The determination of whether a promotional communication is misleading includes, among other things, not only representations made or suggested in the promotional communication, but also the extent to which the promotional communication fails to reveal facts material in light of the representations made or with respect to consequences that may result from the use of the drug as recommended or suggested in the promotional communication.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist promotional communications that are misleading as described above

deficiencies.title: Failure to Use Required Established Name
deficiencies.description: The sponsored links fail to present the established name for Adzenys XR-ODT, thereby misbranding the product.
deficiencies.evidence: The established name of a prescription drug must be presented prominently, in direct conjunction with the proprietary name.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the failure to use required established name

deficiencies.title: Failure to Submit Under Form FDA-2253
deficiencies.description: A copy of the sponsored links was not submitted to FDA under cover of Form FDA-2253 at the time of initial publication as required by 21 CFR 314.81(b)(3)(i).
deficiencies.evidence: FDA regulations require any labeling or advertising devised for promotion of the drug product to be submitted at the time of initial dissemination of the labeling and at the time of initial publication of the advertisement for a prescription drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit the required information under Form FDA-2253

conclusion: For the reasons discussed above, the sponsored links misbrand Adzenys XR-ODT and make the distribution of the drug in violation of the FD&C Act.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1))
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures OTC drug products including cream. Based on the records and information provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested the identity of your incoming components (i.e., ) used in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The use of ingredients contaminated with or has resulted in various lethal poisoning incidents in humans worldwide.
deficiencies.required_action: Without adequate testing you do not have scientific evidence that your raw materials conform to appropriate specifications prior to use in the manufacture of your drug products.

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release (21 CFR 211.165(a))
deficiencies.description: Based on the records and information provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested your finished drug products prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you stated that you only perform pH, viscosity, and “organoleptic comparison” testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including strength and identity testing of the active ingredient, must be performed before drug product release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have adequate scientific evidence to assure that your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.

deficiencies.title: 3. Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products and to use results of stability testing to determine appropriate storage conditions and expiration dates (21 CFR 211.166(a))
deficiencies.description: Based on the records and information provided, you did not demonstrate that your drug products remain acceptable throughout their assigned shelf life.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you indicated that potency and assay testing are not part of your stability program.
deficiencies.required_action: Without appropriate stability studies, you do not have scientific evidence to support whether your drug products meet established specifications and retain their quality attributes through their labeled expiry.

deficiencies.title: 4. Your firm failed to establish written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess (21 CFR 211.100(a))
deficiencies.description: The information provided did not demonstrate that your manufacturing processes are reproducible and controlled to yield a product of uniform character and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, you stated that your firm does not have process validation procedures or process validation studies.
deficiencies.required_action: Process validation evaluates the soundness of design and state of control of a process throughout its lifecycle. Each significant stage of a manufacturing process must be designed appropriately and assure the quality of raw material inputs, in-process materials, and finished drugs.

deficiencies.title: 5. Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products (21 CFR 211.22(a))
deficiencies.description: The records and information you provided demonstrate that your quality unit (QU) did not effectively exercise its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your QU did not adequately exercise its authority in the approval or rejection of components in your Materials System.
deficiencies.required_action: Your QU is responsible for fully exercising its authority and responsibilities. FDA is concerned that your QU may also not be conducting appropriate oversight regarding CGMP operations.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Bioresearch Monitoring Program

deficiencies.title: Failure to Ensure Education, Training, and Experience
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to maintain records of trainings that your staff had received to adequately assess their competency and qualifications to perform their assigned tasks for Studies (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include, but are not limited to, the following: a. There is no documentation of protocol-specific trainings for the following individuals who participated in these studies: Dr. GGCH, the Chairman of the Board, and Test Facility Management who was involved in editing and reviewing the final reports for Study (b)(4) and Study (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Please review 21 CFR § 58.33, which sets forth the regulatory requirements for the role of the study director, and inform FDA how you intend to comply with this and the other GLP requirements.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Ensure Study Director Qualifications
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that the protocol, including any change, is approved as provided by 21 CFR § 58.120 and is followed; that all experimental data, including observations of unanticipated responses of the test system are accurately recorded and verified; and that unforeseen circumstances that may affect the quality and integrity of the nonclinical laboratory study are noted when they occur, and corrective action is taken and documented.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, the section of the study # (b)(4) protocol entitled, “Monitoring of Animal Welfare” required that “signs of animal illness, injury, or pain and distress will be communicated to the Study Director and Sponsor Monitor” and “Recommendations for the treatment of the animals must be approved by the Study Director and Sponsor Monitor.” However, it was not documented that these requirements were met for the following animals: Animal (b)(4) – hemorrhagic diarrhea on 3/2/19 – 3/4/19 and treatment with (b)(4) on 3/2/19.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide documentation of the corrective and preventive actions you have implemented to prevent these types of deviations from recurring in future GLP studies, including a copy of all new or revised SOPs not already provided, and documentation of any related trainings.

deficiencies.title: Failure of QAU to Inspect Studies
deficiencies.description: The QAU failed to inspect each nonclinical laboratory study at intervals adequate to assure the integrity of the study and maintain written and properly signed records of each periodic inspection.
deficiencies.evidence: Your QAU failed to maintain a complete copy of the audit schedule sheet to inspect studies at intervals adequate to assure the integrity of the studies. Your QAU also failed to maintain contemporaneously signed and dated documentation or reports of the Critical Phase inspections.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide audit document templates to accompany your SOP that will serve to demonstrate full compliance with the regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Retain Raw Data
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to retain all raw data, documentation, protocols, final reports, and specimens (except those specimens obtained from (b)(4) tests and (b)(4)) generated as a result of a nonclinical laboratory study.
deficiencies.evidence: Twelve (12) of 48 animals surgically treated with the test or control article were missing original source Laboratory Animal Anesthesia Records, which contain details of the surgical procedure for test or control article administration. Your firm also failed to preserve any source records demonstrating your study director’s adherence to the GLP requirements in regard to his conduct of writing the final reports for Studies (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Please note that good record keeping and an organized archive that allows for the expedient retrieval of raw data are ways in which your firm can ensure the quality and integrity of the data generated from your GLP nonclinical studies.

conclusion: Based on our review of the Establishment Inspection Report (EIR), the documents submitted with that report, and your firm’s response dated November 1, 2021, to the Form FDA 483 (“Response Letter”), signed by Mr. Cello, we determined that you violated GLP regulations in 21 CFR Part 58.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Written Procedures for Production and Process Control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS result investigation systems. The CAPA should include but not be limited to addressing the following: QU oversight of laboratory investigations, identification of adverse laboratory control trends, resolution of causes of laboratory variation, initiation of thorough investigations of potential manufacturing causes whenever a laboratory cause cannot be conclusively identified, adequately scoping of each investigation and its CAPA, revised OOS investigation procedures with these and other remediations.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Process Validation
deficiencies.description: You manufacture over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, such as topical creams, including sunscreens. You failed to demonstrate you have adequately validated the manufacturing processes used to manufacture your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Water System Validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm used water as a component in your OTC drug product manufacturing and equipment cleaning. However, you did not validate your water production process nor was routine maintenance performed on your water system as specified by your procedures.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed program for designing, validating, maintaining, controlling, and monitoring each of your manufacturing processes that includes vigilant monitoring of intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure an ongoing state of control. Also, include your program for qualification of your equipment and facility.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to demonstrate that your microbiological test methods were appropriate to assure that your product conforms to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to ensure adequate aseptic behavior and establish appropriate written procedures designed to prevent microbiological contamination risks during the manufacture of drug products purported to be sterile.
deficiencies.evidence: On August 26, 2024, our investigators observed an operator touching the (b)(4) of the (b)(4) with their gloved hand during the set up and assembly of the aseptic processing line. This unacceptable practice of touching the (b)(4) with gloved hands was permitted in your standard operating procedure (SOP-0870-3.0) for filling and sealing on the ampoule line.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities, including an independent assessment that includes, but is not limited to: ... A detailed remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment.

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your investigations into environmental monitoring (EM) excursion results and out-of-specification (OOS) assay results were inadequate.
deficiencies.evidence: A. Your CAPAs to the persistent contamination of objectionable microorganisms were not robust, as evidenced by the continued recovery of gram-negative organisms and spore formers in the ISO 5 and 7 areas and on personnel since 2022.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate system for cleaning and disinfecting the room and equipment to produce aseptic conditions
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate a procedure for cleaning and disinfecting the room and equipment to produce aseptic conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: A. Your firm failed to establish an adequate system for cleaning and disinfecting the room and equipment to produce aseptic conditions.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent review of your personnel and environmental monitoring program, including but not limited to: ... A plan to fully remediate these programs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: False & Misleading Claims/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Risk Presentation
deficiencies.description: The sponsored links are misleading because they include representations about the efficacy of Cotempla XR-ODT but fail to communicate any risk information.
deficiencies.evidence: Prescription drug advertisements and labeling (promotional communications) misbrand a drug if they are false or misleading with respect to risk. The determination of whether a promotional communication is misleading includes, among other things, not only representations made or suggested in the promotional communication, but also the extent to which the promotional communication fails to reveal facts material in light of the representations made or with respect to consequences that may result from the use of the drug as recommended or suggested in the promotional communication.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting promotional communications that are misleading as described above). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Claims about Efficacy
deficiencies.description: The sponsored links for Cotempla XR-ODT include misleading representations about its efficacy, such as: Cotempla XR-ODT for ADHD – Concerta Substitute Learn how Cotempla XR-ODT may help your child. Talk to your child’s doctor today. Having a hard time finding Concerta? Equivalent option to Concerta is available. Medication Guide. Important Safety Info. Patient Savings Offer.
deficiencies.evidence: The representation that Cotempla XR-ODT is a “substitute” for or is “equivalent” to Concerta is misleading because it suggests that Cotempla XR-ODT is therapeutically equivalent to, and therefore substitutable for, Concerta, when that has not been demonstrated.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting promotional communications that are misleading as described above). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Use Required Established Name
deficiencies.description: The sponsored links fail to present the established name for Cotempla XR-ODT, thereby misbranding the product.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA regulations require any labeling or advertising devised for promotion of the drug product to be submitted at the time of initial dissemination of the labeling and at the time of initial publication of the advertisement for a prescription drug product. Each submission is required to be accompanied by a completed transmittal Form FDA-2253 (Transmittal of Advertisements and Promotional Labeling for Drugs for Human Use) and is required to include a copy of the product’s current professional labeling.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting promotional communications that are misleading as described above). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Submit Under Form FDA-2253
deficiencies.description: A copy of the sponsored links was not submitted to FDA under cover of Form FDA-2253 at the time of initial publication as required by 21 CFR 314.81(b)(3)(i).
deficiencies.evidence: FDA regulations require any labeling or advertising devised for promotion of the drug product to be submitted at the time of initial dissemination of the labeling and at the time of initial publication of the advertisement for a prescription drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting promotional communications that are misleading as described above). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

conclusion: For the reasons discussed above, the sponsored links misbrand Cotempla XR-ODT and make the distribution of the drug in violation of the FD&C Act. 21 U.S.C. 352(a), (e)(1)(B), (n); 321(n); 331(a). See 21 CFR 201.10(g)(1); 202.1(b)(1); 202.1 (e)(5).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's 2022 to 2024 complaint logs showed that not all complaints were entered into your firm's complaint handling system for evaluation, investigation, and MDR evaluation.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided DFI-OP-801(V10) titled Customer complaints. While your firm noted that you updated your procedure to include situations where customer complaint reports would not be issued, the updated procedure document is inadequate.
deficiencies.required_action: Once an adequate complaint handling procedure is established, your firm should provide evidence of training on the new procedure for all personnel who may receive complaints, including the sales department. Additionally, your firm should conduct a retrospective review to determine if corrections are needed in response to other complaints involving the possible failure of the device, labeling, or packaging to meet any of their specifications which were not appropriately investigated.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: For example: The investigator noted that during manufacture of the protein reagent (used in the manufacturing of the CYBOW 11), that the reagent failed the pH test three times but only the third failure was documented in the production record.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided the revised document titled, “2-TCF31D0-10_Manufacture process” and your nonconformity procedure to support this response. The revised manufacturing process document now states “” and the nonconformity product management document provided in your firm’s response includes a reference to “”. However, the documentation provided by your firm does not require that , including a determination of any adverse effect from the the product, shall be documented in the device history record, as required by 21 CFR 820.90(b)(2).
deficiencies.required_action: Once an adequate nonconforming product procedure is established, your firm should provide evidence of training on the new procedures. Additionally, your firm should conduct a retrospective review to determine if other nonconforming product was appropriately handled.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control labeling activities
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's representative, Ms. Yong-Ae Park, stated to the investigator that your firm does not have a written procedure to control labeling activities, and that your firm's device history records (DHR) do not include or refer to the location of the primary identification labeling nor the unique device identifiers (UDI) used for each production unit/lot/batch for the CYBOW 11.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided documentation indicating that you added information relating to labeling to your inspection standard forms for raw and finished products related to the CYBOW 11, as well as an import inspection certificate for the label and a QC inspection report form with a location for the label. However, while the documents mention label integrity, your firm has not provided evidence to show that you have implemented activities to ensure that labels shall be printed and applied so as to remain legible and affixed during the customary conditions of processing, storage, handling, distribution, and where appropriate use as required by 21 CFR 820.120(a).
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide evidence to show that you have implemented activities to ensure that labels shall be printed and applied so as to remain legible and affixed during the customary conditions of processing, storage, handling, distribution, and where appropriate use.

conclusion: We conclude that your firm's response is not adequate. Your firm should provide evidence of training on the new procedures for all personnel who may receive complaints, including the sales department. Additionally, your firm should conduct a retrospective review to determine if corrections are needed in response to other complaints involving the possible failure of the device, labeling, or packaging to meet any of their specifications which were not appropriately investigated.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate several out-of-specification (OOS) laboratory results from October 2024 to June 2025 and lacked corrective action and preventive action (CAPA).
deficiencies.evidence: You manufacture over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, including (b)(4) medications. You failed to thoroughly investigate several out-of-specification (OOS) laboratory results from October 2024 to June 2025 and lacked corrective action and preventive action (CAPA). For example, (b)(4) tablets, lot (b)(4), and (b)(4) tablets, lot (b)(4), failed assay testing at the 9-month stability timepoint. Your retest using additional samples also produced failing results.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS results for U.S. products currently in the U.S. market and within expiry as of the date of this letter and a report summarizing the findings of the analysis...

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: You failed to demonstrate that your sample preparation methods are suitable for testing drugs or to account for how these methods could affect test results.
deficiencies.evidence: You use (b)(4) to prepare samples of your (b)(4) tablets and (b)(4) tablets for high-performance liquid chromatography analysis. However, (b)(4), which can lead to inaccurate results showing OOS levels for (b)(4) content.
deficiencies.required_action: An evaluation of the adequacy of your analytical sample preparation methods by verifying suitability through documented protocol, analyzing recurring errors throughout the methods' history, and implementing CAPA measures to address test method deficiencies...

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to use equipment in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products that is of appropriate design, adequate size, and suitably located to facilitate operations for its intended use and for its cleaning and maintenance
deficiencies.description: Your non-dedicated manufacturing equipment was not designed and maintained appropriately to prevent potential cross-contamination of drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Numerous drug substance residues, including (b)(4), were recovered upon swabbing of (b)(4). Your (b)(4) are used to manufacture multiple drug products, including therapeutics such as (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive retrospective risk assessment that addresses all possible cross-contamination, including but not limited to, highly potent substances such as (b)(4).

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure of quality control unit to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) failed to adequately implement the facility's quality function and ensure quality oversight.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to have an adequate procedure to clean (b)(4). Your approved procedure did not include sufficient directions to clean the (b)(4) ducts, including directions on disassembly and visual inspection for cleanliness.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Component testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You relied on certificates of analysis (COAs) from your suppliers to use incoming API without establishing the reliability of the specifications and characteristics of each supplier's COAs.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: 2. Batch release testing
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate release testing for each batch of your drug product prior to distribution to the U.S. Market.
deficiencies.evidence: You released your OTC drug product without assay testing of the active ingredient.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies.

deficiencies.title: 3. Process validation
deficiencies.description: You failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform process qualification studies and did not validate your manufacturing processes for your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

deficiencies.title: 4. Stability testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have adequate stability data to demonstrate that the chemical and microbiological properties of your drug products met established specifications and remain acceptable throughout their shelf-life.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform stability studies for each drug product in its marketed container-closure system before distribution is permitted.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violation
deficiencies.description: Your SeniorLife.AI mobile application is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, because you do not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE). The SeniorLife.AI mobile application is also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the device into commercial distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples from the SeniorLife Technologies, Inc. website include: “Age Smarter – AI Agents for mobility and cognitive health - built for seniors, their families and care providers.”
deficiencies.required_action: Cease any activities that result in the misbranding or adulteration of SeniorLife.AI.

deficiencies.title: Quality System Regulation Violations
deficiencies.description: This inspection revealed that these devices are adulterated within the meaning of section 501(h) of the Act, in that the methods used in, or the facilities or controls used for, their manufacture, packing, storage, or installation are not in conformity with the current good manufacturing practice requirements of the Quality System regulation.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: failure to adequately establish procedures for corrective and preventive action, failure to adequately establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints by a formally designated unit, failure to establish procedures for quality audits, and failure to establish procedures for training and identifying training needs.
deficiencies.required_action: Take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter.

deficiencies.title: Medical Device Reporting (MDR) Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures as required by 21 CFR 803.17.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, Mr. Ashok Roy, President, and Ms. Shoko Hoshi, Co-Founder and Product Designer, acknowledged that your firm has not developed a written MDR procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter.

deficiencies.title: Labeling Violation
deficiencies.description: Your device is further misbranded within the meaning of section 502(a) of the Act, because your product's website includes false and misleading representations.
deficiencies.evidence: Representations on and available through your website create an impression of official approval of the device and other potential endorsement by FDA and are false and misleading. The Apple and Android store app links on your website lead to the digital marketplaces from where purchasers can download the software.
deficiencies.required_action: Take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter.

conclusion: Your firm should take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter. Failure to adequately address this matter may result in regulatory action being initiated by the FDA without further notice.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/PMA/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control the design of the device in order to ensure that specified design requirements are met
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not adequately controlled and documented the design of your MCH devices.
deficiencies.evidence: i. Your firm changed your specification for microbial load measured by heterotrophic plate count (HPC) from CFU/ml to CFU/ml and removed requirements for testing. Your firm did not validate this change to ensure that it meets the intended uses for the device.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm needs to submit a new premarket notification submission under section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k), to FDA at least 90 days before you proposed to begin the introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution of the modified Modular Cooler-Heater devices (“Modified MCH Devices”).

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not validated your cleaning processes recommended in your product manuals and utilized during servicing of your devices.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm conducted HPC testing on water samples collected pre- and post-cleaning in February 2016 and March 2017; however, these tests do not have the necessary information to support that the process consistently meets specification. For example, the records do not identify sample collection methods, sample sizes, disinfectant residue, or acceptance criteria.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm needs to complete an adequate validation of your cleaning processes and provide evidence that all processes requiring validation have been validated.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: For example, your firm initiated CAPA 77 in response to an increase of complaints for device contamination with NTM on December 14, 2020.
deficiencies.evidence: Your CAPA records state your firm has not linked your device to patient infection; however, your firm has reported events in which you confirmed your device was linked to infection. Additionally, your firm identified revisions to your cleaning procedures as a corrective action for this CAPA. However, your firm has not validated these cleaning procedures despite the closure of this CAPA.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm needs to accurately investigate the cause of quality problems and validate all processes requiring validation.

conclusion: We acknowledge your firm has initiated CAPA 63 to address deficiencies with process validations to include your cleaning processes. However, your firm has not provided evidence you have completed an adequate validation of your cleaning processes. Additionally, your response does not include a systemic review of your processes to verify that all processes requiring validation have been validated.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS results for U.S. products currently in the U.S. market and within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Test Samples of Each Component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform adequate identity testing on each shipment of each lot of incoming components (e.g., glycerin) used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to provide DEG and EG test results from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Adequate Written Procedures for Production
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not have written procedures that describe your methods, facilities, or controls for manufacturing, processing, packing, or holding.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS result investigation systems.

conclusion: Because your methods, facilities, or controls for manufacturing, processing, packing, or holding do not conform to CGMP, your drug products are adulterated within the meaning of section 501(a)(2)(B) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. 351(a)(2)(B).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Batch production records were found torn in plastic bags on your rooftop. Among the torn records were missing batch production records that you were not able to locate during the inspection...
deficiencies.required_action: Investigate retrospectively created batch records and perform assessments regarding data integrity practices and capabilities of quality unit organization.

deficiencies.title: 2. Laboratory Records Inadequacy
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.
deficiencies.evidence: Internal test report for Patch product, batch ..., was missing the individual results for assay, patch weight, peel strength analysis and rolling ball analysis.
deficiencies.required_action: Initiate a deviation to investigate instances where raw data is not available. Conduct a comprehensive assessment on data integrity practices at your site.

deficiencies.title: 3. Computer System Controls Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to exercise appropriate controls over computer or related systems to assure that only authorized personnel institute changes in master production and control records, or other records.
deficiencies.evidence: QC analyst had the ability to manipulate results, dates, and images of in-house and vendor stamp approvals on component certificate of analyses (COAs) from third-party contract laboratories.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement measures for maintaining data integrity and defining user privileges for computer systems, including measures for appropriate oversight and maintenance of data and results from third-party contract laboratories.

conclusion: Your quality system does not adequately ensure the accuracy and integrity of data to support the safety, effectiveness, and quality of the drugs you manufacture.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products and the authority to review production records to assure that no errors have occurred or, if errors have occurred, that they have been fully investigated.
deficiencies.evidence: Our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed plan to ensure you do not receive or deliver adulterated drugs in interstate commerce

deficiencies.title: Failure to qualify contract manufacturers
deficiencies.description: Your firm utilized a CMO to manufacture your ophthalmic drug products. However, your quality unit failed to have adequate supplier qualification procedures to ensure that the drug products received from your CMO were manufactured in compliance with CGMP prior to being distributed in the United States.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, our investigators observed that your firm identified several CGMP deficiencies with Brassica in September 2020, such as a failure to maintain raw testing data. Despite identifying these deficiencies, your firm qualified Brassica as an approved CMO. Furthermore, you requalified Brassica in 2023, without evaluating whether subsequent corrective actions and preventive actions adequately addressed the identified deficiencies.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed plan to ensure you do not receive or deliver adulterated drugs in interstate commerce

deficiencies.title: Failure to evaluate CGMP findings of contract manufacturers
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality unit released Lubricant PM Ointment for distribution without adequate procedures to ensure that the ophthalmic drug products produced for your firm met appropriate quality attributes.
deficiencies.evidence: In your response, you state that your procedures have been revised to assess CGMP findings of your CMOs, and that your Quality Assurance must evaluate corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plans implemented by CMOs. Your response is inadequate. While you have revised your procedures, you did not provide sufficient details on how you will assess findings that include CGMP deficiencies.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed plan to ensure you do not receive or deliver adulterated drugs in interstate commerce

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigator observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications, standards, sampling plans, and test procedures designed to assure that components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, labeling, and drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: You could not provide documentation that the analytical methods used by your contract laboratory for chemical release assays were validated for your specific drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to use equipment in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to use equipment in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products that is of appropriate design, adequate size, and suitably located to facilitate operations for its intended use and for its cleaning and maintenance.
deficiencies.evidence: The (b)(4) water system was not designed and maintained appropriately for its intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: A feasibility study and “evaluate the cost” of modifying the water system, remove the dead leg and add a water loop.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. A feasibility study and “evaluate the cost” of modifying the water system, remove the dead leg and add a water loop.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failed to adequately establish procedures for corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately implement procedures for corrective and preventive action (CAPA) in that you failed to adequately analyze all sources of quality data, failed to identify actions needed to correct nonconforming product, and you did not appropriately verify or validate the change to your device to ensure corrective and preventive actions taken were effective and did not adversely affect the finished device.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of implementation for all corrections and corrective actions

deficiencies.title: Failed to identify the actions needed to control devices already in distribution
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not identify the actions needed to control devices already in distribution. As previously discussed, your firm's justification in June 2016 for not initiating a recall was based on your firm's underestimated risk calculation.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of implementation for all corrections and corrective actions

deficiencies.title: Failed to verify and/or validate the corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to verify and/or validate the corrective and preventive actions to ensure the action taken was effective to prevent recurrence of the nonconformance and did not adversely affect the finished device.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of implementation for all corrections and corrective actions

deficiencies.title: Failed to re-evaluate the effectiveness of your corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.description: You did not re-evaluate the effectiveness of your corrective and preventive actions to determine whether CAPA #299677 should be re-evaluated.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of implementation for all corrections and corrective actions

deficiencies.title: Failed to adequately address cybersecurity vulnerability
deficiencies.description: Your firm initiated CAPA #401464 on June 29, 2018, to address a cybersecurity vulnerability with the remote controllers used with your Medtronic MiniMed 508 Insulin Infusion Pump and your MiniMed Paradigm Insulin Infusion Pumps. However, these corrective actions did not address the devices in the field.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of implementation for all corrections and corrective actions

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventative action (CAPA)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm initiated CAPA 20/028 to address customer complaints reporting DXD100 mobile x-ray units self-propelling crashing into x-ray control panel in one case and almost running into the patient, in the other case. This issue affects all mobile x-ray unit models. However, your firm failed to document an assessment of the risk of the affected units in the field for this issue, when investigating the cause of nonconformities relating to the product.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response states that you will revise procedures PG-031 Customer complaint and PG-020 Risk management procedures to ensure that an investigation is documented properly. You also stated that you will revise procedure PG-019 Corrective and Preventive action and conduct training for the relevant personnel.
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of corrective action, retrospective review as well as training is needed in order to adequately address this observation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm conducted a recall to address unintended movement of MobileDiagnost wDR that could result in life threatening situations and cause the device to stop working as intended. However, the risk analysis for MobileDiagnost wDR (Document IH0532RA_Annex, dated April 26, 2022) fails to include this increased hazard severity (life threatening) to ensure that appropriate risk control measures are implemented.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response states that you will re-evaluate and document the risk analysis for the recall. You also state that you will revise procedures PG-031 Customer complaint and PG-020 Risk management procedures to account for risk analysis.
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of corrective action, retrospective review as well as training is needed in order to adequately address this observation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a written report of a correction or removal of a device
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm initiated CAPA 20/028 to address customer complaints reporting DXD100 mobile x-ray units self-propelling crashing into x-ray control panel in one case and almost running into the patient, in the other case. This issue affects all mobile x-ray unit models. Your firm implemented two corrective actions to address this issue and issued a service note to be distributed to customers. However, your firm failed to submit a report of correction or removal to FDA for these actions.
deficiencies.evidence: Your investigation of the issue concluded that this issue does not pose a risk to safety due to the low speed of the movement and did not consider it as a reportable recall.
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of corrective action, retrospective review as well as training is needed in order to adequately address this observation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct certification of an electronic product
deficiencies.description: Specifically, a review of the design control records revealed that the wDR2.2 was approved and released for distribution in the US without documentation to show that the required performance standard checklist (Technical Report Subchapter J of 21 CFR MobileDiagnost wDR 2.2, Doc #R01005_21CKL) was completed to demonstrate compliance with the performance standard requirements prior to release of the product.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated that you will review and revise procedures PE-062 Design Verification, PG-044 Organization of Project Teams among others.
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation of corrective action, retrospective review as well as training is needed in order to adequately address this observation.

deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'Semaglutide (Ozempic)', 'Tirzepatide (Zepbound)', 'Tirzepatide (generic Zepbound)'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from introducing or delivering misbranded products into interstate commerce

conclusion: Please take immediate action to address any violations, including ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for acceptance of incoming product
deficiencies.description: On at least four occasions, from January 2024 through July 2024, your firm accepted sterilization bag lots that failed your thickness requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: You indicated that CAPA 2024-0021 was opened but did not provide a copy that describes the preventive actions you are planning to address this serious observation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Design Control Procedure, DE-01. Specifically, your firm has not completed design verification and validation activities for 25 different FDA product codes in use at this facility.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should complete the review of components to ensure that only devices with complete DHF’s, including complete component evaluations, are currently being distributed.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: A. Your (b)(4) suppositories batch # (b)(4) three-month long-term (25ºC / 60% relative humidity) stability sample assay result was (b)(4) mg per suppository (acceptable range: (b)(4) mg).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Ensure Laboratory Records Include Complete Data
deficiencies.description: Your laboratory records did not include all testing data to support the drug product analyses performed.
deficiencies.evidence: Incomplete laboratory records Our investigator observed entries were not entered contemporaneously in your laboratory notebook.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a thorough assessment of the adequacy of your test methods used for testing and retesting OOS samples.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Electronic Records Controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have proper controls in place to prevent the deletion or manipulation of your laboratory's electronic data.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your analysts had administrative rights on your chromatographic systems that allowed them to alter and delete data, files, and folders...
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS result investigation systems.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
