deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: The Salivary Cortisol ELISA (REF No. SLV-2930) and Salivary Cortisol ELISA RUO (REF No. SLV-2930R) are adulterated because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE).
deficiencies.evidence: FDA has reviewed evidence collected during the inspection and your firm's website
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a 510(k) for this device, because either it is a new device or if your Salivary Cortisol ELISA RUO represents a change or modification from the device cleared in K051733

deficiencies.title: Misbranding Violations
deficiencies.description: The Salivary Cortisol ELISA and Salivary Cortisol ELISA RUO are misbranded because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce these devices into commercial distribution, with major changes or modifications to the intended use.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA has reviewed evidence collected during the inspection and your firm's website
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a new premarket notification to FDA as required by section 510(k) of the Act

deficiencies.title: Failure to Submit 510(k)
deficiencies.description: Your firm has failed to submit a premarket notification submission to the Food and Drug Administration for significant changes that could significantly affect the safety or effectiveness of the device.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's Warning Letter dated September 19, 2017 issued to your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a new 510(k) is needed due to the use of a new monoclonal (mouse) antibody for the Salivary Cortisol ELISA

deficiencies.title: Failure to Correct Previous Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm was notified in the 2017 Warning Letter that the Salivary Cortisol ELISA devices manufactured by your firm are subject to refusal of admission under section 801(a) of the Act, and FDA is taking steps to refuse entry of these devices into the United States.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's Warning Letter dated September 19, 2017 issued to your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Correct previous violations

deficiencies.title: Uncleared Devices Marketed on Website
deficiencies.description: Your firm's website includes a tab called 'FDA Cleared Tests' and lists several devices for which there is no record of a 510(k) clearance held by your firm.
deficiencies.evidence: Review of our records indicates that DRG Instruments does not have a 510(k) for certain devices listed on the website
deficiencies.required_action: Provide FDA with evidence that those devices are legally marketed or take corrective actions to address any additional uncleared devices marketed on the website

deficiencies.title: Misleading Statement on Website
deficiencies.description: Your firm's website states that DRG Hybrid XL is 'Now FDA Approved' but also separately indicates that the DRG Hybrid XL is 'FDA exempt for Class I'.
deficiencies.evidence: Review of your firm's website
deficiencies.required_action: Clarify this statement on the website because indicating that a class I 510(k) exempt product has been reviewed and approved by FDA could be misleading

conclusion: The FDA will evaluate the information that your firm submits and decide whether the product may be legally marketed.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Manufacture/Packing/Storage/Installation/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure
deficiencies.description: Your firm uses (b)(4) to sterilize your silicone implant devices. Your firm has not qualified this (b)(4) can deliver your (b)(4) process with a high degree of assurance.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's response dated September 24, 2021 acknowledges your firm has not validated the referenced processes and states your firm is working to identify methods of validation. However, there is insufficient information for us to evaluate the adequacy of these corrective actions as no validation plans have been provided.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a validation plan and complete the required validations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately maintain complaint files and establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not adequately documented the nature and details of complaints or the dates and results of investigations conducted as required by 21 CFR 820.198(e).
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's response includes revisions to your complaint procedure to better define requirements for investigations. Additionally, your response includes documentation showing your firm reevaluated the referenced complaints using your revised complaint form.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should conduct and document thorough complaint investigations moving forward.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Specifically, on June 15, 2020, your firm initiated a Corrective and Preventive Action Questionnaire in response to identifying your (b)(4) was (b)(4) degrees instead of (b)(4) degrees as set.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's response states you have made changes to your CAPA processes including revision to your Corrective and Preventive Action Questionnaire to include changes to scope and risk assessment. However, your corresponding CAPA procedure does not include any revisions to detail how to complete these activities.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should revise the CAPA procedure to include details on completing the activities.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's Non-Conforming Products and Corrective and Preventive Action (Document Number QA012 Rev. B, Date Effective January 9, 2003) states when nonconforming products are identified, they should be reported to Quality Assurance for disposition.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's response identifies some modifications to your Non-Conforming Products and Corrective and Preventive Action (Document Number QA012 Rev. B, Date Effective January 9, 2003) involving product rework. However, these revised procedures continue to fail to address how the disposition and review of products will be documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should revise the procedure to include details on documenting the disposition and review of nonconforming products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate design validation procedures
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's Design Control procedure, Document Number QA004 Rev. A, Date effective May 6, 2002 requires design validation to be conducted but does not provide procedures to be followed.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm's response states you have revaluated your validation of the 5 year shelf-life and provided the supporting records. However, these records of your validation do not address the deficiencies with the validation identified during the inspection and noted above.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should revise the design validation procedure to include details on conducting the validation.

conclusion: We have reviewed your responses and determined they are inadequate. Your firm's responses state you have made changes to your CAPA processes, revised complaint procedures, and reevaluated your validation of the 5 year shelf-life. However, these revisions do not address the deficiencies identified during the inspection.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm’s quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity (21 CFR 211.22).
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactured over-the-counter (OTC) isopropyl alcohol (IPA) prep pad drug products, labeled as sterile, that are intended as an antiseptic for preparation of the skin prior to an injection. Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate that your quality unit (QU) effectively exercised its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release (21 CFR 211.165(a))
deficiencies.description: Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested your OTC IPA prep pad drug product for the identity and strength of the active ingredient, IPA, prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

deficiencies.title: 3. Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1))
deficiencies.description: Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate that you adequately tested the identity of your incoming components including glycerin, propylene glycol, ethanol, and IPA that are used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any violations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test that the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not validated the manufacturing process of the SAFE-T-FILL Capillary Blood Collection Systems.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm's procedure, 'QM VA_0026', Validation of Production Machines, Rev I, dated December 19, 2023, requires existing machines and process equipment to be validated using installation qualification, functional qualification, and performance qualification; however, these processes were not validated.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a systemic corrective action plan to address the validation of manufacturing processes for all devices manufactured at this facility including evidence of implementation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that all equipment used in the manufacturing process meets specified requirements and is appropriately designed, constructed, placed, and installed to facilitate maintenance, adjustment, cleaning, and use
deficiencies.description: It appears that machines used in manufacturing have not been qualified for their intended use.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm's CAPA-ID 23G4429_1 described failure of a machine that resulted in damaged capillaries in the finished products for batches 23G4429 and 23H4113. The root cause of the failure was identified as that the 'machine has not been qualified for its use'.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide evidence that machines have been retroactively qualified nor a corrective action to ensure that all future equipment used during manufacturing will be qualified for their intended use.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device in order to ensure that specified design requirements are met
deficiencies.description: Your firm does not have a design control procedure that details design input, design output, design verification, design validation, design transfer, design review, and design history.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm provided flow charts to describe these design control processes in 'QM_AA_0019', Project Process, Rev. I, Effective Date June 19, 2024. Additionally, the design change procedure 'AA_0011', Change of a Medical Device, Effective Date August 07, 2024, and 'VA_0028', Change Management – Change Control, Effective Date March 12, 2024, simply list changes that need to be checked by management before implementation, but do not describe how your firm manages changes to the device to ensure the quality of their products are unaffected.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a systemic corrective action to address design history files for all devices manufactured and sold in the US and provide your plans for remediation of those devices.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm's CAPA procedure 'QM_VA_0006', Deviations, Complaints, and CAPA Management, Effective Date April 03, 2023, does not adequately describe the procedure for conducting root cause investigations, implementing corrective actions effectively, verifying the effectiveness of corrective and preventive actions, and using statistical methodology (to detect trends) where necessary.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm's CAPA 06.2023 was initiated because your firm failed to assess the validation for assembling cannulas with extension tubes which was identified during the external audit conducted by an independent service company that inspects and certifies technical systems. This CAPA was closed on November 23, 2023, without establishing and implementing the corrective actions adequately.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide evidence that the software used to document the design history file is adequately validated.

deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.

Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed (21 CFR 211.192).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Test Samples of Each Component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to perform identity testing of each component lot used in the production of your over-the-counter drug products, including (b)(4), your active pharmaceutical ingredient. You also relied on the certificates of analysis (COAs) from your suppliers and failed to establish the reliability of each of your component suppliers’ test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Exercise Appropriate Controls over Computer Systems
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to exercise appropriate controls over computer or related systems to assure that only authorized personnel institute changes in master production and control records, or other records.
deficiencies.evidence: Your laboratory equipment used to generate analytical data for finished drug product release lacked restricted access and sufficient controls. There was no assurance that your systems had the appropriate controls to prevent data deletion and record modifications.
deficiencies.required_action: A complete assessment of documentation systems used throughout your manufacturing and laboratory operations to determine where documentation practices are insufficient. Include a detailed CAPA plan that comprehensively remediates your firm’s documentation practices, to ensure you retain attributable, legible, complete, original, accurate, contemporaneous records throughout your operation.

conclusion: Based upon the nature of the violations we identified at your firm, we strongly recommend engaging a consultant qualified to evaluate your operations as set forth in 21 CFR 211.34 to assist your firm in meeting CGMP requirements.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Atomix LLC offers products marketed as selective androgen receptor modulators (SARMs) for sale in the United States, which are unapproved new drugs under section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Product labeling, including website and social media posts, establishes that these products are intended to be drugs for human use.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action must be taken to prevent recurrence of violations

deficiencies.title: Misbranding
deficiencies.description: Atomix LLC's products marketed as SARMs are drugs under section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act, because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, and/or intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Product labeling, including website and social media posts, provides evidence of the intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of these products as drugs.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action must be taken to prevent recurrence of violations

deficiencies.title: Failure to Obtain Approval
deficiencies.description: Atomix LLC's products marketed as SARMs are unapproved new drugs, and introducing or delivering these products for introduction into interstate commerce violates sections 301(d) and 505(a) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: No approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action must be taken to prevent recurrence of violations

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to investigate any complaint involving the possible failure of a device, labeling or packaging to meet any of its specifications

G, J; Effective: 4/11/19, 6/30/20).

deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on these corrective actions.

deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on these corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive actions

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Bioresearch Monitoring Program/Institutional Review Board (IRB)

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit an IDE application to FDA and failure to obtain FDA approval prior to beginning an investigation for which FDA's approval is required
deficiencies.description: Sponsors must submit an IDE application to the FDA if the sponsor intends to use a significant risk device in a clinical investigation. In addition, a sponsor may not begin an investigation for which FDA's approval of an application is required until FDA has approved the application.
deficiencies.evidence: Your study, UHP001, was conducted without submission and approval of an IDE application to FDA. The Western Copernicus Group (WCG) Institutional Review Board (IRB) determined that the procedures in the research posed a potential significant risk to subjects and requested documentation of the FDA's non-significant risk (NSR) determination in multiple communications.
deficiencies.required_action: Prompt corrective action is required to address this violation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure proper monitoring of the investigation
deficiencies.description: Sponsors are responsible for ensuring that a clinical investigation is properly monitored.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include, but are not limited to, the following: ... You failed to maintain adequate documentation regarding those monitoring activities that did occur...
deficiencies.required_action: Prompt corrective action is required to address this violation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to secure clinical investigator's compliance with the signed agreement, the investigational plan, applicable FDA regulations, and any other conditions of approval imposed by the reviewing IRB or FDA
deficiencies.description: As part of monitoring responsibilities, a sponsor is responsible for securing the investigator's compliance with the investigational plan to ensure adherence with the study protocol and other regulatory requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include, but are not limited to, the following: ... You failed to obtain and secure the clinical investigator's compliance with the investigational plan and applicable FDA regulations...
deficiencies.required_action: Prompt corrective action is required to address this violation.

conclusion: This letter also requests prompt corrective action to address the violations cited and discusses your written response, dated October 25, 2024, to the noted violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control the design of the device in order to ensure that specified design requirements are met, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(a)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm did not conduct any design validation, risk analysis, or design reviews for the Class II XR-01 Portable X-ray System, despite having shipped the product to the U.S. since 2023.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct design validation and design review; propose additions to your “New Product Design and Development Process Procedures”

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action, as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, review of the CAPAs (19 CAPAs, from 2022 to 2024) revealed your firm did not conduct any verification/validation to ensure that such action is effective and does not adversely affect the finished device prior to implementation, and there is no documented evidence showing that your firm has implemented the proposed corrective actions.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct verification/validation; implement CAPAs with documentation

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints by a formally designated unit, as required by 21 CFR 820.198(a)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, not all complaints are entered into your firm’s complaint handling system; instead many of these complaints (reviewed from 2022 to 2024) were logged and treated as repairs.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints

deficiencies.title: Failure to identify by suitable means the acceptance status of product, to indicate the conformance or nonconformance of product with acceptance criteria, as required by 21 CFR 820.86
deficiencies.description: Specifically, a unit of the XR-01 Portable X-ray System was not identified and/or accompanied with proper documentation throughout the manufacturing/assembly and testing processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Establish procedures for identifying acceptance status of product

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate computer software for its intended use according to an established protocol, as required by 21 CFR 820.70(i)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not validated the (b)(4) software used to perform the final tests of Portable X-ray Systems for its intended use according to an established protocol.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Validate computer software for its intended use

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain acceptance procedures to control labeling activities, as required by 21 CFR 820.120
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has yet to establish procedures to control labeling activities for the Portable X-ray System to include: label integrity; labeling inspection; labeling storage; and labeling operations.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Establish acceptance procedures to control labeling activities

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that DHR's for each batch, lot, or unit are maintained to demonstrate that the device is manufactured in accordance with the DMR and the requirements of this part, as required by 21 CFR 820.184
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm’s manufacturing procedures/working instructions for the Portable X-ray System are incomplete in that the procedures/working instructions do not require the technician(s) to document any of the assembly and packaging steps in the production record (showing that they were performed).
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Establish adequate procedures for DHR's

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures as required by 21 CFR 803.17
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm’s Incident Reporting Process Procedure, Document #A-14-02, is inadequate in that: • It does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s response dated July 12, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures

conclusion: We conclude that your firm's responses are not adequate. Your firm has failed to address the noted violations and provide supporting documentation for our evaluation.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Medical Device Reporting/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to FDA no later than 30 calendar days after the day that your firm received or otherwise became aware of information, from any source, that reasonably suggests that a device that your firm markets has malfunctioned and this device or a similar device that it markets would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury, if the malfunction were to recur.
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to submit an MDR for each of the referenced complaints.
deficiencies.evidence: The responses noted that your firm initiated systemic corrections and corrective actions, such as a root-cause investigation and plan to improve MDR reporting process. However, your firm did not provide documentation or evidence of implementation of its corrective actions as they are still ongoing.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should submit an MDR for each of the referenced complaints and provide documentation or evidence of implementation of its corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a written report to FDA of any correction or removal of a device initiated to remedy a violation of the Act caused by the device which may present a risk to health.
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not submit a written report to FDA of the removal, as required by 21 CFR 806.10.
deficiencies.evidence: As of October 1, 2021, there is no record of Smith Medical ASD, Inc. submitting a Report of Correction or Removal to FDA.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should submit a written report to FDA of any correction or removal of a device initiated to remedy a violation of the Act caused by the device which may present a risk to health.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain design validation procedures to ensure that devices conform to defined user needs and intended uses.
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not validate the Medfusion software version 1.6.1 for use with the firmware bootloader version 1.2; your firm estimated that (b)(4) units potentially exist in the field with an unvalidated software combination.
deficiencies.evidence: Your design change CO-10073172 released a “pre-release” of software version 1.7.0 and base bootloader 1.2 for the Medfusion 4000 that was not validated.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain design validation procedures to ensure that devices conform to defined user needs and intended uses.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for corrective and preventative action, with documentation of activities and results.
deficiencies.description: Your CAPA procedure has not been adequately established.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPAs did not implement changes in methods or procedures to correct or prevent the identified quality problems.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish procedures for corrective and preventative action, with documentation of activities and results.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Compounded Tirzepatide Products
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including tirzepatide, with claims that are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA).

deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'FDA-Approved Active', 'eDrugstore guarantees that all Tirzepatide medications are genuine, FDA-approved formulations for maximum efficacy and safety.'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from introducing or delivering misbranded products into interstate commerce.

conclusion: Please take immediate action to address any violations. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

metadata.company_name: Guangdong Renhe Guozhuang Biotechnology Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. Provide an action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of reserve samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct identity testing on incoming components
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product, including high-risk drug components such as (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide commitment to provide (b)(4) test results from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a retain of a component lot is unavailable, perform retain sample testing of all implicated finished drug product batches for the presence of (b)(4).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a timeline for performing appropriate process performance qualification for each of your marketed drug products. Also include an explanation how you will ensure proper satisfactory process performance qualification studies are performed prior to future distribution of any drug products.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for the identification, documentation, validation or where appropriate verification, review, and approval of design changes before their implementation
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your Design Change Control procedure, SOP002, and Design Change Management - Medical Devices, WI002A, address the processing of design changes, the former document requiring a determination of all actions for a design change, including validation and verification. However, multiple design changes were instead processed using Manufacturing Deviation, SOP025-5.1, intended for temporary deviations from manufacturing procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple design changes were processed using Manufacturing Deviation, SOP025-5.1
deficiencies.required_action: Update your firm's procedure for design changes and to train personnel in the verification and validation activities

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm failed to validate the use and integration of the software and panels used in the Micro-X Rover Mobile X-ray System MXU-RV71. Your firm failed to document justification for the decision not to validate.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to validate the use and integration of the software and panels
deficiencies.required_action: Update the validation plan for MXU-RV71; however, documentation of validation has yet to be provided

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action (CAPA)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, Quality Event and CAPA procedure, SOP006, fails to require that appropriate statistical methodology be employed where necessary to detect recurring quality problems, in order to identify existing and potential causes of nonconforming product.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to include review of manufacturing records, device history files, and field service tickets when investigating a quality event
deficiencies.required_action: Review the SOP006 Quality Event & CAPA and include additional sources of quality data; update the CAPA form to include review of manufacturing records, device history files, and field service tickets

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure complaints are evaluated to determine whether the complaint represents an event which is required to be reported to FDA under 21 CFR Part 803
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the complaints of reported malfunctions of the Micro-X Rover Mobile X-ray Systems did not explicitly include a determination of MDR reportability.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple complaint documents (e.g., CC022-23, CC017-23, CC004-23, CC010-23) did not explicitly include a determination of MDR reportability
deficiencies.required_action: Update the Customer Complaint Management procedure and to review previous customer complaints and include a rationale and justification for non-reporting

deficiencies.title: Failure to report a medical device correction or removal to FDA
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm failed to report the following 2 field corrections conducted to reduce a risk to health posed by Micro-X Rover Mobile X-ray Systems: Service Bulletin, SB006-23, and Service Bulletin, SB007-23-1.0.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to report the field corrections
deficiencies.required_action: Implement corrective actions such as developing a procedure/work instruction for issuing Service Bulletins and regulatory reporting process for design changes to be developed; retroactively report the 806 actions to FDA

deficiencies.title: Failure to comply with EPRC performance standard
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm failed to notify FDA of the following product defects and/or failure(s) to comply with an EPRC performance standard: Service Bulletin, SB003-23, and Service Bulletin, SB002-22.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to notify FDA of the product defects
deficiencies.required_action: Develop a procedure/work instruction for issuing Service Bulletins; regulatory reporting process for design changes to be developed; retroactively report the 806 actions to FDA

conclusion: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

deficiencies.evidence: Representing that the KN95 Mask is a “Non-NIOSH Approved Respirator” that provides
deficiencies.required_action: Cease the sale of any adulterated and misbranded products for the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Lack of method validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate the analytical methods used to test your customers’ drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Lack of method validation
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Inadequate system suitability
deficiencies.description: Your high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) analyses for drug products lacked an appropriate system suitability determination, as outlined in USP <621>, prior to sample injections.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate system suitability
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. The assessment should also include, but not be limited to: ... A complete and final review of the laboratory record for each drug sample and its related information before the QU disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: 3. Inadequate growth promotion testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and follow procedures for growth promotion testing of your microbiological media to assure suitability before use.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate growth promotion testing
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective risk assessment of prior testing to assure suitability of all growth media for the last 3 years of drug microbiological testing to ensure reliable microbial enumeration and appropriate identification of objectionable microorganisms including, but not limited to, Burkholderia cepacia complex, and a commitment to notify customers of any deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: 4. Failure of quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure of quality control unit
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. The assessment should also include, but not be limited to: ... A complete and final review of the laboratory record for each drug sample and its related information before the QU disposition decision.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity strength, and quality.
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures over-the-counter (OTC) drugs both for topical and oral use. You failed to adequately test incoming components, such as glycerin, for identity before using them in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to perform adequate identity testing on a representative sample using the USP identification test, which detects these hazardous impurities
deficiencies.required_action: Provide DEG and EG test results from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a retain of a component lot is unavailable, perform retain sample testing of all implicated finished drug product batches for the presence of DEG and EG.

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products manufactured have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate your manufacturing processes for your OTC drug products (e.g., ...). You are responsible for assuring your manufacturing processes will consistently result in drug products meeting predefined quality attributes.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to validate manufacturing processes
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a thorough remediation plan to install and operate a suitable water system to produce water adhering to USP monograph specifications and appropriate microbial limits.

deficiencies.title: 3. Your firm’s quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) lacked adequate control over your OTC drug product manufacturing operations and failed to ensure that you had adequate procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to establish an adequate ongoing stability program, perform periodic product reviews, use of adequate laboratory control mechanisms
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). Take prompt and appropriate actions to determine the safety of all lots of the component(s) and any related drug product that could contain DEG or EG.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Institutional Review Board (IRB)

deficiencies.title: IRB failed to prepare, maintain, and follow written procedures governing the functions and operations of the IRB
deficiencies.description: The ACMH IRB failed to prepare, maintain, and follow written procedures describing the IRB's functions and operations that are specified in the regulations.
deficiencies.evidence: a. The ACMH IRB failed to follow, and to prepare and maintain adequate documentation of, written procedures for reporting its findings and actions regarding initial and continuing review to the investigator and the institution [21 CFR 56.108(a)(1); 21 CFR 56.115(a)(6)].
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a projected timeline for the transfer of the ACMH IRB's responsibilities to another registered IRB; any process regarding such a transfer; and any written procedures (or any revised written procedures in development) for the review of research until the ACMH IRB transfers its responsibilities to another registered IRB, including (1) procedures for the IRB's functions and operations, as described in FDA regulations, and (2) procedures for the preparation and maintenance of adequate documentation of IRB meeting minutes.

deficiencies.title: IRB failed to prepare and maintain adequate documentation of IRB activities, including minutes of IRB meetings
deficiencies.description: The ACMH IRB is required to prepare and maintain adequate documentation of IRB activities, including minutes of IRB meetings, which must be in sufficient detail to show attendance at the meetings; actions taken by the ACMH IRB; the votes on these actions, including the number of members voting for, against, and abstaining; the basis for requiring changes in or for disapproving research; and a written summary of the discussion of controverted issues and their resolution.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. On May 18, 2021, at a convened meeting, the ACMH IRB reviewed and discussed continuing review for five FDA-regulated studies; however, the meeting minutes do not document the members who attended this meeting.
b. On September 15, 2020, at a convened meeting, the ACMH IRB reviewed and discussed continuing review of an FDA-regulated humanitarian-use device study; however, the meeting minutes do not document the vote on actions taken by the ACMH IRB, including the number of members voting for, against, and abstaining.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a projected timeline for the transfer of the ACMH IRB's responsibilities to another registered IRB; any process regarding such a transfer; and any written procedures (or any revised written procedures in development) for the review of research until the ACMH IRB transfers its responsibilities to another registered IRB, including (1) procedures for the IRB's functions and operations, as described in FDA regulations, and (2) procedures for the preparation and maintenance of adequate documentation of IRB meeting minutes.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies related to the ACMH IRB's procedures or to the ACMH IRB's review and approval of protocols. It is your responsibility to ensure adherence to each requirement of the law and relevant FDA regulations.

metadata.company_name: Wuxi Medical Instrument Factory Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Insanitary Conditions
deficiencies.description: Your drug products were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: Numerous dead insects were inside the tubing used to transfer the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API), (b)(4), from storage drums to (b)(4) tanks, as well as a (b)(4) used for storing API transfer hoses and (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide comprehensive, independent risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Since the previous inspection, your firm discontinued sterilization via (b)(4) without implementing a new sterilization procedure. Furthermore, your firm lacked a procedure for conducting environmental monitoring during manufacturing operations and failed to ensure operators adhered to proper gowning requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide detailed remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct appropriate laboratory testing to determine whether each batch of drug product purporting to be sterile and pyrogen-free conforms to such requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: You manufactured and distributed an (b)(4) product labeled as 'sterile.' However, you failed to perform sterility testing. It is essential that you conduct sterility testing on these and other purportedly sterile drugs prior to batch disposition decisions.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide list of chemical and microbial test methods and specifications used to analyze each batch of your drug product before making a batch disposition decision, and the associated written procedures.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm relied solely on certificates of analysis (COAs) for incoming lots of (b)(4), instead of conducting identity testing and verification for purity, strength, and quality or qualifying your component supplier.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide chemical and microbiological quality control specifications you use to test and release each incoming lot of components for use in manufacturing.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: You assigned a (b)(4) expiration date to your sterile (b)(4) despite having only (b)(4) of stability data. Additionally, your firm's procedure did not address ongoing stability testing to monitor your drug’s integrity throughout its shelf life.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

conclusion: The

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: You failed to verify your device design to confirm that the design output meets the design input requirements, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(f).
deficiencies.evidence: EYE-SYNC Design Input Requirements, DIR-10101, Rev 007, includes a design requirement related to tracking performance accuracy that states, “...” However, your TR-10332 Eye Tracking Performance Test Result – ..., identifies that the average accuracy was ..., and the test report was approved on August 16, 2021, even though this requirement was not met.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Design Controls, Software Design, Development and Maintenance Procedure, Section 6.4

deficiencies.title: Failure to identify, document, validate or where appropriate verify, review, and approve of design changes before their implementation
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Design Controls, Software Design, Development and Maintenance Procedure, Section 6.7.iii, which requires that “Changes are identified, documented, validated or where appropriate verified, reviewed, and approved prior to the implementation of the change.”
deficiencies.evidence: ECO 24-0033 released EYE-SYNC tablet software version 1.2.9_25_1234 to correct an issue which allowed the device to continue collecting data in the event the headset is removed, potentially resulting in the incorrect color of the data quality indicator. However, associated software testing in TR-10139, Rev 34 did not verify that version 1.2.9_25_1234 corrected this issue.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Design Controls, Software Design, Development and Maintenance Procedure, Section 6.7

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish design inputs to ensure that the design requirements relating to a device are appropriate
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Design Controls, Software Design, Development and Maintenance Procedure, Section 6.2 Requirements, which explain that design inputs “…”
deficiencies.evidence: EYE-SYNC Design Input Requirements, DIR-10101, Rev 007, lists requirements that are not related to the current device.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Design Controls, Software Design, Development and Maintenance Procedure, Section 6.2

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate your device design
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Risk Management Procedure, SOP-10011, Rev 002, which states that the level of risk is determined by Risk Priority Numbers (RPN) and that the design will use the severity and occurrence rating scales described in the procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: Your software RA-10110, Rev 006, does not use RPNs, and the impact and probability scale used to calculate inherent and residual risk are not adequately defined within SOP-10011.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Risk Management Procedure, SOP-10011

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Supplier Management Procedure, SOP-10025, Rev 004.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPA 202110081 was opened on October 8, 2021, to address higher than expected assessment scores. The CAPA included a corrective action that stated, “To correct the core non-conformity, the raw data capture implementation of the eye tracker software must be updated to appropriately capture head and eye position data to accurately reflect scores within the typical range.”
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Supplier Management Procedure, SOP-10025

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for acceptance activities
deficiencies.description: You did not implement your Release for Shipment Procedure, SOP-10029, Rev 003.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, you confirmed the employee tasked with finished device inspection works and does not review test reports or perform a visual inspection as required by your procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement Release for Shipment Procedure, SOP-10029

conclusion: We reviewed your firm response dated February 27, 2025, and concluded that it is not adequate. We understand that you opened several CAPAs to identify necessary updates to your design control processes; however, your response does not provide sufficient detail to ensure that these failures will not recur and that your current devices meet all your design control requirements.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for finished device acceptance
deficiencies.description: You failed to implement section 6.13 of your “Sterilization Process Control” procedure (QA-0041; Rev 3; Effective: 1/27/23) which requires release of sterile products to inventory via Form 0070 once accompanying dosimetry records and certificates of processing (COPs) are received back from the contract sterilizer.
deficiencies.evidence: Subsequently, on 03/21/24, your Director of Quality Assurance released the above nonconforming lots of I-PACK injection kits, which are intended for administration of intravitreal injections during eye surgery, as sterile to your customer on 3/12/24 despite never shipping these products to the customer-approved contract sterilizer (or elsewhere) for sterilization.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide updates on the progress of these corrective actions and corrections, including any supporting evidence.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance
deficiencies.description: You failed to implement sections 5.1.2 and 5.2.1 of your “Process Validation” procedure (QA-0063; Effective: 11/8/23) which requires that Operations (with input from Quality) determine “*** if re-validation is needed for changes to validation manufacturing processes ***” and that Quality “*** Reviews and approves the assessments of validation needs and revalidation determinations ***,” respectively.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you authorized the release of lots [insert lot numbers] on [insert dates]; [insert lot numbers] on [insert dates]; and [insert lot numbers] on [insert dates], containing medical devices, such as punctum plugs, customized injection trays, microkeratome blades, microkeratome tubing, and ophthalmic convenience kits, for distribution without documented justification for why a revalidation was not required, prior to switching to a new contract sterilizer for [insert sterilization method] on 3/19/24.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that sampling plans are written and based on a valid statistical rationale when used
deficiencies.description: You acknowledged there was no statistical rationale documented for the sampling plan in section 6.1.2 of the “Seal Inspection” procedure (QA-0042; Effective: 6/30/24) to inspect the first [insert number] pouches for seal integrity.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you did not conduct a validation of the [insert inspection method] outlined in section 6.1.2 of the “Seal Inspection” procedure (QA-0042; Effective: 6/30/24) for testing the seal integrity of sterile pouches containing Microkeratome Blades, to ensure that manufacturing employees are able to repeatably and reproducibly able to detect conforming and nonconforming seal pouches with a high degree of assurance as a part of finished device acceptance.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies.

metadata.company_name: Choice All Natural, Inc. dba Om Botanical

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to maintain the buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of a drug product in a clean and sanitary condition and to keep them free of infestation by rodents, birds, insects, and other vermin.

Detailed procedures that demonstrate your firm can maintain buildings free from pests and that they remain in a clean and sanitary state.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations

deficiencies.required_action: Comprehensive assessment of laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to assure that the drug product bore an expiration date that was supported by appropriate stability testing.

deficiencies.required_action: Comprehensive assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. deficiencies.title: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you with an opportunity to address them.
deficiencies.description: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you with an opportunity to address them.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Lack of testing for batch release
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform appropriate testing on your over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release and distribution into the U.S. market.
deficiencies.evidence: Testing is essential to ensure that the drug products you manufacture conform to all predetermined quality attributes appropriate for their intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. Provide an action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: 2. Lack of component testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately test incoming components, including your active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), for identity before using the components to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that the components conform to appropriate specifications prior to use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. Provide chemical and microbiological quality control specifications you use to test and release each incoming batch of components for use in manufacturing.

deficiencies.title: 3. Lack of process validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: Process validation evaluates the soundness of design and state of control of a process throughout its lifecycle.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle. Provide a timeline for performing appropriate PPQ for each of your marketed drug products.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Unlicensed Biological Product Violations
deficiencies.description: Your products are unapproved new drugs in violation of section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. § 355(a). Your introduction or delivery for introduction of your product[s] into interstate commerce, or the causing thereof, is prohibited under sections 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. § 331(d).
deficiencies.evidence: Based on information and records reviewed by FDA prior to, during, and/or after the inspection, including information on your website, www.skyebiologics.com...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: During the inspection, FDA investigators documented evidence of significant violations from CGMP requirements, including violations from section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act and 21 CFR parts 210 and 211.
deficiencies.evidence: The CGMP violations include, but are not limited to, the following:...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not validated the manufacturing processes for your products with respect to identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm has not validated the manufacturing processes for your products with respect to identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have an adequate system for monitoring environmental conditions
deficiencies.description: Your written procedures for environmental monitoring of your firm’s aseptic processing areas are not sufficient to detect problems and ensure low bioburden levels prior to sterilization.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you do not perform nonviable particulate monitoring or viable microbial monitoring within your ISO 5 laminar flow hoods in association with all production shifts...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: You have not established appropriate specifications to assure that your products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you failed to perform endotoxin testing on final product batches...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy
deficiencies.description: You failed to investigate three action-level excursions for environmental monitoring observed in the aseptic processing area...
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your batch production records do not include documentation of the accomplishment of each significant step in the manufacture of your products or the person(s) performing each of those steps...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to prepare batch production and control records
deficiencies.description: Your batch production records do not include documentation of the accomplishment of each significant step in the manufacture of your products or the person(s) performing each of those steps...
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your batch production records do not include documentation of the accomplishment of each significant step in the manufacture of your products or the person(s) performing each of those steps...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow a written testing program
deficiencies.description: Your firm has assigned a five-year expiration date for your products without supporting data regarding the stability characteristics of the products...
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm has assigned a five-year expiration date for your products without supporting data regarding the stability characteristics of the products...
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required.

conclusion: We acknowledge receipt of your letter received on January 3, 2023, which provides a response to FDA’s inspectional observations (FDA-483). We have reviewed the represented corrective actions outlined in the response, and these actions are not adequate to address the above-noted violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: A. Adulterated Drugs
deficiencies.description: Failure of your quality unit to exercise its responsibility to ensure that APIs manufactured at your facility are in compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed that your firm lacked adequate quality unit (QU) oversight for the receipt of materials, repackaging, relabeling, and related controls for your APIs...
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed list containing API name, manufacturer name, manufacturer lot number, date of entry, import entry number, port of entry, order number, quantity, grade, usage, date of receipt, and information regarding the first date distributed, such as customer name and address, for all drugs received by your firm within three years from the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to Adequately Investigate Quality-Related Complaints and Recalls
deficiencies.description: Your QU failed to extend product quality complaint investigations to other lots of APIs potentially associated with the root cause, failure, or deviation.
deficiencies.evidence: You distributed API from this lot [b](4) times to [b](4) different customers... Your firm returned the remaining amount of lot [b](4) in Darmerica's inventory to the API manufacturer but did not contact your additional [b](4) customers or investigate further whether they had also received complaints about any potential contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an updated recall procedure that requires future client contact to be documented.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to Ensure Adequate Pre-Release Testing of APIs
deficiencies.description: Your firm lacked adequate QU oversight for the relabeling and repackaging of your API.
deficiencies.evidence: You released APIs for distribution prior to your QU completing its review... Though a sample was sent to your third-party testing laboratory for analysis, the [b](4) distribution of the [b](4) API occurred prior to your firm's own QU release of the [b](4) API on [b](4) and before the certificate of analysis (COA) was reviewed and authorized by your third-party lab on [b](4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed list containing API name, manufacturer name, manufacturer lot number, date of entry, import entry number, port of entry, order number, quantity, grade, usage, date of receipt, and information regarding the first date distributed, such as customer name and address, for all drugs received by your firm within three years from the date of this letter.

conclusion: In addition to the above-mentioned violations, Darmerica has distributed drugs that are misbranded within the meaning of section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(a), due to false or misleading labeling...

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established Design Control procedures for your firm's private label manufactured endoscopic medical devices for the U.S. market, including the Class II Rigid Nasopharyngoscope.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish a Design Control procedure according to 21 CFR 820.30 and provide a copy to the Agency for review

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for corrective and preventive action (CAPAs) and did not conduct any CAPAs.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish a system for the identification and input of quality data which may require corrective and/or preventive action, and provide details such as processes, work operations, quality records and other quality data to be analyzed

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain complaint files and to establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established customer complaint handling procedures to ensure it is receiving complaints from its sole U.S. distributor.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish a written agreement with its U.S. distributor requiring all complaints be provided for review and evaluation

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for control and distribution of finished devices
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for the control and distribution of its private label manufactured endoscopic medical devices for the U.S. market, including the Rigid Nasopharyngoscope.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain procedures for the control and distribution of finished devices intended for the U.S.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain device master records (DMR's)
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not maintained Device Master Records (DMR’s) for its private label manufactured endoscopic medical devices intended for the U.S. market, including the Rigid Nasopharyngoscope.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain DMR Control procedures as required by 21 CFR 820.181

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that device history records (DHR’s) are maintained
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established DHR procedures to ensure DHRs are maintained for each device batch, lot, or unit.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain a DHR to demonstrate its device is manufactured in accordance with the device DMR as required by 21 CFR 820.184

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain a quality system record (QSR)
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s Quality System does not include, or refer to the location of procedures and the documentation of activities required by the Quality System regulation.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain a Quality System and Quality Manual procedures as required by 21 CFR 820.186

deficiencies.title: Failure to have sufficient personnel with the necessary education, background, training, and experience
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not demonstrated it had any prior knowledge of U.S. FDA regulations and requirements including 21 CFR Part 820 - Quality System regulation.
deficiencies.required_action: Demonstrate adequate employee training for all personnel, related to its Quality System as required by 21 CFR 820.25

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because you did not provide supportive documentation for evaluation or adequate evidence of corrective actions taken to bring your operations into compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform appropriate testing on your drug products prior to release for distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: You rely on physical and organoleptic testing, such as appearance and odor, without performing adequate testing on the finished drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate written testing program
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct adequate stability studies on the OTC drug products, including full shelf-life studies evaluating the OTC drug products for the entire length of your established expiry dates.
deficiencies.evidence: No scientific data was provided to support that a 90-day accelerated study can justify a (b)(4) expiration for the (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate your manufacturing processes for your OTC drug products, including equipment qualification and cleaning validation studies.
deficiencies.evidence: You have not provided details on performing process validation studies for each OTC drug product or your equipment qualification plans.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: [list of required actions]
