deficiencies.title: Failure to review, evaluate, and investigate any complaint involving the possible failure of a device, labeling, or package to meet any of its specification
deficiencies.description: Complaints involving potential device failures were not investigated as required.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigator identified Complaint number 23.058, titled 'Osseointegration guarantee,' as lacking both an investigation and a reference to a prior investigation.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct or document the required technical assessment

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, your firm acknowledged the procedure Post Market Surveillance procedure A002 as its written MDR procedure, and agreed with the investigator that the procedure has the following deficiencies: a) The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements...
deficiencies.required_action: Revised MDR procedure and conduct staff training

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to FDA no later than 30 calendar days after the day that your firm received or otherwise became aware of information, from any source, that reasonably suggests that a device that your firm markets may have caused or contributed to a death or serious injury
deficiencies.description: Complaints describing events where patients experienced osseointegration failure after using your firm's K3Pro dental implants were not reported as required.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, your firm acknowledged that Complaints 23.058, 23.060, and 23.062 describe events where patients experienced osseointegration failure after using your firm's K3Pro dental implants...
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a serious injury MDR for each of these adverse events

deficiencies.title: Failure to prominently place the unique device identifier (UDI) on the label and device package
deficiencies.description: The labels of the K3Pro Kronus Dental implant devices do not bear a UDI.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, it was found that the labels for the K3Pro Kronus Dental implant devices do not include a device identifier within the meaning of 21 CFR 801.3 or 830.3...
deficiencies.required_action: Submit information required by 21 CFR Part 830, subpart E, to FDA's Global Unique Device Identification Database (GUDID)

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm's response dated August 21, 2024, cannot be determined at this time.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)
deficiencies.description: Your products are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have approved applications for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or approved applications for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g).
deficiencies.evidence: https://covidinstanttest.net and https://ushealthdiagnostics.com/ on March 30, 2021, and April 9, 2021
deficiencies.required_action: Cease the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)
deficiencies.description: Your products are misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the devices into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k).
deficiencies.evidence: https://covidinstanttest.net and https://ushealthdiagnostics.com/ on March 30, 2021, and April 9, 2021
deficiencies.required_action: Cease the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Misleading Representation
deficiencies.description: Your websites represent that the COVID-19 Test Kits are “FDA Submitted/EUA Approved,” “FDA EUA Authorized,” or “EUA/FDA Certified.” These representations create a false impression that your products have been approved or authorized for emergency use by FDA and are misleading.
deficiencies.evidence: https://covidinstanttest.net and https://ushealthdiagnostics.com/ on March 30, 2021, and April 9, 2021
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the misleading representations on your websites.

deficiencies.title: Unauthorized Use of FDA Logo
deficiencies.description: The FDA logo is for the official use of the FDA and not for use on private sector materials. Such use may send a misleading message that the FDA favors or endorses your products.
deficiencies.evidence: https://ushealthdiagnostics.com
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the unauthorized use of the FDA logo on your website.

conclusion: You should take immediate action to address the violations cited in this letter. This letter is not meant to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist in connection with your products or operations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple complaints related to the presence of mold, black specks, and discoloration on the product container and inside the product container.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive investigation into customer complaints related to mold and black speck samples deviations, including an evaluation of customer complaint samples from affected batches.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish and Follow Written Procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Air flow visualization studies (i.e., smoke studies) performed for your ISO 5 (Grade A) traditional aseptic filling line did not demonstrate unidirectional airflow.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide new air flow visualizations in the filling lines, and implement training and update procedures to improve aseptic practices.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Maintain Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate system for cleaning and disinfecting the room and equipment to produce aseptic conditions, and for maintaining equipment used to control the aseptic conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: The ISO 5 (Grade A) filling line is cleaned and sanitized using a method that may not be effective in preventing contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive remediation plan based on review and recommendations by a qualified third party with specific expertise in aseptic processes, including timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment.

conclusion: Your response is inadequate. You do not clearly specify how you will improve your air flow visualization, interventions in your ISO5 area, and how you intend to address potential deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your facility's drug products, such as Semaglutide Injection 1mg/0.2ml and Tirzepatide Injection 2.5mg/0.5ml, did not include the following on the label: 'Office Use Only.' 2. Your facility did not submit adverse event reports to FDA in accordance with the content and format requirements established through guidance or regulation under section 310.305 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. You did not perform adequate product evaluation and take appropriate corrective action after microbial contamination was recovered within the ISO 5 aseptic processing area. 2. Your firm failed to perform adequate smoke studies under dynamic conditions to demonstrate unidirectional airflow within the ISO 5 area.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: CGMP violations at your facility, that caused your drug products to be adulterated.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed (21 CFR 211.192). 2. Your firm failed to establish an adequate system for monitoring environmental conditions in aseptic processing areas (21 CFR 211.42(c)(10)(iv)).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.required_action: Obtain FDA approval

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You compound drug products that are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct labeling

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA.

metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate the aseptic process used to manufacture over (b)(4) units of PalinGen (b)(4) and PalinGen (b)(4) since your manufacturing operations began in March 2020.
deficiencies.evidence: Media fill simulations did not follow your commercial manufacturing process for the products. Critical manufacturing steps were not simulated during the validations.
deficiencies.required_action: Thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or the failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.description: Your finished product testing is limited to sterility testing and endotoxin testing as measurements of product attributes, and such testing does not demonstrate, for example, the identity and/or strength of your products.
deficiencies.evidence: Finished product testing only includes sterility testing and endotoxin testing
deficiencies.required_action: Establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or the failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Between July 2020 and February 2022, your firm failed to investigate 8 sterility failures.
deficiencies.evidence: You identified the contaminating organisms but did not conduct an investigation to determine the root cause of the failures or implement corrective and preventative actions.
deficiencies.required_action: Thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or the failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications

deficiencies.title: Failure to have an adequate system for monitoring environmental conditions in an aseptic processing area
deficiencies.description: Environmental monitoring samples within your ISO- (b)(4) are not positioned in critical areas that pose the most microbiological risk to your products during aseptic processing operations.
deficiencies.evidence: Passive (b)(4) were positioned on a (b)(4), within the (b)(4) portion of the ISO- (b)(4), during aseptic filling operations
deficiencies.required_action: Establish an adequate system for monitoring environmental conditions in an aseptic processing area

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate system for cleaning and disinfecting the room to produce aseptic conditions
deficiencies.description: Your cleaning validation is inadequate.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to validate the efficacy of the disinfectants used to clean and disinfect your cleanroom
deficiencies.required_action: Establish an adequate system for cleaning and disinfecting the room to produce aseptic conditions

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain equipment and utensils at appropriate intervals to prevent contamination that would alter the safety, identity, strength, quality or purity of the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm performed HEPA filter integrity testing (b)(4) on your ISO- (b)(4), despite the detection of a gross leak in the HEPA filter during (b)(4) testing.
deficiencies.evidence: You used ISO- (b)(4) to aseptically process your (b)(4) products which are purported to be sterile
deficiencies.required_action: Maintain equipment and utensils at appropriate intervals to prevent contamination that would alter the safety, identity, strength, quality or purity of the drug product

conclusion: Neither this letter nor the observations noted on the Form FDA-483, which were discussed with you at the conclusion of the inspection, are intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs & Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your compounded retatrutide drug products are unapproved new drugs and misbranded drugs introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a), 502(f)(1), 301(a) and 301(d) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no FDA-approved applications on file for your compounded retatrutide drug product. Under sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. §§ 355(a) and 331(d)] a new drug may not be introduced into or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce unless an application approved by FDA under section 505 of the FDCA is in effect for the drug.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Claims
deficiencies.description: Your claims concerning compounded semaglutide products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. §§ 352(a) and 352(bb)] resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. § 331(a)].
deficiencies.evidence: The following claims concerning compounded semaglutide products appear on your website: • 'The same active ingredient as Wegovy and Ozempic' • 'Semaglutide is the generic drug in Ozempic (injectable) and Rybelsus (oral) diabetes control medications, and Wegovy (injectable) weight loss medication.'
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

conclusion: For the reasons discussed above, your compounded retatrutide drug products are unapproved new drugs and misbranded drugs introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a), 502(f)(1), 301(a) and 301(d) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. §§ 355(a), 352(f)(1), 331(a) and 331(d)]. In addition, your claims concerning compounded semaglutide drug products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. §§ 352(a) and 352(bb)] resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA [21 U.S.C. § 331(a)].

deficiencies.title: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)
deficiencies.description: The COVxRDA Antigen Test Kits were offered for sale in the United States without marketing approval, clearance, or authorization from FDA.
deficiencies.evidence: https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/emergency-situations-medical-devices/coronavirus-covid-19-and-medical-devices
deficiencies.required_action: Take immediate corrective action to prevent the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)
deficiencies.description: The COVxRDA Antigen Test Kits are also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the devices into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k).
deficiencies.evidence: https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/emergency-situations-medical-devices/coronavirus-covid-19-and-medical-devices
deficiencies.required_action: Take immediate corrective action to prevent the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Prohibited Acts
deficiencies.description: The introduction or delivery for introduction of these products into interstate commerce is prohibited under section 301(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 331(a).
deficiencies.evidence: https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/emergency-situations-medical-devices/coronavirus-covid-19-and-medical-devices
deficiencies.required_action: Take immediate corrective action to prevent the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Prevent Future Violations
deficiencies.description: You should take immediate action to prevent future violations. This letter is not meant to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist in connection with your products or operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Within 48 hours, please send an email to COVID-19-Task-Force-CDRH@fda.hhs.gov describing the specific steps you have taken to prevent future violations.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you with an opportunity to address them. If you cannot take action to address this matter completely within 48 hours, state the reason for the delay and the time within which you will do so.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately test the over-the-counter (OTC) external analgesic drug products you manufacture under contract for identity, and strength of each active ingredient prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to ensure that incoming lots of active pharmaceutical ingredients (API) were suitable for use in manufacturing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct process validations for the OTC drug products you manufacture intended for the U.S. market.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Records and information provided by your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct testing on incoming components, including limit testing for high-risk drug components

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.evidence: Records and information provided by your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals

deficiencies.title: Failure to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Records and information provided by your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct testing on finished drug products prior to release and distribution

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Records and information provided by your firm
deficiencies.required_action: Establish a qualified consultant to evaluate operations and assist in meeting CGMP requirements

conclusion: Based on the nature of the violations we identified at your firm, you should engage a consultant qualified as set forth in 21 CFR 211.34 to evaluate your operations and to assist your firm in meeting CGMP requirements if your firm intends to resume manufacturing drugs for the U.S. market.

deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'The same active ingredient in branded weight loss medications.', 'FDA-Approved Medication', 'GENERIC'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from introducing or delivering for introduction into interstate commerce these misbranded products

conclusion: Please take immediate action to address any violations, including ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Misbranded/Failure to Register

deficiencies.title: Adulteration Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.evidence: See FDA's guidance document Quality Systems Approach to Pharmaceutical CGMP Regulations for help implementing quality systems and risk management approaches to meet the requirements of CGMP regulations 21 CFR, parts 210 and 211 at https://www.fda.gov/media/71023/download.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function

deficiencies.title: Establishment Registration and Drug Listing Violations
deficiencies.description: “Green Glo, NDC 17238-920,” “BioGlo, NDC 17238-900,” and “Vista Gonio Eye Lubricant, NDC 77790-022” are manufactured at (b)(4) and (b)(4), establishments that are not duly registered with FDA
deficiencies.evidence: Under section 510 of the FD&C Act, firms that manufacture, prepare, propagate, compound, or process drugs in the United States or that are offered for import into the United States must be registered with the FDA.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly

deficiencies.title: Use of Contract Manufacturers
deficiencies.description: You are responsible for the quality of your drugs regardless of agreements in place with your contract facilities
deficiencies.evidence: FDA regards contractors as extensions of the manufacturer.
deficiencies.required_action: Ensure that drugs are made in accordance with section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act to ensure safety, identity, strength, quality, and purity

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.company_name: Shantou S.E.Z. Baojie Industry Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide the chemical and microbiological quality control specifications you use to test and release each incoming lot of component for use in manufacturing. A summary of results obtained from testing all components to evaluate the reliability of the COA from each component manufacturer. Include your standard operating procedure that describes this COA validation program.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: You use (b)(4) as a component in your drug products and for cleaning production equipment. However, you have not established that the (b)(4) system is adequately designed, maintained, and monitored to ensure that it consistently produces (b)(4) suitable for its intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive remediation plan for the design, control, and maintenance of the (b)(4) system, including a robust monitoring program. A (b)(4) system validation report, along with a summary of any improvements made to the system design and the ongoing control and maintenance program.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products
deficiencies.description: You did not have adequate stability data to demonstrate that the quality attributes of your drug product remain acceptable throughout the labeled expiry period.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. Your remediated program should include, but not be limited to: stability indicating methods, stability studies for each drug product in its marketed container-closure system before distribution is permitted, an ongoing program in which representative batches of each product are added each year to the program to determine if the shelf-life claim remains valid.

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality control unit to exercise responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) did not provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: All procedures that describe these and other elements of your remediated stability program, as well as a commitment to ongoing stability studies.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Adulterated and Misbranded Products, including Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Adulterated and Misbranded Products
deficiencies.description: The AP 50/30 Insufflator, Insuflow Port, and Pneuview XE are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act. The products are also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the devices into commercial distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: The AP 50/30 Insufflator, Insuflow Port, and Pneuview XE are intended for use in the diagnosis of disease or other conditions or in the cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.required_action: Please submit data to support these statements. FDA informed your firm in written communications following the submission of 510(k) notifications for a different accessory device that certain claims required the submission of clinical data and that literature reviews provided were not adequate to support these indications.

deficiencies.title: Misleading Claims
deficiencies.description: The Pneuview XE is misbranded under § 502(a) of the Act because there are statements on your website and social media sites that constitute false or misleading representations with respect to another device.
deficiencies.evidence: The information makes misleading comparisons between your device, a one-way insufflator, and competing devices, two-way insufflators. For example, relevant pictures, illustrations, videos, literature, and written/verbal statements include the following: ...
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide data demonstrating that Pneuview XE is more effective than other insufflation devices.

conclusion: The introduction or delivery for introduction of the product into interstate commerce is prohibited under section 301(a) of the Act. In addition, it is a prohibited act under section 301(k) of the Act to do any act with respect to a device while the device is held for sale after shipment in interstate commerce and results in the device being misbranded.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations

deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: 1. Laboratory records
deficiencies.description: Failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.
deficiencies.evidence: Your laboratory records did not include complete data to support the analysis performed. For example, Neogen Dermalogy Day-Light Protection Airy Sunscreen product batch NJ09 were tested several times (i.e., re-test) for assay during batch release and stability study from September 2022 to March 2023. The out of specification (OOS) results obtained were not recorded in the batch release documents and not investigated by the quality control laboratory at the time.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Computer or related systems
deficiencies.description: Failed to exercise appropriate controls over computer or related systems to assure that only authorized personnel institute changes in master production and control records, or other records.
deficiencies.evidence: Your laboratory equipment used to generate analytical data for finished drug product release lacked restricted access and sufficient controls. There is no assurance that your systems have the appropriate controls to prevent data deletion and record modifications. Specifically, electronic raw data used for release testing of batches (e.g., (b)(4)) currently in the U.S. market was deleted and not accessible.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of computer system performance and security. Provide a report that identifies vulnerabilities in the design and controls, and a thorough CAPA plan for each of your laboratory computer systems...

deficiencies.title: 3. Quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Failed to establish a quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU did not adequately exercise its authority and responsibilities, including but not limited to, establishing effective procedures and oversight. There is lack of adequate procedure for out-of-specification (OOS) investigation of the over the counter (OTC) drug product Neogen Dermalogy Day-Light Protection Airy Sunscreen.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function...

deficiencies.title: 4. Written procedures
deficiencies.description: Failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm’s process validation and equipment qualification programs were not adequately implemented. There is no evidence of a comprehensive review of all laboratory instruments.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Nikkiso Medical America, Inc. is marketing the Nikkiso DBB-06 (DBB-06) in the United States without the necessary marketing clearance or approval
deficiencies.description: The DBB-06 is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g), for the device as described and marketed.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: • In the online version of your firm’s product brochure found at http://nikkisoamerica.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/08/NIKKISO-DBB06-RV09262016-V3.pdf, your firm claims that the DBB-06 Haemo-Master Blood Volume Monitor (BVM) uses “adaptive feedback” to regulate the “blood volume trend.” • The website also includes the following statement: “The unique adaptive feedback feature of the BVM allows for automatic changes of fluid removal to achieve desired outcomes.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a justification for inclusion of this feature with supportive performance data for its safe and effective use.

deficiencies.title: Nikkiso Medical America, Inc. is introducing or delivering for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution the DBB-06 device with a significant change or modification
deficiencies.description: The DBB-06 is also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution this device with a significant change or modification, specifically the introduction of a physiological closed loop controller (PCLC) system (also referred to as PCLC function or functionality in this letter) which is a change or modification in the device that could significantly affect the safety or effectiveness of the device, without submitting a new premarket notification to FDA as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k), and 21 CFR 807.81(a)(3).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: • In the online version of your firm’s product brochure found at http://nikkisoamerica.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/08/NIKKISO-DBB06-RV09262016-V3.pdf, your firm claims that the DBB-06 Haemo-Master Blood Volume Monitor (BVM) uses “adaptive feedback” to regulate the “blood volume trend.” • The website also includes the following statement: “The unique adaptive feedback feature of the BVM allows for automatic changes of fluid removal to achieve desired outcomes.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a justification for inclusion of this feature with supportive performance data for its safe and effective use.

conclusion: Based on the information reviewed by FDA, Nikkiso’s marketing of the DBB-06 device with the PCLC function, which has not been cleared or approved, has resulted in the misbranding and adulteration of the device.

metadata.subject: CGMP/Blood & Blood Components/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure of the responsible physician to approve reinstatement of the donor into the plasmapheresis program when the donor's protein composition values have returned to an acceptable level
deficiencies.description: There is no documentation that the responsible physician approved the reinstatement of this donor into the plasmapheresis program as required.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, a serum protein electrophoresis test for a sample collected on March 22, 2024, from donor (b)(6) showed results with a total protein less than 6.0 grams per deciliter.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the violation by ensuring that the responsible physician approves reinstatement of donors into the plasmapheresis program when their protein composition values have returned to an acceptable level.

deficiencies.title: Failure of personnel responsible for the collection, processing, compatibility testing, storage or distribution of blood or blood components to have adequate training and experience
deficiencies.description: Nine employees approved as physician substitutes by your Medical Director failed to complete the (b)(4) minimum training program required by your Standard Operating Procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: Since September 27, 2022, nine of (b)(4) employees approved as physician substitutes by your Medical Director failed to complete the (b)(4) minimum training program required by your Standard Operating Procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the violation by ensuring that personnel responsible for the collection, processing, compatibility testing, storage or distribution of blood or blood components have adequate training and experience.

deficiencies.title: Failure to follow written standard operating procedures for all steps in the collection, processing, storage, and distribution of blood and blood components
deficiencies.description: Freshly collected units of Source Plasma were placed immediately inside cardboard boxes in freezer (b)(4), which deviated from your Standard Operating Procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: On April 15, 2024, FDA investigators observed freshly collected units of Source Plasma being placed immediately inside cardboard boxes in freezer (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the violation by ensuring that all steps in the collection, processing, storage, and distribution of blood and blood components are followed according to written standard operating procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to make and record a thorough investigation, including the conclusions and follow-up, of any unexplained discrepancy or the failure of a lot or unit to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Twenty Internal Investigation Reports (IIRs) reviewed during the inspection were not completed in accordance with Quality Assurance Procedures Manual.
deficiencies.evidence: Out of 26 IIRs reviewed during the inspection, 20 were not completed in accordance with Quality Assurance Procedures Manual.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the violation by ensuring that all investigations are thorough and recorded according to written standard operating procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain records concurrently with the performance of each significant step in the collection, processing, compatibility testing, storage and distribution of each unit of blood and blood components
deficiencies.description: Donor Adverse Event (DAE) Reports were documented with notes and signatures from your firm's responsible physician and were dated at times when the responsible physician was not at your facility.
deficiencies.evidence: The following DAE Reports were documented with notes and signatures from your firm's responsible physician and were dated at times when the responsible physician was not at your facility: i. On March 9, 2024, the responsible physician signed DAE # 03-24-002 and applied a temporary deferral due to a Hematoma/Bruise (Complicated).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the violation by ensuring that all records are maintained concurrently with the performance of each significant step in the collection, processing, compatibility testing, storage and distribution of each unit of blood and blood components.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to establish an adequate quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection of your facility our Investigator observed that your 2021 Tablets “PROCESS VALIDATION” report had data added, backdated signatures, and replaced pages. Your Quality Assurance Manager, Quality Control Manager, Formulation Research & Development Manager, and Production Manager admitted to participating in these practices.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to exercise appropriate controls over computer or related systems
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to exercise appropriate controls over computer or related systems to assure that only authorized personnel institute changes in master production and control records, or other records.
deficiencies.evidence: Your ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectrophotometer computer system lacked appropriate controls to assure the integrity of electronic test data, such as an audit trail and defined user access levels. This UV-Vis system was used for release testing of drug product batches.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan for computer system security and integrity.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish the accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility of its test methods
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish the accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility of its test methods.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not use a United States Pharmacopeia (USP) method to test multiple drug products nor did you show equivalency or superiority for your alternate methods. For example, your E. coli test method for Solution and Tablets drug products only required sub-culturing if turbidity was observed.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies.

metadata.company_name: Louisiana State University Health Science Center IRB
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Institutional Review Board (IRB)

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conduct Continuing Review
deficiencies.description: The IRB failed to conduct continuing review of research at intervals of not less than once per year for the following FDA-regulated studies: ... (list of specific studies)
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, Investigator Amegadje presented and discussed with you the Form FDA 483, Inspectional Observations. We acknowledge receipt of the IRB's August 16, 2023, written response to the Form FDA 483.
deficiencies.required_action: The IRB must conduct continuing review of research within the required interval, as required under 21 CFR part 56.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Prepare and Maintain Written Procedures
deficiencies.description: The IRB failed to prepare, maintain, and follow required written procedures for ensuring prompt reporting to the IRB, appropriate institutional officials, and FDA of any suspension or termination of IRB approval.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, Investigator Amegadje presented and discussed with you the Form FDA 483, Inspectional Observations. We acknowledge receipt of the IRB's August 16, 2023, written response to the Form FDA 483.
deficiencies.required_action: The IRB must prepare and maintain adequate documentation of IRB activities, including written procedures for ensuring prompt reporting to the IRB, appropriate institutional officials, and FDA of any suspension or termination of IRB approval.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our findings and provides you with an opportunity to address the deficiencies described above. Within 15 business days of your receipt of this letter, you should notify this office in writing of the actions that the IRB has taken or plans to take, to address any violations and to prevent the recurrence of similar violations in the future.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Lack of Process Validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate your manufacturing processes to demonstrate that your processes are reproducible and controlled to consistently yield drug products of uniform character and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's guidance for industry, Process Validation: General Principles and Practices
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Lack of Cleaning Validation
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform appropriate cleaning validation studies for non-dedicated equipment used to manufacture your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's guidance for industry, Microbiological Quality Considerations in Non-Sterile Drug Manufacturing
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed program for designing, validating, maintaining, controlling, and monitoring each of your manufacturing processes that includes vigilant monitoring of intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure an ongoing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Lack of Stability Program
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have adequate stability data to demonstrate that the chemical and microbiological properties of your drug products met established specifications and remain acceptable throughout their labeled shelf-life.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's guidance for industry, Stability Testing: A Review of the Current State of Knowledge
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: Lack of Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: You lacked appropriate sampling and testing to ensure the suitability of water used as a component in your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's guidance for industry, Microbiological Quality Considerations in Non-Sterile Drug Manufacturing
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA's guidance for industry, Quality Control Unit Responsibilities
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your quality control unit.

conclusion: In addition to the above-mentioned deficiencies, we have not received a response from your firm stating the actions you are taking to address the deficiencies identified during the inspection and cited on our Form FDA 483.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not adequately test incoming raw materials, including identity testing of each shipment of each component lot (e.g., ethanol, glycerin, propylene glycol) used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products. You stated that you were aware of the legal requirement for testing components, but accepted the at-risk components based on their suppliers’ certificate of analysis (COA).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier’s test analyses at appropriate intervals
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier’s test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.evidence: You relied on your suppliers’ COA, and you have not established the reliability of your component suppliers’ test analyses at appropriate intervals. In addition, the supplier’s COA for ethanol did not include test results for methanol.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to always conduct at least one specific identity test for each incoming component lot. In the case of glycerin, propylene glycol, and certain additional high-risk components we note that this includes the performance of parts A, B, and C of the USP monograph.

deficiencies.title: Failure to assure that the drug product bore an expiration date that was supported by appropriate stability testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to assure that the drug product bore an expiration date that was supported by appropriate stability testing.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform any stability studies for your OTC hand sanitizer and antibacterial hand soap drug products to support their assigned (b)(4) expiry period. Also, you did not have an approved stability program procedure at the time of inspection.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan for continuing stability studies for your drug products through expiry, including detailed timelines. This plan should also include an assessment of the stability of drug product currently on the U.S. market.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not have assurance that you are capable of consistently manufacturing your drug product with defined quality attributes. Your firm lacked a process validation program and data to support fundamental process parameters such as mixing speed, time, and temperature.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to completing process validation studies and equipment qualification activities.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: [list of required actions]

deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims observed on your website and from the labeling, including the outer packaging, that provide evidence of the intended use of these products as drugs include, but may not be limited to the following:

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your “Hard Steel” and “Gold Hard Steel Plus” products are misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(a), because their labeling is false or misleading in any particular.
deficiencies.evidence: The labeling for your “Hard Steel” and “Gold Hard Steel Plus” products do not declare that the products contain a PDE-5 inhibitor or acetaminophen.
deficiencies.required_action: Introduction or delivery of these misbranded drug products is a prohibited act under section 301(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 331(a).

deficiencies.title: Failure to Disclose Ingredients
deficiencies.description: The labeling for your “Hard Steel” and “Gold Hard Steel Plus” products lacks adequate warnings for the protection of users.
deficiencies.evidence: There is potential for serious health risks associated with these products, particularly since anyone who takes your “Hard Steel” and “Gold Hard Steel Plus” products would be unaware of the presence of the undeclared drug ingredient(s) and placed at risk for its associated adverse events.
deficiencies.required_action: Introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of these misbranded drug products is a prohibited act under section 301(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 331(a).

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you an opportunity to address them.

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm did not perform identity testing for the following raw materials: (b)(4), which are used in the production of (b)(4) batch (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified, and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment
deficiencies.description: You lacked the cleaning validation studies required by your written procedures for OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate removal of residues from manufacturing equipment during cleaning can lead to the contamination of drug products subsequently manufactured on nondedicated equipment.
deficiencies.required_action: An interim protocol for your cleaning verification with acceptance criteria, sampling methods, locations, and other considerations relevant to your equipment cleaning program.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct process validations for all your over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, including but not limited to your (b)(4) products.
deficiencies.evidence: Process validation evaluates the soundness of design and the state of control of a process throughout its lifecycle.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit
deficiencies.description: You lacked adequate quality unit (QU) oversight for the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: For example: You have not performed an annual product review (APR) of your OTC drug products manufactured since 2022.
deficiencies.required_action: A procedure governing your program for ongoing control, maintenance, and monitoring that ensures the remediated system consistently produces (b)(4) that meets (b)(4) monograph specifications and appropriate microbial limits.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: [list of required actions]

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to conduct quality control testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct quality control testing for your over-the-counter (OTC) drug products, such as [b](4), and [b](4) Ointment prior to release and distribution to the U.S. market.
deficiencies.evidence: You state that you are in the process of implementing a quality management system (QMS) with robust procedures for establishing finished products specifications for all new and existing medicinal products. You commit to testing the last two batches of products distributed to the U.S. market to “confirm the proper quality of medicinal product from microbiological point of view.”
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to test incoming raw materials
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test incoming raw materials, including active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), to determine their identity, purity, strength, and other appropriate quality attributes.
deficiencies.evidence: You state that you identified a GMP laboratory with valid European Union issued GMP certificates for testing incoming materials. Your response is inadequate. You do not provide documentation that the laboratory is GMP compliant.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish adequate written procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: You state that you are in the process of establishing your QMS and you will create procedures related to process validation. Your response is inadequate. You fail to provide sufficient details of your corrective actions.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

metadata.company_name: Empower Clinic Services, LLC dba Empower Pharmacy
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: A. Compounded Drug Products Under the FDCA
deficiencies.description: Serious deficiencies in your practices for producing drug products, which put patients at risk.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigators noted that drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health,
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions are required to address these deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: B. Violations of the FDCA
deficiencies.description: Adulterated drug products due to insanitary conditions, lack of adequate routine environmental monitoring, and failure to appropriately sterilize equipment located in the ISO 5 area.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Your firm failed to perform adequate routine environmental monitoring. Specifically, your cleanroom certification consists of nonviable airborne particulate and viable airborne particulate sampling of locations surrounding your (b)(4) filling line, but does not include sampling within the critical area where aseptic processing occurs.
2. Your media fills were not performed under the most challenging or stressful conditions. Therefore, there is a lack of assurance that your firm can aseptically produce drug products within your facility.
3. Your firm failed to appropriately sterilize equipment located in the ISO 5 area. Specifically, your firm did not sterilize the stopper sorting bowl, supply hopper, and insertion station that come into contact with stoppers used in the production of injectable drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions are required to address these violations.

deficiencies.title: C. Corrective Actions
deficiencies.description: Some corrective actions appear adequate, but others require further information or supporting documentation.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Regarding your certification of your ISO-5 filling room (Room 1267), your response makes reference to sampling in accordance with ISO standard 14644-1:2015. However, we note that the nonviable particulate sampling conducted during your April 2024 and October 2024 certifications did not conform to this standard in that the sampling locations did not appear to be divided across the whole cleanroom and did not appear to be of equal area.
2. Regarding your media fills, we acknowledge your commitments to expand your media fill batch sizes and strengthen your personnel qualification as well as revise your existing procedures. However, it is unclear when you plan to conduct media fills within your ISO 5 filling machine in Room 1267 as you did not provide completed media fills according to the recent changes outlined in Protocol – B&S Process Verification Media Fill.
3. We reviewed your (b)(4) validation for sterilizing the stopper sorting bowl, supply hopper, and insertion station and acknowledge your process improvements. In your response you stated that, “Empower has also implemented a (b)(4) schedule for the stopper sorting bowl and associated components to improve sterility assurance while awaiting additional spare parts.” It is unclear how you determined the adequacy of “a (b)(4) schedule” to ensure the equipment remains sterile.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions are required to address these deficiencies.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Unlicensed Biological Product Violations
deficiencies.description: Your product is an unapproved new drug in violation of section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C § 355(a). Your product is also an unlicensed biological product in violation of section 351(a)(1) of the Public Health Service Act (PHS Act), 42 U.S.C. § 262(a)(1).
deficiencies.evidence: Your website, www.mvtissues.com, last visited December 2025
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a biologics license application (BLA)

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: FDA's inspection of your facility documented evidence of significant CGMP violations.
deficiencies.evidence: Form FDA-483, List of Inspectional Observations (Form FDA-483)
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the CGMP violations and validate the manufacturing process for your product

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet Minimal Manipulation Criterion
deficiencies.description: Your processing of hypodermis tissue alters the original relevant characteristics of the tissue related to its utility for reconstruction, repair, or replacement.
deficiencies.evidence: Processing of hypodermal tissue using (b)(4) and (b)(4)
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the minimal manipulation criterion violation

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet Homologous Use Criterion
deficiencies.description: Your product is not intended to perform the same basic function or functions in the recipient as in the donor.
deficiencies.evidence: Labeling, advertising, and other indications of your firm's objective intent
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the homologous use criterion violation

conclusion: You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Manufacture/Packing/Storage/Installation/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish your “Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA)” procedure (SOP-019 Rev. B, Effective Date: 10/30/2024), as your firm did not adequately conduct and document CAPA investigations.
deficiencies.evidence: 2 out of 17 CAPA records selected for review by our investigators did not identify the root causes and necessary corrective and preventive actions as required by your CAPA procedure. For example, CAPA 24-007 dated 9/11/2024: Your firm failed to conduct adequate investigations as required by your CAPA procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that your firm initiated CAPA 24-028. You indicate that your firm made immediate corrections to CAPA 24-007; plans to contract with a third-party to retrospectively review all open and closed CAPAs, including the CAPAs cited during the last inspection; revised the CAPA process; and completed relevant training on the revised CAPA procedure including revised work instructions. However, the CAPA activities are still ongoing, and your responses do not clearly specify the target completion date for the overall CAPA 24-028 record.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your complaint procedure, “Complaint Management,” (SOP-025, Rev. G, Effective Date: 10/28/2024) has not been adequately established as your firm did not adequately conduct and/or document complaint investigations.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, Complaint #00003531 described a failure to acquire live video feed upon attaching the scope to the instrument driving mechanism (IDM). Your firm did not adequately investigate the cause(s) of camera image loss. Additionally, your firm failed to conduct and/or document complaint investigations for other complaints such as #00002229, #00003614, and #00003610.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that your firm initiated CAPA 24-025, CAPA 24-026, and CAPA 24-015. Your responses indicate that your firm has made some progress including completion of the root cause investigation for CAPA 24-015. However, the CAPA activities are still ongoing for all three CAPA records, and your responses do not clearly specify the target completion dates for each overall CAPA record.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for design control
deficiencies.description: Your design control procedure, “Design Controls” (SOP-002, Rev. G, Effective Date: 10/21/2024), has not been adequately established.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your firm failed to adequately conduct design validation for the biopsy tools, such as listed on page 23 of IFU Rev J, Effective Date: 09/25/2024. Additionally, your firm only tested biopsy tool passage in a configuration of the bronchoscope instead of configuration to account for bending of the bronchoscope.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that your firm initiated CAPA 24-016 and CAPA 24-018. You indicate “as part of the Corrective Action, all instances of Design Verification and Validation of Requirements are being reviewed and where assumptions were made, these are being tested against documented evidence” (your firm’s response dated January 21, 2025). The CAPA activities are still ongoing for all three CAPA records, and your responses do not clearly specify the target completion dates for each overall CAPA record.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your procedure, “Nonconforming Material,” (SOP-018 Rev. G, Effective Date: 09/24/2024,), has not been adequately implemented.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, NCR 0367 (Dated 04/11/2024) involves bronchoscopes failing leak test due to in the working channels (part ). However, your firm did not conduct investigations to identify the root cause(s) as required by your nonconforming material procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that your firm initiated CAPA 24-007. You indicate that your firm made immediate corrections to CAPA 24-007; plans to contract with a third-party to retrospectively review all open and closed CAPAs, including the CAPAs cited during the last inspection; revised the CAPA process; and completed relevant training on the revised CAPA procedure including revised work instructions. However, the CAPA activities are still ongoing, and your responses do not clearly specify the target completion date for the overall CAPA 24-028 record.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Unapproved New Drug

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: FIRST Unit-of-Use Kits, including FIRST Lansoprazole, FIRST Pantoprazole, FIRST Metronidazole, and FIRST Mouthwash BLM are drugs as defined by section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(g)(1), because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease and/or intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: The labeling published on your website http://www.azurity.com for the above-listed FIRST Unit-of-Use Kits provides evidence of the intended uses of these products as drugs.
deficiencies.required_action: No FDA-approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed (21 CFR 211.192).
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU did not investigate numerous complaints involving crystallization of two Injection mg/mL batches.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and failures.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products and to use results of stability testing to determine appropriate storage conditions and expiration dates (21 CFR 211.166(a)).
deficiencies.evidence: Stability testing of process performance qualification batches and annual stability batches of this drug product resulted in numerous OOS individual unknown impurity results during related substances testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide the following: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and failures. An independent assessment of your CAPA program. An independent, retrospective review of all complaints and associated investigations for your drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B

deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, FDA investigators noted that drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed: 1. You did not perform adequate product evaluation and take appropriate corrective action after microbial contamination was recovered within the ISO 5 aseptic processing area. 2. Your firm failed to perform adequate smoke studies under dynamic conditions to demonstrate unidirectional airflow within the ISO 5 area.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed: 1. Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes. 2. Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You compound drug products that are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners; therefore, adequate directions for use cannot be written so that a layman can use these products safely for their intended uses.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed: You compound drug products that are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners; therefore, adequate directions for use cannot be written so that a layman can use these products safely for their intended uses.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action is required

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA.
