metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: Failure to test a specimen from an anonymous or directed reproductive donor of cells or tissue, whether viable or non-viable, for evidence of infection due to relevant communicable disease agents
deficiencies.description: This includes testing for West Nile Virus (WNV) using an FDA-licensed Nucleic Acid Test (NAT) donor screening test for HCT/Ps recovered between June 1st and October 31st every year.
deficiencies.evidence: oocytes were recovered from anonymous donor OD, on , without being tested for WNV NAT. This donor was determined “eligible” on September 7, 2018.

deficiencies.title: Failure to screen a donor of reproductive cells or tissue by reviewing the donor’s relevant medical records for risk factors for and clinical evidence of relevant communicable disease agents and diseases
deficiencies.description: Your Green Valley Fertility Partners Infectious Disease Questionnaire is used as a relevant medical record to determine donor eligibility. However, the questionnaire does not include screening for all relevant conditions and/or behaviors that increase a donor’s communicable disease risk.
deficiencies.evidence: the form is missing questions, timeframes, and/or deferral periods to assess a donor’s relevant communicable disease risk for the following: ...

deficiencies.title: Failure to determine as ineligible a donor who is identified as having a risk factor for, or clinical evidence of, any of the relevant communicable disease agents or diseases
deficiencies.description: For example, directed oocyte donor answered “yes” to Question #19 on the Green Valley Fertility Partners Infectious Disease Questionnaire, “Have you lived cumulatively for 5 years or more in Europe from 1980 until the present (note this criterion includes time spent in the U.K. from 1980 through 1996)?” Donor was determined “eligible” on October 10, 2022, and August 8, 2022.

deficiencies.title: Failure to determine whether a donor is eligible based upon the results of donor screening in accordance with 21 CFR 1271.75 and donor testing in accordance with 21 CFR 1271.80 and 21 CFR 1271.85
deficiencies.evidence: Directed oocyte donor was determined “eligible” on January 30, 2024. However, the physical examination was not completed until , and the results of donor testing were not received until January 31, 2024.

deficiencies.title: Failure to omit the donor’s name and other personal information on the accompanying records
deficiencies.evidence: the names and birthdates of anonymous oocyte donors are included on the Summary of Records, which is considered an accompanying record as described in 21 CFR 1271.55(a).

deficiencies.title: Failure to affix a distinct identification code to the HCT/P container
deficiencies.evidence: your firm identifies anonymous oocyte donors with “OD” followed by their first name on the Summary of Records, which is considered an accompanying record as described in 21 CFR 1271.55(a).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Without adequate testing and establishing the reliability of your component suppliers' test analyses at appropriate intervals, you do not have scientific evidence that your raw materials conform to appropriate specifications prior to use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: As a manufacturer, you have a responsibility to sample, test, and examine drug components before use in production to assure adequate quality, including testing for the presence of methanol in ethanol.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, the SOPURE ANTISEPTIC SKIN CLEANSER hand sanitizer batch records that you provided do not include an appropriate assay test for active ingredient content or adequate impurity testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including strength and identity testing of the active ingredient, must be performed before drug product release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have adequate scientific evidence to assure that your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products and to use results of stability testing to determine appropriate storage conditions and expiration dates.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you provided long-term stability studies and reported that the expiry period assigned and applied to your hand sanitizer product is [b](4). However, your stability data does not include adequate testing for identity and strength.
deficiencies.required_action: Without appropriate stability studies, you do not have scientific evidence to support whether your drug products meet established specifications and retain their quality attributes through their labeled expiry.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The records and information you provided demonstrate that your quality unit (QU) did not effectively exercise its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Your QU is responsible for fully exercising its authority and responsibilities. FDA is concerned that your QU may not be conducting appropriate oversight regarding CGMP operations.

conclusion: Based on the records and information you provided, significant violations were observed including, but not limited to, the following: ...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment.
deficiencies.required_action: Confirmation of whether you will discontinue manufacturing drug products on shared equipment in your facility and implement appropriate controls to prevent cross-contamination. If you intend to continue to manufacture both pharmaceutical and non-pharmaceutical products at your facility, provide a plan to show how you will maintain appropriate separation with dedicated manufacturing equipment for your pharmaceutical manufacturing and industrial product manufacturing operations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct adequate finished product release testing for each batch of your hand sanitizer drug products, including but not limited to, testing of the identity and strength of each active ingredient, testing for impurities, and testing for objectionable microorganisms.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate identity testing on each shipment of each lot of incoming components (e.g., benzalkonium chloride, ethanol, glycerin) used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. If you intend to accept any results from your supplier’s Certificates of Analysis (COA) instead of testing each component lot for strength, quality, and purity, specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier’s results through initial validation as well as periodic re-validation.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including semaglutide and tirzepatide. Your claims concerning these products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'Clinically proven success', 'Clinically proven weight loss treatments', 'The same active ingredients as Ozempic or Mounjaro', 'Prescription weight loss with the same active ingredients as Mounjaro & Zepbound.'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from using language that misbrands the product. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide products are misbranded drugs under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the FDCA, introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'Clinically proven success', 'Clinically proven weight loss treatments', 'The same active ingredients as Ozempic or Mounjaro', 'Prescription weight loss with the same active ingredients as Mounjaro & Zepbound.'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from using language that misbrands the product. Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including seizure and injunction.

conclusion: Please provide a written response within 15 working days describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Lack of Testing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform testing for identity and strength of the active ingredient prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including strength and identity testing of the active ingredient, must be performed before drug product release and distribution.

deficiencies.title: 2. Lack of Expiration Date
deficiencies.description: You failed to establish and maintain an adequate stability testing program to support the labeled expiry on your distributed drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: There is no assurance that your drug products will remain acceptable throughout the assigned 5-year expiry period without an ongoing stability program.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and maintain an adequate stability testing program to support the labeled expiry on your distributed drug products.

deficiencies.title: 3. Lack of Quality Control
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: You lacked a quality unit (QU) with appropriate oversight for the manufacture of your over-the-counter (OTC) topical analgesic drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: Engage a consultant qualified as set forth in 21 CFR 211.34 to evaluate your operations and assist your firm in meeting CGMP requirements.

conclusion: Based on the nature of the violations we identified at your firm, you should engage a consultant qualified as set forth in 21 CFR 211.34 to evaluate your operations and assist your firm in meeting CGMP requirements.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written production and control procedures for the charge-in of components designed to assure that the drug products produced have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of all batches of any drug product distributed in the United States by your firm, including lot numbers and expiry dates, and a full reconciliation of all material you distributed, including a list of customers and location for where the product was distributed.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm also failed to prepare batch production and control records with complete information relating to the production and control of each batch of drug product produced.
deficiencies.evidence: Your batch production records for your drug products do not include adequate production instructions, including but not limited to, identification of critical steps in your manufacturing processes such as critical parameters (e.g., times) or the weight or volume of each formulation component.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide your master production and control records for your drug products, to demonstrate that they fully document each significant and validated manufacturing step.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to conduct an adequate identity test on each shipment of each lot of components (including the ethanol active ingredient), used in the production of your drug products. You also failed to determine whether each component conformed with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality before use.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not adequately implemented your Complaint Handling, Medical Device Reporting and Vigilance Reporting, Document Number 000978, Revision 26, Dated 20-May-2024, to ensure complaints are processed in a uniform and timely manner.
deficiencies.evidence: On November 25, 2024, your firm received Complaint CR-03201 documenting explantation of an Esteem II System due to patient infection. The physical device was received on December 5, 2024, and the incident was reported as a serious injury Medical Device Report (MDR) on December 18, 2024.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm's response includes documentation to demonstrate closure of CR-03201 and CR-03165, but only a progress report for CR-03042 was provided rather than the complete updated complaint record.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's CAPA procedures, Corrective and Preventive Actions, Document Number 000422, Revision 17, Dated September 20, 2024; Quality Event Risk Assessment & CAPA Escalation, Document Number 006411, Revision 03, Dated May 15, 2024; and CAPA Work Instruction, Document Number 006342, Revision 08, Dated September 20, 2024, have not been adequately implemented concerning two critical CAPAs involving high particulate counts in your firm's ISO Class 8 Cleanrooms.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm opened CAPA-00684 on March 2, 2022, following the discovery of high particulate counts during cleanroom monitoring conducted on February 16, 2022. The monitoring, performed by a third-party service identified particulate counts of at location in your large cleanroom, which exceeded the ISO Class 8 requirement of below 3,520,000 particulates/m³.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response includes CAPA CA-00712 that addresses the immediate product impact issues both CA-00684 and CA-00708 failed to evaluate. However, CAPA CA-00712 does not provide for an investigation to correct the underlying CAPA system deficiencies that allowed a fundamental failure in risk assessment to occur.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain design validation procedures
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm's implementation of your primary risk assessment procedure 'Risk Management Process', Document Number 001000, Revision 11, dated April 17, 2025, did not ensure routine updating of hazard evaluation documentation and the consistent assessment of infection-related risks.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPA CA-00701 identified that of used in assembly processes may have incomplete due to excursions in your firm's large ISO Class 8 cleanroom. However, the risk documentation for the Esteem II Sound Processors which includes Esteem II System HA, Document Number 003682, Revision 12, dated April 20, 2017, and Esteem II FMECA, Document Number 006411, Revision 12, dated April 20, 2017, do not include the hazard identified from CA-00701, to evaluate the risk and its potential effects on the Esteem II System.
deficiencies.required_action: Your response includes CAPA CA-00716 and identified training deficiencies and procedural gaps as root causes. However, your response does not adequately address why your risk analysis system failed to identify and incorporate critical hazards such as effects on

conclusion: We have received no updated response(s) from your firm for September/October 2025 to demonstrate the investigation completion of CR-03042 and its closure.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug
deficiencies.description: Your 'ADVANCE KING' product is a drug as defined by section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(g)(1), because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease and/or intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims observed on your website and from the labeling that provide evidence of the intended use of these products as drugs include, but may not be limited to the following: ...
deficiencies.required_action: Introduction or delivery of this product into interstate commerce without an approved application violates sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d).

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug
deficiencies.description: Your 'ADVANCE KING' product is misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(a). Under section 201(n) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(n), provides that, in determining whether an article's labeling or advertising 'is misleading there shall be taken into account ...'
deficiencies.evidence: The labeling for your 'ADVANCE KING' product does not declare that the product contains dexamethasone, diclofenac, and methocarbamol. As previously mentioned, these undeclared ingredients in your 'ADVANCE KING' product may pose serious health risks because consumers with underlying medical issues may take the products without knowing that they can cause serious harm or interact in dangerous ways with other drugs they may be taking.
deficiencies.required_action: The failure to disclose these ingredients in the product labeling renders your 'ADVANCE KING' product misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(a).

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA)

deficiencies.title: 1. The study director failed to assure that all experimental data, including observations of unanticipated responses of the test system are accurately recorded and verified, and unforeseen circumstances that may affect the quality and integrity of the nonclinical laboratory study are noted when they occur, and corrective action is taken and documented [21 CFR 58.33(b) and 58.33(c)]
deficiencies.description: Study records for several studies showed incorrect information on weight gain for multiple animals. Specifically, that multiple animals in the same study gained the exact same weight (0.5 gram) between weight timepoints, which is improbable.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to conduct nonclinical laboratory studies in accordance with the protocols [21 CFR 58.130(a)]
deficiencies.required_action: Complete and accurate study data are necessary to allow FDA to fully assess the overall safety and risk of a device with an associated premarket submission.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to conduct nonclinical laboratory studies in accordance with the protocols [21 CFR 58.130(a)]
deficiencies.description: Raw data for rabbit pyrogen tests fail to include documentation of calibration of the temperature probes before each use, as required by each associated study protocol.
deficiencies.evidence: Lack of adequate calibration is a protocol deviation, as USP <151> is the basis for rabbit pyrogen test and is identified as the guideline to follow in the rabbit pyrogen tests.
deficiencies.required_action: The validity of those previously conducted rabbit pyrogen studies (e.g., ) is questionable.

conclusion: Your written response is inadequate. The response provided shows that relevant SOPs were revised, amendments and re-testing were done, and staff training was recorded, but it does not provide: (1) documentation of actions that have been or will be taken to fully address and correct the specific violations observed during the studies and the conditions which allowed them to occur; (2) clarification regarding whether the study directors are required to train on your revised procedures and reference documents related to accurately recording data, completing individual animal health assessments, and performing appropriate follow-up; and (3) any planned preventive actions, such as frequency (i.e., quarterly, annual) of audits or future training for new staff, including study directors, and/or new procedures, as appropriate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately test batches of your drug products for the identity and strength of your active ingredients (e.g., ethanol and chloroxylenol) before release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug products that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to ensure that components were suitable for use in manufacturing your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A summary of results obtained from testing all components to evaluate the reliability of the certificate of analysis (COA) from each component manufacturer. Include your standard operating procedure (SOP) that describes this COA validation program.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test incoming component ethyl alcohol (ethanol) at risk of methanol contamination for identity before using it as an active pharmaceutical ingredient (API)
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately test your incoming component ethyl alcohol (ethanol) at risk of methanol contamination for identity before using it as an API in manufacturing your OTC topical drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a retain of a component lot is unavailable, perform retain sample testing of all implicated finished drug product batches for the presence of DEG and EG.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test incoming components at risk for diethylene glycol (DEG) or ethylene glycol (EG) contamination for identity before using them
deficiencies.description: You also failed to adequately test your incoming components at high risk of DEG or EG contamination for identity before using them to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). Take prompt and appropriate actions to determine the safety of all lots of the component(s) and any related drug product that could contain DEG or EG, including customer notifications and product recalls for any contaminated lots.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test water used as a component in drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm uses water as a component in the manufacture of your drug products. Your firm has not demonstrated that the water is suitable for its intended use in pharmaceutical manufacturing.
deficiencies.required_action: A procedure governing your program for ongoing control, maintenance, and monitoring that ensures your water system consistently produces water that meets USP monograph specifications and appropriate microbial limits.

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality control unit to exercise its responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) lacked adequate control over your OTC drug product manufacturing operations and failed to ensure that you had adequate procedures.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Unapproved and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for control and distribution of finished devices
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established and maintained procedures for the control and distribution of your SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Qualitative Test system to ensure only devices approved for release are distributed, and that purchase orders are reviewed to ensure that ambiguities and errors are resolved before devices are released for distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: No records were maintained to demonstrate that these devices were approved for release. We acknowledge you have opened CAPA #2021-002 and created new standard operating procedures to address Purchase Management and Control and Distribution of your products, in addition to completing personnel training on the new procedures and processes. You did not provide evidence of implementation of your new SOPs, or evidence demonstrating that your CAPA is effective in preventing noted violations from recurring.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions remain in progress

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for acceptance activities
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for incoming product and finished device acceptance activities. There are no acceptance records of your SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Qualitative Test system to ensure that specified requirements for your devices are met and meets the acceptance criteria.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no acceptance records of your SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Qualitative Test system to ensure that specified requirements for your devices are met and meets the acceptance criteria. We acknowledge you opened CAPA #2021-003 and created a new acceptance activity work instruction for incoming and finished devices, and completed personnel training on the new procedures and work instructions. You did not provide evidence of implementation of your new work instruction and evidence demonstrating that your CAPA is effective in preventing noted violations from recurring.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions remain in progress

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established and maintained procedures to ensure that nonconforming product is identified, documented, evaluated, segregated, and dispositioned.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigators observed 13 cartons of SARS-CoV-2 Antigen Rapid Qualitative Tests co-mingled in a storage room with multiple cartons of returned nonconforming test kits, samples used for product evaluation, and damaged controls, all of which was slated for destruction. The 13 cartons of test kits were not identified as nonconforming and no records were maintained to demonstrate if an investigation was needed or the disposition of nonconforming products.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions remain in progress

conclusion: We request that you take immediate action to cease the sale and distribution of such unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Some of your facility's drug products, such as Prednisolone/Moxifloxacin HCl/Bromfenac PF Sterile Ophthalmic Solution and Prednisolone/Moxifloxacin HCl PF Sterile Ophthalmic Solution, did not include the established name of the drug.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products

deficiencies.evidence: The investigator noted that your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit an application approved by FDA under section 505 of the FDCA

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You compound drug products that are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners; therefore, adequate directions for use cannot be written so that a layman can use these products safely for their intended uses.
deficiencies.evidence: The investigator noted that your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit an application approved by FDA under section 505 of the FDCA

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not validated the design of the EpiCare-Zenith Family of Laser Systems per your established procedure, Product Design Validation (Document QSP-7307; Revision 00; Effective 08/13/2021).
deficiencies.evidence: There is no evidence that a validation was performed to demonstrate that the EpiCare-Zenith™ Laser System’s (all models) hardware will consistently perform as intended when attempting specified treatment optimization parameters under actual or simulated conditions, including challenging conditions such as procedures with (b)(4) and (b)(4) settings.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide the results of your firm's planned corrective actions when they become available and if your firm intends to perform a validation utilizing EpiCare-Zenith lasers as part of your planned post hoc design validation processes.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain complaint files and failure to establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm is not processing complaints in a uniform and timely manner, as required by 21 CFR 820.198(a)(1).
deficiencies.evidence: All 52 complaints received in calendar year 2024 remain open; 20 of 63 complaints received in calendar year 2023 remain open; and 71 of 72 complaints received in calendar year 2022 remain open.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide our office with the results of the retrospective review of previously received complaints to determine if an investigation is required or if a review for reportability under 21 CFR 803 will be performed.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to analyze processes, work operations, concessions, quality audit reports, quality records, service records, complaints, returned product, and other sources of quality data to identify existing and potential causes of nonconforming product, or other quality problems.
deficiencies.evidence: Complaints were received reporting the following failures: 23 complaints related to power drops/loss, 8 complaints for overheated handpiece, and 13 complaints for calibration issues.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide our office with the results of the reassessment of previous CARs and revisions made to relevant procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain device history records (DHR's)
deficiencies.description: Your firm does not have a procedure for the creation or maintenance of device history records.
deficiencies.evidence: Per your Regulatory/QA Director, the DHRs are still in draft and have not been implemented.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide our office with the results of the reassessment of previous CARs and revisions made to relevant procedures.

conclusion: We acknowledge that your firm initiated CAPAs to address these observations. However, we believe that these responses appear to be inadequate.

metadata.company_name: Sanitation & Environmental Technology Institute dba SDWH
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA)

deficiencies.title: Study Director Failed to Assure
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that the protocol, including any change, is approved as provided by 58.120 and is followed; that all experimental data, including observations of unanticipated responses of the test system are accurately recorded and verified; and that unforeseen circumstances that might affect the quality and integrity of the nonclinical laboratory study were noted when they occurred and corrective action was taken and documented.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include but are not limited to: i. All data resulting from in vitro cytotoxicity tests have not been reported and/or used in the analysis. ii. Samples from multiple study protocols are simultaneously tested for in vitro cytotoxicity tests on the 96 well plates. iii. Protocols specific for the testing of in vitro cytotoxicity testing state that cell suspensions will be evaluated with a measurement at ... nm and reference wavelength at ... nm. iv. Electronic records for in vitro cytotoxicity testing document measurements have only been recorded at mean optical density (OD) of ... .
deficiencies.required_action: The study director must ensure that the protocol is followed, all experimental data are accurately recorded and verified, and unforeseen circumstances that may affect the quality and integrity of the nonclinical laboratory study are noted when they occur and corrective action is taken and documented.

deficiencies.title: Study Director Failed to Transfer Raw Data
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that all raw data, documentation, protocols, specimens, and final reports were transferred to the archives during or at the close of the study.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include but are not limited to: i. Not all raw data were transferred to the archives. ii. Only an incomplete version of transcribed absorbance data was verified and sent to the archives by the study director.
deficiencies.required_action: The study director must ensure that all raw data, documentation, protocols, specimens, and final reports are transferred to the archives during or at the close of the study.

deficiencies.title: Study Director Failed to Follow GLP Regulations
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that all applicable good laboratory practice regulations were followed.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include but are not limited to: i. For Study ... the sponsor submitted a change application to amend the name/description of the test material to ... in the final report. ii. The signed final report does not definitively state there is a difference between the test article; nor does it include any description of a deviation in the identification of the test article.
deficiencies.required_action: The study director must ensure that all applicable good laboratory practice regulations are followed, including but not limited to: documenting changes to protocols, ensuring accurate recording and verification of experimental data, and noting unforeseen circumstances that may affect the quality and integrity of the nonclinical laboratory study.

conclusion: Failure to follow GLP regulations impacts the quality and reliability of data contained within the final study report. This adversely impacts a manufacturer’s and FDA’s ability to assess the overall safety and risk of the subject device prior to use in humans as a marketed devices or for the purposes of beginning clinical trials.

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your facility is a contract testing laboratory that conducts chemical and microbiological testing of various over-the-counter drug products for your customers. Your firm lacked adequate investigations into multiple test results that failed the microbiological specifications for drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: You repeatedly accepted this conclusion and CAPA in at least five investigations without sufficient scientific justification to support your identified root cause.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS results for U.S. products for the last three years from the initial date of inspection and a report summarizing the findings of the analysis.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish adequate written responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) failed to exercise adequate oversight over your contract testing operations. For example, your QU failed to adequately: Establish procedures for critical operations including responsibilities and procedures applicable to a QU, handling of deviations, change control, and cleaning procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: You identify the lack of a quality management system as the root cause for this deficiency.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately validate processes used to produce devices labeled as “sterile” according to 21 CFR 820.75.
deficiencies.description: Your Process Validation procedure is inadequate. Your firm failed to perform aseptic media fills annually, and there is no procedure governing how to validate the mixing and heat treatment step used as part of sterilization of your medical devices.
deficiencies.evidence: The Process Validation procedure dated 12/11/1998 is missing requirements per 820.75 including: -820.75(b)(1) that validations are performed by qualified individuals; -820.75(b)(2) the procedure requires validations be documented but does not require all specifics of this regulation such as date performed, and where appropriate, the individuals performing and equipment used are documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Please review 820.75 and update your procedure and processes to meet all requirements. Please ensure future validation activities include clearly pre-defined acceptance criteria.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure equipment used to manufacture devices labeled as “sterile” is routinely calibrated according to 21 CFR 820.70.
deficiencies.description: Your Inspection, Measuring and Testing Equipment procedure dated 2/2/2003 is inadequate because it doesn’t include specific directions and limits for accuracy and precision, or remedial action when such limits are not met for all applicable equipment.
deficiencies.evidence: The Temperature Recorder located on the used to devices during mixing has not been calibrated since 2016. This was cited during our 2017, 2022, and current 2024 inspections.
deficiencies.required_action: Please review your firm’s response and conclude that it is not adequate. You state you will pursue the issue but provide no timeline, corrective action plan, or if you plan to address this prior to further manufacturing.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish design control procedures and implement requirements to devices labeled as “sterile” according to 21 CFR 820.30.
deficiencies.description: Your Design Controls procedure dated 12/11/1998 was not implemented for the Class II, Sereine Extra Strength Daily Cleaner ophthalmic medical device product.
deficiencies.evidence: The design file does not include: design input, design output, design review, design verification, design validation, design transfer, or risk analysis for this product.
deficiencies.required_action: Please review 820.30 and ensure your procedure and design files meet all requirements.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish CAPA procedures according to 21 CFR 820.100.
deficiencies.description: Your CAPA procedure dated 10/19/2007 is not adequate and has not been implemented since 2010.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, it does not include requirements to: -Analyze sources of quality data to identify existing and potential causes of nonconforming product. -Verify or validate corrective and preventive action to ensure such action is effective and does not adversely affect the finished device.
deficiencies.required_action: Please review 820.100 and ensure your procedure and related records meet all requirements.

conclusion: Adulteration under section 501(f)(1)(B) for Failure to Have an Approved PMA or IDE and Misbranding under section 502(o) for Failure to Submit a 510(k)

Based on our review of your website these products for animals are drugs under section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(g)(1), because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease in animals and/or intended to affect the structure or any function of the body of an animal.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims from your website that provide evidence of the intended use of these products as animal drugs include, but are not limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: You can find specific information about how FDA regulates cannabis-derived products at https://www.fda.gov/news-events/public-healthfocus/fda-regulation-cannabis-and-cannabis-derived-products-including-cannabidiol-cbd.

conclusion: Introduction of these adulterated drugs into interstate commerce is prohibited under section 301(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 331(a).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/API/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality. Your firm also failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to provide testing results from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your QU did not provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your drug products. For example, your QU failed to perform assay testing for the strength of the active ingredient in certain drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Laboratory Records Inadequacy
deficiencies.description: Your laboratory records lacked complete and original data to support the analyses performed. For example, your laboratory records included missing data and discrepancies between the original and re-written versions of testing records.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan to ensure that laboratory records include complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: A description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. If you intend to accept any results from your supplier's certificate of analysis (COA) instead of testing each component lot for strength, quality, and purity, specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier's results through initial validation as well as periodic revalidation.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. The assessment should also include, but not be limited to: a determination of whether procedures used by your firm are robust and appropriate; provisions for QU oversight throughout your operations to evaluate adherence to appropriate practices; a complete and final review of each batch and its related information before the QU disposition decision; oversight and approval of investigations and discharging of all other QU duties to ensure identity, strength, quality, and purity of all products.

deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications designed to assure drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.

metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Some of your facility's drug products, such as Fentanyl 50 mcg/10 mL in 0.9% Sodium Chloride and Phenylephrine Hydrochloride 800 mcg/10 mL in 0.9% Sodium Chloride, did not include the following statement on the label: 'This is a compounded drug.'
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the FDA investigator noted that drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action required

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: The FDA investigator observed an operator placing components within the ISO (b)(4) work area that had the potential to block the movement of first air to critical in-process operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action required

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.
deficiencies.evidence: The FDA investigator noted CGMP violations at your facility, including failure to establish an adequate system for monitoring environmental conditions in aseptic processing areas.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action required

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. If you intend to accept any results from your supplier's COA instead of testing each component lot for strength, quality, and purity, specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier's results through initial validation as well as periodic revalidation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed program for designing, validating, maintaining, controlling and monitoring each of your manufacturing processes that includes vigilant monitoring of intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure an ongoing state of control. Also, include your program for qualification of your equipment and facility.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate manufacturing process
deficiencies.description: You did not adequately demonstrate that your manufacturing processes are reproducible and controlled to consistently yield drugs of uniform character and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification, and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate cleaning process
deficiencies.description: You have not validated your cleaning process for your non-dedicated manufacturing equipment.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a CAPA plan, based on the retrospective assessment of your cleaning program, that includes appropriate remediations to your cleaning processes and practices, and timelines for completion. Provide a detailed summary of vulnerabilities in your process for lifecycle management of equipment cleaning.

metadata.company_name: TRYM Health, Inc. dba TRYM Health
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your claims concerning compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'Clinically Proven to Work', 'Contains the same active ingredient as Mounjaro and ZepBound [sic].', 'Contains the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy.'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from using the language cited above that misbrands the product.

deficiencies.title: Introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of misbranded products
deficiencies.description: The introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of these misbranded products is a prohibited act under section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims on your website: 'Clinically Proven to Work', 'Contains the same active ingredient as Mounjaro and ZepBound [sic].', 'Contains the same active ingredient as Ozempic and Wegovy.'
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist from using the language cited above that misbrands the product.

conclusion: Please provide a written response to the Office of Compounding Quality and Compliance (OCQC) within the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)'s Office of Compliance describing the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately investigate contamination identified in your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple cross-contamination events over approximately 3 months, inadequate cleaning methods, and failure to consider all impacted batches and manufacturing equipment involved.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish and Follow Written Procedures
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure adequate aseptic behavior and adequate airflow simulations to prevent potential contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: Poor aseptic techniques during start-up and filling, inadequate airflow simulations, and failure to conduct adequate process validation studies.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities. Provide a detailed remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Adequate Written Procedures for Production and Process Control
deficiencies.description: Your process performance qualifications (PPQ) lack adequate supporting data to assess variability in your manufacturing processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate statistical sampling plans, failure to establish and follow written procedures for production and process control, and lack of supporting data for PPQ.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a thorough retrospective review and risk assessment that evaluates how poor aseptic technique and cleanroom behavior may have affected the quality and sterility of your drugs. Provide an evaluation of the design of the stopper conveyer track to assess the adequacy of unidirectional airflow interacting with your equipment.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)
deficiencies.description: Your COVID-19 Test Kits are intended for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19 in people and thus, they are devices under section 201(h) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (the “Act”), 21 U.S.C. § 321(h). The products are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g). Your products are also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the devices into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k).
deficiencies.evidence: https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/emergency-situations-medicaldevices/coronavirus-covid-19-and-medical-devices
deficiencies.required_action: Take immediate action to cease the sale of such unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

deficiencies.title: Misleading Representations
deficiencies.description: The At-Home COVID-19 Test Kit, COVID Test Kit (25 Tests), and COVID-19 Antibodies Test Kit are also misbranded under section 502(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(a), because your website represents that these products are “FDA Approved” or “FDA Authorized.” These representations create a false impression that your products have been approved or authorized for emergency use by FDA and are misleading.
deficiencies.required_action: Review your websites, product labels, and other labeling and promotional materials to ensure that you are not misleadingly representing your products as safe and/or effective for a COVID-19-related use for which they have not been approved, cleared, or authorized by FDA.

conclusion: You should take immediate action to address the violations cited in this letter. This letter is not meant to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist in connection with your products or operations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for monitoring and control of process parameters for a validated process, as required by 21 CFR 820.75(b)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm conducts functional acceptance testing on your Dexcom G6 and G7 Continuous Glucose Monitoring Systems. This testing accepts or rejects the glucose sensors based on values of glucose sensitivity slope, accuracy in the presence of acetaminophen (G6 only), and low oxygen response (G6 only); however, your firm has not established procedures to adequately monitor and control the glucose and acetaminophen concentrations used during the process.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Our investigators observed your firm does not monitor the glucose concentration of the glucose test dishes throughout production runs which run for approximately ... . The investigators also observed conditions which may cause fluctuation in concentration such as evaporation, carry over, and spillage.
b. Your procedures require dish ... to have an acetaminophen concentration of ... mg/dL; however, your firm does not have requirements to monitor this concentration during manufacturing of your devices.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a justification for the hazard assessment method.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures to adequately validate a process whose results cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, as required by 21 CFR 820.75(a)
deficiencies.description: For example, your firm's test method validation for the System is documented in Test Method Validation for System (PTL-902713, Rev. 001) and reported in Test Method Validation Report (RPT-904480, Rev. 001). The documents show your firm conducted this validation by testing sensors by operators and documented the results as pass/fail, rather than documenting the measured results.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Your firm's test method validation does not support the method is capable for producing repeatable or reproducible results.
b. During this inspection, your firm provided a memo stating the error in the glucose slope generated by the system is approximately ... based on the uncertainty in experimental conditions.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide ongoing updates to these activities through completion.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for design input, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(c)
deficiencies.description: For example, the special controls for integrated continuous glucose monitoring systems defined in 21 CFR 862.1355(b) apply to your G6 and G7 CGMs. Your design inputs do not clearly define all of these requirements to ensure appropriate design control activities can be conducted.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. The predicted initial and final in vivo sensitivities of the glucose sensors (M0 and Mf, respectively) for the Dexcom G6 and Dexcom G7 CGM Systems are calculated using in vitro slope measurements obtained through the system.
b. Your firm documented design input for initial glucose sensor sensitivity; however, your firm failed to document requirements for the expected 10.5-day lifetime of the devices.
deficiencies.required_action: Please continue to provide updates to this CAPA through completion and include the established trace matrices when finalized.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for design changes, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(i)
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm conducted a design change to a component used in the resistance layer of your sensors from ... to ... . This change is a significant change and your firm failed to adequately evaluate and validate this change.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Your firm conducted a clinical study (...) to determine the equivalence of these two components; this study resulted in determining your primary endpoints were not met.
b. You conducted additional in vivo studies to atte...
deficiencies.required_action: Please describe what specific actions your firm is planning to further evaluate and validate this specification.

conclusion: We request you provide ongoing updates to these activities through completion.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated/Unapproved New Drug

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate you conducted adequate finished drug product testing on your drug products before releasing them for distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including strength and identity testing of the active ingredient, must be performed before drug product release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have adequate scientific evidence to assure your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Based on the records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate you adequately performed identity testing on incoming components including, but not limited to, glycerin and propylene glycol, which are at high-risk of diethylene glycol (DEG) or ethylene glycol (EG) contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Identity testing for these and certain other high-risk drug components include a limit test in the United States Pharmacopeia (USP) to ensure that the component meets the relevant safety limits for levels of DEG or EG. Because you did not perform sufficient identity testing, you failed to assure the acceptability of these components for use in manufacture of your drug products.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products and to use results of stability testing to determine appropriate storage conditions and expiration dates.
deficiencies.evidence: Based on the stability records and information you provided, you did not demonstrate the quality attributes of your drug products remain acceptable throughout the labeled expiry period. For example, you did not include active ingredient testing as part of your stability program.
deficiencies.required_action: Without appropriate stability studies, you do not have scientific evidence to support whether your drug products meet established specifications and retain their quality attributes through their labeled expiry.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to prepare batch production and control records with complete information relating to the production and control of each batch of drug product produced.
deficiencies.evidence: Based on the records and information you provided, your batch production records were inadequate. For example, your batch production records for OTC drug products lacked documentation that each significant step in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of the batch was accomplished.
deficiencies.required_action: Without adequate and complete batch records, you cannot assure the uniformity of your drug products from batch to batch and may impact your ability to adequately investigate any product deviations.

conclusion: Based on their labeled intended uses, “disinpro multi-purpose ANTIBACTERIAL WIPES” and “Antibacterial Wet Wipes” are “drugs” as defined by section 201(g)(1)(B) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(g)(1)(B), because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, and/or under section 201(g)(1)(C) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(g)(1)(C), because they are intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct adequate investigations, lacking data supporting the assigned root cause(s), and not formally documented in your deviation system.
deficiencies.evidence: Visible contamination on the (b)(4) filling line before incubation
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Review Electronic Data from Air Samplers
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to review electronic data from (b)(4) air samplers used for environmental monitoring and ensure a timely and appropriate investigation of numerous aborted air sampler events.
deficiencies.evidence: At least 16 aborted air samples collected over (b)(4) in February 2025, within the (b)(4) areas and corridors
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an independent, comprehensive assessment of microbiological contamination risk for all distributed drug products and batches remaining within expiry that were manufactured on the (b)(4) and ampoule lines using (b)(4) tubing for (b)(4) assemblies on (b)(4) tanks.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish an Adequate System for Maintaining Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate system for maintaining equipment used to control the aseptic conditions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to design and construct buildings and facilities used in the manufacture of your API in a manner to minimize potential contamination, facilitate appropriate cleaning, maintenance, and operations.
deficiencies.description: You produce higher-risk APIs in an inadequate facility including one that lacks appropriate separation for high pharmacological activity or toxic materials including (b)(4) drugs.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, our investigator observed your facility was in a state of disrepair, with manufacturing areas open to the outdoor environment with inadequate protection of materials. Our investigator also observed that (b)(4) drugs were manufactured in a common area.
deficiencies.required_action: Your corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment.

deficiencies.title: Failure to demonstrate that your manufacturing process can reproducibly manufacture an API meeting its predetermined quality attributes and failure to adequately validate written procedures for the cleaning and maintenance of equipment.
deficiencies.description: Process Validation You failed to appropriately validate your processes and adequately qualify the equipment used to manufacture your drugs.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, you have not completed process validation for commercial size lots of your APIs. For example, you rely on studies conducted on development lot sizes for (b)(4) API intermediates manufactured utilizing different equipment than that used for commercial production.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the drug lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have originals or copies of records readily available and promptly retrievable during the retention period.
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure adequate document control over paper and electronic records.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you provided a written statement on firm letterhead admitting to backdating a QC lab document and you signed a declaration confirming commercial drugs shipped to the US were not supported by appropriate original data.
deficiencies.required_action: A management strategy for your firm that includes the details of your global CAPA plan.

deficiencies.title: Failure to design a documented, on-going stability testing program to monitor the stability characteristics of API and to use the results to confirm appropriate storage conditions and retest or expiry dates.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution is an unapproved new drug marketed in violation of section 505(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355(a).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims observed on BPI's Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution labeling, including the product label, that provide evidence of its intended use (as defined in 21 C.F.R. 201.128) include, but may not be limited to, the following: 'Vasoconstrictor for Topical Application' and 'For use as a nasal decongestant'.
deficiencies.required_action: FDA is not aware of any adequate and well-controlled clinical trials in published literature that support a determination that Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution is GRASE for use under the conditions prescribed, recommended, or suggested in its labeling.

deficiencies.title: Misbranding Violations
deficiencies.description: BPI's unapproved Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution product is labeled for use as a topical nasal decongestant, to be '[a]ppl[ied] locally as drops or spray, or with a sterile swab, as required.' Notwithstanding its intended use as a topical drug, the container closure system for BPI's unapproved epinephrine nasal solution appears identical to that used for BPI's FDA-approved epinephrine injection product.
deficiencies.evidence: The Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution glass vial and its similarity to the glass vials for your FDA-approved Epinephrine Injection may mislead users into believing the product is intended for injection and thereby, increase the risk of erroneous administration.
deficiencies.required_action: FDA has received reports of confusion between your firm's unapproved Epinephrine Chloride Nasal Solution and FDA-approved Epinephrine Injection resulting in the erroneous preparation of your unapproved nasal product for parenteral administration.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.
