metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: False & Misleading Claims/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Misbranding
deficiencies.description: The video misbrands Zepbound within the meaning of the FD&C Act and makes its distribution violative.
deficiencies.evidence: The video fails to communicate any risk information, omits material facts regarding Zepbound's limitations of use, and does not submit under Form FDA-2253 at the time of initial publication as required by 21 CFR 314.81(b)(3)(i).
deficiencies.required_action: Cease and desist promotional communications that are misleading as described above.

deficiencies.title: Omission of Material Facts
deficiencies.description: The video fails to provide material information regarding Zepbound's limitations of use, creating a misleading suggestion about the FDA-approved indication and use for Zepbound.
deficiencies.evidence: The INDICATIONS AND USAGE section of the PI states that ZEPBOUND is indicated in combination with a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity, but fails to disclose the limitations of use associated with Zepbound.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective communication(s) should include information to correct this concern.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Submit Under Form FDA-2253
deficiencies.description: A copy of the video was not submitted to OPDP under cover of Form FDA-2253 at the time of initial publication as required.
deficiencies.evidence: The video was not submitted with a completed transmittal Form FDA-2253 and did not include a copy of the product's current professional labeling.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit corrective communication(s) under Form FDA-2253 at the time of initial publication as required by 21 CFR 314.81(b)(3)(i).

conclusion: The video misbrands Zepbound within the meaning of the FD&C Act and makes its distribution violative.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain informed consent
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain informed consent in accordance with the provisions of 21 CFR part 50 before involving pediatric subjects in research.
deficiencies.evidence: Subjects (b)(6) and (b)(4) were enrolled in Protocol (b)(4) without obtaining permission from their parent or guardian.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain written consent
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain legally effective written consent from two subjects before their enrollment in Protocol (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: Subjects (b)(6) and (b)(4) were enrolled in Protocol (b)(4) without obtaining written consent from their parent or guardian.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our findings and provides you with an opportunity to address the deficiencies noted above. Within 15 business days of your receipt of this letter, you should notify this office in writing of the actions you have taken to prevent similar violations in the future.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Nonprescription/OTC

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigator observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS/OOL (including in-process and release/stability testing) results for U.S. products currently in the U.S. market and within expiry as of the date of this letter...

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to perform adequate identity testing on each shipment of each lot of incoming components (e.g., ethanol, glycerin) used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS/OOL result investigation systems...

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately test your incoming component ethyl alcohol (ethanol), at risk of methanol contamination, for identity before using it as an active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) in manufacturing your OTC topical drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The use of ethanol contaminated with methanol has resulted in various lethal poisoning incidents in humans worldwide.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS/OOL result investigation systems...

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: You also failed to adequately test your incoming components at high risk of DEG or EG contamination for identity before using them to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: This includes but is not limited to testing of propylene glycol and glycerin to determine their appropriate identity, before their use in manufacturing your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS/OOL result investigation systems...

conclusion: We acknowledge that you initiated a voluntary recall on July 17, 2025, for products that initially failed for TAMC and those retains that failed for Burkholderia cepacia. On August 27, 2025, you expanded the voluntary recall to include all lots of all products that contain water.

deficiencies.evidence: The INDICATIONS AND USAGE section of the PI states the following (underlined emphasis added): ... Limitations of Use: ZEPBOUND contains tirzepatide. Coadministration with other tirzepatide containing products or with any glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist is not recommended.
deficiencies.required_action: Disseminate truthful, non-misleading, and complete corrective communication(s) about the concern(s) discussed in this letter

conclusion: The video misbrands Zepbound and makes the distribution of the drug in violation of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act)

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to adhere to the requirements of the investigational plans for Protocols (b)(4) and (b)(4), which included ensuring that subjects met all eligibility criteria before enrollment in the studies.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, Subject (b)(6) was documented as having symptoms of possible (b)(4) on January 26, 2021, approximately 15 days before the subject was randomized on February 10, 2021. This is more than the protocol-required time frame for (b)(4) symptom onset of within 7 days before randomization.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address this deficiency and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to retain records required to be maintained under 21 CFR 312
deficiencies.description: You failed to retain electronic case report forms for all randomized subjects, which were required to be retained until two years after the investigation was discontinued and FDA was notified.
deficiencies.evidence: For Protocols (b)(4) and (b)(4), you failed to retain electronic case report forms for all randomized subjects. You were required to retain case histories, including electronic case report forms, until two years after the investigation was discontinued and FDA was notified, which was on May 2, 2024; however, these records were not available for review during the inspection conducted at your clinical site.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address this deficiency and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies with your clinical study of an investigational drug. It is your responsibility to ensure adherence to each requirement of the law and relevant FDA regulations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow adequate control procedures to monitor the output and to validate the performance of those manufacturing processes that may be responsible for causing variability in the characteristics of in-process material and the drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment of your in-process monitoring and sampling operations, focusing on each process step that can introduce variability. Provide your remediation plan to improve: (1) in-process detection of variation; (2) controls; and (3) sampling plans.

deficiencies.title: Component Testing
deficiencies.description: You failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to perform adequate identity testing of each component lot used in the manufacture of your drug products and you failed to verify the reliability of your suppliers’ certificates of analysis (COAs).
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: Water Quality
deficiencies.description: You purchase water and use it as a component in your drug products, including drug products you manufacture under conditions attempting to render a drug sterile, without adequately demonstrating that your water was suitable for its intended use.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to test the water or water to determine whether they are of adequate identity, purity, strength, and other appropriate quality attributes. Additionally, you accept the water without an accompanying COA.
deficiencies.required_action: Testing of purchased water to meet water for pharmaceutical purposes by December 31, 2025.

deficiencies.title: Glycerin Testing
deficiencies.description: You use glycerin as a component in the manufacturing of your drug products such as Bryophyllum Avena Homeopathic Liquid. You failed to adequately test each shipment of each lot of glycerin for identity, a component at high-risk of diethylene glycol (DEG) or ethylene glycol (EG) contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: Because you did not perform identity testing on each shipment of each lot using the USP identification test that detects these hazardous impurities, you failed to assure the acceptability of these components for use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Identity testing of glycerin for DEG and EG testing for current inventory and going forward.

deficiencies.description: The video misbrands Wegovy, Ozempic, and Victoza within the meaning of the Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act) and makes its distribution violative.
deficiencies.evidence: The video features patient testimonials from those who struggle with obesity and excessive weight, including those who have used these drug products to help lose weight. One such patient testimonial includes a mother and her teenage daughter who share their story of the daughter's weight loss journey and experience with the use of GLP-1s in adolescence, specifically the use of Victoza.

conclusion: We are concerned that Novo Nordisk is promoting Wegovy, Ozempic, and Victoza in a manner that fails to present the serious risks of these drug products in a truthful and nonmisleading manner, despite FDA's prior comments.

deficiencies.required_action: Cease any activities that result in the misbranding or adulteration of the above devices such as the commercial distribution of the devices for the uses discussed above.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Correct Violations
deficiencies.description: On May 26, 2021, the FDA sent a letter to the US agent for Shenzhen Moyeah Intelligent Life Technology Co that the MOYEAH products appear to meet the definition of a device under the FD&C Act and that they do not appear to be Class I exempt medical devices. FDA sent follow-up emails on March 3, 2023, and April 21, 2023, noting that pictures and/or content on your website continued to show that you were marketing your products as CPAP cleaner/sanitizer devices. To date, you have not responded to our correspondence.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease any activities that result in the misbranding or adulteration of the above devices such as the commercial distribution of the devices for the uses discussed above.

conclusion: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, as well as an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again. Include documentation of the corrections and/or corrective actions (which must address systemic problems) your firm has taken.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: False & Misleading Claims/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: False or Misleading Risk Presentation
deficiencies.description: The video is a 42-minute special program, hosted by Oprah Winfrey, concerning the “new drugs sparking... a weight loss revolution...that have captured the world’s attention” due to “so-called miracle medications like Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and Zepbound.” The video features testimonials from those who struggle with obesity and excessive weight, including those who have used these drug products to help lose weight. The video also prominently features two paid consultants of Eli Lilly, Drs. Butsch and Velazquez, who are asked about their experiences with treating patients who are obese or have excess weight, and the use of these medications.
deficiencies.evidence: The video includes numerous representations about the benefits of Zepbound and Mounjaro made by representatives of Eli Lilly, but omits important risk information and otherwise minimizes the risks of these drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: We are concerned that Eli Lilly is promoting Zepbound and Mounjaro in a manner that fails to present the serious risks of these drug products in a truthful and non-misleading manner despite FDA’s prior comments.

deficiencies.title: Omissions of Important Risk Information
deficiencies.description: The video omits an important concept from the boxed warning for both drugs; specifically the contraindication in patients with a personal or family history of MEN 2 due to the risk of thyroid c-cell tumors.
deficiencies.evidence: While Dr. Velazquez does provide a limited discussion of the thyroid risk to “anybody [who] has a history specifically of medullary thyroid cancer,” the risk to patients with a history of MEN 2 is omitted.

conclusion: We are concerned that Eli Lilly is promoting Zepbound and Mounjaro in a manner that fails to present the serious risks of these drug products in a truthful and non-misleading manner despite FDA’s prior comments.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality unit to ensure that quality-related complaints are investigated and resolved
deficiencies.description: Inadequate root cause determination, inadequate investigations, ineffective corrective actions and preventive actions (CAPAs), and lack of evidence to support that the metallic particles present are inherent to the manufacturing process.
deficiencies.evidence: Your investigation into a complaint of black particles in API identified this was the first lot manufactured after a facility modification. You stated some particles may have not been removed completely during equipment cleaning but concluded “no exact root cause identified.”
deficiencies.required_action: An action plan and timelines for conducting full evaluation of retain samples for particulate contamination to determine the quality of all lots of drug product distributed to the United States that are within retest dating as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Ineffective corrective actions and preventive actions (CAPAs)
deficiencies.description: Inadequate investigations, ineffective CAPAs, and lack of evidence to support that the metallic particles present are inherent to the manufacturing process.
deficiencies.evidence: A customer reported foreign material contamination involving eight lots of API. Your investigation found CAPAs were implemented for similar complaints prior to the manufacture of the complaint lots. However, our inspection found approximately 28 additional complaints reporting foreign material contamination after you completed your investigation.
deficiencies.required_action: An action plan and timelines for conducting full evaluation of retain samples for particulate contamination to determine the quality of all lots of drug product distributed to the United States that are within retest dating as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have a system for evaluating suppliers of critical materials
deficiencies.description: Deficiency in supplier qualification procedure, failure to implement timely corrective actions, and lack of comprehensive root cause analysis of supplier qualification system’s failure to identify and respond to ongoing quality issues.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm received repeated customer complaints about particles in your APIs that you concluded most likely originated from your supplier of drums and lids. Despite notifying the supplier of these quality issues and continuing to receive customer complaints, your firm failed to implement timely corrective actions, such as qualifying alternate suppliers, enhancing supplier oversight, or discontinuing use of defective materials from this supplier.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Charges
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality. Your firm also failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.evidence: You manufacture aqueous oral homeopathic drug products intended for children as young as 2 years old.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system including, but not limited to: evaluating all suppliers of materials (components, containers, and closures) to determine if they are reliable and appropriately qualified

deficiencies.title: CGMP Charges
deficiencies.description: You failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to adequately investigate finished drug product sample results that were out-of-limit (OOL) for microbiological contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of the design and control of your firm's manufacturing operations, with a detailed and thorough review of all microbiological hazards.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Charges
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory testing, as necessary, required to be free of objectionable microorganisms.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm released finished drug product Temper Tamer lot# S22-26424D without testing for critical microbial attributes (e.g., total count, absence of objectionable microorganisms).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedures
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you failed to define and document procedures for process validation. For example, the following processes were not validated or adequately validated to ensure your Clinical Temperature Monitoring Probes (OT-1; ESO-1; RET-1; SST-1; SST-2) and Needle Microprobe (MT-D) meet pre-determined specifications for sterility and laser welding:
deficiencies.evidence:
A. Sterilization
B. Laser Welding
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for finished device acceptance
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the finished device acceptance activities contained in the respective assembly procedures of your Clinical Temperature Monitoring Probes (OT-1; ESO-1; RET-1; SST-1; SST-2) are inadequate because they fail to establish requirements to verify that these devices meet the claimed 37°C tolerance specification stated in your marketing brochure.
deficiencies.evidence: Assembly Instructions ***OT-1***, Assembly Instructions ***ESO-1***, etc.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address this deficiency.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description:
Specifically, your nonconforming product procedure titled “Part 06: Device Investigation” (Rev 2; Effective: 5/17/10) is inadequate in that it fails to require:
A. Identification, documentation, evaluation, segregation, and disposition of nonconformances found during inspections of incoming raw materials,
B. Documentation of the evaluation of a nonconformance to determine the need for an investigation
deficiencies.evidence: Design Engineer stated that they do not document nonconformances found during incoming inspections, including during the incoming inspection of thermocouple wires conducted per the “NIST Compliance Wire Test” procedure (SOP-EG-006; Rev B; Effective: 11/7/24)
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a response on how you plan to address this deficiency.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm’s response and conclude that it is not adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan, including ensuring that subjects met all eligibility criteria before enrollment and removing from treatment subjects who met certain protocol-defined criteria.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of these failures include, but are not limited to, the following: a. Subject (b)(6) was enrolled in Protocol (b)(4) without confirming the subject's documentation of an (b)(4). b. Subject (b)(6) continued to receive study drug beyond disease progression until January 3, 2022.
deficiencies.required_action: As part of your corrective and preventive actions, you stated that upon determination of ineligibility by the study sponsor, the IRB was notified, the sponsor unlocked the electronic data capture (EDC), and the EDC was updated to note that the subject did not meet eligibility requirements.

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain informed consent
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain legally effective informed consent for Subject (b)(6), who was enrolled in Protocol (b)(4). Specifically, the protocol required subjects to sign a separate special consent for inherited genetic analysis.
deficiencies.evidence: The IRB approved the separate consent for optional genetic analysis on January 10, 2021. However, for Subject (b)(6), a separate consent was not obtained before collecting blood samples for genetic analysis at two timepoints: Cycle 2, Day 1 (on May 25, 2021) and Cycle 3, Day 1 (on June 15, 2021).
deficiencies.required_action: As part of your corrective and preventive actions, you stated that research center staff alerted the sponsor and requested destruction of the blood samples when the oversight was initially discovered in 2022, and the IRB was notified.

conclusion: We emphasize that as the clinical investigator, it is your responsibility to ensure that studies are conducted in accordance with the investigational plan, both to protect the rights, safety, and welfare of subjects and to ensure the integrity of study data.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Laboratory records lacked complete and reliable original data
deficiencies.description: Your laboratory records lacked complete and reliable original data to support the chromatographic analyses performed on over-the-counter (OTC) drug products and components.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple examples of fabricated laboratory records were documented during the inspection, including statements from your Laboratory Manager and a senior Quality Assurance Manger that laboratory investigations were fabricated in preparation for the FDA inspection.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct a comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting. This should include interviews with current and former employees to identify the nature, scope, and root cause of data inaccuracies.

deficiencies.title: 2. Lack of restricted access and sufficient controls over computer or related systems
deficiencies.description: Your laboratory equipment lacked restricted access and sufficient controls to prevent the deletion or record manipulation of your laboratory's electronic raw data.
deficiencies.evidence: Numerous examples of deleted electronic raw data files were documented during the inspection, and analysts had administrative rights capable of altering and deleting data, files, and folders on your chromatographic systems.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct a comprehensive retrospective evaluation of the nature of the testing/manufacturing/other DI deficiencies. This should include an assessment of the extent of DI deficiencies at your facility and identify omissions, alterations, deletions, record destruction, non-contemporaneous record completion, and other deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish an adequate quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your QU did not provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your products.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated that you could not provide records to support the CGMP requirements for the manufacture of drugs, including but not limited to finished product original release testing data, original stability testing data, sample collection and preparation, procedures for quality control data review, batch production and control record.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. This should include provisions for QU oversight throughout your operations to evaluate adherence to appropriate practices.

metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Lack of Adequate Documentation
deficiencies.description: Your firm lacks significant CGMP documentation for your microbiological testing. For example, you routinely failed to document specific attributes of each test such as: equipment number, sample size, media batch number, sample dilutions, incubation temperatures, name/signature of performing analyst.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your testing operations and records. Based on the outcome of the review, your firm plans to update the affected data and test forms.

deficiencies.title: Lack of Appropriate Media Qualification
deficiencies.description: You lack adequate controls to ensure the reliability of your microbiological testing results. For example, the media used to test your customers' drug products was inadequately qualified.
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective risk assessment of gaps found in your growth media qualification and suitability testing practices over the past three years. Describe how these gaps will be remediated to ensure reliable microbial enumeration and appropriate identification of objectionable microorganisms.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Quality Control Unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, test results, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Training Program
deficiencies.description: Your training program is inadequate. CGMP training is not regularly performed at your firm as required by your established procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOL/OOS (including raw material, in-process, and release/stability testing) results for the last three years from the initial date of inspection and a report summarizing the findings of the analysis.

deficiencies.title: Data Integrity Remediation
deficiencies.description: Your quality system does not adequately ensure the accuracy and integrity of data to support the safety, effectiveness, and quality of the drugs you test.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting including results of the data review for drugs distributed to the United States. Include a detailed description of the scope and root causes of your data integrity lapses.

deficiencies.title: Failure to determine as ineligible a donor who is identified as having a risk factor for, or clinical evidence of, any of the relevant communicable disease agents or diseases
deficiencies.description: You failed to determine as ineligible donors who have a documented medical diagnosis of sepsis.

deficiencies.required_action: Revised procedure, SOP-006 Donor Eligibility Determination Criteria, should be revised so that it determines as ineligible donors who have a documented medical diagnosis of sepsis.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish written procedures for production and process controls
deficiencies.description: You only required certain donor batches to meet your process qualification acceptance criteria, even if some batches failed to meet the criteria.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have an adequate system necessary to prevent contamination or mix-ups during aseptic processing
deficiencies.description: Your action limits for microbiological monitoring were observed to be greater than 100 CFUs, and you do not perform sampling of critical surfaces in the BSC for microorganisms.
deficiencies.evidence: Microbiological monitoring data
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications and test procedures designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: You have not established scientifically sound and appropriate specifications and test procedures designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Laboratory controls data
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications and test procedures designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.

deficiencies.title: Drug Listing Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not fulfilled its listing obligations under section 510(j) of the FD&C Act, which is a prohibited act under section 301(p) of the FD&C Act. In addition, Mylanta 2go Antacid Double Strength (NDC 84177-0801), and Microlax Enema (NDC 84177-0020) are not properly listed with FDA, causing them to be misbranded drugs under section 502(o) of the FD&C Act.

deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should investigate and determine the causes of any violations and take prompt actions to correct the violations and bring the products into compliance.

conclusion: Please notify FDA in writing, within 15 working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to address any violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain complaint files and establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Complaints files are not adequately maintained, as required by 21 CFR 820.198(a). For example, alleged deficiencies of your PrimeStore MTM devices received via thirteen (13) emails between 2020 and 2022 were not documented in controlled records.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the complaint handling procedure to ensure that all complaints are processed in a uniform and timely manner; oral complaints are documented upon receipt; and complaints are evaluated to determine whether the complaint represents an event which is required to be reported to FDA under part 803 of this chapter, Medical Device Reporting.

deficiencies.title: Failure to review and evaluate all complaints to determine whether an investigation is necessary
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not review and evaluate whether the two (2) complaints of ingestion in 2022 and two (2) complaints of skin exposure in 2021 required investigations or document reasons for why no investigations were necessary.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the complaint handling procedure to ensure that all complaints are reviewed and evaluated to determine whether an investigation is necessary, as required by 21 CFR 820.198(b).

deficiencies.title: Failure to investigate any complaint involving the possible failure of a device, labeling or packaging to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm does not have any documented investigations for the following complaints which involve the possible failure of your PrimeStore MTM device, labeling, or packaging to meet any of its specifications: Leakage of “(b)(4)” between 2020 and 2022; Release of cyanide after usage of device on incompatible systems (RES 86189)
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the complaint handling procedure to ensure that all complaints involving the possible failure of a device, labeling or packaging to meet any of its specifications are investigated as required by 21 CFR 820.198(c).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have a procedure for implementing corrective and preventive action (CAPA) until during this inspection, as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the CAPA procedure to ensure that all processes, work operations, complaints, and other sources of quality data are analyzed to identify existing and potential causes of nonconforming product or other quality problems; investigate the cause and identify actions needed to prevent recurrence along with the verification or validation of the effectiveness of such actions as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a) through (d).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not have procedures to control the design of your PrimeStore MTM devices until during this inspection, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(a).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the design control procedure to ensure that specified design requirements are met to demonstrate that the design was developed in accordance with the design control requirements as required by 21 CFR 820.30(a) through (j).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications, standards, sampling plans, and test procedures designed to assure that components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, labeling, and drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not perform impurity testing prior to release and during stability for (b)(4). You did not establish scientifically justified specifications to monitor impurities during stability testing of drug products containing Naphazoline Hydrochloride or Tetrahydrozoline Hydrochloride active pharmaceutical ingredient (API).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Aseptic Processing
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Operators blocked first air by placing their gloved hands directly over open sterilized bottles without clearing them from the aseptic filling line. Operators used their gloved hands instead of using appropriate sterile tools to remove jammed bottles. Operator movements in the critical areas were not always slow and deliberate.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide your plan to ensure appropriate aseptic practices and cleanroom behavior during production. Include steps to ensure routine and effective supervisory oversight for all production batches. Also describe the frequency of quality unit (QU) oversight (e.g., audit) during aseptic processing and its support operations.

deficiencies.title: Adequate Smoke Studies
deficiencies.description: Your smoke studies did not adequately demonstrate unidirectional air flow in the ISO 5 classified areas used for the aseptic filling of ophthalmic drug products on the “(b)(4)” and “(b)(4)” aseptic filling lines.
deficiencies.evidence: Your smoke studies lacked simulation of aseptic line set-up, dynamic operations, and interventions that occur during aseptic manufacturing operations. Generation of smoke was not sufficient to demonstrate unidirectional air flow.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities, including an independent assessment that includes, but is not limited to: ... A detailed remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment. Describe specific tangible improvements to be made to aseptic processing operation design and control.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately take action to correct the cause of nonconformities in order to prevent recurrence.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPA 157738
deficiencies.required_action: Perform an investigation, determine the need for containment, and assess the risk associated with the issue.

deficiencies.title: Failure to revalidate a validated process when changes or process deviations occurred
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not revalidate your demineralization press when a major design change was made.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPA 157738
deficiencies.required_action: Revalidate the process and verify that it is sufficient to identify potential particulate contaminants in your firm's demineralized bone matrix (DBM).

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm manufactures over-the-counter (OTC) drug products such as [b](4). You failed to perform adequate identity testing of each component lot used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products, including for [b](4) and active ingredients [b](4) and [b](4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to adequately validate your production and process controls used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products. You also lacked adequate documentation that the equipment used to manufacture and test your drug product was qualified and suitable for its intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm did not establish an adequate ongoing stability testing program to demonstrate that your OTC drug products met established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity for the duration of their labeled [b](4) shelf lives.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

conclusion: Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that the components conform to appropriate specifications before use in the manufacture of your drug products.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Laboratory records
deficiencies.description: Failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.
deficiencies.evidence: Operators did not test all of the (b)(4) used during manufacturing of sterile injectable drug products. Indeed, in videos retained, this practice was observed for three batches you manufactured.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan to ensure individuals responsible for data integrity lapses are removed from positions where they can influence CGMP-related data anywhere in your facility.

deficiencies.title: 2. Batch production and control records
deficiencies.description: Failed to prepare batch production and control records with complete information relating to the production and control of each batch of drug product produced.
deficiencies.evidence: Operators documented in production records for three batches of sterile injectable drug products that the stopper (b)(4) bowl and cap (b)(4) bowl for your (b)(4) were cleaned and sterilized without performing these activities.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan to ensure equipment records will be controlled for accuracy and completeness, and video retention period will be sufficient for Quality Assurance to review video footage as part of batch release.

deficiencies.title: 3. Aseptic procedures
deficiencies.description: Failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Operators failed to adequately follow your written procedures for aseptic behaviors and interventions performed for manufacturing numerous sterile injectable drug products on multiple (b)(4) restricted access barrier system (RABS) filling lines.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan to ensure operators will follow your written procedures to reduce the excessive number of manually intensive interventions performed during manufacturing and ensure adequate clearance of open vials impacted by interventions.

deficiencies.title: 4. Investigation of unexplained discrepancies
deficiencies.description: Failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: Investigation into the media fill failure concluded that vial breakage led to spilled media at the (b)(4) conveyor and contamination of the (b)(4) and (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a plan to ensure appropriate interventions and vial clearance for glass breakage during manufacturing, and an independent assessment and remediation plan for your CAPA program.

metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide DEG and EG test results from testing reserve samples for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a reserve sample of a component lot is unavailable, perform reserve sample testing of all implicated finished drug product batches for the presence of DEG and EG.

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately investigate multiple microbial excursions of your system used to manufacture drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Complete investigations into all test results that were above your microbial action limit (whether or not later invalidated) in the previous three years from the date of this letter. The investigations should detail your findings regarding the root causes of the contamination.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards
deficiencies.description: Your laboratory records did not include complete data to support the analysis performed.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive review and remediation plan for your OOS result investigation systems, including but not be limited to addressing quality unit oversight of laboratory investigations, identification of adverse laboratory control trends, resolution of causes of laboratory variation, initiation of thorough investigations of potential manufacturing causes whenever a laboratory cause cannot be conclusively identified, and revised OOS investigation procedures with these and other remediations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately test batches of your drug products for the identity and strength of your active ingredients before release and distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: According to your batch records, your finished product testing only includes pH, viscosity, color, appearance, and odor. Additionally, it appears your firm is not performing microbiological testing for each batch of your drug products prior to release.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate identity testing of each component lot used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: You relied on your suppliers' certificate of analysis (COA) without establishing the reliability of your component suppliers' test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure of quality control unit to exercise its responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) did not provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your QU failed to ensure the following: adequate validation of your production and process controls used in the manufacture of your drug products, including to qualify the equipment used to manufacture your drug products; establishment of adequate QU procedures; adequate training for employees engaged in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products; appropriate batch production and control records; establishment of an adequate, ongoing stability program; and adequate equipment cleaning and cleaning procedures.
deficiencies.required_action: A summary of results obtained from testing all components to evaluate the reliability of the COA from each component manufacturer. Include your standard operating procedure (SOP) that describes this COA validation program.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide the following: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a lot disposition decision; an action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter; a summary of all results obtained from testing retain samples from each batch. If such testing reveals substandard quality drug products, take rapid corrective actions, such as notifying customers and product recalls.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Adulterated and Misbranded Products Related to Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19)

deficiencies.title: Adulterated and Misbranded Products
deficiencies.description: Your products are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have approved applications for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or approved applications for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g). Your products are also misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the devices into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k).
deficiencies.required_action: Please take immediate corrective action to prevent the sale of any unapproved, uncleared, and unauthorized products for use in the mitigation, prevention, treatment, diagnosis, or cure of COVID-19.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release (21 CFR 211.165(a))
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not demonstrate that you conducted adequate finished drug product testing on your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The certificates of analysis (COA) you provided, including your % Isopropyl Rubbing Alcohol, did not include an appropriate assay test for active ingredient content or microbiological testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including strength and identity testing of the active ingredient, must be performed before drug product release and distribution.

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products (21 CFR 211.166(a))
deficiencies.description: The records and information you provided did not demonstrate that your firm has an adequate stability program for the OTC drug products that you manufacture.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm initially provided a stability test report which noted three years of stability data for batch of % Isopropyl Rubbing Alcohol that was tested between 2017 and 2020. When asked recently to provide data to support the expiry period for each drug product formula, your firm responded with stability data for batch of % Isopropyl Rubbing Alcohol that was tested between 2017 and 2020.
deficiencies.required_action: The stability program must show appropriate chemical and microbiological testing was being performed on an ongoing basis to support the expiry period for your OTC drug products shipped after 2017 to present.

deficiencies.title: 3. Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1) and 211.84(d)(2))
deficiencies.description: You did not demonstrate that you are adequately testing the identity of incoming components used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response indicates that you do not test your active ingredient, isopropyl alcohol, for methanol. Relying solely on supplier’s certificate of analysis (COA) for identity testing is insufficient.
deficiencies.required_action: You must sample, test, and examine drug components before use in production to assure adequate quality, including testing for the presence of methanol in isopropyl alcohol.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Misbranded and/or Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: The RU-FIT (Model SR-3053) is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act. The RU-FIT (Model SR-3053) is also misbranded under section 502(o) the Act, because your firm introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution this device with major changes or modifications to the intended use without submitting a new premarket notification to FDA as required by section 510(k) of the Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: From the RU-Fit (Model SR-3053) User Guide • This device is intended to screen for possible head injuries in the cases of head impact where there is not any visible blood, other than minor scraps [sic]. • The SR-3053 should be used…to compare recognition and choice reaction times of an injured or non-injured individual. From the RU-Fit (Model SR-3053) Training Presentation “Head Impact Trauma Screen: Analysis” “Recognition reaction times for the small, index fingers and thumb” “Cognitive reaction times for the small, index fingers and thumb” “Summary Sheet For Concussive Impact”
deficiencies.required_action: For a device requiring premarket approval, the notification required by section 510(k) of the Act is deemed satisfied when a PMA is pending before the agency. The kind of information that your firm needs to submit in order to obtain approval or clearance for the device is described on the Internet at http://www.fda.gov/MedicalDevices/DeviceRegulationandGuidance/HowtoMarketYourDevice/default.htm.

deficiencies.title: Medical Device Reporting Violations
deficiencies.description: Our inspection also revealed that your firm has failed to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures as required by 21 CFR 803.17. For example, during the inspection, the firm identified the document titled “Complaint MDR SOP ROI-000-0007,” dated 12/11/2019 as its written MDR procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements, as required by 21 CFR 803.17(a)(1). For example: a. There are no definitions of what your firm will consider to be a reportable event under 21 CFR Part 803. The exclusion of definitions from 21 CFR 803.3 for the terms ‘become aware’, ‘caused or contributed’, ‘malfunction’, ‘MDR reportable event’, and ‘serious injury’, and the definition for the term ‘reasonably suggests’, found in 803.20(c)(1) may lead your firm to make an incorrect reportability decision when evaluating a complaint that may meet the criteria for reporting under 21 CFR 803.50(a). b. Your procedure does not include instructions for how your firm will evaluate information about an event to make MDR reportability determinations in a timely manner.
deficiencies.required_action: A device is deemed to be misbranded under section 502(t)(2) of the Act, if there was a failure or refusal to furnish any material or information required by or under section 519 of the Act, respecting the device. Additionally, based on our records, as of October 29, 2024, there is no evidence that your firm established an active Electronic Submissions Gateway (ESG) production account for the electronic submission of MDR reports.

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm’s response dated August 13, 2024, cannot be determined at this time. Your firm’s response outlined plans to implement corrections and corrective actions, including creating an eMDR account and updating their MDR procedure. However, your firm did not provide documentation confirming the completion of these

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Sponsor/Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failed to submit an Investigational New Drug application (IND) for the conduct of a clinical investigation with an investigational new drug
deficiencies.description: Verdure Sciences, Inc. failed to submit an IND before initiating a clinical investigation of the investigational drug Maplifa®, which is subject to section 505 of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. 355].
deficiencies.evidence: The investigators representing FDA reviewed the role of Verdure Sciences, Inc. as the sponsor of a clinical investigation (Protocol 005, “Randomized, Double-Blind Evaluation of Maple leaf extract (Maplifa for Photoaging”) of the investigational drug Maplifa®.
deficiencies.required_action: Verdure should address this deficiency and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: The clinical investigation conducted under Protocol 005 does not meet the exemption criteria in 21 CFR 312.2(b)(1) and is subject to the IND requirements under 21 CFR 312.
deficiencies.description: Protocol 005 does not qualify for any of the exemptions listed at 21 CFR 312.2 from the application of 21 CFR 312.
deficiencies.evidence: The final study report for Protocol 005 stated that Maplifa® is a proprietary, standardized, phenolic-enriched A[cer] rubrum extract formula that is intended to whiten skin and reduce wrinkle production, primarily due to its anti-oxidative and anti-glycating properties.
deficiencies.required_action: Verdure should address this deficiency and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Verdure is the sponsor of the clinical investigation conducted under Protocol 005 as defined in 21 CFR 312.3.
deficiencies.description: As a sponsor, Verdure Sciences, Inc. is responsible for complying with IND requirements, including submitting and having in effect an IND before initiating a clinical investigation.
deficiencies.evidence: The CSA and work order collected during the inspection and provided in your written response identify Verdure Sciences, Inc., as the “Sponsor” and the third-party vendor as the “Service Provider.”
deficiencies.required_action: Verdure should address this deficiency and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our findings and provides you with an opportunity to address the deficiencies noted above. Within 15 business days of your receipt of this letter, you should notify this office in writing of the actions you have taken to prevent similar violations in the future.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Finished Drug Products/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Insanitary Conditions
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures over-the-counter (OTC) drug products that are marketed to [b](4). Your drug products are adulterated under section 501(a)(2)(A) of the FD&C Act because they were prepared, packed, and held under insanitary conditions.
deficiencies.evidence:
Harborage areas immediately outside the first floor of the facility where filling, packaging, and [b](4) production operations are performed. This area consisted of discarded piping with window-grate openings to the exterior of the facility.
A broken window precluding the protection of open processing [b](4) lines (lower right of photo) from the outside environment surrounding the facility.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive plan to remediate the facility, given the pervasive nature of the insanitary conditions. Detailed procedures that demonstrate your firm can maintain buildings free from pests and that they remain in a clean and sanitary state.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to maintain buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products in a good state of repair (21 CFR 211.58).
deficiencies.evidence:
The first floor of your facility contained dirt residue-covered flooring as well as water damage, which resulted in inadequate maintenance of the [b](4) production system.
The [b](4) floor of your facility showed evidence of significant water damage with brown discoloration of the ceiling and walls and green mold-like residue on the ceiling in retained product storage areas.
deficiencies.required_action: Your corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1)).
deficiencies.evidence: You continued to use the Indian Pharmacopoeia (IP- [b](4)) specification for [b](4) solution which did not require impurities or identity testing for [b](4). You have not updated sampling procedures to ensure that sampling was properly documented and adequately represented each shipment of each lot of high-risk components.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide [b](4) test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a retain of a component lot is unavailable, perform retain sample testing of all implicated finished drug product batches for the presence of [b](4).

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess (21 CFR 211.100(a)).
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate [b](4) System Your firm uses [b](4) as a component to manufacture OTC [b](4) drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: [list of required actions]

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to establish and follow written procedures for preventing microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigator observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following. You lacked appropriate controls to ensure the sterility of (b)(4) drug products that purport to be sterile. For example, you relied on the (b)(4) sterilization process without monitoring pre-sterilization bioburden.
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish and follow written procedures for preventing microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all sterilization processes.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to the specifications for the rate of release of each active ingredient
deficiencies.description: You failed to provide adequate data demonstrating your drug products, (b)(4), have a (b)(4) of the active ingredient.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you claim your (b)(4) lasts for up to (b)(4) and that your (b)(4) products have (b)(4) effect for (b)(4). Your release and stability data for dissolution does not provide data that drug (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to the specifications for the rate of release of each active ingredient.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures (b)(4) sterilized, (b)(4). You lacked adequate scientific rationale to demonstrate that your drug manufacturing processes are appropriately validated, reproducible, and controlled to consistently yield drug products of uniform character and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you increased the commercial production batch size and released drug product without appropriate validation. You also failed to include critical process parameters in your batch records.
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.

deficiencies.title: 4. Failure to establish an adequate quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) did not exercise its responsibility to oversee your contract testing laboratory to ensure the appropriate finish product testing was being conducted.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you failed to ensure your contract testing laboratory completed the method validation protocol for (b)(4). The Method Validation Report did not identify potential impurities of (b)(4) referenced in the method validation protocol.
deficiencies.required_action: You should establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.

conclusion: This is a repeat violation from your 2019 and 2021 inspections.

deficiencies.title: Failure to prepare and maintain adequate and accurate case histories
deficiencies.description: In multiple instances, subject ability assessment scores were not entered into tablets at the time of assessment.
deficiencies.evidence: Gaps between assessment end time and data entry ranged from seven minutes to 25 days with no source documentation of 144 assessment values for four subjects over the course of nine visits.
deficiencies.required_action: Investigate and determine the causes of any violations and take prompt actions to implement corrections

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the signed investigator statement, the investigational plan, and applicable regulations
deficiencies.description: Times and/or dates for the aforementioned 144 assessment values were subsequently edited months after the assessment.
deficiencies.evidence: These values were originally entered into tablets between November 12, 2019 and February 8, 2020, by blinded raters, but their times and/or dates were later edited by non-study personnel on May 14, July 23, and July 28, 2020 and on July 12, 2022 to match the hand-written assessment times without source documentation of assessment values.
deficiencies.required_action: Investigate and determine the causes of any violations and take prompt actions to implement corrections

deficiencies.title: Failure to retain records required to be maintained for a period of 2 years following the date a marketing application is approved
deficiencies.description: As a clinical investigator, you are required to maintain records, including case histories, medical records, and doctor’s notes pertaining to the investigation for a period of 2 years following the date a marketing application is approved for the drug indication for which it is being investigated.
deficiencies.evidence: Recalled having written notes at the time of the two study visits, however she admitted that they may have been discarded. Discarding records prior to the 2-year post-approval period, whether intentional or unintentional, is a failure to retain records.
deficiencies.required_action: Investigate and determine the causes of any violations and take prompt actions to implement corrections

deficiencies.required_action: No FDA-approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355, are in effect for these products.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: IBSPOT.com distributed 'Taoscare Motion Sickness Patch' and 'Navabelle Motion Sickness Patches' products that are misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: The respective labeling for 'Taoscare Motion Sickness Patch' and 'Navabelle Motion Sickness Patches' does not declare that the products contain the ingredients diphenhydramine, diphenhydramine N-oxide, or diclofenac.
deficiencies.required_action: The failure to disclose these ingredients in the products' labeling renders 'Taoscare Motion Sickness Patch' and 'Navabelle Motion Sickness Patches' misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(a).

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain complaint files
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately maintain complaint files, as required by 21 CFR 820.198(a). Specifically, section 5.1.2 of your complaint procedure requires all employees to report any product complaint or feedback from the field.
deficiencies.evidence: i. (b)(4), reported on November 6, 2023, listing the patient effect description as “Guardian Device was removed from the patient due to a recurrent pocket infection post implant.”
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures to ensure that all complaints are processed in a uniform and timely manner.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures to ensure that all complaints are processed in a uniform and timely manner.
deficiencies.evidence: i. Complaint-00077: On July 17, 2024, your firm became aware of a reported issue where the physician was unable to retrieve data from the IMD implant. The complaint record was not opened until September 5, 2024 (50 days after becoming aware).
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to review, evaluate, and investigate any complaint involving the possible failure of a device, labeling, or packaging to meet any of its specifications.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: a. (b)(4) was created on June 27, 2024, for IMD batteries that were sent to your contract manufacturer for use in production were returned due to nonconformances (bent prongs).
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to evaluate the cause of the bent probes and the nonconformance record does not include a rationale for not investigating.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design, including adequate risk analysis.
deficiencies.evidence: a. Your firm has received three complaints of infection with the IMD portion of the Guardian System out of a total of (b)(4) IMDS implanted as of February 25, 2025.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to reassess the risk when estimated risks from a hazard differed from the actual risk observed in post-market CAPA and complaint data.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm’s responses and conclude that they are not adequate.
