deficiencies.title: Inspection of Your Contract Manufacturing Organizations (CMOs)
deficiencies.description: Kilitch Healthcare India Limited, an inspection at Kilitch was conducted by FDA from October 12 to 20, 2023, and revealed insanitary conditions and numerous CGMP violations.
deficiencies.evidence: https://www.fda.gov/drugs/drug-safety-and-availability/fda-warns-consumers-not-purchase-or-use-certain-eye-drops-several-major-brands-due-risk-eye
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed plan to ensure you do not receive or deliver adulterated drugs in interstate commerce, in violation of sections 301(a) and 301(c) of the FD&C Act.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Distribution Records
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s records for the distribution of finished drug products are deficient.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. Failure to promptly and adequately address this matter may result in regulatory or legal action without further notice including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Recall Delay
deficiencies.description: FDA held a teleconference with your firm and your CMO, Kilitch Healthcare India Limited, on October 25, 2023. During this call, FDA informed you and Kilitch that our inspection revealed that the products produced at Kilitch do not meet CGMP requirements and are considered adulterated.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct any violations promptly. Failure to promptly and adequately address this matter may result in regulatory or legal action without further notice including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Use of Contract Manufacturers
deficiencies.description: Drugs must be manufactured in conformance with CGMP. FDA is aware that many drug manufacturers use independent contractors such as production facilities, testing laboratories, packagers, and labelers.
deficiencies.required_action: You are required to ensure that drugs you deliver into interstate commerce are not adulterated.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: You filled batch (b)(4) of your (b)(4) Spray from vessels (b)(4). Your QU rejected the drug product units filled from vessel (b)(4) because they failed assay test release specification for (b)(4). You attributed insufficient (b)(4) of the corresponding batch in vessel (b)(4) as the root cause. However, you released drug product filled from vessels (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Investigate Deviations
deficiencies.description: You failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: Objectionable microorganism Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus) was identified during drug product batch testing, including but not limited to: On November 11, 2024, objectionable microorganism S. aureus was found in your (b)(4) Cream, batch (b)(4). You released the referenced batch for commercial distribution based on a passing retest result obtained without conducting a thorough investigation into the root cause of the contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Lack of Controls over Computer or Related Systems
deficiencies.description: Your firm lacked sufficient controls over your liquid chromatography (LC) and gas chromatography (GC) instrument used to test drug products prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: You do not review audit trails and raw analytical data captured by these analytical instruments. You do not have written procedures to review electronic data or integration to ensure data reliability for batch release.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of computer system performance and security. Provide a report that identifies vulnerabilities in the design and controls, and a thorough corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan for each of your laboratory computer systems.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. A comprehensive independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean equipment and utensils to prevent contamination or carry-over of a material that would alter the quality of the intermediates and API beyond the official or other established specifications.
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures (b)(4) API intended for the U.S. market. You lacked adequate procedures for cleaning and maintenance of manufacturing equipment.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA documented an unidentified (b)(4) residue, apparent rust, and unidentified liquids inside or on product contact surfaces of various non-dedicated process equipment used in the production of (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a CAPA plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that materials are handled and stored in a manner to prevent degradation, contamination, and cross-contamination.
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not adequately prevent contamination of materials and intermediates. For example, you stored bags of raw materials outside your facility without adequate protection.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple bags of material, containing API, that were open and exposed to the environment were observed adjacent to an area undergoing apparent construction, an activity which may generate airborne dust and debris.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a CAPA plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an impurity profile for identified and unidentified impurities and failure to ensure all test procedures are scientifically sound and appropriate to ensure that your API conform to established standards of quality and purity.
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct a comprehensive evaluation of your manufacturing processes to identify potential impurities.
deficiencies.evidence: Chromatograms from your unvalidated method showed unknown, unintegrated impurities that were not quantified. As a result, these peaks were not fully considered when making your release decisions.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test the identity of each batch of incoming production material and appropriately qualify suppliers to rely upon their Certificate of Analysis.
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct an identity test on each lot of raw material used in the manufacture of your API. You instead relied on the certificates of analysis (COAs) from your supplier without adequately qualifying them.
deficiencies.evidence: You accepted (b)(4) raw material without performing identity testing or performing vendor qualification.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system, including but not limited to: evaluating all suppliers of materials to determine if they are reliable and appropriately qualified; an assessment of all materials to determine whether they are consistently of acceptable quality; a review to ensure assigned expiration or retest dates are appropriate (supported by data); and adequacy of the supply chain.

conclusion: In your response to this letter, provide: Your corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to perform identity testing on a batch of [b](4) containing up to [b](4)% [b](4)
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform identity testing on a batch of [b](4) containing up to [b](4)% [b](4). You used this material as a solvent for [b](4) tablets with homeopathic active pharmaceutical ingredients to manufacture numerous drug product batches for the U.S. market.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to perform testing on homeopathic active pharmaceutical ingredients
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate testing on homeopathic active pharmaceutical ingredients. For example, you used a batch of [b](4) that was more than 14 years past its expiration to manufacture numerous batches of finished drug products without determining whether the component continued to meet quality attributes.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The review should also determine whether incoming material controls are adequate to prevent use of unsuitable components, containers, and closures.

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality control unit to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. The assessment should also include, but not be limited to: a determination of whether procedures used by your firm are robust and appropriate; provisions for QU oversight throughout your operations to evaluate adherence to appropriate practices; a complete and final review of each batch and its related information before the QU disposition decision; oversight and approval of investigations and discharging of all other QU duties to ensure identity, strength, quality, and purity of all products.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) for Nonclinical Laboratory Studies

deficiencies.title: Failure to prepare a final report
deficiencies.description: You failed to prepare a final report for each nonclinical laboratory study that includes, but is not limited to, the following: (1) name and address of the testing facility; (2) name of the study director and other scientists or professionals involved in the study; (3) locations where all specimens, raw data, and final reports are to be stored; and (4) study director's signature and date.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the Manager of Quality Assurance stated that Vedic personnel do not serve as study directors, Vedic does not maintain study archives, Vedic has never had an animal facility, and Vedic is not a testing facility where the toxicology studies are conducted. Additionally, in your February 3, 2025, written response to the Form FDA 483, you acknowledged that Vedic outsourced Studies (b)(4) to (b)(4), and Study (b)(4) to (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to include accurate and complete information in the final study report
deficiencies.description: The final reports for studies (b)(4) did not accurately identify the testing facility that performed the studies, the study director and other scientists or professionals involved in the study, the storage location for all specimens, raw data, and final reports, and the study director's signature and date.
deficiencies.evidence: The final reports for studies (b)(4) did not accurately identify the testing facility that performed the studies. Specifically, the testing facility that performed studies (b)(4) was (b)(4) ((b)(4)), and the testing facility that performed Study (b)(4) was (b)(4) ((b)(4)).
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide final reports for all three studies
deficiencies.description: You failed to provide final reports for Studies (b)(4) that Vedic submitted to the sponsor, which did not contain complete and accurate information for the above-described items that were required to be included in the final report.
deficiencies.evidence: The final reports for studies (b)(4) did not accurately identify the testing facility that performed the studies. Specifically, the testing facility that performed studies (b)(4) was (b)(4) ((b)(4)), and the testing facility that performed Study (b)(4) was (b)(4) ((b)(4)).
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: We further emphasize that nonclinical laboratory studies submitted to FDA must be accurately and completely reported in accordance with FDA regulations, to ensure the quality and integrity of study data.

metadata.company_name: Chromatography Institute of America dba Compounder’s International Analytical Laboratory
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/API/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Lack of adequate laboratory control mechanisms
deficiencies.description: Your firm conducts chemical and microbiological testing of APIs, drug products compounded by outsourcing facilities registered under section 503B of the FD&C Act, and finished drug products for human and animal use. Records and information collected during the inspection demonstrate that you use the same methods and procedures whether you are testing APIs, drug products compounded by outsourcing facilities registered under section 503B of the FD&C Act, or finished drug products for human and animal use.
deficiencies.evidence: You lacked adequate controls to ensure the reliability of your microbiological testing results. For example, you documented sterility failures for several samples prepared in a (b)(4) media lot, but you were unable to show that you performed growth promotion testing on this lot before use.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to evaluate and remediate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Lack of adequate investigations
deficiencies.description: Your investigations into multiple failed sterility test results were inadequate. In 2024, you had nine drug product sterility test failures, for which your investigation concluded that the probable root cause was “technician error – environmental contamination.”
deficiencies.evidence: You did not recover any microorganisms on settle plates from your ISO 7 sterility suite, on ISO 5 “compounding aseptic isolators” where your sterility samples are prepared, or on contact plates used for monitoring of personnel who prepared the sterility samples.
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS (including in-process and release/stability testing) results for U.S. products for the last three years from the initial date of inspection, and a report summarizing the findings of the analysis.

deficiencies.title: Lack of adequate Quality Unit oversight
deficiencies.description: Your Quality Unit (QU) failed to exercise adequate oversight over your contract testing operations. For example, your QU failed to ensure: Establishment of restricted access and appropriate controls on computerized systems to prevent any potential alteration or deletion of laboratory data.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to review and approve an uncontrolled document, “Sample Prep Tips and Tricks,” used by laboratory staff for preparation of analytical samples.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure that your QU is given the authority and resources to function effectively.

metadata.company_name: Taizhou Kangping Medical Science and Technology Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures designed to prevent micro-contamination in products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: Your (b)(4) are labeled as 'Sterile unless (b)(4)'. However, your (b)(4) are not sterilized, which you confirmed in your response on May 10, 2025: '…(b)(4)… is Nonsterile product....' Sterile (b)(4) may be used by healthcare practitioners as a (b)(4). Sterile (b)(4), if labeled as sterile, must have controls in place to ensure sterility.
deficiencies.evidence: The inspection documented that you do not perform sterility testing of the drug product, and that the Specification and Analysis Method on (b)(4) tests the Finished Product Quality Standards to Microbial Limits USP <61> method, with acceptance criteria of ≤ (b)(4) cfu/gram of total aerobic bacteria and ≤ (b)(4) cfu/gram of mold, yeast.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting, including results of the data review for drugs distributed to the United States. Include a detailed description of the scope and root causes of your data integrity lapses.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish appropriate product specifications and scientifically sound testing procedures
deficiencies.description: A. Your high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) analyses for (b)(4) Assay Content lacked an appropriate determination of system suitability, as outlined in USP <621>, before sample injections. Your quality approved test method, Quality Specification and Operating Procedures for Testing (b)(4), is not scientifically sound in that your Quality Control (QC) analysts are instructed to generate a chromatographic standard curve '…(b)(4)….' This method of calculation from the standard curve has the practical effect of masking system drift and performance issues that affect the quantitative accuracy of batch-specific test results, because the frequency is conducted '…(b)(4)…'.
deficiencies.evidence: B. Your firm lacked sufficient controls over GC and HPLC data acquisition systems that are used to test assay label claims for drug products before release. Specifically, Excel worksheets used to perform chromatogram calculations were not retained, and access accounts to the GC and HPLC workstations were shared by job title and were not attributable to the individual analyst.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting, including results of the data review for drugs distributed to the United States. Include a detailed description of the scope and root causes of your data integrity lapses.

deficiencies.title: Product Specifications
deficiencies.description: Upon further review of the records and information you provided, process controls for the (b)(4) appear to be inconsistent with the applicable conditions for an OTC (b)(4) formulated with (b)(4) as an active ingredient. For OTC (b)(4) drug products marketed under 505G of the FD&C Act, such as the (b)(4), the allowable concentration for the active ingredient (b)(4) is (b)(4)%-(b)(4)%. However, throughout the product lifecycle for the (b)(4), your operations seem to far exceed the allowable concentration of (b)(4) as indicated in your process validation.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting, including results of the data review for drugs distributed to the United States. Include a detailed description of the scope and root causes of your data integrity lapses.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Some of your facility's drug products did not include the following information on the label: a list of active and inactive ingredients, identified by established name and the quantity or proportion of each ingredient.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, FDA investigators noted that drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action: Provide adequate labeling for all compounded drug products.

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed several violations of CGMP and aseptic processing practices that could lead to contamination of drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action: Implement adequate procedures for preventing microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow written procedures applicable to the quality control unit, and failed to ensure that manufacturing personnel wear clothing appropriate to protect drug product from contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigators observed several CGMP violations, including inadequate procedures for maintaining equipment used to control aseptic conditions and failure to prepare batch production and control records with complete information.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action: Implement adequate CGMP procedures and maintain accurate records of batch production and control.

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears that you produced drugs that violate the FDCA. You are required to take corrective actions to address these deficiencies.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct an adequate investigation into recurring problems with visible particulate matter contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: In 2024, your firm initiated an investigation when two batches of ropivacaine hydrochloride injection, supplied in polypropylene bags, exceeded your acceptance criteria for visual inspection. You identified the polypropylene bags as the source of the inordinately high levels of particulate contamination (predominately, white fibers).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications, standards, sampling plans, and test procedures designed to assure that components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, labeling, and drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, we observed the preparation and reading of bacterial endotoxin tests and identified numerous deficiencies: Failure to completely discharge the micropipette solution during preparation of water for injection samples, which may compromise the accuracy and reproducibility of the test results. Transfer of gel-clot sample tubes from the incubator to the waste container in a single rapid motion during microbiological analysis, without the crucial pause required to inspect the firmness of the gel for endotoxin detection.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your laboratory controls are scientifically sound and appropriate.

conclusion: We encourage the use of suitable visual inspection methods for particulates to augment the 100% visual inspection program.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to prevent contamination of equipment or product by substances that could reasonably be expected to have an adverse effect on product quality
deficiencies.description: Specifically, USP water sampling procedures utilized by your firm for testing of conductivity, Total Organic Carbon (TOC), endotoxin, and microbial contamination contain inconsistent and conflicting requirements that ensure water being sampled is representative of water used in production of the Artegraft Collagen Vascular Graft devices.
deficiencies.evidence: USP water sampling procedures utilized by your firm for testing of conductivity, Total Organic Carbon (TOC), endotoxin, and microbial contamination contain inconsistent and conflicting requirements that ensure water being sampled is representative of water used in production of the Artegraft Collagen Vascular Graft devices.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an assessment of the new sanitant on the biocompatibility of the product or any other differences in ingredients (e.g., potential contaminants) and how your firm plans to monitor residual sanitant levels.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to adequately control environmental conditions that could reasonably be expected to have an adverse effect on product quality
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your environmental monitoring procedure, SOP 101, does not allow for environmental samples to be collected in a manner that is reflective of the production environment.
deficiencies.evidence: Section 6.5.2.3 of SOP 101 related to surface sampling states that each designated workstation surface shall be cleaned per the method as described in SOP 102 prior to sample collection.
deficiencies.required_action: In your response, please provide an update on the status of these corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate with a high degree of assurance and approve according to established procedures, a process whose results cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm failed to adequately validate the sterilization process for the Artegraft Collagen Vascular Graft in accordance with recognized standards.
deficiencies.evidence: According to your Sterilization Validation and Requalification document (WI-QA-012), your firm based the process sterilization validation on the requirements found in - - - for validating the sterilization process used to sterilize your Artegraft devices.
deficiencies.required_action: In your response, please provide an update on the status of these corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes to ensure that the specified requirements continue to be met
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not provided a documented rationale establishing the frequency for bioburden testing was determined to be adequate to monitor and control the Artegraft grafts.
deficiencies.evidence: Section 6.1 of SOP 100, Routine Product Bioburden Testing, states that Product Bioburden testing will be performed on a calendar basis, at a minimum .
deficiencies.required_action: There is no documented evaluation to determine if the testing frequency should be updated when out of specification bioburden test results are received.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm’s response and conclude that it is not adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Postmarketing Adverse Drug Experience Reporting Requirements

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop written procedures for the surveillance, receipt, evaluation, and reporting of postmarketing adverse drug experiences (ADEs) to FDA as required by 21 CFR 314.80(b).
deficiencies.description: You failed to develop written procedures that ensured you and your contractor acting on your behalf reported all serious and unexpected ADEs to FDA within 15 calendar days, in accordance with 21 CFR 314.80(c)(1)(i).
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically: a. Your written procedure Q014048, “Handling of Adverse Events and Other Safety Information” (Ver: 28.0, Effective Date: 03 Oct 2024), allowed ADEs reported to NNI to be rejected or cancelled if the ADE was considered by the reporter to be unrelated to the product.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions including but not limited to: (1) revising procedures and other documents to remove certain terminology that created ambiguity and led to serious and unexpected ADEs not being reported to FDA; (2) updating definitions and documentation of the term “spontaneous cases”; and (3) performing a retrospective review of cases to ensure that all valid cases were submitted to FDA.

deficiencies.title: Failure to correctly receive, review, and process adverse drug experience information.
deficiencies.description: You and your contractors inappropriately invalidated 15-day Alert reports because of a lack of patient identifiers; however, during the inspection, FDA identified valid patient identifiers in source documents for these reports.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically: you failed to capture the patient identifier in Argus Case #1342548, which included a consumer reporting a death for a male patient who received semaglutide. During the inspection, FDA found the patient identifier in the source documents for this case.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions including but not limited to: (1) conducting retrospective reviews of cases to ensure that all valid cases were submitted to FDA; (2) drafting work instructions related to ICSR quality checks and verification, including source data verification; (3) planning a phased approach to transition safety case intake from the contracted call-center agents to dedicated insourced patient safety agents, who are health care professionals; (4) performing an assessment of the vendor management program; and (5) creating a Vendor Management team, a Vendor Quality team, and a Training team.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug
deficiencies.description: Your “AlzClipp” product is misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act because your website includes statements that misleadingly suggest that the drug product is approved or endorsed by FDA in some way.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your website https://www.alzclipp.com/ includes an image of an FDA logo with the words “FDA approved” below it. The website also includes an image of a certificate with an FDA logo and the headline, “FDA Approves AlzClipp: A Groundbreaking Brain Health Innovation” next to the image of “AlzClipp.”

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you an opportunity to address them.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain process control procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish production and process control procedures to ensure that devices conform to specifications.
deficiencies.evidence: The document titled, “Ultra-High Molecular Weight Polyethylene (UHMWPE) ETS-0121 Rev. J”, released 02/23/2022 and “Compression Molded UHMW Polyethylene ETS-0122 Rev. L”, released 06/27/2022 call out specifications and testing for polymer powder fabricated into UHMWPE molded products to ensure device components meet specified requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must establish and maintain process control procedures that describe any process controls necessary to ensure conformance to specifications when deviations from device specifications could occur as a result of the manufacturing process.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventative action
deficiencies.description: The procedure for Corrective and Preventive Actions Procedure, 701-103-137, Rev. AB, dated 6/11/2025 does not identify production events as nonconformities or quality data.
deficiencies.evidence: A Production Event (PE) is defined as an event generated in utilized for processing electronic device history records, due to a deviation from pre-established product or process specifications. Production Events are not analyzed as quality data sources or escalated to CAPAs where appropriate.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventative action that describe the actions to be taken in the event of a nonconformity, including the identification of production events as nonconformities or quality data.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: The procedure for Corrective and Preventive Actions Procedure, 701-103-137, Rev. AB, dated 6/11/2025 does not describe the actions to be taken in the event of a nonconformity, including the identification of production events as nonconformities or quality data.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements, including the identification of nonconforming products and the actions to be taken in the event of a nonconformity.

conclusion: We request that you take prompt action to correct these violations. Please provide us with a written response within 15 working days detailing the specific steps your firm will take to correct these violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: SpiroSonic AIR is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, or an approved application for an investigational device exemption under section 520(g) of the Act. This device is also misbranded under section 502(o) the Act, because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the device into commercial distribution.
deficiencies.evidence: During an inspection of your firm located in Boglarka Utca # 17, Street Budapest, Budapest, 1119 Hungary on January 27, 2025, through January 30, 2025,
deficiencies.required_action: Cease any activities that result in the misbranding or adulteration of your devices.

deficiencies.title: Quality System Regulation (QSR) Violations
deficiencies.description: The following QSR violations were observed during the inspection: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design, failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action, failure to validate with a high degree of assurance a process where the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, and failure to establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: We received responses from Mr. Zoltan Zsoter, Chief Operating Officer Uscom Europe dated February 19, 2025, and March 31, 2025, concerning our investigator’s observations noted on the Form FDA 483 (FDA 483), List of Inspectional Observations, which was issued to your firm.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these violations by implementing procedures for validating the device design, implementing corrective and preventive action, validating processes, and controlling nonconforming product.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate with a high degree of assurance a process where the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test
deficiencies.description: The cleaning and disinfection process for SpiroThor used during production of the device has not been validated.
deficiencies.evidence: On January 30, 2025, your firm initiated a CAPA to define testing tasks in order to validate disinfection based on the new procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Complete the validation of the cleaning and disinfection process for SpiroThor by October 31, 2025.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm does not identify, document, and evaluate nonconforming SpiroThor products.
deficiencies.evidence: On January 30, 2025, your firm initiated a CAPA to separate scrap and non-conforming products, maintain statistics on them, and investigate their potential impacts.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct this violation by implementing procedures for controlling nonconforming product and completing the CAPA by December 31, 2025.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately maintain complaint files
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to review and evaluate all complaints to determine whether an investigation is necessary.
deficiencies.evidence: We reviewed your firm’s February 19, 2025 and March 31, 2025 responses and conclude that they are not adequate.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct this violation by implementing procedures for maintaining complaint files and reviewing and evaluating all complaints to determine whether an investigation is necessary.

conclusion: We request that your firm cease any activities that result in the misbranding or adulteration of your devices. We also request that you correct these violations by implementing procedures for validating the device design, implementing corrective and preventive action, validating processes, controlling nonconforming product, and maintaining complaint files.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Two garbage bags containing torn analytical records, including chromatographic results, unidentified number lists, and impurity method validation spreadsheets with handwritten notes. For the method validation study, official records contained only handwritten values, while the original data was discarded in the garbage bags.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies and omissions in data records and reporting.

deficiencies.title: Data Integrity Lapses
deficiencies.description: Your firm lacked procedures to control the use of unofficial personal diaries and to ensure proper retention of all CGMP data.
deficiencies.evidence: Laboratory staff used unofficial personal diaries to record procedures, analytical observations, results, method modifications, and deviation descriptions.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive retrospective evaluation of the nature of the testing data integrity deficiencies.

deficiencies.title: Unexplained Discrepancy or Failure
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: An unofficial audit form was used to document laboratory deficiencies found on July 7, 2025, including a discrepancy in which microbiological sample plates could not be located in an incubator despite a logbook entry indicating the plates had been placed there earlier.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Unexplained Out of Specification (OOS) Results
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately investigate OOS results. On August 31, 2023, you tested for Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC), and on September 11, 2023, reported the results as “Too Numerous to Count (TNTC).”
deficiencies.evidence: On September 30, 2023 and November 3, 2023, you issued revised test reports for the same sample and test date, listing the TAMC result as passing, with a result of < cfu/g.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive retrospective evaluation of the nature of the testing data integrity deficiencies.

metadata.company_name: Jiangsu Kerbio Medical Technology Group Co.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) for Nonclinical Laboratory Studies

deficiencies.title: Study Director Failed to Assure Protocol Approval
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that the protocol, including any change, is approved as provided by § 58.120 and is followed.
deficiencies.evidence: SOP -028 states that, “The study personnel should fill out the animal observation records in the corresponding experimental records daily.” However, based on the study records reviewed by the FDA investigators for GPMT and intracutaneous reactivity studies, there were no daily animal observations raw data included in the experimental records.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action to address this violation is required.

deficiencies.title: Inaccurate Recording of Experimental Data
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that all experimental data, including observations of unanticipated responses of the test system are accurately recorded and verified.
deficiencies.evidence: Raw data for multiple studies conducted by two different study directors showed that the same person reportedly conducted two separate study related activities at the same or overlapping times. Additionally, the raw data for multiple studies showed that other study personnel were reportedly using the same equipment at the same time.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action to address this violation is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Follow Study Protocol
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that studies were conducted in accordance with the protocol, all experimental data are accurately recorded and verified, and that applicable GLP regulations are followed.
deficiencies.evidence: On January 21, 2025, one of the FDA investigators directly observed study personnel, (b)(6), retroactively completing records for an ongoing subacute systemic toxicity study, (b)(4), including clinical observations and animal weight data from Day 1 of the study up to Day 26.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action to address this violation is required.

deficiencies.title: Animal Reuse
deficiencies.description: The studies utilized rabbits that had been previously used in other studies, which contradicts the study protocols.
deficiencies.evidence: Raw data records for ten intracutaneous reactivity studies conducted in (b)(4) and two that were conducted in (b)(4) included an animal reuse record (document # (b)(4) 32 Version 3.2) indicating that the studies utilized rabbits that had been previously used in other studies.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action to address this violation is required.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Follow SOP
deficiencies.description: The study director failed to assure that the protocol was followed, including the requirement for guinea pigs to be acclimatized for at least 5 days.
deficiencies.evidence: SOP -030 Skin Sensitization Test (Guinea pig maximization test) requires that guinea pigs shall be acclimatized for at least 5 days. However, the study records for GPMT studies (b)(4) and (b)(4) show that this SOP was not followed.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective action to address this violation is required.

conclusion: Based on these failures, FDA has concerns about the quality and integrity of the data generated from the nonclinical laboratory studies conducted at your testing facility. Complete and accurate study data are necessary to allow FDA to fully assess the overall safety and risk of a device with an associated premarket submission.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded/Botox Blitz

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Certain products offered for sale by www.acecosm.com are drugs within the meaning of section 201(g) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 321(g)] because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease and/or because they are intended to affect the structure or function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: claims “Prevention of chronic migraine in adults,” “Treatment of urinary incontinence due to neurogenic detrusor overactivity, resulting from conditions such as spinal cord injury or multiple sclerosis,” and “Management of focal armpit hyperhidrosis.”
deficiencies.required_action: No approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: A drug is misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 352(f)(1)] if its labeling fails to bear adequate directions for use.
deficiencies.evidence: The labeling for these drug products fail to bear adequate directions for use, causing them to be misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take prompt action to address any violations of the FD&C Act (which may include the offer for sale of similarly misbranded and/or unapproved new drugs other than the drug products noted above).

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.acecosm.com because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase misbranded and unapproved new drugs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Deficient Design
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to use equipment in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products that is of appropriate design, adequate size, and suitably located to facilitate operations for its intended use and for its cleaning and maintenance.
deficiencies.evidence: The system was unsuitable to ensure both chemical and microbiological quality attributes. For example, your (b)(4) design did not ensure (b)(4). Notably, the system consistently failed to meet the (b)(4) specification. The performance qualification report for your (b)(4) included over (b)(4) of (b)(4)-sampling data, showing that most samples failed USP (b)(4) standards.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive remediation plan for the design, control, and maintenance of the (b)(4), including a comprehensive evaluation of vulnerabilities of (b)(4) design and control, and summarize all deficiencies found in the system. The summary should, at a minimum, describe various system characteristics that were assessed for adequacy (for example, system temperature, materials of construction, slope, identified dead-legs, stagnant locations, unsanitary fittings, flow velocity, maintenance requirements).

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Laboratory Testing
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct appropriate laboratory testing, as necessary, for each batch of drug product required to be free of objectionable microorganisms.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to follow your procedure SOP L6082, “Identification of Gram-Negative Rod Bacteria and Yeast,” which requires the identification of all gram-negative rods found in your samples. You had not performed the required identification of gram-negative rods recovered from your drug products since January 2025, despite seeing an increase in colony forming units (CFUs) from September 2024 through January 2025.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Laboratory Record-Keeping
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not contemporaneously record negative results for sample plates on which no microbiological growth was observed. Your procedure required positive plates to be entered contemporaneously, while blank entries were later automatically documented as zero values.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, analyst competencies, and management oversight. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

conclusion: Your quality system does not adequately ensure the accuracy and integrity of data to support the safety, effectiveness, and quality of the drugs you manufacture.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the (b)(4) dose audits conducted as a process monitoring activity to ensure that the specified sterility assurance level (SAL) of (b)(4) of your firm's validated E-Beam sterilization process continues to be met for your BEAR Implant devices, is inadequate.
deficiencies.evidence: historical (b)(4) dose audit data for sterility testing shows that (b)(4) was unable to detect the presence of a strict anaerobe, Tissierella Praeacuta, which was found to only be present in (b)(4) during a (b)(4) dose audit on 10/7/22
deficiencies.required_action: provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies and continue to provide updates on the progress of your corrections and/or corrective actions, including any supporting evidence

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: You failed to implement sections 1.0 and 4.2 of your 'Nonconforming Materials and Processes' procedure (SOP-0017; Rev. B; Effective: 4/12/23) which state 'products that do not conform to specification requirements are identified, segregated, investigated and dispositioned'
deficiencies.evidence: Lot # (b)(4) exceeded the product requirement specification (PRS) of (b)(4) CFU/device (average) at (b)(4) CFU/device (average) and Lot # (b)(4) exceeded the PRS of (b)(4) CFU/device (average) at (b)(4) CFU/device (average)
deficiencies.required_action: provide a response on how you plan to address the above deficiencies and continue to provide updates on the progress of your corrections and/or corrective actions, including any supporting evidence

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that the design requirements relating to a device are appropriate
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you failed to implement the instructions in the 'BEAR Scaffold Design Requirements Trace Matrix' (DCR 2021-011; Rev. D; Effective: 10/1/21) which requires you to consider areas such as performance and intended use, reliability, and safety when identifying user needs and/or design input requirements for your resorbable BEAR Implant devices
deficiencies.evidence: The design input 'The BEAR Implant shall be able to absorb whole blood (autologous blood injected during the surgical process)' is ambiguous because it does not identify parameters (time, volume, technique, etc.) under which the implant must be able to be fully hydrated while remaining intact
deficiencies.required_action: identify a new or existing user need as the basis of the design input; develop a design output specification tied to the new or revised design input; verify that the design output specification meets the design input specification; and develop a test to provide evidence that the new or revised user need is met

conclusion: We reviewed your firm's response and conclude that it is not adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Finished Pharmaceuticals/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.V10Suppliers.com offers tramadol and oxycodone marketed as 'OL-TRAM TABLETS 100mg' and 'Tramadol 100mg', and 'Oxylan 40mg' and 'Oxycodone 40mg GL Pharma UK' respectively, without approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no approved drug applications in effect for these products. FDA-approved tramadol and oxycodone are indicated for the management of pain severe enough to require an opioid analgesic and for which alternative treatments are inadequate, and are only available pursuant to a prescription from a licensed practitioner.
deficiencies.required_action: www.V10Suppliers.com must cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: The labeling for these drugs fail to bear adequate directions for use, causing them to be misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Prescription drugs, as defined in section 503(b)(1) of the FD&C Act, can be used safely only at the direction, and under the supervision, of a licensed practitioner. Because the aforementioned drugs are prescription drugs intended for conditions that are not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by a layperson, adequate directions cannot be written such that a layperson can use the product safely for the intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: www.V10Suppliers.com must cease offering any misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Offering Prescription Drugs Without a Prescription
deficiencies.description: www.V10Suppliers.com offers prescription drugs for sale without a prescription, which is an act that results in the drug being misbranded while held for sale.
deficiencies.evidence: Under U.S. law, prescription drugs can be dispensed only pursuant to a prescription from a healthcare practitioner licensed by law to administer prescription drugs.
deficiencies.required_action: www.V10Suppliers.com must cease offering any prescription drugs without a prescription for sale to U.S. consumers.

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.V10Suppliers.com because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase misbranded and unapproved new drugs.

metadata.company_name: Right Value Drug Stores, LLC dba Carie Boyd Pharmaceuticals
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Some of your facility's drug products, such as Ketoconazole 2% Cream, Testosterone Cypionate 200 mg/ml injections, and Testosterone Cypionate 200 mg/ml + Testosterone Propionate 10 mg/ml injections, did not include the following information on the label: The quantity or proportion of each inactive ingredient.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigator noted that drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide adequate labeling for compounded drug products

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigator observed that your firm failed to perform adequate smoke studies under dynamic conditions to demonstrate unidirectional airflow within the ISO 5 area. Additionally, media fills were not performed under the most challenging or stressful conditions.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement corrective actions to prevent contamination and ensure aseptic manufacturing processes

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigator noted CGMP violations at your facility, including failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, failure to clean and sterilize equipment, and failure to provide equipment for adequate control over air pressure, micro-organisms, dust, humidity, and temperature.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement corrective actions to ensure compliance with CGMP requirements

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigator noted that your firm compounds drug products without FDA approval, which is a prohibited act under section 301(k) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.required_action: Obtain FDA approval for compounded drug products

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Your firm compounds drug products that are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners; therefore, adequate directions for use cannot be written so that a layman can use these products safely for their intended uses.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA investigator noted that your firm's labeling fails to bear adequate directions for the intended uses of compounded drug products, which is a prohibited act under section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide adequate labeling for compounded drug products

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drug products that violate the FDCA. We have reviewed your facility's responses to the Form FDA 483. Some of your corrective actions appear adequate; however, we are unable to fully evaluate some of your corrective actions due to lack of adequate supporting documentation.

deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform operations within specifically defined areas of adequate size and to have separate or defined areas or such other control systems necessary to prevent contamination or mix-ups in aseptic processing areas.
deficiencies.evidence: Specific violations including, but not limited to, the following: ...

deficiencies.title: Integrity Testing
deficiencies.description: Your integrity testing program for was found to be deficient. For example: ...

deficiencies.title: Protective Equipment Materials
deficiencies.description: You failed to implement other adequate equipment control systems to prevent contamination during aseptic processing.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Microbiology Laboratory Data Integrity
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test every batch for sterility as required by your procedures. For example, sterility samples that should have been under incubation from batches produced just prior to the FDA inspection start, were not present and a review of sterility test records compared to biometric access data for the sterility testing room did not align.
deficiencies.evidence: Interviews with the sole analyst that is trained for sterility testing revealed that it is his routine practice to not test all batches for sterility and fabricate records for those samples not tested. Furthermore, when sterility testing was performed for batches manufactured during the FDA inspection, four failing sterility tests, including one U.S. product, were observed during the incubation period.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting. Your investigation should include: ... A detailed corrective action plan that describes how you intend to ensure the reliability and completeness of all the data you generate including analytical data, manufacturing records, and all data submitted to FDA.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Sterility Test Method Validation
deficiencies.description: The test method validated for sterility testing is not adequate. You failed to perform method validation for all drug product formulations intended for distribution to the United States.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, sterility testing was observed to have multiple steps or materials used that were not included as part of the initial validation.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Poor Practices in the Aseptic Processing Areas
deficiencies.description: Operators touching the inside of empty sterile tubes, prior to loading onto the filling line. Operators leaning over the filling line with their head and torso, near the filling station, and blocking unidirectional airflow.
deficiencies.evidence: Operators in the ISO 5 area leaning their head and torso over the bulk drug product to manually check the fill level of the sterile bulk drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your aseptic processing areas, including retraining operators on aseptic behaviors and implementing design changes to minimize operator interaction with the aseptic processing line.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Gowning for Aseptic Processing
deficiencies.description: Gowning worn by operators during aseptic processing activities was observed to be stained and torn.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your gowning procedures, including retraining operators on proper gowning practices and implementing design changes to minimize contamination risks.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean equipment and utensils
deficiencies.description: You failed to have adequate procedures for the cleaning and maintenance of manufacturing equipment and facilities.
deficiencies.evidence: • (b)(4) colored residues were observed inside the (b)(4) later this same day. This (b)(4) is used to manufacture (b)(4). • (b)(4) was observed on the inside and outside of the equipment's product discharge area.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent retrospective assessment of your cleaning effectiveness

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality unit
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately investigate a complaint of (b)(4) particles in the intermediate, (b)(4), Stage- (b)(4), batch (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: The root cause was attributed to improper cleaning/usage of the (b)(4) bag. A CAPA to change the (b)(4) bag more frequently (i.e., after (b)(4) batches instead of (b)(4) batches) was proposed.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures

deficiencies.evidence: Batch production records are not completed contemporaneously. Batch records were observed to be stored in the Quality Assurance (QA) department while manufacturing operations were ongoing.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain complaint files and to adequately maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you failed to document, review, and evaluate complaints as required by your procedure “Complaint Handling,” MD34, Rev A, dated 05/01/2023.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you received e-mails with alleged deficiencies potentially related to the identity, quality, durability, reliability, safety, effectiveness, or performance of your absorbable PDO surgical suture devices. These complaints were not logged, assigned a complaint number, assessed for complaint category type, investigated, reviewed for reportability, evaluated to determine if your device met all specifications and claims, nor evaluated to determine if corrective and preventive actions were needed as required by your procedure MD34.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address this matter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you failed to implement your procedure “Corrective and Preventive Action,” MD30, Rev A, dated 05/01/2023, which requires your firm to document corrective and preventive actions (CAPA), and to verify or validate that CAPAs are effective.
deficiencies.evidence: Your procedure MD30 Section 7.2 requires CAPAs to be documented on form “Corrective and Preventive Action Report,” MD30-RF1, and to be logged on the “Corrective and Preventive Action Report Log,” MD30-LB1. In your written response to FDA on 05/31/2023, responding to your previous inspection, you detailed your activities performed in response to your FDA Inspectional Observations that included opening CAPA 23-001, CAPA 23-002, CAPA 23-003, and CAPA 23-004. However, this inspection found that your CAPA Log MD30-LB1 has no entries, and you have no documented CAPA records.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address this matter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you do not have a procedure that describes the process by which suppliers, contractors, or consultants are evaluated and how their approval status is documented.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not document the results of any evaluation performed by you that qualifies your supplier of absorbable PDO surgical sutures.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address this matter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that all personnel are trained to adequately perform their assigned responsibilities
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your Clinical Specialist has not been trained on your current complaint handling procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: At least five complaints concerning absorbable PDO surgical sutures, received between (b)(4) and (b)(4), were not documented, investigated, or handled as complaint records as required by your procedure MD34.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address this matter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your procedure MD33 does not establish internal systems that provide for a standardized review process or procedure to determine when an event meets the criteria for reporting under this part.
deficiencies.evidence: Although Sections 6.2-6.3 of your procedure MD33 include a requirement to conduct investigations of MDR reportable events, your procedure lacks the instructions on how to perform the investigation and evaluate the cause of such events.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should take prompt action to address this matter.

conclusion: We strongly recommended that you conduct the performance testing appropriate for barbed sutures and submit a new 510(k) notification, including identifying a suitable predicate device to the Agency.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Lack of testing for each batch of drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: You released over-the-counter (OTC) drug products without adequate quality control testing, including but not limited to the identity and strength of each active ingredient. For example, identity and potency testing was not performed for the active ingredient in your ethyl alcohol antiseptic wipes distributed in the U.S. market.
deficiencies.required_action: In response to this letter, provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before a batch disposition decision, and the associated written procedures.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to test samples of each component for conformity with all appropriate written specifications
deficiencies.description: You failed to perform adequate identity testing on each lot of ethyl alcohol and benzylkonium chloride used to manufacture your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Because you did not perform adequate identity testing on each shipment of each lot, you failed to assure the acceptability of these components for use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: In response to this letter, provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to assure that the drug product bore an expiration date
deficiencies.description: Your firm assigned a three-year expiry period to ethyl alcohol antiseptic wipes without scientific rationale to support the labeled expiry.
deficiencies.evidence: At the time of inspection, you lacked stability data to substantiate the three-year expiry period and there was no assurance that your drug products will remain acceptable throughout their labeled expiry period without an ongoing stability program.
deficiencies.required_action: In response to this letter, provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: 4. Failure of quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU lacked adequate control over your OTC drug manufacturing operations and failed to ensure that you had adequate procedures. For example, your QU failed to ensure: Adequate validation of manufacturing processes for ethyl alcohol antiseptic wipes (21 CFR 211.100(a)).
deficiencies.required_action: In response to this letter, provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

metadata.company_name: Premium Health Management Inc. dba Premium Health
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers compounded drug products, including semaglutide and tirzepatide drug products, with false or misleading claims.
deficiencies.evidence: https://premiumhealth.us
