metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded/Botox Blitz

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Certain products offered for sale by www.aesthetic-essentials.com are drugs within the meaning of section 201(g) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 321(g)] because they are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease and/or because they are intended to affect the structure or function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: claims “Blepharospasm”, “sweating of palms and armpits (hyperhidrosis)”, and “Cervical dystonia (spastic torticollis)” under “Indications”
deficiencies.required_action: No approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act are in effect for these products.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: A drug is misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 352(f)(1)] if its labeling fails to bear adequate directions for use.
deficiencies.evidence: Prescription drugs, as defined in section 503(b)(1) of the FD&C Act [21 U.S.C. § 353(b)(1)], include those that, because of their toxicity or other potentiality for harmful effect, and/or the method of their use, and/or the collateral measures necessary for their use, are not safe for use except under supervision of a practitioner licensed by law to administer them.
deficiencies.required_action: Consequently, the labeling for these drug products fail to bear adequate directions for use, causing them to be misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act.

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.aesthetic-essentials.com because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase misbranded and unapproved new drugs.

metadata.company_name: Everlaan Organics, Inc. dba Maple Organics
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/OTC/Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release (21 CFR 211.165(a)).
deficiencies.evidence: You did not demonstrate that you adequately test your OTC finished drug products prior to release for distribution to the United States.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including adequate test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before a batch disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1)).
deficiencies.evidence: You did not demonstrate that you are adequately testing each shipment of each lot of incoming components.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of incoming materials are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products (21 CFR 211.166(a)).
deficiencies.evidence: You did not demonstrate that you have an adequate stability program for the OTC drug products you manufacture.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to prepare batch production and control records that include documentation of the accomplishment of each significant step in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of the batch, for each batch of drug product (21 CFR 211.188(b)).
deficiencies.evidence: The batch records you provided lacked adequate production details regarding significant manufacturing steps.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide your master production and control records for your drug products, to demonstrate that they fully document each significant and validated manufacturing step.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products (21 CFR 211.22(a)).
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU did not effectively exercise its responsibilities to oversee the quality of your drug manufacturing operations.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

conclusion: Introduction or delivery for introduction of a misbranded product into interstate commerce is prohibited under section 301(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 331(a).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for corrective and preventive action (CAPA) as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a).
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not ensure sources of quality data were analyzed to identify existing and potential causes of nonconforming product, or other quality problems.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA discovered during its inspection that starting in July 2023, your firm received at least 41 US market complaints related to syringe pump incompatibility and/or incorrect volume detection for these syringes. However, your firm did not adequately analyze these complaints to determine if a CAPA should be initiated.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to investigate the cause of nonconformities relating to product, processes, and the quality system, as required by 21 CFR 820.100(a)(2).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design, including risk analysis, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(g).
deficiencies.description: Your Risk Management Report for Disposable Syringes without Needles did not include the hazard of use in an infusion pump.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm updated this report (Ver C0) on November 10, 2023, to include the hazard of “The syringe is connected to pump”, with an assigned risk level of 4 (which your firm considers acceptable), a severity level of S2 “serious” and a probability level of P2 “Very few happened”.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to verify that the risk control measures identified in Version C0 for this hazard were implemented or effective.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to the FDA no later than 30 calendar days after the day that your firm received or otherwise became aware of information, from any source, that reasonably suggests that a device that it markets has malfunctioned and this device or a similar device that your firm markets would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury, if the malfunction were to recur, as required by 21 CFR 803.50(a)(2).
deficiencies.description: Your firm received complaints that reasonably suggest that your syringes malfunctioned while in use.
deficiencies.evidence: The information included for Complaint Nos. 20230909, 20230910, 20231001, 20231003, 20231012, 20231102, 20231202, 20231204, 20240101 and 20240102 reasonably suggests that your firm’s syringes malfunctioned while in use.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm failed to submit a report to the FDA within 30 calendar days after receiving or becoming aware of this information.

metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA) Adulterated Device

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately document design input requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not adequately define the physical, performance, and other design requirements for design inputs to include tip compression, aspiration/suction pump, tip deflection, and treatment location for your firm's 072 Aspiration System (Hippo), which are identified in your documents titled, Product Specification 072 Aspiration System (PS 00922, Rev. G) and Customer Requirement Specification Form (CRS 00848, Rev. D).
deficiencies.evidence: Product Specification 072 Aspiration System (PS 00922, Rev. G) and Customer Requirement Specification Form (CRS 00848, Rev. D)
deficiencies.required_action: Adequately define specifications for tip elongation and compression (PS-11.5.2), aspiration flow rate (PS-11.5.3) and tip deflection (PS-11.5.9) that are identified in your document titled, Product Specification, PS 00922, Revision H.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide accurate information
deficiencies.description: The submission K222786 did not provide images or information to show expansion of the distal tantalum markers or compression of tip length of the nitinol tip as shown in the images included in the data report of tip compression testing (Hippo Tip Vacuum Ball Testing).
deficiencies.evidence: Data report of tip compression testing (Hippo Tip Vacuum Ball Testing)
deficiencies.required_action: Provide accurate information and images to show expansion of the distal tantalum markers or compression of tip length of the nitinol tip.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide adequate description of principle of operation and mechanism of action
deficiencies.description: The response provided in the document “002_Cover Letter_RTA_Final.pdf” of the K222786/S001 submission dated October 24, 2022, stated that the 072 Aspiration Catheter tip had “the same Internal (0.072”) and External (0.0855”) dimensions (ID, OD) as the rest of the catheter’s usable length. The length of the tip is [insert length]. The flexible tip is a passive structure that does not have an active mechanism of action and is not self-expanding.”
deficiencies.evidence: Document “002_Cover Letter_RTA_Final.pdf” of the K222786/S001 submission dated October 24, 2022
deficiencies.required_action: Provide adequate description of principle of operation and mechanism of action.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide accurate information about technological characteristics of nitinol tip
deficiencies.description: The information gathered in the inspection conducted July 29, 2024, through August 8, 2024, at your firm raises concerns that the information provided to FDA for review in K222786 did not accurately disclose the technological characteristics of the nitinol tip and the changes in diameter and length of the distal tip that could be exhibited during use conditions for aspiration of thrombus from patients experiencing acute ischemic stroke.
deficiencies.evidence: Inspection conducted July 29, 2024, through August 8, 2024
deficiencies.required_action: Provide accurate information about technological characteristics of nitinol tip.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide adequate documentation of design history
deficiencies.description: The “Design History” notes that revision E updates included, “[insert description]”. The version of the drawing provided in K222786/S001, Appendix 27, was revision D and did not include descriptions of [insert description].
deficiencies.evidence: “Design History” notes and drawing CS00788, revision D
deficiencies.required_action: Provide adequate documentation of design history.

deficiencies.title: Failure to provide accurate information about changes in diameter and length of distal tip
deficiencies.description: The submission K222786 did not provide images that showed compression of the nitinol tip nor flaring of the distal petals of the nitinol tip as shown in videos presented in the “[insert description]”.
deficiencies.evidence: Submission K222786 and videos presented in the “[insert description]”
deficiencies.required_action: Provide accurate information about changes in diameter and length of distal tip.

metadata.company_name: Anhui Hanbon Daily Chemical Co., Ltd.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to test samples of each component for specific identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform adequate identity testing on each incoming component lot used in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. If you intend to accept any results from your supplier's COA instead of testing each component lot for strength, quality, and purity, specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier's results through initial validation as well as periodic re-validation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and document the accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility of its test methods
deficiencies.description: You failed to establish the adequacy of release testing procedures, and you released batches of drug products for distribution without conducting adequate testing to confirm critical quality attributes.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow a written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate stability program, which includes data from long-term or accelerated storage conditions to demonstrate that your product remains acceptable throughout its labeled expiry period.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and CAPA plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program. Your remediated program should include, but not be limited to: stability-indicating methods, stability studies for each drug product in its marketed container-closure system before distribution is permitted, an ongoing program in which representative batches of each product are added each year to the program to determine if the shelf-life claim remains valid.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate the manufacturing process for your drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for PPQ and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug
deficiencies.description: Your “Pico IV” product is an unapproved new drug introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act).
deficiencies.evidence: marketing material found on your website states that “CBD, and other cannabinoids, are considered dietary supplements by the FDA . . . even if they’re administered intravenously.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.description: Your “Pico IV” product is a drug as defined by section 201(g)(1) of the FD&C Act, because it is intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, and/or intended to affect the structure or any function of the body.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims observed on your social media websites and website (www.picoiv.com) that establish the intended use of “Pico IV” as a drug include, but may not be limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Lack of General Recognition
deficiencies.description: Your “Pico IV” product is not generally recognized as safe and effective for the above referenced uses and, therefore, is a “new drug” under section 201(p) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: No approved application pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355 is in effect for your product.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our concerns and provides you an opportunity to address them. Failure to adequately address this matter may result in legal action including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit
deficiencies.description: Failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Discarded original CGMP records found stacked in a bag underneath a vehicle and in a nearby trash can, as well as unrestricted access to blank production batch records and other CGMP documents without QA issuance
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QA department is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Aseptic Processing Areas
deficiencies.description: Failed to perform operations within specifically defined areas of adequate size and to have separate or defined areas or such other control systems necessary to prevent contamination or mix-ups in aseptic processing areas.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate physical separation between Grade A and Grade B classified areas, lack of smoke studies to demonstrate airflow in the transition between the Grade A and Grade B areas, and inadequate sterilization of equipment and utensils.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities, including an independent assessment that includes all human interactions within the ISO 5 area, equipment placement and ergonomics, air quality in the ISO 5 area and surrounding room, facility layout, personnel flows and material flows.

deficiencies.title: Data Integrity
deficiencies.cfr_reference: FDA’s guidance document Data Integrity and Compliance With Drug CGMP for guidance on establishing and following CGMP compliant data integrity practices at https://www.fda.gov/media/119267/download.
deficiencies.description: Your quality system does not adequately ensure the accuracy and integrity of data to support the safety, effectiveness, and quality of the drugs you manufacture.
deficiencies.evidence: Inaccuracies in data records and reporting, including omissions, alterations, deletions, record destruction, non-contemporaneous record completion, and other deficiencies.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting, including a detailed investigation protocol and methodology, interviews of current and former employees to identify the nature, scope, and root cause of data inaccuracies, and an assessment of the extent of data integrity deficiencies at your facility.

conclusion: Your response is inadequate. You must provide a comprehensive remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment and data integrity investigation.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Your facility compounded drug products using a bulk drug substance from an unregistered establishment under section 510 of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your facility’s drug products, such as Semaglutide 5mg/2ml (2.5mg/ml) 2ml, Tirzepatide 60mg/3ml (20mg/ml) 3ml, and Vancomycin 25mg/ml 10ml, did not include the following on the label: the statement “This is a compounded drug”; the name, address, and phone number of the applicable outsourcing facility; the established name of the drug; the dosage form; the statement “Office Use Only”; and a list of active and inactive ingredients, identified by established name and the quantity and proportion of each ingredient.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies to ensure compliance with section 503B of the FDCA.

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your firm’s operator was observed filling drug product intended to be sterile in a manner that directly blocked first pass air over uncapped filled vials.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies to ensure compliance with section 501(a)(2)(A) of the FDCA and prevent adulteration of your drug products.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your facility failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures for aseptic processing, environmental monitoring, quality control, and manufacturing practices.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies to ensure compliance with CGMP regulations and prevent adulteration of your drug products.

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA. Correct these deficiencies to ensure compliance with section 503B of the FDCA and prevent adulteration of your drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct adequate manufacturing investigations into out-of-specification (OOS) results obtained by your external laboratory for your (b)(4), an over-the-counter (OTC) drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: Batch (b)(4) reported a (b)(4) assay for (b)(4) content of 0.423% (specification (b)(4)).
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct adequate manufacturing investigations into OOS results and process deviations that may impact product quality.

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean, maintain, and sanitize and/or sterilize equipment and utensils at appropriate intervals
deficiencies.description: You failed to maintain your drug manufacturing facility in a good state of repair.
deficiencies.evidence: The investigator observed a layer of dirt and a dead insect on the lid of your mixing tank that was only partially covered by a plastic sheet.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality unit failed to have procedures for GMP training, handling OOS results, and change management.
deficiencies.evidence: The deviations procedure lacks details for the investigation process, and the complaints procedure states they are managed by your firm's only customer but lacks information about how your customer will communicate when complaints are received.
deficiencies.required_action: Establish adequate written responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Bioresearch Monitoring Program/IRB

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that information given to subjects as part of informed consent is in accordance with 21 CFR 50.25
deficiencies.description: The IRB failed to require that ICFs for the following clinical investigations included a disclosure of appropriate alternative procedures or courses of treatment, if any, that might be advantageous to the subject.
deficiencies.evidence: Protocol #211100051 was approved following full board review on 12/23/2021 and subsequently re-approved on 12/13/2022. Protocol #2211000807 was approved following full board review on 1/27/2023.
deficiencies.required_action: Proposed Corrective Action: Contact investigators of the identified protocols and request amendments to revise the ICFs to include additional language, as well as to amend your ICF template for FDA-regulated research. However, the response fails to include a description of the standard of care approaches that will be added to the ICFs for the identified protocols.

deficiencies.title: Failure to require that ICFs for clinical investigations involving more than minimal risk include an explanation as to whether any compensation is available if injury occurs and, if so, what it consists of, or where further information may be obtained
deficiencies.description: The IRB failed to require that ICFs for a clinical investigation involving more than minimal risk include an explanation as to whether any compensation is available if injury occurs and, if so, what it consists of, or where further information may be obtained.
deficiencies.evidence: Protocol #2201000535 was classified as research involving more than minimal risk by the IRB on 4/4/2022. However, the ICF associated with this protocol, which was approved by your IRB on 4/4/2022 following full board review and subsequently reapproved on 1/19/2023, did not contain an explanation of whether any compensation would be available to subjects if injury occurs or where further information regarding such compensation may be obtained.
deficiencies.required_action: Proposed Corrective Action: Developing a template for a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) that can be tailored to the degree of engagement by each collaborating institution. However, without reviewing the approved MOU and the revised Operational Policies and Procedures mentioned in your plan, we are unable to perform an informed evaluation at this time.

deficiencies.title: Failure to prepare and maintain adequate documentation of IRB activities
deficiencies.description: The IRB failed to prepare and maintain adequate documentation of IRB activities, including in the following instances:
deficiencies.evidence: Meeting minutes contained discrepancies, including the number of voting members documented in the meeting minutes as present does not match the number of members documented in the minutes as having voted on IRB actions during convened meetings. The IRB failed to prepare and maintain a list of IRB members identified by name; earned degrees; representative capacity; indications of experience sufficient to describe each member's chief anticipated contributions to IRB deliberations.
deficiencies.required_action: Proposed Corrective Action: Providing your staff with additional alerts for FDA-regulated studies. However, this issue continues to persist, indicating that the proposed CAPA is not adequate to address this problem.

deficiencies.evidence: Examples from the 'Classic Whip Sunscreens' product labeling, including your website and social media page listed above, that provide evidence of the intended uses (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of the products as drugs include, but may not be limited to, the following:

deficiencies.title: Misleading packaging
deficiencies.description: Your 'Classic Whip Sunscreens' are misbranded under Section 502(i)(1) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(i)(1), because they are packaged in containers that resemble food canisters customarily purchased by U.S. consumers.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your sunscreens are presented in metal canisters with a 'authentic ‘tilt valve’ actuator' that outputs a star-shaped foam and have a strong overall resemblance to the metal canisters ordinarily used to package whipped cream products and similar dessert toppings.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within 15 working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your “Mithra+ 10% Lidocaine,” “J-PRO Cream,” and TKTX branded numbing creams are misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act.

deficiencies.required_action: No FDA-approved applications pursuant to section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355, are in effect for these drug products identified above.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your “Mithra+ 10% Lidocaine,” “J-PRO Cream,” and TKTX branded numbing creams do not conform to the conditions of use specified in the external analgesics monograph for the reasons described below.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: TKTX Numbing Creams are unapproved new drugs introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d). TKTX Numbing Creams are also misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples from the “TKTX Numbing Creams” products’ labeling, including the website listed above, that provide evidence of the intended uses (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of the products include, but may not limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: Take action to correct these violations

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: TKTX Numbing Creams are intended for use as external analgesic drug products. As described below, these drug products are unapproved new drugs marketed in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C 355(a) and 331(d).
deficiencies.evidence: A drug product is a “new drug” within the meaning of section 201(p) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 321(p), if it is not generally recognized as safe and effective (GRASE) for use under the conditions prescribed, recommended, or suggested in its labeling.
deficiencies.required_action: Take action to correct these violations

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conform to Conditions of Use
deficiencies.cfr_reference: M017: External Analgesic Drug Products for Over-the-Counter Human Use
deficiencies.description: TKTX Numbing Creams do not conform to the conditions of use specified in M017. Specifically, your labeling purports that “Yellow TKTX Numbing Cream 40%,” “White TKTX Numbing Cream 40%,” “Gold TKTX Numbing Cream 40%,” and “Black TKTX Numbing Cream 40%” have a concentration of lidocaine at 5%. This concentration is above the 0.5% to 4% concentration range of lidocaine permitted in M017.
deficiencies.evidence: As formulated, the concentration of active ingredient in your “TKTX Numbing Creams” does not conform to the conditions of use set forth in M017.
deficiencies.required_action: Take action to correct these violations

conclusion: Based on the above labeling evidence “TKTX Numbing Creams” are intended for use as external analgesic drug products. As described below, these drug products are unapproved new drugs marketed in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C 355(a) and 331(d).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow adequate written procedures for cleaning and maintenance of equipment.
deficiencies.evidence: Our investigators observed significant contamination in multiple ducts of non-dedicated equipment used in the preparation of drug products manufactured at your facility.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent retrospective assessment of your cleaning effectiveness to evaluate the scope of cross-contamination hazards. Include the identity of residues, other manufacturing equipment that may have been improperly cleaned, and an assessment whether cross-contaminated products may have been released for distribution.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products in a good state of repair
deficiencies.description: Bird droppings and feathers were observed during the inspection in the AHU area, specifically on the air purification units, ducts, a tank, a cleaning unit, and on the floors surrounding multiple equipment inside your drug manufacturing facility.
deficiencies.evidence: The conditions in your AHU area raise concerns about potential contamination affecting the air supplied to critical manufacturing equipment utilized in your drug manufacturing process.
deficiencies.required_action: A CAPA plan and timeline to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of facilities contamination issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.title: Failure to routinely calibrate, inspect, or check according to a written program designed to assure proper performance of automatic, mechanical, electronic equipment, or other types of equipment
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately inspect and maintain your AHUs to ensure air filters would be effective at preventing contamination.
deficiencies.evidence: The procedure for HEPA filter integrity and particle count testing did not clearly define the quality unit’s role in verifying the condition of critical AHU components, such as ducts, μ HEPA filters, and μ filters, during preventive maintenance, including assessing accumulated residues, filter integrity, and overall cleanliness.
deficiencies.required_action: A CAPA plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to have laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: Batch records demonstrate that you do not always conduct finished product testing of the final bulk formulation. For example, in the manufacturing of McD Anti-Microbial Handwash batch 010726, you pulled a sample after phase A, but did not document whether you pulled a sample for final release testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. Provide an action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of reserve samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug products distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct identity testing
deficiencies.description: You lacked adequate identity testing for your incoming components, including glycerin, used to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your identity test method for glycerin did not include a limit test for the presence of diethylene glycol (DEG) and ethylene glycol (EG). You did not perform adequate identity testing using the USP identification test to detect these hazardous impurities in each shipment of each lot of glycerin you received.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity. Specify how you will robustly establish the reliability of your supplier's results through initial validation as well as periodic revalidation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow an adequate written testing program
deficiencies.description: You failed to establish and follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, you informed our investigator that reserve samples stored are also considered stability samples. You indicated that you only inspect them visually for color appearance and do not record the results.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: Failure of quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm's quality control unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Your batch record for McD Antibacterial Hand Soap, lot 010726, is incomplete as you did not document the mixing time, lot numbers of the active ingredient used, and results from release testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide all required information and corrective actions as outlined above.

metadata.company_name: Sperm Bank, Inc. dba Fertility Center of California
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: Failure to test a specimen from an anonymous or directed reproductive donor of cells or tissue, whether viable or non-viable, for evidence of infection due to relevant communicable disease agents
deficiencies.description: For example, the following repeat anonymous semen donors were not tested for West Nile Virus (WNV) using an FDA-licensed nucleic acid test (NAT) donor screening test at the time of the first donation that is recovered within the June 1st through October 31st testing period.
deficiencies.evidence: a. Semen was collected from donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) between (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). However, testing for WNV was performed on a specimen that was collected on (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). Donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) was determined eligible on 2/19/2024.
deficiencies.required_action: You must keep these HCT/Ps in quarantine.

deficiencies.title: Failure to perform a complete donor screening procedure on a donor
deficiencies.description: For example, a complete donor screening includes reviewing the donor’s relevant medical records. Relevant medical records (21 CFR 1271.3(t)) include the donor medical history interview and a physical assessment of the donor.
deficiencies.evidence: a. A complete donor screening procedure was performed on (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) for repeat anonymous semen donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C), and semen was collected from this donor on (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). However, semen from this donation was released from quarantine on 5/20/2024 for distribution within the U.S. without a complete donor screening procedure within the previous six months.
deficiencies.required_action: You must keep these HCT/Ps in quarantine.

deficiencies.title: Failure to collect a donor specimen for testing for relevant communicable diseases at the time of recovery of cells or tissue from the donor; or up to seven days before or after recovery
deficiencies.description: For example, semen was collected from donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) between (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). However, a specimen for communicable disease testing was collected from the donor on (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). Donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) was determined eligible on 2/19/2024.
deficiencies.evidence: a. Semen was collected from donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) between (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). However, a specimen for communicable disease testing was collected from the donor on (b)(6), (b)(7)(C). Donor (b)(6), (b)(7)(C) was determined eligible on 2/19/2024.
deficiencies.required_action: You must keep these HCT/Ps in quarantine.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for all steps performed in testing, screening, determining donor eligibility, and complying with all other requirements of Subpart C “Donor Eligibility” in 21 CFR Part 1271
deficiencies.description: For example, your procedures do not include WNV NAT as a required test for relevant communicable disease agents and diseases at the time of the first donation that is recovered within June 1st through October 31st of each year.
deficiencies.evidence: a. Your procedures do not include WNV NAT as a required test for relevant communicable disease agents and diseases at the time of the first donation that is recovered within June 1st through October 31st of each year.
deficiencies.required_action: You must keep these HCT/Ps in quarantine.

conclusion: We request that you respond in writing within fifteen (15) working days from your receipt of this letter, outlining the specific steps you have taken or plan to take to correct the noted violations, including an explanation of how you plan to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Medical Device Reporting/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: a. The procedure includes the definition from 21 CFR 803.3 for the term “MDR reportable event”. However, the procedure omits definitions of the terms “become aware”, “caused or contributed”, “malfunction” and “serious injury” from 21 CFR Part 803.3, and the definition for the term “reasonably suggests” found in 21 CFR 803.20(c)(1).
deficiencies.required_action: Remove references to baseline reports from MDR procedure.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit an MDR report within 30 days of receiving or otherwise becoming aware of information that reasonably suggests that a marketed device may have caused or contributed to a death or serious injury
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to submit an MDR following the report from a healthcare facility of a second degree burn that was greater than 10 square inches requiring debridement and a skin graft.
deficiencies.evidence: The patient underwent debridement and skin grafting. Debridement and skin grafting is considered medical intervention necessitated to preclude permanent impairment of a body function or permanent damage to a body structure.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit an MDR report for the referenced event.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a Report of Correction or Removal of a device initiated to reduce a risk to health posed by the device
deficiencies.description: CAPA #22001 was initiated on 05/02/2022 following numerous customer complaints reporting EA POST failure alarms for the WC77 Temperature Management Controller.
deficiencies.evidence: Numerous customer complaints reporting EA POST failure alarms continue to be received following the implementation of the corrective actions.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a Report of Correction or Removal to FDA within 10 working days of initiating the medical device correction and removal actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to document the acceptance or rejection of incoming product
deficiencies.description: Multiple incoming component inspection records could not be located upon inspectional requests.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, inspection records were not available for review for the following incoming components: Power Patient Warmer PCBA, part number (b)(4), under Purchase Order 70113463 indicating receipt of a quantity of (b)(4) units on (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: Document the acceptance or rejection of incoming product.

metadata.company_name: Gussyup LLC dba Private Label Grooming
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit
deficiencies.description: Failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Laboratory Practices
deficiencies.description: Failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies.

deficiencies.title: Stability Program
deficiencies.description: Failed to establish an adequate stability program
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: Documentation Systems
deficiencies.description: Failed to maintain adequate documentation systems
deficiencies.required_action: A complete assessment of documentation systems used throughout your manufacturing and laboratory operations to determine where documentation practices are insufficient.

deficiencies.title: Component Supplier Qualification
deficiencies.description: Failed to test incoming components used in manufacturing your finished OTC drug products to determine identity, purity, strength, and quality
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following. ... Your firm failed to conduct adequate investigations for your (b)(4) solutions, (b)(4) solutions, and (b)(4) spray drug products distributed to the U.S. market.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish and Follow Written Procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Cleaning, Decontamination, and Maintenance ... You failed to adequately clean, decontaminate, and maintain your (b)(4) used for aseptic drug product manufacturing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your change management system. This assessment should include, but not be limited to, your procedure(s) to ensure changes are justified, reviewed, and approved by your quality unit.

deficiencies.title: Deficient Environmental Monitoring
deficiencies.description: Your environmental monitoring program is deficient as it fails to adequately monitor the aseptic processing line.
deficiencies.evidence: Environmental Monitoring ... Your inadequate and limited monitoring deprive you of sufficient meaningful data for determining risk to product during batch operations.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your environmental monitoring program. This assessment should include, but not be limited to, the suitability of actual practices based on extensive retrospective review and prospective observation of aseptic processing operations.

conclusion: We acknowledge you have subsequently recalled some of these products, but your response is inadequate because you do not adequately address your failure to identify and correct these issues in a timely manner.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not provide sufficient details regarding the specific tests to be performed for each batch of finished drug product. You also did not consider a risk assessment or retrospective review of products that have been released without appropriate testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Process Validation
deficiencies.description: You did not have data to demonstrate that you adequately validate your manufacturing processes used to manufacture your OTC drug products and demonstrate that your processes are reproducible and controlled to consistently yield drugs of uniform character and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Your validation plan, “VALIDATOIN MASTER PLAN (VMP),” describes requirements for process validation and hold time studies. However, the protocol implementing your validation planed, “PROCESS VALIDATION PROTOCOL,” failed to include operating parameters, such as bulk hold times, or processing limits, such as acceptance criteria for process parameters.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Control of (b)(4) System
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure that your (b)(4) system is suitable for producing (b)(4) used in the formulation of your drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: You turned your (b)(4) system off when not in use, stopping its circulation, which deviates from its design use as a continuously recirculating system. In addition, you failed to demonstrate that your (b)(4) system is adequately monitored to ensure it consistently produces (b)(4) that meets appropriate chemical and microbial attributes.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide: A comprehensive remediation plan for the design, control, and maintenance of the (b)(4) system. Also include the summary of any improvements made to system design and to the program for ongoing control and maintenance.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: The Sphygmomanometer Unit (Adult and Large Adult), Single Use, Latex Free device is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect. The device is also misbranded under section 502(o) the Act because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the device into commercial distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of the corrective actions, including documentation and confirmation that design controls were applied to the Sphygmomanometer Unit (Adult and Large Adult), Single Use, Latex Free device. Please provide all responses and documentation in English or with English translations.

deficiencies.title: Quality System Regulations (QSR) Violations
deficiencies.description: The device is adulterated within the meaning of section 501(h) of the Act because the methods used in, or the facilities or controls used for, their manufacture, packing, storage, or installation are not in conformity with the current good manufacturing practice requirements of the Quality System regulation.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide updated documentation including, but not limited to, an updated Design and Development Procedure, Purchasing Procedure, DHRs, and DMRs for the Sphygmomanometer Unit (Adult and Large Adult), Single Use, Latex Free device. Please provide all responses and documentation in English or with English translations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not require review and evaluation of all complaints to determine whether an investigation is necessary, nor does it require the manufacturer to maintain a record that includes the reason no investigation was needed and the name of the individual responsible for the decision not to investigate.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide evidence of updated documentation including, but not limited to, updates to the Customer Claim Handling procedures and forms as well as evidence of the corrective actions. Please provide all responses and documentation in English or with English translations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for quality audits
deficiencies.description: There is no documented evidence to show that the scheduled items/processes of the quality system were audited for 2022 or 2023.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an updated documentation including, but not limited to, documentation confirming audit performance and updated Audit Process. Please provide all responses and documentation in English or with English translations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that equipment surfaces in contact with raw materials, intermediates and API do not alter the quality of the intermediates and API beyond the official or other established specifications.
deficiencies.description: Numerous grooves and scratches on the API contact surface of a (b)(4) table, where you perform (b)(4) and initial processing of organs such as pancreases, used in the manufacture of pancrelipase.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because it does not provide a preventive maintenance plan to ensure that your resurfaced (b)(4) table is suitable for manufacturing. Additionally, your response lacks an evaluation of whether the (b)(4) table is made of a material that is adequate to minimize the introduction of foreign metal particles into your APIs.
deficiencies.required_action: A retrospective, independent review of all batches within expiry that could have been affected by (b)(4) introduced as a result of your manufacturing process, including an evaluation of retain samples for (b)(4) and a review of consumer complaints for the last three years.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow written procedures for investigating critical deviations or the failure of intermediates and API lots to meet specifications.
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct adequate investigations into out-of-specification (OOS) results. For example: Your investigation into (b)(4) assay failures during stability testing of (b)(4) thyroid-powder lots identified your sampling procedure as the potential root cause.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because it does not demonstrate that you adequately identify root cause, implement effective remediations, and expand the investigation to evaluate the effect(s) on other batches or APIs. Additionally, your response fails to provide adequate details about your CAPAs and fails to provide supporting documentation for your CAPAs.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

conclusion: The deviations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deviations that exist at your facility.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for validating device design
deficiencies.description: The eBchek Blood Glucose Monitoring System design was not validated using initial production units, lots, batches or their equivalents.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your firm has no documentation of the parts or software version used in the manufacturing of the devices used for, or production records or dates of production, for units(s) used in the “development pilot product” tests, electrical safety test, and EMC test. Also, during the inspection, Mr. Chiu-Yi Huang (Ivan), R&D Manager, confirmed that your firm did not have the documentation for the devices used for testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct a retrospective review of all change(s) to your device, including changes in performance in light of the recent data provided in your response, and consider whether such changes could significantly affect the safety or effectiveness of your device

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Limiting investigations of complaints that only cause customer dissatisfaction is inadequate.
deficiencies.evidence: As of the date of the inspection, your firm has not documented receiving any complaints for blood glucose monitoring systems which is unusual for this type of device.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement training to ensure new hires receive training on the updated complaint handling procedures by integrating it into the existing Quality System training for new employees

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm’s response dated March 13, 2025, cannot be determined at this time.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Deviations
deficiencies.description: Failure of your quality unit to exercise its responsibility to ensure the API manufactured at your contract facility is in compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigator observed specific deviations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan for your QU oversight, including qualification of your contract manufacturers, to evaluate adherence to appropriate practices.

deficiencies.title: Establishment Registration and Drug Listing Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm utilized a contract manufacturing organization (CMO), (b)(4), to perform manufacturing, processing, and packaging activities on your behalf.
deficiencies.evidence: You received drugs from this CMO and declared your firm as the manufacturer on import records. Additionally, you registered your firm with the FDA (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: It remains your responsibility to ensure that (b)(4) and all products manufactured at any of your establishments or distributed under your name and label comply with all registration and listing requirements under section 510 of the FD&C Act.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Consultant Recommended
deficiencies.description: Based upon the nature of the deviations we identified, you should engage a consultant qualified to evaluate your operations and to assist your firm in meeting CGMP requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: The qualified consultant should also perform a comprehensive audit of your entire operation for CGMP compliance and evaluate the completion and efficacy of your corrective actions and preventive actions before you pursue resolution of your firm's compliance status with FDA.

conclusion: Correct any deviations promptly. FDA may withhold approval of new applications or supplements listing your firm as a drug manufacturer until any deviations are completely addressed and we confirm your compliance with CGMP.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Opioids
deficiencies.description: Onlineotcmeds.com introduces into interstate commerce unapproved and misbranded opioids, including fentanyl, hydromorphone HCl, and oxymorphone HCl.
deficiencies.evidence: Evidence obtained from your website establishing that these products are drugs intended for human use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) includes claims such as 'Fentanyl is a prescription opioid drug' and 'Hydromorphone belongs to a class of drugs known as opioid (narcotic) analgesics'.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Benzodiazepines
deficiencies.description: Onlineotcmeds.com introduces into interstate commerce unapproved and misbranded benzodiazepines, including brotizolam, diazepam, and lorazepam.
deficiencies.evidence: Evidence obtained from your website establishing that these products are drugs intended for human use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) includes claims such as 'Brotizolam is a triazole-benzodiazepine derivative with sedative, hypnotic, anxiolytic and anticonvulsant activities' and 'Diazepam is a benzodiazepine. It affects chemicals in the brain that may be unbalanced in people with anxiety'.
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

conclusion: FDA requests that onlineotcmeds.com cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to verify or validate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA)
deficiencies.description: CAPAs 2021-4, 2021-13, and 2021-16 were initiated to address production non-conformities associated with the STAT-IntraOperative-Intact-PTH Immunoassay Kit. However, the firm's Operations Manager, Mr. Jochem van Woezik, stated there was no objective evidence available for review.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigator requested to review the firm's documentation of the verification or validation of the corrective action to ensure the action is effective and does not adversely affect the finished device.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must provide written procedures for CAPA and ensure that all CAPAs are verified or validated to ensure their effectiveness.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures to control product that does not conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: The relative humidity for the (b)(4) compartment, used to store and are used during the calibration process, was found to be out of specification when re-calibrated. Specifically, the calibration report documented that the following relative humidity readings were out of range: ...
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigator determined that a non-conformance report was not initiated.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must establish and maintain procedures for identifying, documenting, evaluating, segregating, and dispositioning nonconforming product.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for identifying valid statistical techniques
deficiencies.description: For recall Z-1016-2023, your firm documented the investigation in Investigation report NCP2022-14, dated 29 November 2022. The report documents, among other things, experiments to investigate the problems with the seals and accusphere integrity.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the investigator asked about your firm's justification for selecting (b)(4) plates. Your Head of Research & Science, Dr. Lindhout, stated there is no statistical basis for selecting a sample size of five plates.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must establish procedures for identifying valid statistical techniques and ensure that sampling plans are adequate for their intended use.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish procedures for quality audits
deficiencies.description: Your firm's procedure, SOP-048, Internal Audit, version 30, effective 10 Nov 2022 states, 'The audit frequency will be such that at least once a year a total facility audit will be performed.' However, previous versions of the procedure include the same requirement.
deficiencies.evidence: A list of audit topics for 2020, 2021, and 2022 was reviewed but it did not include Management responsibility, Design controls, or Corrective and Preventive Actions subsystems.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must establish procedures for quality audits and conduct such audits to ensure that the quality system is in compliance with the established quality system requirements.

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written Medical Device Reporting (MDR) procedures
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, your firm identified the document titled 'SOP Medical Device Reporting,' SOP-087, Rev. 14, dated 11/7/2022, as its MDR procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.

deficiencies.title: Failure to submit a report to FDA
deficiencies.description: The information included for Complaints NCP 2022-14 and NCP 2020-29 reasonably suggests that your firm's STAT-IntraOperative-Intact-PTH (STAT-IO-I-PTH) Immunoassay Kit malfunctioned.
deficiencies.evidence: Although the information from the complaints state that there was no indication of patient impact, your firm should evaluate each malfunction for reportability.
deficiencies.required_action: The firm must submit a report to FDA within 30 calendar days after the day that your firm received or otherwise became aware of information, from any source, that reasonably suggests that a device has malfunctioned and this device or a similar device would be likely to cause or contribute to a death or serious injury if the malfunction were to recur.

conclusion: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to record all quality-related activities at the time they are performed.
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) failed to ensure the integrity of CGMP records. For example, during the inspection, a member of your management stated that two of your operators admitted to falsifying temperature data for a drying oven that was not turned on during the manufacture of a batch, which later failed to meet the residual solvents specification.
deficiencies.evidence: Your documentation practices were not indicative of a facility that is in compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function. The assessment should also include, but not be limited to: ... A complete and final review of each batch and its related information before the QU disposition decision.

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean equipment and utensils to prevent contamination or carry-over of a material that would alter the quality of the APIs beyond the official or other established specifications.
deficiencies.description: FDA documented rust-like residues inside non-dedicated equipment used in the production of API. In addition, FDA documented bare footprints inside another piece of equipment used in the production of API.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequately cleaned and maintained manufacturing equipment can lead to potential cross-contamination that could compromise your API's quality and safety.
deficiencies.required_action: Your CAPA plan to implement routine, vigilant operations management oversight of facilities and equipment. This plan should ensure, among other things, prompt detection of equipment/facilities performance issues, effective execution of repairs, adherence to appropriate preventive maintenance schedules, timely technological upgrades to the equipment/facility infrastructure, and improved systems for ongoing management review.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test the identity of each batch of incoming production material.
deficiencies.description: Your incoming raw material used to manufacture API intended for the U.S. market was not adequately tested. For example, you did not test the raw material used as a raw material in the production of API for identity.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate. You state that you “initiated the activity” to test the raw material for identity. However, you do not address whether all other raw materials are tested for identity or how you will prevent this deviation from recurring with new raw materials.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of raw materials, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product

metadata.company_name: AmWiner & Raphe Holdings, LLC  dba rapheGenerics Corp
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigator observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess, and your firm’s quality control unit did not review and approve those procedures, including any changes.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to complete equipment and process performance qualification (PPQ) prior to releasing your OTC drug products. Likewise, you lacked appropriate documentation, qualification, maintenance, and monitoring of your purified water system, which is used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A remediation plan that better assures ongoing management oversight throughout the manufacturing lifecycle of all drug products. Provide a more data-driven and scientifically sound program that identifies sources of process variability, and assures that manufacturing (including both production and packaging) operations meet appropriate parameters and quality standards.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product. Your firm also failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier’s test analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to adequately test your incoming components, including propylene glycol and active ingredients such as minoxidil, for identity before using them in the manufacture of your OTC drug products. Additionally, you relied on your suppliers’ certificates of analysis (COAs) without establishing the reliability of each of your suppliers’ analyses at appropriate intervals.
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products. Alternatively, if a retain of a component lot is unavailable, perform retain sample testing of all implicated finished drug product lots for the presence of DEG and EG.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A list of chemical and microbial specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each lot of your drug products before a batch disposition decision. An action plan and timelines for conducting full chemical and microbiological testing of retain samples to determine the quality of all batches of drug product distributed to the United States that are within expiry as of the date of this letter.
