metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product. Your firm also failed to validate and establish the reliability of your component supplier's test analyses at appropriate intervals (21 CFR 211.84(d)(1) and 211.84(d)(2)).
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because you failed to provide supportive documentation for evaluation or adequate evidence of corrective actions taken to bring your operations into compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: Products Containing Ethyl Alcohol and Isopropyl Alcohol
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately test your incoming component ethyl alcohol (ethanol), used as an active pharmaceutical ingredient (API), for methanol. The use of ethanol contaminated with methanol has resulted in various lethal poisoning incidents in humans worldwide.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because you do not provide adequate details to demonstrate identity testing meets all compendial requirements. Furthermore, your response does not address testing your high-risk drug components for DEG or EG contamination, or test ethanol for the presence of methanol contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to always conduct at least one specific identity test for each incoming component lot. In the case of alcohol, isopropyl alcohol, glycerin, and certain additional high-risk components we note that this includes the performance of parts A, B, and C of the USP monograph.

deficiencies.title: Products Containing Ingredients at Risk for Diethylene Glycol or Ethylene Glycol (DEG or EG) Contamination
deficiencies.description: You also failed to adequately test your incoming components at high risk of diethylene glycol (DEG) or ethylene glycol (EG) contamination for identity before using them to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because you do not provide adequate details regarding your plan for performing water system validation, maintenance, and testing of the water system. Furthermore, you do not consider retrospective testing of retained finished drug products that used water.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). Take prompt and appropriate actions to determine the safety of all lots of the component(s) and any related drug product that could contain DEG or EG.

deficiencies.title: Water System Validation
deficiencies.description: You used “(b)(4) water” as a component to manufacture your OTC topical drug products. However, you have not established that the water system is adequately designed, maintained, and monitored to ensure that it consistently produces water suitable for its intended use.
deficiencies.evidence: Your response is inadequate because you do not provide your initial water system validation, nor do you provide adequate details regarding your plan for performing water system validation, maintenance, and testing of the water system.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a timeline for the validation of your methods, nor do you consider retrospective testing of retained finished drug products that used water.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates. The chemical and microbiological quality control specifications you use to test and release each incoming lot of components for use in manufacturing.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your CORRECTIVE AND PREVENTIVE PROCEDURE, Doc.ID: 410-0013-01, Revisions 12, 13, and 14, are inadequate in that: ... CAPA-00041 was initiated on July 20, 2022, to address ... and the Health Risk Assessment/Health Hazard Evaluation has been in draft form since January 3, 2023, and was still open during the current FDA inspection.
deficiencies.evidence: CAPA-00040 was initiated on May 13, 2022, after becoming aware of a software bug, indicates that your out-of-business firmware vendor had previously conducted a software code review, but no further documentation of such review is available, and no further root cause investigation is documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses document that you have implemented corrections to the specific instances detailed above. Your responses also indicate that you plan to implement additional corrective actions by updating related written procedures and work instructions, as well as implementing training.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the ECO-00089 Impact Assessment and FAI-3552, do not document that your firm evaluated the apparent risk of the continued use of the Rev B ... or evaluated to ensure that specified design requirements could continue to be met, as required per the CHANGE ORDER PROCESSING, Doc.ID: 410-0011-01, Rev: 17 and CHANGE CONTROL, Doc.ID: 410-0011-01, Rev: 13 procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm has no supporting evidence to demonstrate verification of the remaining inventory quantities of the Rev B ... nor does your firm have documentation to demonstrate how long the Rev B component continued to be used by your supplier/vendor after the implementation of ECO-00089.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses document that FAI-3552 has been updated to include a justification for the continued use of the Rev B ... component and that your firm planned to update the CHANGE ORDER PROCESSING, Doc.ID: 410-0011-01 on/around November 15, 2023.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your risk analysis is inadequate in that it was not updated as required per Risk Management Procedure, 410-0009-01, Revision: 17.
deficiencies.evidence: The Ivenix Large Volume Pump’s Software Failure Analysis, 550-0015-04, Rev 2.0, did not include the hazard of extended start-up time to achieve the stated flow rate accuracy of +/-5%, nor was it revised to include this hazard after becoming aware of a product defect that allowed the device to exhibit this issue when infusing fluids at a rate of >50 ml/hr. and <200 ml/hr., as documented and identified in CAPA-00038 and FAI-4446.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses document that your risk analysis has been updated and that your firm planned to update the related risk analysis procedures and provide training to affected personnel on/around ... .

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for the identification, documentation, validation, or where appropriate verification, review, and approval of design changes
deficiencies.description: Specifically, the design change documented in FAI-3552, which was meant to address an issue found in reliability testing of the Ivenix Large Volume Infusion Pumps, was not verified or validated as required by the verification requirements outlined in FAI-3552, or as required by your PROBLEM RESOLUTION PROCESS, 410-0053-01, Rev: 02 procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm planned to update your Change Order Processing and Defect Resolution procedures, on/around ... and ... respectively.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that you planned to update your Change Order Processing and Defect Resolution procedures, on/around ... and ... respectively.

deficiencies.title: Failure to report to us the information required by § 803.52
deficiencies.description: For example, the information included for Complaints CMT-00401, CMT-00429, CMT-00460, CMT-00477, CMT-00487, CMT-00493, CMT-00494, CMT-00511, CMT-00512, CMT-00519, and CMT-00535 reasonably suggests that your firm’s Ivenix Infusion System malfunctioned (i.e. loss of electrical function) while in use.
deficiencies.required_action: Your responses indicate that your firm conducted a retrospective review of complaints associated with all the malfunctions identified during the inspection and has submitted the newly identified MDRs. Furthermore, your firm committed to rectifying regulatory gaps in your complaint investigation process and are in the process of conducting a retrospective review of all complaints received since March 1, 2022, in alignment with the revised process.

conclusion: Our inspection also revealed that your firm’s Ivenix Infusion System is misbranded under section 502(t)(2) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(t)(2), in that your firm failed or refused to furnish material or information respecting the device that is required by or under Section 519 of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360i, and 21 CFR Part 803 - Medical Device Reporting.

deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: failure to perform adequate product evaluation and take appropriate corrective action after microbial contamination was recovered within the ISO 5 aseptic processing area; production of drug products while construction was underway without adequate controls to prevent contamination of the product environment and products.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the insanitary conditions and ensure sterility assurance

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Violations include failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, failure to maintain the buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of a drug product in a clean and sanitary condition, and failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: failure to investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications; failure to maintain the buildings used in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of a drug product in a clean and sanitary condition; failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the CGMP violations

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: failure to obtain an approved application for the drug products
deficiencies.required_action: Obtain an approved application for the drug products

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: The labeling fails to bear adequate directions for use, causing the drug products to be misbranded.

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA.

deficiencies.title: Your firm failed to conduct an adequate risk analysis as part of design validation, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(g).

deficiencies.description: The STIMULATE: Red Light Therapy Wrap is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, intended for use in the treatment of disease or other conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will “Reverse Penile Atrophy” and “maximize erection quality and size by boosting blood-flow, testosterone, and cellular repair” show an intent for use in the treatment of disease or other conditions.

deficiencies.description: The DEEP: The Ultimate Penis Extender is a device under section 201(h) of the Act, intended for use in the treatment of disease or other conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Claims or statements that indicate that this product will “Reverse shrinkage” and “straighten curvature” show an intent for use in the treatment of disease or other conditions.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to adhere to the requirements of the investigational plan, including calculating the volume of the investigational product administered to enrolled subjects according to their body weight and ensuring that subjects met all eligibility requirements before enrollment.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include but are not limited to: (a) Subject (b)(6)'s body weight was documented as 68.9 kg at study entry, but the calculated volume of IP received by this subject was more than two times the amount of IP that the subject should have received according to the protocol; and (b) Two subjects did not meet the protocol definition of being at 'higher' risk of progression to hospitalization or death.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective and preventive actions taken or planned include: (1) reporting the protocol deviations to the IRB; (2) requiring investigators to personally write prescriptions and review all source documents to ensure that subjects are given the correct dose of IP; (3) implementing quality checks of all source documents to ensure that the source documents are accurate and complete; (4) including a visit checklist on source documentation for all future studies, to ensure that all required assessments are completed and documented properly; (5) creating a detailed inclusion and exclusion criteria checklist for all future studies, to ensure that eligibility requirements are met; and (6) implementing an onboarding/training plan for all current and new site staff.

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain informed consent in accordance with the provisions of 21 CFR part 50
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain legally effective informed consent for two subjects before their enrollment in Protocol (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: Subjects (b)(6) and (b)(6) signed the 'Phase III Main Informed Consent Form' on January 17, 2022; however, neither subject signed the 'Informed Consent Form Addendum for Study Drugs'.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective and preventive actions taken or planned include: (1) submitting the deviation to the IRB; (2) contacting the subjects to come onsite to obtain the missed consent; (3) updating site informed consent standard operating procedures (SOPs) to include the use of a study-specific informed consent checklist and process document, as well as providing staff training on the updated SOPs; and (4) hiring a quality-control professional to manage IRB approval notifications and implementation.

conclusion: We emphasize that as the clinical investigator, you are responsible for ensuring that studies are conducted in accordance with the investigational plan, both to protect the rights, safety, and welfare of subjects and to ensure the integrity of study data. Your failure to conduct the clinical study in accordance with the protocol resulted in an overdose of the investigational product to a subject, as well as the enrollment of subjects who did not meet all eligibility requirements.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Medical Device Reporting/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written Medical Device Reporting (MDR) procedures
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no definitions of what your firm will consider to be a reportable event under 21 CFR Part 803. The exclusion of definitions from 21 CFR 803.3 for the terms “become aware,” “caused or contributed,” “malfunction,” “MDR reportable event,” and “serious injury,” and the definition for the term “reasonably suggests,” found in 803.20(c)(1) may lead your firm to make an incorrect reportability decision when evaluating a complaint that may meet the criteria for reporting under 21 CFR 803.50(a).
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish internal systems that provide for a standardized review process to determine when an event meets the criteria for reporting under this part
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not include instructions for conducting an investigation of each MDR reportable event and evaluating the cause of the event.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no instructions for conducting an investigation of each MDR reportable event and evaluating the cause of the event.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish internal systems that provide for timely transmission of complete medical device reports
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not include instructions for how to obtain and complete the FDA 3500A form, or specify who makes the decision for reporting events to FDA.
deficiencies.evidence: There are no instructions for how to obtain and complete the FDA 3500A form. The procedure does not specify who makes the decision for reporting events to FDA.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

deficiencies.title: Failure to describe how your firm will address documentation and recordkeeping requirements
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not include information that was evaluated to determine if an event was reportable, or documentation of the deliberations and decision-making processes used to determine if a device-related death, serious injury, or malfunction was or was not reportable.
deficiencies.evidence: There is no documentation of adverse event related information maintained as MDR event files. There is no information that was evaluated to determine if an event was reportable. There is no documentation of the deliberations and decision-making processes used to determine if a device-related death, serious injury, or malfunction was or was not reportable.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations, including an explanation of how your firm plans to prevent these violations, or similar violations, from occurring again.

conclusion: Your firm should investigate and determine the causes of any violations and take prompt actions to address any violations and bring the products into compliance.

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to establish and follow written procedures

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Deviations/Biologics License Application (BLA)

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Unlicensed Biological Product Violations
deficiencies.description: Your products are unapproved new drugs in violation of section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. § 355(a). Your products are also unlicensed biological products in violation of section 351(a)(1) of the Public Health Service Act (PHS Act), 42 U.S.C. § 262(a)(1).
deficiencies.evidence: Based on information and records reviewed by FDA, your products are intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease or conditions in humans.
deficiencies.required_action: You must cease introducing or delivering for introduction into interstate commerce any unapproved new drug or unlicensed biological product.

deficiencies.title: CGMP and/or CGTP Violations
deficiencies.description: FDA's inspection of your facility documented evidence of significant CGMP and CGTP violations.
deficiencies.evidence: The violations applicable to your products include, but are not limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: You must take prompt action to correct these violations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish a Quality Unit

deficiencies.evidence: The responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit are not in writing and followed, as required by 21 CFR 211.22(a) and 211.22(d).
deficiencies.required_action: You must establish a quality unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all labeling and drug products.

conclusion: In conclusion, your firm is responsible for ensuring that your products comply with CGMP requirements regardless of who manufactures, processes, packs, or holds them.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: Your products are unapproved new drugs in violation of section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. § 355(a).
deficiencies.evidence: Your website states that “Restor+ facilitates healing by secreting powerful bioactive molecules, creating an optimal environment for tissue repair and regeneration.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please submit your response in writing within fifteen (15) working days from your receipt of this letter...

deficiencies.title: Unlicensed Biological Products
deficiencies.description: Your products are unlicensed biological products in violation of section351(a) of the Public Health Service Act (PHS Act), 42 U.S.C. § 262(a)(1).
deficiencies.evidence: Your website states that “Restor+ facilitates healing by secreting powerful bioactive molecules, creating an optimal environment for tissue repair and regeneration.”
deficiencies.required_action: Please submit your response in writing within fifteen (15) working days from your receipt of this letter...

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet Criteria for Regulation as HCT/Ps
deficiencies.description: Your Restor+, Regain, and Renyte products fail to meet the minimal manipulation criterion set forth in 21 CFR 1271.10(a)(1) and defined for structural tissue in 21 CFR 1271.3(f)(1), because the processing alters the original relevant characteristics of the umbilical cord tissue related to its utility for reconstruction, repair, or replacement.
deficiencies.evidence: The processing of the umbilical cord from the form of a conduit into an injectable form, significantly alters the physical state of the HCT/P.
deficiencies.required_action: Please submit your response in writing within fifteen (15) working days from your receipt of this letter...

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet Criteria for Regulation as Biological Products
deficiencies.description: Your Restor+, Regain, and Renyte products are not regulated solely under section 361 of the PHS Act, 42 U.S.C. § 264, and the regulations in 21 CFR part 1271.
deficiencies.evidence: Your product is not intended solely to perform the same basic function or functions of the HCT/P in the recipient as in the donor (e.g., serving as a conduit for umbilical cord).
deficiencies.required_action: Please submit your response in writing within fifteen (15) working days from your receipt of this letter...

deficiencies.title: Additional Concerns
deficiencies.description: We have concerns about the following products: Restor Charge and Rexo Charge.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA’s review of your website and other records found that you market additional products, an umbilical cord derived product named Restor Charge and an exosome-based product named Rexo Charge.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a customer list for each of these products and indicate whether you believe you sell them in compliance with the FD&C Act. If so, include your explanation why; if not, include your plan for coming into compliance.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violation
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures disinfectants used to disinfect dialysate delivery systems and water purification systems used in hemodialysis (i.e. Diasol Bicarb, Diasol Concentrate, Diasol Additives, and CirtiSol Concentrate) for treatment of patients with renal failures or toxemic conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or an approved application for an investigational device exemption (IDE) under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g).
deficiencies.required_action: You were required to submit a premarket notification (510(k)) for your solutions of Citric Acid in 50% and 20% concentrations indicated for use in the cleaning, decalcification, and heat disinfection of hemodialysis machines.

deficiencies.title: Quality System Regulation Violation(s)
deficiencies.description: This inspection revealed that these devices are adulterated within the meaning of section 501(h) of the Act (21 U.S.C. § 351(h)), in that the methods used in, or the facilities or controls used for, their manufacture, packing, storage, or installation are not in conformity with the current good manufacturing practice requirements of the Quality System regulation found at Title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (CFR), Part 820.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm did not adequately establish procedures for finished device acceptance, as required by 21 CFR § 820.80(d).
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide documented evidence that your firm is committed to updating your DMRs to comply with the applicable standards.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately validate a process whose results cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test according to established procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm conducted a cleaning validation for the re-usable drums under Drum Cleaning Validation (Protocol #75.007, dated 09/04/2019). The reusable drums are cleaned with water using a prior to filling them with a new batch of acid concentrate after they are returned from your customers and stored in your warehouse for an undetermined amount of time.
deficiencies.evidence: The study did not demonstrate there are no chemical residues from the earlier batches to prevent cross contamination. These drums are used to package and distribute Diasol and Citrisol acid concentrates that are made with differing raw materials such as acetic acid and citric acid.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide justification demonstrating how weight verification substitutes for chemical testing requirements for glucose content or perform the required chemical testing.

conclusion: We understand that you entered into an agreement with to perform testing side-by-side with other labs such as and to determine the test result error for specific flagged batches, or to determine if it is your firm’s production error, with the electrolyte ranges not meeting the standard. Your firm should also recognize that your specification ranges are not compliant, instead of solely concentrating on determining if the laboratory testing method (method) is accurate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug product has the identity, strength, purity, and quality it purports or is represented to possess
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your written protocol, reflected in your Process Performance Qualification Report for Placental Collagen Matrix (PCM) (dated 2/20/23),is deficient in that there is no assessment of AXIOFILL™ identity, you have failed to identify tests to assess product quality, and your testing for purity is incomplete.
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by establishing adequate written procedures for production and process control

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications designed to assure that drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm has not established laboratory controls that include appropriate final specifications for testing and release of AXIOFILL™ to assure that each batch conforms to appropriate standards of identity and purity during routine production.
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by establishing laboratory controls with scientifically sound and appropriate specifications

deficiencies.title: 3. In-process control procedures are deficient in that your control procedures do not include bioburden testing
deficiencies.description: According to your Process Performance Qualification Report for Placental Collagen Matrix (PCM) (effective 2/20/23), your firm removed bioburden testing from routine commercial production of AXIOFILL™ after completing “validation production.” Your product is sterilized. In-process bioburden testing is critical for assuring that bioburden levels do not exceed the validated sterility assurance level (SAL).
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by including bioburden testing in your in-process control procedures

deficiencies.title: 4. Drug product samples are not representative of the entire batch
deficiencies.description: For example, samples of AXIOFILL™ tested for residual moisture as a measure of purity per your finished product specification are not representative of the entire batch. According to CSOP220.116, Product Visual Inspection (revision 54, effective 12/09/22), only [b](4) of each lot of your Placental Collagen Matrix product is collected for residual moisture testing. There are no considerations for batch size. This is not representative because, when products are lyophilized,moisture content can vary across the lot.
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by ensuring that drug product samples are representative of the entire batch

deficiencies.title: 5. Your firm has failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product using specific identity tests, if they exist
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm does not perform at least one test to verify the identity of each lot of “Placental Collagen Matrix (PCM) [b](4)” using the specific identity test that exists. Although your firm indicated that the test used to determine the concentration of PCM [b](4) is considered an identity test, only concentration results and not identity are reported to you by the contract laboratory. PCM [b](4) is used to manufacture AXIOFILL™.
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by conducting at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product using specific identity tests, if they exist

deficiencies.title: 6. Your firm failed to withhold each lot of components from use until the lot has been sampled, tested, or examined, as appropriate, and released for use by the quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your firm failed to withhold each lot of PCM [b](4) from use in AXIOFILL™ processing until the lot had been tested to assure compliance with your specification and released for use by your quality unit. According to your PCM [b](4) specification, SPC300.034 (effective 8/24/22), results of “composition” testing (also referred to as “concentration analysis”) are not required for release. Although your specification requires an investigation if results are failing, this procedure allows your use of components whose testing has not been reviewed prior to release, which creates the potential for your use of noncomplying components in drug processing.
deficiencies.required_action: Address the deficiency by withholding each lot of components from use until the lot has been sampled, tested, or examined, as appropriate, and released for use by the quality control unit

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Water System
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately validate your water production process to ensure the system was consistently producing water of appropriate quality for its intended use.
deficiencies.evidence: You did not provide supportive documentation for evaluation or adequate evidence of corrective actions taken to bring your operations into compliance with CGMP.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your water system design, control, and maintenance. A thorough remediation plan to install and operate a suitable water system. Include a robust ongoing control, maintenance, and monitoring program to ensure the remediated system design consistently produces water adhering to USP monograph specifications and appropriate microbial limits.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Process Validation
deficiencies.description: You have not validated the processes you use to manufacture your OTC drug products. You failed to ensure adequate documentation and control over critical processing steps used for your large-scale, weight-based manufacturing processes.
deficiencies.evidence: You distributed drug products with intra-batch potency variability, which indicated inadequate controls for mixing to ensure uniformity.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle. Describe your program for process performance qualification and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies
deficiencies.description: You failed to adequately investigate multiple microbiological out-of-limit (OOL) results from your water system used to manufacture topical drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your investigation did not include an adequate corrective action and preventive action plan (CAPA) to prevent the use of components with potentially objectionable microbial contamination from being used in the manufacture of your drug product.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification (OOS) results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your IVIZIA sterile lubricant eye drops product is an unapproved new drug introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d). In addition, this product is misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims from the product labeling and your website that provide evidence of the intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of this product as a drug include, but may not be limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your IVIZIA sterile lubricant eye drops product does not conform with the conditions for lawful marketing of an OTC ophthalmic demulcent drug product as set forth in M018.
deficiencies.evidence: According to your products labeling, your IVIZIA sterile lubricant eye drops is formulated with the active ingredients hyaluronic acid and trehalose. However, hyaluronic acid and trehalose are not active ingredients permitted for ophthalmic demulcent drug products under M018.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your IVIZIA sterile lubricant eye drops is misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee), because this product is a nonprescription drug subject to section 505G of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355h, but does not comply with the requirements for marketing under that section and is not the subject of an application approved under section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355.
deficiencies.evidence: This product is a nonprescription drug subject to section 505G of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355h, but does not comply with the requirements for marketing under that section and is not the subject of an application approved under section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Unapproved New Drug/Misbranded/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: The aseptic processes used to manufacture your product have not been validated since your firm's manufacturing operations for the subject product began in February 2020.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, the aseptic processes used to manufacture your product have not been validated (e.g., by performing media fill simulations) since your firm's manufacturing operations for the subject product began in February 2020. Your product purports to be sterile and is expected to be sterile.
deficiencies.required_action: To ensure the sterility of products purporting to be sterile, all aseptic and sterilization processes must be adequately validated in accordance with 21 CFR 211.113(b). Typically, aseptic process validation is performed using a media fill that includes exposing microbiological growth medium to product contact surfaces of equipment, container closure systems, critical environments, and process manipulations to closely simulate where the product is exposed to the environment during processing.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish laboratory controls that include scientifically sound and appropriate specifications, standards, sampling plans, and test procedures designed to assure that components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, labeling, and drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity
deficiencies.description: You have not established scientifically sound and appropriate specifications to assure that your product conforms to appropriate standards of identity.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, you have not established scientifically sound and appropriate specifications to assure that your product conforms to appropriate standards of identity. Your specification for finished product testing does not include a requirement to perform identity testing.
deficiencies.required_action: Documentation related to proposed release and stability tests using these results have not been provided for review. Further, you have not described any actions to be taken for violative product on the market that remains within the labeled expiry.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish a written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products and to use results of such stability testing to determine appropriate storage conditions and expiration dates
deficiencies.description: Your certificate of analysis for your product states that the expiry period is [insert date], while your instructions for use (IFU) labels your product as having a '[insert date]'.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, your certificate of analysis for your product states that the expiry period is [insert date], while your IFU states that your product has a '[insert date]'. Neither of these statements about the stability of your product are supported with stability data.
deficiencies.required_action: A suitable expiration date, supported with data, is needed to ensure product quality over the shelf life of the product.

deficiencies.title: Misbranding Violation
deficiencies.description: Your product is also misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(a), because your labeling is false or misleading.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically, the IFU for your product indicates that '[insert description]'. This description implies that your product is provided as [insert description] when it is actually a [insert description].
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide your revised IFU for review. Further, you have not described any actions to be taken for violative product on the market that contain these errors and are within labeled expiry.

conclusion: Neither this letter nor the observations noted on the Form FDA-483 are intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies that may exist at your facility. It is your responsibility to ensure full compliance with the FD&C Act, PHS Act, and all applicable regulations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B, including compounding drug products using bulk drug substances that are not eligible for use in compounding under section 503B.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your facility compounded drug products using bulk drug substances that are not eligible for the use in compounding under section 503B, including 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium iodide (5-Amino-1MQ) and nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+). Drug products compounded using 5-Amino-1MQ and NAD+ are not eligible for the exemptions provided by section 503B, because 5-Amino-1MQ and NAD+ do not appear on the 503B bulks list and are not used to compound a drug that appears on the drug shortage list.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. An operator blocked first air by placing gloved hands directly over open sterile containers.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: CGMP violations at your facility, that caused your drug products to be adulterated within the meaning of section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: 1. Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes (21 CFR 211.113(b)).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

conclusion: Because your compounded drug products have not met all of the conditions of section 503B, they are not eligible for the exemptions in that section from the FDA approval requirements of section 505, the requirement under section 502(f)(1) that labeling bear adequate directions for use, and the Drug Supply Chain Security Act requirements described in section 582 of the FDCA.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your Revitalize Energizing Eye Drops is an unapproved new drug introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims from the product labeling, your website, and your social media site posts that provide evidence intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of this product as a drug include...
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Revitalize Energizing Eye Drops is misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, because the “FDA” logo is displayed on your product label and website as “FDA COMPLIANT” (“Passed All Required FDA testing”), “MADE IN A FDA REGISTERED FACILITY” (“Trusted Manufacturing”), and “FDA GRAS APPROVED” (“Formulated within FDA Guidance”).
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within fifteen working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive statement of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: This is a repeat violation noted in previous inspections. Your response states that you will outline the control procedures to ensure that manufacturing processes are properly validated. You also state that you will follow your current standard operating procedure (SOP) for data collection on the drug products you manufacture.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to follow an adequate written testing program designed to assess the stability characteristics of drug products
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test an adequate number of drug product batches as defined by your written stability procedure.
deficiencies.evidence: This is a repeat violation noted in previous inspections. Your response states that you will continue placing batches of drug product on stability and commit to updating your stability procedure based on market demands.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment and corrective action and preventive action (CAPA) plan to ensure the adequacy of your stability program.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure of quality control unit to exercise its responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality control unit failed to exercise its authority and responsibilities, including but not limited to, implementing effective procedures and conducting adequate oversight of the drug products you manufacture.
deficiencies.evidence: For example, your QU failed to ensure: Adequate procedures for change controls, batch record release and CAPA. Adequate review of control records (i.e., finished product testing) prior to release and distribution. Appropriate procedures are established and followed for the cleaning and maintenance of your drug manufacturing equipment.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

conclusion: Based on the above labeling evidence, (b)(4) and (b)(4) are intended for use as combination topical first aid antiseptic and external analgesic drug products, among other intended uses. As described below, these drug products are unapproved new drugs marketed in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.weightcrunchshop.com offers semaglutide drug products marketed as “Orsema 0.25 mg” injectable and “Orsema 0.50 mg” injectable, which are not approved in the U.S.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA-approved versions of semaglutide on the market in the U.S., but no approved drug applications for “Orsema 0.25 mg” or “Orsema 0.50 mg” offered by www.weightcrunchshop.com
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.weightcrunchshop.com offers prescription drugs for sale without a prescription, including semaglutide drug products marketed as “Ozempic 0.25 mg,” “Ozempic 0.5 mg,” and “Ozempic 1 mg”
deficiencies.evidence: FDA-approved product Ozempic has been approved for marketing by FDA and is limited by a new drug application to use under the supervision of a practitioner licensed to administer such drugs
deficiencies.required_action: Cease offering misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers

deficiencies.title: Dispensing Prescription Drugs Without a Prescription
deficiencies.description: www.weightcrunchshop.com offers oxycodone 15 mg and 30 mg tablets described as “moderately potent opioid analgesic, generally indicated for the relief of moderate to severe pain” without a prescription
deficiencies.evidence: FDA-approved oxycodone is indicated for the management of pain severe enough to require an opioid analgesic and for which alternative treatments are inadequate and is only available pursuant to a prescription from a licensed practitioner
deficiencies.required_action: Cease dispensing prescription drugs without a prescription

conclusion: FDA requests that www.weightcrunchshop.com cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple instances where required finished drug product samples were either not taken or taken but not appropriately tested
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: You lacked appropriate sampling and testing to ensure your drug products conform to appropriate standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.evidence: Multiple instances where required finished drug product samples were either not taken or taken but not appropriately tested
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conduct Identity Testing on Incoming Components
deficiencies.description: You failed to conduct adequate identity testing on incoming components, including APIs, used in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to perform adequate identity testing on each shipment of each lot using the USP identification test that detects DEG or EG
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Misbranded/OTC

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your EYELIVIO lubricant eye ointment product is an unapproved new drug introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of sections 505(a) and 301(d) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. 355(a) and 331(d). In addition, this product is misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee).
deficiencies.evidence: Examples of claims from the EYELIVIO lubricant eye ointment product labeling, your website and your Instagram site listed above, that provide evidence of the intended use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) of this product include, but may not be limited to, the following:
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within 15 working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your EYELIVIO lubricant eye ointment drug product does not conform to the conditions specified in M018 for the reasons described below.
deficiencies.evidence: The botanical extracts (Aloe vera, Arnica montana, and Calendula officinalis) included in this formulation are not active ingredients permitted for ophthalmic emollient (lubricant) drug products under M018.14. Additionally, your EYELIVIO lubricant eye ointment fails to include the amounts (concentrations) for the permitted active ingredients, mineral oil and white petrolatum, on the product label/labeling.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within 15 working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Violations
deficiencies.description: Your EYELIVIO lubricant eye ointment drug product is misbranded under section 502(ee) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 352(ee), because this product is a nonprescription drug subject to section 505G of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355h, but does not comply with the requirements for marketing under that section and is not the subject of an application approved under section 505 of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. 355.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify FDA in writing, within 15 working days of receipt of this letter, of the specific steps you have taken to correct any violations.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that may exist in connection with your products. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to investigate all critical deviations
deficiencies.description: Approximately 20 percent of bioreactor runs attempted between January 2022 and July 2024 were rejected for contamination or other quality failures. This rate is excessive and calls into question the state of control of your process.
deficiencies.evidence: A. Two lots of (b)(4) and three lots of (b)(4) were out-of-specification (OOS) for appearance due to the presence of (b)(4) particles and were not adequately investigated. B. Three in-process high-pressure events associated with product leakage during the transfer of bulk (b)(4) through a (b)(4) to (b)(4) L shipping bags were not adequately investigated.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an update on your investigation into reducing particle load in your process, including an updated control and removal strategy, and provide a comprehensive assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, OOS results, and failures.

deficiencies.title: Failure to demonstrate that your manufacturing process can reproducibly manufacture an API meeting its predetermined quality attributes
deficiencies.description: You repeatedly deviated from your validated manufacturing process for the production of (b)(4).
deficiencies.evidence: A. You performed (b)(4) of the (b)(4) during (b)(4) of the (b)(4) bioreactor runs while the manufacturing process was validated with (b)(4). B. Your manufacturing personnel were observed to use a written work instruction (Pre-Job Briefing) describing itself as “… not a GMP document/process…” to deviate from your validated manufacturing procedure by replacing the (b)(4) with (b)(4) L shipping bag.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an assessment of each drug production process to ensure that there is a data-driven and scientifically sound program that identifies and controls all sources of variability, and provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle.

deficiencies.title: Failure to have equipment of the appropriate design and suitability for their intended use for the manufacture of APIs
deficiencies.description: Your firm used equipment unsuitable for its intended use in microbiologically controlled manufacturing environments.
deficiencies.evidence: A. Mobile carts used in the setup of (b)(4) units required operators to get down to the floor and manually lock and unlock the cart brakes despite previously determining equipment proximity to the floor as a contributing root cause in microbiological contamination events. B. (b)(4) used to establish temporary sterile boundaries for tubing continue to be used despite being identified in a June 2023 microbial contamination event investigation as possessing a “design flaw” that may allow microbial ingress.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide an assessment of each piece of equipment and facility to ensure that it is suitable for its intended use, and provide a detailed program for designing, validating, maintaining, controlling and monitoring each of your manufacturing processes.

metadata.company_name: Fresenius Medical Care AG & Co. KGaA
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive actions
deficiencies.description: Specifically, your Corrective Action and Preventive Action, Procedure No. S100016-03, Rev E. Dated January 30, 2023, is inadequate in that: ... You did not take a corrective and preventative action until almost 2-years after you learned of a potential issue impacting your Hemodialysis Machines that contain peroxide crosslinked silicone tubing.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on the status of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to document all activities and their results required under this section (Corrective and preventive action)
deficiencies.description: Specifically: CAPA 1100660 dated June 15, 2022, opened for the PCB related compounds issue failed to adequately document all the activities you performed after you became aware of PCB related compounds finding.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on the status of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for validating the device design
deficiencies.description: Specifically, Design Validation Procedure, S100021-13, was not followed when conducting retrospective design validation of your 2008T, 2008 K and 2008 K2 Hemodialysis Machines to determine if the devices can be used safely and effectively by the intended users.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on the status of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to report a correction or removal
deficiencies.description: Specifically, you initiated a field action starting on November 25, 2022, for your 2008 Hemodialysis Machines by going to customers’ sites to change the silicone tubing associated with PCBA related compounds leaching.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide an update on the status of this CAPA.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Investigational Device Exemptions (IDE)/Premarket Approval Application (PMA)

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain FDA approval prior to implementing a change to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to obtain FDA approval for changes to an investigational plan that required FDA approval prior to implementation and no exception applies to these circumstances.
deficiencies.evidence: Review of your “Emergency/Compassionate Use Requests” log indicates that investigational devices, specifically Seraph 100 devices, were shipped to (b)(4), (b)(6) Hospital on April 29, 2024, for Emergency/Compassionate Use (CU).
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions should include a plan to conduct a complete accounting of all investigational devices distributed to (b)(4), (b)(6) Hospital and other entities listed on the “Emergency/Compassionate Use Request” log.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure proper monitoring of the investigation and failure to secure clinical investigator’s compliance with investigational plans
deficiencies.description: A sponsor is responsible for ensuring proper monitoring of an investigation. As part these monitoring responsibilities, a sponsor is responsible for securing its clinical investigator’s compliance with the investigational plan.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection on May 30, 2025, your firm was asked to provide documentation demonstrating that informed consent was obtained from enrolled subjects for the Purify Study (IDE G210074) and Oscar Study (IDE G230144) and that the required monitoring procedures were conducted. However, your firm was unable to provide evidence of verification and attestation that informed consent was signed for enrolled subjects and that monitoring procedures were followed.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions should include a plan to ensure proper monitoring of the investigation and secure clinical investigator’s compliance with investigational plans.

conclusion: This letter also requests prompt corrective action to address the violations cited and discussed in your written responses dated June 25, 2025, July 25, 2025, and September 15, 2025.

metadata.company_name: Pamela K. Den Besten, DDS, MS
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator (Sponsor)

deficiencies.title: Failed to submit an Investigational New Drug application (IND) for the conduct of a clinical investigation with an investigational new drug that is subject to 21 CFR 312.2(a)
deficiencies.description: You failed to submit an IND before conducting Protocol 15-18297, in which 43 subjects were enrolled and received study drug between September 2016 and January 2020.
deficiencies.evidence: FDA records indicate that you failed to submit an IND before conducting the clinical investigation of Livionex under Protocol 15-18297
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failed to determine whether IND regulations apply to future clinical investigations of products regulated by FDA
deficiencies.description: Your response is inadequate because you did not include sufficient information about how you will determine whether IND regulations apply to future clinical investigations of products regulated by FDA.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated in your written response that it was your understanding at the time that all products used in this study were lawfully marketed, and that the study met all IND exemption criteria.
deficiencies.required_action: You should notify this office in writing of the actions you have taken to prevent similar violations in the future.

conclusion: This letter notifies you of our findings and provides you with an opportunity to address the deficiencies noted above. Within 15 business days of your receipt of this letter, you should notify this office in writing of the actions you have taken to prevent similar violations in the future.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to clean, maintain, and, as appropriate for the nature of the drug, sanitize and/or sterilize equipment and utensils at appropriate intervals to prevent malfunctions or contamination that would alter the safety, identity, strength, quality, or purity of the drug product beyond the official or other established requirements.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm manufactures topical over the counter (OTC) hand sanitizer drug products. You also manufacture commercial industrial chemical products, labeled with warnings such as “cause severe skin burns” and “may cause allergic skin reactions,” on the same manufacturing equipment as your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including for identity and strength must be performed prior to batch release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to have, for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory determination of satisfactory conformance to final specifications for the drug product, including the identity and strength of each active ingredient, prior to release.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm failed to adequately test your drug products for the identity and strength of the active ingredient, benzalkonium chloride (BZK), prior to release and distribution.
deficiencies.required_action: Full release testing, including for identity and strength must be performed prior to batch release and distribution. Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that your drug products conform to appropriate specifications before release.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to conduct an identity test on each shipment of each lot of components used in the manufacture of your drug products, (e.g., BZK). You also failed to determine whether each component conformed with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality before using them.
deficiencies.required_action: Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that the components conform to appropriate specifications before use in the manufacture of your drug products.

conclusion: The violations cited in this letter are not intended to be an all-inclusive list of violations that exist at your facility. You are responsible for investigating and determining the causes of any violations and for preventing their recurrence or the occurrence of other violations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not maintain documentation of validation for the following processes: Ethylene Oxide (EO) sterilization of the probe cover kit containing the sterile gel pouch, Formulation calculation software for mixing of the ultrasound gel, and Cleaning of the mixing tank equipment used for ultrasound gel manufacture.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided four updates corresponding to the listed examples regarding process validation. However, the response is inadequate because your firm did not provide adequate records of the gel lots used during the sterilization validation, and there is no evidence of procedure effectiveness and documentation of training on the new procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Please notify this office in writing within fifteen business days from the date you receive this letter of the specific steps your firm has taken to address the noted violations...

deficiencies.title: Failure to identify by suitable means the acceptance status of the product, to indicate the conformance or nonconformance of product with acceptance criteria
deficiencies.description: Your firm had several pallets of in-process product held next to finished products in an area designated for finished sterile product ready for release. Additionally, the sterility status of product was not identified (e.g., stickers affixed directly to finished product).
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided an updated warehouse layout where you proposed storage of sterilized semi-finished products and stated that “labeling on the rack is corrected immediately”. However, the response is inadequate because no evidence of the labeling on the rack was provided, and there is no documentation demonstrating personnel were trained on the updated layout and procedure.

deficiencies.title: Quality Control Unit failed to exercise its responsibility
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit (QU) failed to adequately document and investigate multiple out-of-specification (OOS) results.

deficiencies.title: Appropriate procedures are not followed by your QU

deficiencies.evidence: However, this batch was reworked in March 2022 without a pre-approved rework protocol, as required by your procedure. The rework conducted was not contemporaneously documented.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively exercise its responsibility.

deficiencies.title: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: A comprehensive investigation into the extent of the inaccuracies in data records and reporting including results of the data review for drugs distributed in the United States.
