metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Medical Device/Adulterated/Misbranded/Lacks PMA and/or 510(k)

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not implemented design controls for your products, including the Inverness Home Ear Piercing Kits, Ear Care Antiseptic, and Ear Care Solution.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, these records were requested, and your Quality Control Manager affirmed that your firm has not established any design controls or developed design plans for your firm's products. Specifically: a. Your firm defines requirements in your Design Control procedure, SOP-003, Ver. 0, 10/10/2024 for design and development planning, design input, design output, design verification, design validation, design transfer, design review, and design history for your products, but does not maintain design documents that support these established requirements, including any documentation of design control requirements from the original design of the products. b. Your firm has not addressed expiration dating as part of the design controls.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm's response (CAPA-25-004) is inadequate because it provides only partial coverage of the design control violations and lacks implementation specifics. While the CAPA acknowledges the need for design controls, it does not comprehensively address all identified deficiencies and fails to detail how each aspect of the design control process will be implemented.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test that the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not validated the manufacturing process and equipment used to produce Inverness Ear Care Solution and Ear Care Antiseptic.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically: a. Equipment used to establish and to monitor critical parameters such as tank fill weight, water quality, raw material weight, mixing speed, and mixing times is not validated. b. Your firm's procedure, SOP-021, requires determination of process variables and that they be understood and controlled through validation, e.g., batch size. However, three different batch sizes of Ear Care Antiseptic Batch (Lot # [redacted] at [redacted] lbs., Lot # [redacted] at [redacted] lbs., and Lot# [redacted] at [redacted] lbs.) were produced without establishing critical process parameters through validation for each batch size, to consistently meet predetermined specifications.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm's response (CAPA-25-007) is inadequate because it lacks specificity and essential documentation. The CAPA should be revised to comprehensively address this design control violation, including specific implementation plans for each design control element; establish clear procedures for expiration date documentation and validation; and demonstrate how SOP-003 compliance will be achieved and maintained throughout your design processes.

conclusion: We conclude that your firm's products are adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (the Act), 21 U.S.C. § 351(f)(1)(B), because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360e(a), or an approved application for an investigational device exemption under section 520(g) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360j(g). Additionally, your firm's products are misbranded under section 502(o) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(o), because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the products into interstate commerce for commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to record and justify any deviations from required laboratory control mechanisms
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to record and justify any deviations from required laboratory control mechanisms.
deficiencies.evidence: Numerous non-viable dark particles were observed in (b)(4) plates used for microbiological analyses in your laboratory.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile.
deficiencies.evidence: The presence of excessive smoke prevented visualization of airflows.
deficiencies.required_action: An independent assessment of the training provided to personnel responsible for the execution of airflow study protocols. An independent assessment of the CAPA effectiveness in response to previous adverse findings associated with your conduct of airflow studies.

deficiencies.title: 3. Failure to establish an adequate system for maintaining equipment used to control the aseptic conditions
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate system for maintaining equipment used to control the aseptic conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Rough metal surfaces and an incomplete (b)(4) were observed on the Line (b)(4) Restricted Access Barrier System (RABS) framework in the area above the stopper loading chute.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment of all lines and equipment therein to ensure appropriate design and suitability of materials of construction and equipment.

deficiencies.title: 4. Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications.
deficiencies.evidence: Neither of the scientific publication sources cited in your investigation support your root cause.
deficiencies.required_action: A scientifically sound investigation into the tip blockage issue. The investigation should identify root cause(s), CAPA, and the mechanism(s) for determining CAPA effectiveness.

deficiencies.title: 5. Failure to establish an adequate quality unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality unit.
deficiencies.evidence: The responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit are not in writing and fully followed.
deficiencies.required_action: An independent evaluation of your product quality complaint handling procedures and practices including relevant procedures and practices used by contracted third parties that perform complaint handling activities on your behalf.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies. Based on this review, provide a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Compounded Drug Products Under the FDCA
deficiencies.description: The FDA investigators noted that drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions, whereby they may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to health, causing your drug products to be adulterated under section 501(a)(2)(A) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Your firm failed to perform adequate smoke studies under dynamic conditions to demonstrate unidirectional airflow within the ISO 5 area. Therefore, your products intended to be sterile are produced in an environment that may not provide adequate protection against the risk of contamination.
2. Production areas have difficult to clean or contain porous, particle generating, or visibly dirty equipment or surfaces.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions appear inadequate

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: The FDA investigators noted that your firm released and distributed drug products with strengths that differed from their labeled amounts.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Your Fluorescein 2% Ophthalmic Solution was subpotent (lots # 01242025@, potency of 85.865% and 03272025@, potency of 76.1475%).
2. Your Tirzepatide 17mg/mL / Glycine 5mg/mL / Methylcobalamin 5mg/mL Injection (lot # 04072025@) failed sterility testing.
3. Your Semaglutide 2.5mg/mL Injection was subpotent (lot # 03042025@, potency of 79.876%) and your Tirzepatide 10mg/mL Injection was subpotent (lots # 01172025@, potency of 86.779% and 02112025@, potency of 89.13%).
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions appear inadequate

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: The strength of compounded drugs, for example your Fluorescein 2% Ophthalmic Solution, Semaglutide 2.5mg/mL Injection, and Tirzepatide 10mg/mL Injection, differed from and fell below their labeled strength.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. The labeling of these drug products is false or misleading.
2. These drug products are misbranded under section 502(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.required_action: Corrective actions appear inadequate

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears that you produced drug products that violate the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA).

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: Failure to comply with section 704(a) of the FD&C Act
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to provide requested records and information within a reasonable timeframe, despite multiple extensions and follow-up requests.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide the requested information and records

deficiencies.title: Refusal to permit access to or copying of any record required by section 704(a) of the FD&C Act
deficiencies.description: Your firm's failure to provide requested records and information, despite multiple extensions and follow-up requests, constitutes a refusal to permit access to or copying of any record required by section 704(a) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide the requested information and records

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to control the design of the device
deficiencies.description: Your firm has not established procedures for design controls and was unable to provide records demonstrating conformance with design input, design output, design review, design verification, design validation, design transfer, design changes, and design history file for the surgical N95 respirators (Model DE2322) and surgical masks (Model FE2311 and Model FE2311B).
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm did not provide procedures for design controls for surgical N95 respirator model DE2322 or surgical mask models FE2311 and 2311B. Your firm indicated that it had a “design control program file” approved December 30, 2019 but only provided design history file for surgical N95 respirator model DE2322.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must establish procedures for design controls and provide records demonstrating conformance with design input, design output, design review, design verification, design validation, design transfer, design changes, and design history file for the surgical N95 respirators (Model DE2322) and surgical masks (Model FE2311 and Model FE2311B).

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate with a high degree of assurance and approve according to established procedures
deficiencies.description: Process Validation records were requested for the following processes that are used for manufacturing surgical N95 respirators and surgical masks: automated elastic cutting machines, automated elastic-stapler machines, automated stapler machines, automated metal nose piece attachment machines, semi-automated stapler machines, individual mask package sealer machines, and elastic band pull test. However, your firm was unable to provide records demonstrating where the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process is validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedures.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided IQ/OQ/PQ reports for the semi-automated face mask production machines used for manufacturing surgical N95 respirators and surgical masks; however, your firm was unable to provide a rationale for the sample size used during testing. Additionally, the validation reports were not dated and signed by the individual approving the validation.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must validate with a high degree of assurance and approve according to established procedures all processes that are used for manufacturing surgical N95 respirators and surgical masks.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain Device Master Records (DMRs)
deficiencies.description: Device Master Records (DMRs) for N95 respirator mask or the single-use surgical mask were requested; however, your firm was unable to produce these records for review.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm provided a DMR for surgical N95 respirator model DE2326. However, your firm stated during the inspection that only model DE2322 was manufactured and shipped for the U.S. market. A DMR was not provided for surgical N95 respirator model DE2322 or any of the surgical masks.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must maintain Device Master Records (DMRs) for all devices, including N95 respirator mask and single-use surgical mask.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish procedures for receiving, reviewing and evaluating complaints
deficiencies.description: Your firm’s Customer Feedback Procedure, No. MSP-20-D23-009, Rev. A/0, dated 2020-March-28, does not include a process for determining whether a complaint represents an event which is required to be reported to FDA under part 803, Medical Device Reporting.
deficiencies.evidence: You stated that you have modified the customer feedback procedure to “improve the missing content of the program file”. However, no documentation was provided for FDA to evaluate. In addition, you did not indicate whether there will be a retrospective review to determine if any old complaints needed to be reported as MDRs and whether there will be training on the new procedure.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must establish procedures for receiving, reviewing and evaluating complaints and ensure that all information reasonably known to you is submitted for each event.

deficiencies.title: Failure to develop, maintain, and implement written Medical Device Reporting (MDR) procedures
deficiencies.description: The document titled “Procedure for the Control of Advisory Notifications and Adverse Event Reporting for Medical Device Products”, MSP-20-D23-006, Ver. A/1, dated 5/11/2020, as your firm’s MDR procedure does not contain information that would indicate that it is an MDR procedure created in accordance with the requirements in 21 CFR 803.17.
deficiencies.evidence: The document does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements, as required by 21 CFR 803.17(a)(1).
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm must develop, maintain, and implement written Medical Device Reporting (MDR) procedures that comply with the requirements in 21 CFR 803.17.

conclusion: The adequacy of your firm’s response cannot be determined at this time.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: 1. Failure to conduct laboratory testing for each batch of drug product
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct for each batch of drug product, appropriate laboratory testing, as necessary, required to be free of objectionable microorganisms.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: A list of chemical and microbiological specifications, including test methods, used to analyze each batch of your drug products before batch disposition decisions.

deficiencies.title: 2. Failure to conduct identity testing for each component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct at least one test to verify the identity of each component of a drug product.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to perform adequate identity testing of each component lot used in the manufacture of your OTC drug products, such as (b)(4) and (b)(4).
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent review of your material system to determine whether all suppliers of components, containers, and closures, are each qualified and the materials are assigned appropriate expiration or retest dates.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test incoming component lots for conformity with specifications
deficiencies.description: Without adequate testing, you do not have scientific evidence that components conform to appropriate specifications prior to use in the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: You failed to adequately test each shipment of each lot of (b)(4) for identity, a component at high-risk of (b)(4) or (b)(4) contamination.
deficiencies.required_action: A description of how you will test each component lot for conformity with all appropriate specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Unapproved New Drugs/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug and Unlicensed Biological Product Violations
deficiencies.description: Your product is an unapproved new drug in violation of section 505(a) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), 21 U.S.C. § 355(a). Your introduction or delivery for introduction of your product into interstate commerce is prohibited under section 301(d) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. § 331(d).
deficiencies.evidence: Your website (https://xobiologix.co/) and promotional material obtained during your inspection states that MaviX™ is intended for use in the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease or conditions in humans and/or is intended to affect the structure or function of the body.
deficiencies.required_action: You must ensure that all phases of the production of your product comply with CGMP requirements regardless of who manufactures, processes, packs, or holds it.

deficiencies.title: Current Good Manufacturing Practice Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm is responsible for ensuring that all phases of the production of your product comply with CGMP requirements regardless of who manufactures, processes, packs, or holds it.
deficiencies.evidence: Refer to Warning Letter number CBER 25-679023 for discussion of CGMP violations.
deficiencies.required_action: You must ensure that all phases of the production of your product comply with CGMP requirements regardless of who manufactures, processes, packs, or holds it.

deficiencies.title: Misbranding Violation
deficiencies.description: Your product is also misbranded under section 502(a) of the FD&C Act, 21 U.S.C. § 352(a), because your labeling is false or misleading.
deficiencies.evidence: The instructions for use (IFU) for your product indicates that “the tissue is processed and may be cut into predetermined size patches using sterile cutting tools.” This description implies that your product is provided as sheets when it is actually a liquid solution.
deficiencies.required_action: You must correct the labeling of your product to ensure that it does not contain false or misleading representations about its stability, composition, or intended use.

conclusion: Neither this letter nor the observations noted on the Form FDA-483, List of Inspectional Observations that was issued to (b)(4), are intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies that may exist. It is your responsibility to ensure full compliance with the FD&C Act, PHS Act, and all applicable regulations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to perform operations within specifically defined areas
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to perform operations within specifically defined areas of adequate size and to have separate or defined areas or such other control systems necessary to prevent contamination or mix-ups.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive, independent assessment of the design and control of your firm's manufacturing operations with a detailed and thorough review of all equipment, and other active ingredient, cross-contamination hazards.

deficiencies.title: Failure to clean, maintain, and sanitize equipment
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure your cleaning procedures are adequate to prevent cross-contamination of OTC drug products with the potent active ingredients (e.g., hormones).
deficiencies.required_action: A risk assessment for all drug products you have previously produced on shared equipment. For each product, assess the risk of potential contamination due to the shared equipment.

deficiencies.title: Failure to test batches of drug product
deficiencies.description: You released finished drug products without adequate testing. For example, you did not perform adequate microbiological monitoring and chemical testing on your finished drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish an adequate quality unit
deficiencies.description: You lacked adequate quality unit (QU) oversight for the manufacture of your drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent assessment of your quality control unit's responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit are not in writing and fully followed.

metadata.company_name: Janssen Vaccines Corp., A Johnson & Johnson Company
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Thoroughly Investigate Unexplained Discrepancies or Failures
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, and complaints. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

deficiencies.title: Failure of Quality Unit to Ensure Compliance with CGMP
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit failed to exercise its responsibility to ensure drug products manufactured are in compliance with CGMP, and meet established specifications for identity, strength, quality, and purity.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective review of product quality complaints received within the last 3 years for all Janssen products to identify any circumstances in which your firm did not submit a FAR or BPDR, as applicable, when such a report should have been submitted.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Submit Biological Product Deviation Reports (BPDRs) in a Timely Manner
deficiencies.description: Your quality unit does not consistently follow your procedures for the submission of regulatory notification of product quality defects.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective review of product quality complaints received within the last 3 years for all Janssen products to identify any circumstances in which your firm did not submit a FAR or BPDR, as applicable, when such a report should have been submitted.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: I. Drug Manufacturing Operations
deficiencies.description: Your facility is registered under section 510 of the FD&C ACT and manufactures multiple drug products pursuant to New Drug Applications (NDA) and Abbreviated New Drug Applications (ANDA).
deficiencies.evidence: CGMP violations at your facility, that caused your drug products to be adulterated within the meaning of section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FD&C Act.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS (including in-process and release/stability testing) results for US products currently in the U.S. market and within expiry as of the date of this letter

deficiencies.title: 1. Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed (21 CFR 211.192).
deficiencies.description: You invalidated, without an adequate root cause investigation, failing growth promotion results, which are used to determine suitability of media used for sterility testing of finished drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Your investigation concluded there was no product impact because retesting of the media ultimately achieved passing results. Your investigation documented human performance as the cause of the failing testing events for (b)(4), lot (b)(4), and (b)(4) other media lots; however, no specific human error was identified.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a retrospective review of all investigations and train personnel

deficiencies.title: 2. Your firm failed to ensure that laboratory records included complete data derived from all tests necessary to ensure compliance with established specifications and standards (21 CFR 211.194(a)).
deficiencies.description: You failed to accurately document microbiology testing results. In addition, test results are not verified by a second person for accuracy.
deficiencies.evidence: On November 6, 2024, your analyst recorded fewer colony forming units for active air environmental monitoring samples than what was observed by our investigator during the examination.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices, procedures, methods, equipment, documentation, and analyst competencies

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: SOP10-001, Version 6 (v6), titled “Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA)” dated, February 2, 2023, describes the procedures and documentation for implementing preventive actions.
deficiencies.evidence: No documents were provided as evidence that the three CAPAs noted during the inspection were corrected nor were evidence or training provided to ensure that the procedures for implementing corrective and preventive actions are adequate or have been implemented.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide evidence that CAPA record CP2019/017 was corrected and that the corrective actions were verified or validated. Your firm should also provide a copy of their revised and new documents and evidence of training. Further, your firm should perform a retrospective review to ensure that other CAPAs were appropriately handled.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that formal documented reviews of the design results are planned and conducted at appropriate stages of the device's design development
deficiencies.description: SOP12-002 Version 7 (v7), titled “New Product Design and Development” dated May 2022, describes your firm’s procedures for design review.
deficiencies.evidence: Records of design reviews for the TransFix/EDTA Vacuum Blood Collection Tubes were requested; however, Mrs. Nicki Kaenzig, Chief Scientific Officer/Management Representative, stated that the results of the design review have not been maintained.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a copy of the revised procedure for review and evidence of training. Your firm should provide their plans to remediate the TransFix/EDTA Vacuum Blood Collection Tubes DHF and their plan to ensure other DHFs are complete.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that the device design is correctly translated into production specification
deficiencies.description: SOP12-002 Version 7 (v7), titled “New Product Design and Development” dated May 2022, describes your firm’s procedures for design transfer.
deficiencies.evidence: Design transfer records for the TransFix/EDTA Vacuum Blood Collection Tubes were requested; however, Mrs. Nicki Kaenzig, Chief Scientific Officer/Management Representative, stated that no design transfer records were created as part of the Design History File.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a copy of the revised procedure for review and evidence of training. Your firm should provide their plans to ensure that the TransFix/EDTA Vacuum Blood Collection Tubes were correctly translated into production and their plan to ensure other devices were correctly translated in addition to evidence of implementation.

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, that the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedures
deficiencies.description: Your firm uses a (b)(4) process for packing and labeling of TransFix Vacuum Blood Collection Tubes.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, the process validation records were requested; however, Mrs. Nicki Kaenzig, Chief Scientific Officer/Management Representative, stated that you have chosen to verify the process via a visual check.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should include the validation protocols and final reports for the heat sealing process. The protocols should contain or refer to objective and measurable acceptance criteria and describe how appropriate statistical method for data collection and analysis are used.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that equipment is routinely calibrated, inspected, checked, and maintained
deficiencies.description: PLAN-VAL-005v3, titled “Validation of the (b)(4) Flow Cytometer for use in the QC of CE/IVD TVTs and FDA cleared TVTs for the US Market”, dated Feb 2020, describes procedures to validate the (b)(4) Flow Cytometer for use the Quality Control testing of CE/IVD TVTs and FDA-cleared TVTs.
deficiencies.evidence: During the inspection, validation records were requested, and the records showed that the “Reviewer/Approver” signatures had not been signed. Further, on May 8, 2014 (b)(4) performed onsite maintenance of instrument and reported to your firm that the instrument is run in a room with “no or little air conditioning” which may affect the instrument.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a copy of the (b)(4) Flow Cytometer qualification protocol and report. Your firm should also provide their corrective action to ensure that all equipment will be suitable for its intended purpose and capable of producing valid results.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control labeling activities
deficiencies.description: SOP07-009 Version 12 (v12), titled “Dispensing and Packaging Cytomark Products Containing TransFix” dated January 15, 2021, describes your firm’s process for labeling and packaging the product.
deficiencies.evidence: No record of performance qualification (PQ) phase. Mrs. Nicki Kaenzig, Chief Scientific Officer/Management Representative had agreed to correct the deficiency.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide a copy of their procedure for review and evidence of training. Your firm should also conduct a systemic review to determine if other equipment has been appropriately qualified.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm’s response and concluded that it is not adequate.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/API/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient CGMP Deviations
deficiencies.description: Failure to ensure that, for each batch of API, appropriate laboratory tests are conducted to determine conformance to specifications.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm did not demonstrate that you adequately test incoming lots of (b)(4), which is used to manufacture (b)(4). For example, you relied on your vendor's COA for (b)(4) testing that did not include potentially harmful impurities (e.g., (b)(4)).
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of the design and control of your firm's manufacturing operations, with a detailed and thorough review of all hazards that may impact chemical and microbiological attributes of your APIs.

deficiencies.title: Finished Drug Product CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: Your (b)(4) system used to manufacture (b)(4) incorporated as a component of your drug products was not designed and maintained appropriately for its intended use. For example, your (b)(4) system included a dead leg and was not continuously circulating, which could foster the development of biofilms.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. Describe your program for process performance qualification (PPQ), and ongoing monitoring of both intra-batch and inter-batch variation to ensure a continuing state of control.

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile, and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm supplied aseptically filled (b)(4) to the US without adequate validation of your aseptic process. For example, during the inspection you stated that you have not performed media fills.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide details about the improvements you are making to your facility and processes to ensure the sterility of products purporting to be sterile.

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: ...

metadata.company_name: King Systems Corp. dba Ambu, Inc.
metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/PMA/Adulterated/Misbranded

deficiencies.title: Adulterated device
deficiencies.description: The King LTS-D branded supraglottic airway with gastric access lumen is adulterated because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect.
deficiencies.required_action: Please remove these unsupported performance claims from your website, or please provide a 510(k) with this proposed indication for use.

deficiencies.title: Misbranded device
deficiencies.description: The device is misbranded because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the device into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act.
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide a 510(k) with this proposed indication for use.

deficiencies.title: Unsupported performance claims
deficiencies.description: Statements on your website promote the device with an intended use for pediatric populations weighing less than 5 kg, 5-12kg, 12-25kg, and 25-35 kg. However, the cleared indication for use for your device is for adult patients.
deficiencies.required_action: Please remove these unsupported performance claims from your website, or please provide a 510(k) with this proposed indication for use.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain informed consent
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain informed consent in accordance with the provisions of 21 CFR part 50 [21 CFR 312.60 and 21 CFR 50.20].
deficiencies.evidence: Subject (b)(6) was enrolled in Protocol (b)(4), before the collection of optional aqueous humor samples.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to obtain legally effective informed consent
deficiencies.description: You failed to obtain legally effective informed consent for Subject (b)(6), who was enrolled in Protocol (b)(4), before the collection of optional aqueous humor samples.
deficiencies.evidence: Subject (b)(6) underwent the collection of aqueous humor from the study eye on March 5, 2024 (Week 20), even though the subject declined consent on September 26 and October 18, 2023, for the collection and submission of optional aqueous humor samples.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies with your clinical study of an investigational drug.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Notice of Unlawful Sale of Unapproved Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drugs
deficiencies.description: www.cheaptrustedpharmacy.com introduces into interstate commerce certain drugs for which the FDA-approved version is subject to a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program, is an opioid, semaglutide, an injectable, and/or is indicated to treat serious conditions such as HIV/AIDS or cancer.
deficiencies.evidence: Evidence obtained from your website establishing that these products are drugs intended for human use (as defined in 21 CFR 201.128) includes the claim 'Indication: Multiple myeloma, Lepra reaction' and categorization 'Life Saving Drugs / Anti Cancer'.
deficiencies.required_action: cease offering any unapproved and misbranded drugs for sale to U.S. consumers

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drugs
deficiencies.description: A drug is misbranded under section 502(f)(1) of the FD&C Act if its labeling fails to bear adequate directions for use.
deficiencies.evidence: Because the aforementioned drugs are prescription drugs intended for conditions that are not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by a layperson, adequate directions cannot be written such that a layperson can use the products safely for their intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: cease offering these drugs for sale to U.S. consumers

conclusion: FDA is sending this warning letter to www.cheaptrustedpharmacy.com because of the inherent risks to consumers who purchase misbranded and unapproved new drugs.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceutical/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish an Adequate Quality Control Unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish an adequate quality control unit with the responsibility and authority to approve or reject all components, drug product containers, closures, in-process materials, packaging materials, labeling, and drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Establish Laboratory Controls
deficiencies.description: You did not adequately establish laboratory controls to ensure all drug products you manufactured possessed appropriate quality attributes, including microbiological quality and safety.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices and a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Drug Product Testing
deficiencies.description: The records and information you provided indicate that you did not test numerous drug product batches for Burkholderia cepacia complex (BCC) organisms, which is a known organism of concern in drugs containing docusate sodium.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of the design and control of your firm's manufacturing operations, with a detailed and thorough review of all microbiological hazards.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conduct Sterility Testing
deficiencies.description: You did not adequately study sterility testing in your finished drug products to evaluate the ability of the sterilization system to inhibit the growth of certain objectionable organisms.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of your laboratory practices and a detailed plan to remediate and evaluate the effectiveness of your laboratory system.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Conduct Microbiological Testing
deficiencies.description: You did not test microbiological quality (total counts and identification of bioburden to detect any objectionable microbes) of each batch, which is a critical control point in your manufacturing process.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive assessment of the design and control of your firm's manufacturing operations, with a detailed and thorough review of all microbiological hazards.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Compounding Pharmacy/Adulterated Drug Products

deficiencies.title: Failure to Meet the Conditions of Section 503B
deficiencies.description: Drug products produced by your facility failed to meet the conditions of section 503B.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Your facility compounded drug products using a bulk drug substance from (b)(4), which is not a registered establishment under section 510 of the FDCA.
2. Some of your facility's drug products, such as (b)(4) and Glutathione Solution 2000mg/10ml (200mg/ml), did not include the following information on the container: information to facilitate adverse event reporting: www.fda.gov/medwatch and 1-800-FDA-1088.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: CGMP violations at your facility, that caused your drug product(s) to be adulterated within the meaning of section 501(a)(2)(B) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Your firm failed to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications, whether or not the batch has already been distributed (21 CFR 211.192).
2. Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess (21 CFR 211.100(a)).
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: Adulterated Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Drug products intended or expected to be sterile were prepared, packed, or held under insanitary conditions.
deficiencies.evidence:
1. Aseptic operators reaching into the ISO 5 laminar flow hood past their elbows during aseptic production.
2. Your media fills were not performed under the most challenging or stressful conditions.
3. Your firm failed to perform adequate smoke studies under dynamic conditions to demonstrate unidirectional airflow within the ISO 5 area.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct these deficiencies

deficiencies.title: Unapproved New Drug Products
deficiencies.description: You do not have any FDA-approved applications on file for drug products that you compound.
deficiencies.required_action: Obtain FDA approval

deficiencies.title: Misbranded Drug Products
deficiencies.description: Your compounded drug products are intended for conditions not amenable to self-diagnosis and treatment by individuals who are not medical practitioners.
deficiencies.required_action: Correct the labeling

conclusion: Based on this inspection, it appears you produced drugs that violate the FDCA. We have reviewed your facility's response to the Form FDA 483. We acknowledge that on September 30, 2024, your firm initiated a voluntary recall of one lot of Ascorbic Acid Solution for Injection 25,000mg/50mL (500mg/mL), 50mL single use vial, due to the presence of glass particulates.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: Premarket Approval/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: The TabloCart with Prefiltration is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, or an approved application for an investigational device exemption under section 520(g) of the Act.
deficiencies.evidence: The evidence and marketing materials reviewed at the inspection and found on your website [www.outsetmedical.com] on March 15, 2023, indicate that the TabloCart with Prefiltration is a device.
deficiencies.required_action: You have not submitted any premarket notification to the Agency for the TabloCart with Prefiltration.

deficiencies.title: Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: The Tablo Hemodialysis System is adulterated under section 501(f)(1)(B) of the Act, because your firm does not have an approved application for premarket approval (PMA) in effect pursuant to section 515(a) of the Act, or an approved application for an investigational device exemption under section 520(g) of the Act.
deficiencies.evidence: The evidence reviewed at the inspection and marketing materials found on your website [www.outsetmedical.com] on May 11, 2023, show promotion of the Tablo Hemodialysis System for continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) treatment.
deficiencies.required_action: You currently have clearance for the Tablo Hemodialysis System under K223248 for the following indications: ... However, you confirmed that CRRT is distinct from the cleared indications for use, device specifications, and treatment modalities for the Tablo Hemodialysis System, and that PIRRT transitions to CRRT at [b](4) of use 1. Patients who require CRRT are typically hospitalized with acute illness resulting in acute kidney injury and possible severe hemodynamic instability, and the devices that provide CRRT treatment have unique features to enable continuous treatment (> 24 hours) for this patient population.

conclusion: Your firm should take prompt action to address any violations identified in this letter. Failure to adequately address this matter may result in regulatory action being initiated by the FDA without further notice.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: CGMP Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and follow appropriate written procedures that are designed to prevent microbiological contamination of drug products purporting to be sterile and that include validation of all aseptic and sterilization processes.
deficiencies.evidence: During our inspection, our investigators observed specific violations including, but not limited to, the following.
deficiencies.required_action: Comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Equipment
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to use equipment in the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of drug products that is of appropriate design, adequate size, and suitably located to facilitate operations for its intended use and for its cleaning and maintenance.
deficiencies.evidence: You use (b)(4) as a component to manufacture your sterile and non-sterile OTC drug products. Your (b)(4) system is inadequately designed and maintained for its intended use.
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed remediation plan with timelines to address the findings of the contamination hazards risk assessment

deficiencies.title: Lack of process validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm lacked adequate qualification of equipment and validation of processes used to manufacture your drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: The “current validated” sterilization manufacturing process provided in your response is fundamentally flawed as evidenced by: insanitary conditions in your facility; use of a (b)(4) not sufficient for sterile (b)(4)); reliance on a manually intensive process; lack of batch sterility testing (your firm performed “CFU testing”).
deficiencies.required_action: Process validation evaluates the soundness of design and state of control of a process throughout its lifecycle.

deficiencies.title: Lack of cleaning validation
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to conduct cleaning validation studies to demonstrate that your cleaning, disinfection, and sterilization practices, as applicable, are adequate to remove contaminants and residues from your drug product manufacturing equipment.
deficiencies.evidence: Your procedure for cleaning production equipment used to manufacture (b)(4) drug products, “Special Cleaning Procedure For Other Equipment,” relies on (b)(4) (e.g., (b)(4)) as the “sterilization” agent.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive remediation plan for the design, control, and maintenance of the (b)(4) system

conclusion: In response to this letter, provide: Comprehensive risk assessment of all contamination hazards with respect to your aseptic processes, equipment, and facilities...

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to adhere to these requirements. Examples of this failure include, but are not limited to, the following: ... According to the protocol, the maximum dose allowed for these subjects was 1.5 mg/day. As a result, Subjects (b)(6) and (b)(6) received daily doses of (b)(4) that exceeded the daily dose allowed by the protocol.
deficiencies.evidence: Examples include: ... Subject (b)(6), a 9-year-old female, was administered 3 mg/day of (b)(4) oral solution from August 16, 2023, through August 31, 2023. b. Subject (b)(6), a 9-year-old male, was administered 3 mg/day of (b)(4) oral solution from August 16, 2023, through September 6, 2023.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies with your clinical study of an investigational drug.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish adequate written responsibilities and procedures applicable to the quality control unit
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to ensure the reliability and integrity of quality control data during component release testing at your facility.
deficiencies.evidence: Biometric access records documented that the responsible analysts were not physically present at the facility during testing, despite their absence, they documented both the testing process and results as if they had conducted the analyses.
deficiencies.required_action: Implement a Data Integrity Remediation Plan, which includes appointing a Data Integrity Lead to oversee and enhance compliance efforts.

deficiencies.title: Failure to thoroughly investigate any unexplained discrepancy or failure of a batch or any of its components to meet any of its specifications
deficiencies.description: Your investigations into discrepancies and out-of-specification (OOS) results lacked adequate scientific rationale to support root cause determinations.
deficiencies.evidence: A. (b)(4) mg tablets, batches (b)(4) and (b)(4), exhibited OOS and out-of-trend (OOT) results during the dissolution test by high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) at the 18-month and 3-month long-term stability timepoints, respectively.
deficiencies.required_action: Perform an extended evaluation across the rest of the laboratory to evaluate your laboratory practices and controls.

deficiencies.title: Failure to handle failing, out-of-specification, out-of-trend, or other unexpected results
deficiencies.description: Your response is inadequate. The conclusions of your OOS investigations lack the necessary rigor and scope to thoroughly identify root causes, assess the extent of deviations, and evaluate their impact on drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: You repeatedly accepted these conclusions, despite insufficient justification.
deficiencies.required_action: Conduct a retrospective, independent review of all invalidated OOS results for U.S. products for the last 3 years from the initial date of inspection and provide a report summarizing the findings of the analysis.

deficiencies.title: Failure to operate an effective quality system
deficiencies.description: Significant findings in this letter demonstrate that your firm does not operate an effective quality system in accord with CGMP.
deficiencies.evidence: Your QU is not enabled to exercise proper authority and/or has insufficiently implemented its responsibilities.
deficiencies.required_action: Immediately and comprehensively assess your company's global manufacturing operations to ensure that your systems, processes, and products conform to FDA requirements.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Clinical Investigator/Sponsor

deficiencies.title: Failure to ensure that the investigation was conducted according to the investigational plan
deficiencies.description: You failed to ensure that subjects met all eligibility criteria before enrollment in the study.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically: a. Subject (b)(6)’s screening visit blood pressure was 103 / 72 mmHg, which was outside the protocol-specified range for eligibility... b. Subject (b)(6)’s screening visit blood pressure was 163 / 92 mmHg, and the subject’s eGFR was 41 ml/min/1.73m², both of which fell outside the protocol-specified ranges for eligibility...
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

deficiencies.title: Failure to promptly report all changes in research activity to the Institutional Review Board (IRB)
deficiencies.description: You failed to promptly report all changes in research activity to the IRB, including a change in study design from a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized clinical trial to a two-part clinical trial that included an initial pilot feasibility study of 10 to 12 participants, followed by a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized clinical trial.
deficiencies.evidence: Specifically: for Protocol (b)(4), you did not promptly report to the IRB that the study design of the clinical investigation was modified from a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized clinical trial to a two-part clinical trial that included an initial pilot feasibility study of 10 to 12 participants, followed by a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized clinical trial.
deficiencies.required_action: You should address any deficiencies and establish procedures to ensure that any ongoing or future studies comply with FDA regulations.

conclusion: This letter is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of deficiencies with your clinical study of an investigational drug. It is your responsibility to ensure adherence to each requirement of the law and relevant FDA regulations.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Misbranded and/or Unapproved Device Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm made changes to the device without submission of a new 510(k). You changed from using the cleared Orchestrate 3D to the non-cleared iRok OrthoRx 4.0 for the development of the treatment plans for your UniSmile Clear Aligner System.
deficiencies.evidence: Your firm did not submit any new 510(k) for the referenced modification to the UniSmile Clear Aligner System.
deficiencies.required_action: Submit a new premarket notification submission under section 510(k) of the Act, 21 U.S.C. § 360(k), to FDA at least 90 days before you proposed to begin the introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution of the modified UniSmile Clear Aligner System.

deficiencies.title: Quality System Regulation (QSR) Violations
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to adequately establish and maintain procedures for receiving, reviewing, and evaluating complaints by a formally designated unit as required by 21 CFR 820.198.
deficiencies.evidence: 11 out of 13 complaint records reviewed during our inspection did not include an evaluation for MDR reportability nor an evaluation to determine if an investigation was necessary.
deficiencies.required_action: Review and update procedures to comply with MDR reporting requirements within 6 weeks of your response and conduct training.

deficiencies.title: Failure to validate software used as part of the quality system for its intended use according to an established protocol, as required by 21 CFR 820.70(i)
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to validate your complaint handling system software.
deficiencies.evidence: You have not provided evidence of the effectiveness of this complaint handling system.
deficiencies.required_action: Generate a software validation report, approach, testing methodology, and results of the validation within 6 weeks of your response to FDA.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements, including evaluation of suppliers, contractors, and consultants, as required by 21 CFR 820.50(a)
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to define the type and extent of control to be exercised over supplier services.
deficiencies.evidence: You only provided a Manufacturing and Supply Agreement with Xi’an Henghui Technologies Co., Ltd.
deficiencies.required_action: Review the supplier evaluation process and update necessary documents as soon as possible, and conduct auditing of all suppliers, within 12 weeks of the response.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes to ensure that the specified requirements continue to be met, as required by 21 CFR § 820.75(b), and failure to ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure, including the validation of major equipment, as required by 21 CFR 820.75(a)
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish and maintain procedures for monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes.
deficiencies.evidence: You have not provided a procedure that addresses major equipment qualification or process validation for your 3D printing process.
deficiencies.required_action: Provide a procedure that addresses major equipment qualification or process validation for your 3D printing process.

conclusion: We reviewed your firm’s response and concluded that the adequacy of your firm’s response cannot be determined at this time.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Water System
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to establish adequate written procedures for production and process control designed to assure that the drug products you manufacture have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate Water System
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive independent remediation plan for the design, control, and maintenance of the water system. A detailed risk assessment addressing the potential effects of the observed water system failures on the quality of all drug product lots currently in U.S. distribution or within expiry.

deficiencies.title: Inadequate Process Validation
deficiencies.description: You failed to provide sufficient data to demonstrate you have adequately validated your manufacturing processes used to manufacture your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.evidence: Inadequate Process Validation
deficiencies.required_action: A detailed summary of your validation program for ensuring a state of control throughout the product lifecycle, along with associated procedures. A timeline for performing appropriate PPQ for each of your marketed drug products.

deficiencies.title: Failure to Test Samples of Each Component
deficiencies.description: Your firm failed to test samples of each component for identity and conformity with all appropriate written specifications for purity, strength, and quality.
deficiencies.evidence: Failure to Test Samples of Each Component
deficiencies.required_action: A commitment to provide DEG and EG test results, no later than 30 calendar days from the date of this letter, from testing retains for all lots of high-risk drug components used in the manufacture of drug products.

deficiencies.title: Products Contain Ingredients at Risk for Diethylene Glycol or Ethylene Glycol (DEG or EG) Contamination
deficiencies.description: You manufacture multiple drugs that contain glycerin. The use of ingredients contaminated with DEG or EG has resulted in various lethal poisoning incidents in humans worldwide.
deficiencies.evidence: Products Contain Ingredients at Risk for Diethylene Glycol or Ethylene Glycol (DEG or EG) Contamination
deficiencies.required_action: A full risk assessment for drug products that are within expiry which contain any ingredient at risk for DEG or EG contamination (including, but not limited to, glycerin). Take prompt and appropriate actions to determine the safety of all lots of the component(s) and any related drug product that could contain DEG or EG.

deficiencies.title: Insanitary Conditions
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures homeopathic and over-the-counter (OTC) drug products. Your drug products are adulterated under section 501(a)(2)(A) of the FD&C Act because they were prepared, packed, and held under insanitary conditions.
deficiencies.evidence: Apparent rodent excreta pellets throughout the finished product storage area, as well as areas containing drug packaging and packaging containers (boxes and uncapped vials).

deficiencies.evidence: Your QU failed to provide adequate oversight for the manufacture of your OTC drug products.
deficiencies.required_action: A comprehensive assessment and remediation plan to ensure your QU is given the authority and resources to effectively function.

The device is also misbranded because your firm did not notify the agency of its intent to introduce the device into commercial distribution, as required by section 510(k) of the Act.

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)
metadata.subject: Deviations/CFR/Regulations for Human Cells, Tissues & Cellular Products (HCT/Ps)

deficiencies.title: Failure to screen a donor of reproductive cells or tissue by reviewing the donor's relevant medical records for risk factors for and clinical evidence of relevant communicable disease agents and diseases
deficiencies.description: Your firm's Donor Medical History Interview Form is used as a relevant medical record to determine donor eligibility. However, the form does not include screening for all conditions and/or behaviors that increase a donor's relevant communicable disease risk.
deficiencies.evidence: a. Question 5 states, “Have you had sex in the preceding 12 months with any person described in the previous 4 items in this section or with any person known or suspected to have HIV infection, clinically active hepatitis B infection, or hepatitis C infection?” To adequately and appropriately reduce the risk of transmission of these diseases, the question needs to ask about hepatitis B infection (both active and inactive) and clinically active (symptomatic) hepatitis C infection.

deficiencies.title: Failure to determine as ineligible a donor who is identified as having a risk factor for, or clinical evidence of, any of the relevant communicable disease agents or diseases
deficiencies.description: For example: Directed semen donor (b)(6) answered “yes” to Question #26 on the Spanish version of the Donor Medical History Interview Form, “Have you lived cumulatively for 5 years or more in Europe from 1980 until the present?”

deficiencies.title: Failure to determine whether a donor is eligible based upon the results of donor screening in accordance with 21 CFR 1271.75 and donor testing in accordance with 21 CFR 1271.80 and 21 CFR 1271.85
deficiencies.description: For example: Directed oocyte donor (b)(6) was determined “eligible” on February 20, 2025, but the results of donor testing were not received until February 22, 2025.

deficiencies.title: Failure to collect a donor specimen for testing for relevant communicable diseases at the time of recovery of cells or tissue from the donor; or up to seven days before or after recovery
deficiencies.description: For example: Semen was collected from directed donor (b)(6) on (b)(6). However, a specimen for communicable disease testing was collected from the donor on (b)(6).

deficiencies.title: Failure to include in the summary of records a statement noting the reason(s) for the determination of ineligibility
deficiencies.description: For example: A review of the donor records collected during the inspection, revealed that at least 21 directed semen donors were determined “ineligible”, however, the summary of records does not state the reason for the determination of ineligibility.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain procedures for all steps performed in testing, screening, determining donor eligibility, and complying with all other requirements of Subpart C “Donor Eligibility”

metadata.issuing_office: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research (CDER)
metadata.subject: Unlawful Sale of Unapproved and Misbranded Drugs to United States Consumers Over the Internet

deficiencies.title: Misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including semaglutide and tirzepatide. Your claims concerning these products are false or misleading under sections 502(a) and 502(bb) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA), resulting in products being introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce in violation of section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website claims that your compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide products are the same as FDA-approved products when they are not. This implies that your products are FDA-approved, which is false or misleading.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product). Failure to adequately address any violations may result in legal action without further notice, including, without limitation, seizure and injunction.

deficiencies.title: Introduction or delivery of misbranded drugs
deficiencies.description: The introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of these misbranded products is a prohibited act under section 301(a) of the FDCA.
deficiencies.evidence: Your website offers various compounded drug products, including semaglutide and tirzepatide. These products are not FDA-approved and your claims imply that they are FDA-approved, which is false or misleading.
deficiencies.required_action: You should take immediate action to address any violations (including, for example, ceasing and desisting from using the language cited above that misbrands the product). metadata.issuing_office: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
metadata.subject: CGMP/QSR/Medical Devices/Adulterated

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to control the design of the device in order to ensure that specified design requirements are met
deficiencies.description: Your firm manufactures various implantable bone grafting materials, including the OsseoConduct, Socket Graft, Immediate Graft, and Socket Graft Injectable, which require design controls and you have not established design control procedures.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Your firm's “Quality Management Systems Manual,” dated June 1, 2017, includes high level requirements indicating design activities will be conducted and meet the requirements outlined in 21 CFR 820.30; however, this manual does not include procedures on describing how these activities will be conducted or documented.
b. You verbally confirmed your firm's only design control procedures were your “Design Input Requirements,” SOP 010-02, dated June 1, 2017. However, these procedures do not include procedures for design output, design reviews, design verification, design validation, design transfer, or changes, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(d) through (i).
c. Your firm's “Design Input Requirements,” SOP 010-02, dated June 1, 2017, does not include a mechanism for addressing ambiguous, incomplete, or conflicting requirements, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(c).
d. Your firm has not established and maintained procedures for design verification, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(f). For example, your firm's “Quality Management Systems Manual,” dated June 1, 2017, states design verification will be conducted to ensure design outputs have satisfied design input requirements.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain adequate procedures for design control, including design output, design reviews, design verification, design validation, design transfer, or changes, as required by 21 CFR 820.30(d) through (i).

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately ensure that when the results of a process cannot be fully verified by subsequent inspection and test, the process shall be validated with a high degree of assurance and approved according to established procedure
deficiencies.description: Your firm uses a sterilization process to sterilize your Oral Bond and Socket Graft devices. Your firm does not have records demonstrating these processes were validated as set forth in your firm's “Process Validation,” SOP 018 and as required by 21 CFR 820.75(b)(1).
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Your firm’s “Process Validation,” SOP 018, revision 2, dated June 1, 2017, establishes high level requirements for process validation; however, these procedures do not establish any specific requirements for the sterilization process validation for each of your devices.
b. Your firm does not have records demonstrating these processes were validated as set forth in your firm’s “Process Validation,” SOP 018 and as required by 21 CFR 820.75(b)(1).
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain adequate procedures for process validation, including specific requirements for the sterilization process validation for each of your devices.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that all purchased or otherwise received product and services conform to specified requirements
deficiencies.description: Your firm's “Purchasing & Suppliers,” SOP 410, Version 0, dated June 1, 2017, requires suppliers to be evaluated and reviewed as part of a continuous and iterative process. However, your firm did not provide any records to support that these activities have been conducted.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Your firm's “Purchasing & Suppliers,” SOP 410, Version 0, dated June 1, 2017, requires suppliers to be evaluated and reviewed as part of a continuous and iterative process.
b. You did not provide any records to support that these activities have been conducted.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain adequate procedures for purchasing control, including supplier evaluation and review, as required by 21 CFR 820.50.

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures for acceptance activities
deficiencies.description: Section 9 of your device history records (DHR) identifies specific acceptance criteria for each device. However, you do not have records required by 21 CFR 820.80(e) demonstrating these acceptance activities were conducted or documented for eight out of nine DHRs reviewed.
deficiencies.evidence:
a. Section 9 of your device history records (DHR) identifies specific acceptance criteria for each device.
b. You do not have records required by 21 CFR 820.80(e) demonstrating these acceptance activities were conducted or documented for eight out of nine DHRs reviewed, as follows:
a. Socket Graft Plus lot 061422
b. OsseoConduct lot 060822
c. BioDensification lot 072622
d. Ridge Graft lot 060922
e. Immediate Graft lot 100322
f. Sinus Graft lot 072622
g. Socket Graft lot 061322
h. Socket Graft Injectable lot 090722
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should establish and maintain adequate procedures for acceptance activities, including inspections, tests, or other verification activities, as required by 21 CFR 820.80(a).

deficiencies.title: Failure to establish and maintain adequate procedures to ensure that equipment is routinely calibrated, inspected, checked, and maintained

conclusion: We reviewed your firm's response dated November 21, 2022, and conclude it is not adequate.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately establish and maintain procedures for implementing corrective and preventive action
deficiencies.description: Your firm's procedure titled CAPA System (Corrective and Preventive Actions), ITS PB-7.6-2 Edition 9 Effective June 17, 2021, does not include adequate requirements to ensure investigations of the cause of nonconformities, or adequate requirements to ensure the implementing and recording changes in methods and procedures needed to correct and prevent identified quality problems and corrective and preventive actions.
deficiencies.evidence: Your revised procedure is accompanied by documentation (i.e., signatures on Form F-6.2.2.2) of training to four 'management staff'. However, you have not provided evidence of training of additional personnel directly responsible for assuring the quality of product or the prevention of product quality problems.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm should provide documentation of training of all relevant personnel responsible for assuring the quality of products. Also, confirm that you have conducted review of prior CAPA(s) per the revised procedure and taken necessary actions to ensure that the revised procedure is adequately implemented, including CAPA investigations are adequately documented.

deficiencies.title: Failure to maintain adequate device history records (DHR's)
deficiencies.description: Your firm did not maintain the primary identification label and labeling used for each production unit in your DHR as required by 21 CFR 820.184 (e). During walkthrough of your facility, the FDA investigator observed that your firm 'do check the accuracy of the label prior to initiating labeling operations'. However, the FDA investigator also noted that 'this is not documented in the DHR'.
deficiencies.required_action: Your firm's future response should address what systemic measures were taken to address this deficiency and provide evidence of implementation of your corrective actions.

deficiencies.title: Failure to adequately develop, maintain, and implement written MDR procedures
deficiencies.description: The procedure does not establish internal systems that provide for timely and effective identification, communication, and evaluation of events that may be subject to MDR requirements, as required by 21 CFR 803.17(a)(1). For example: The procedure omits the definitions from 21 CFR Part 803.3 for the terms 'become aware' and 'caused or contributed', and the definition for the term 'reasonably suggests', found in 803.20(c)(1).
deficiencies.required_action: Please provide your revised procedure incorporating the missing procedural requirements outlined above. Also, confirm that you have retrospectively reviewed relevant MDRs to ensure appropriate actions are taken per the revised procedure.

deficiencies.required_action: Provide a comprehensive, independent assessment of your overall system for investigating deviations, discrepancies, complaints, out-of-specification results, and failures. Provide a detailed action plan to remediate this system.

metadata.company_name: Central Coast Multispecialty Medical Group, Inc.
